# Thread: Caroline's journal

### caroline — 2013-01-14T04:25:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-70635>

I have never thought of doing a journal before but this morning I thought I really should be keeping track of all this stuff. I love reading everyone's journal and I learn so much from them.

I want to be fully engaged and 100% in. I will retest in about two months and I want to give it my very best effort and really see what a difference I can make for myself.

I want to keep track of light - really big for me
I want to go to the ocean every day and at least walk along the beach and hopefully swim in the rockpool too. I want to C/T every second day - Darleen is definitely my inspiration for this - such a rockstar!

I am so grateful for this forum and all the wonderful and diverse people here working on their own masterpiece. We have one life to live ... and live it we shall. This is an amazing opportunity that Dr. Kruse has given us and I want to make the most of it. Thanks doesn't seem nearly enough....

### Charlotte — 2013-01-14T06:12:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-70641>

great idea, Caroline. I look forward to reading it. I love everybodies success stories too, they are so inspiring. And it keeps amazing me, what you can do for yourself with only a few changes. Last night I watched a commercial on TV on weight watchers. This skinny, famous actress was marvelling how well she was doing on WW. I really had to laugh. As long as I knew her, she has never been anything else but skinny. And my collegue has been on it since she is working in the company and thats well over 5 years. She never lost anything since. Now she does hCG and she is losing. Now she wants to try Epi-Paleo too, because she has seen me lose all that weight and inbetween eating BAB and all the fatty stuff without gaining.

### Martin — 2013-01-14T10:00:52+0200

Love your goals you listed!

Thanks for all your encouragement to all of your fellow members. It means a lot.

### caroline — 2013-01-14T17:15:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-70726>

Thankyou so much everyone - it feels so good to be here - I feel more fully engaged and accountable. Actually what started this - when I was talking to Dr. K. - I was asking about C/T and he simply said ....well ... you aren't doing it enough... and I thought to myself - no - I am not. So let's get this show on the road!
I really need to work on my sleep. The last few nites I have had a really good sleep and woke up feeling great - but last nite was pretty poor and I am kinda dragging.
This hasn't happened in a long time. I had 2 mugs of tea after 6pm and I wonder if this is what did it? I really enjoy my BPC but I think I should give it up. I still haven't been able to watch the Jan webinar.
It is an absolutely stunning day here. I had mentioned on "our successes" thread that I had bought a tank top for the first time ever ....and yesterday I actually wore it at the beach. I hope I wasn't too scary

I don't really care much anymore about what people think of how I look......I really do care about how I feel about myself tho.
Of course - if I run into a hot older guy on the beach - I might change my mind! Altho guys my age are in walking frames and probably not on the beach

### caroline — 2013-01-17T04:44:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71169>

yesterday
swim in rockpool
long walk on beach - but no C/T - the day kinda got away from me..

today
swim in rock pool
walk on beach and now I am going to go and lay under some bags of ice for awhile

I had a great sleep last nite - fell asleep pretty fast and don't remember waking up at all until about 5:30.
I unplugged my clock radio - wonder if that has something to do with it????
I finally listened to the Webinar - Dan sent me a copy - now I have to listen to it 19 more times!
I \[stupidly\] missed the first 40 minutes of the Q&A - I can't believe I did that! I was so concerned about getting the time right - I didn't check the date! Are the emails coming later or is it just my imagination?
I seem to be really, really hungry lately - don't know where that is coming from....

### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-17T20:12:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71307>

Umm, bods on the beach. The first was an over 60s guy with a huge, I mean, HUGE pot belly. He dipped in to the sea and got out after about 2 minutes. Then another over 60s guy with a dog very interested in my dog who was much more interested in putting her tennis ball in the hole she was digging. More over 60s guys, none of them worth a second look. Then yes, a lovely young man, 20s with dog and a lovely young woman. Obviously a couple. Followed by another guy joining me in the water - he stayed all of 60 seconds. Dived in, swam 5 strokes, then out. Very wrinkly over 70 I would say. I hope your swims have had more inviting humans to look at than mine were this morning.

I've been dealing with sudden hunger pangs by eating avocado - not sure if that is a helpful idea for you.

Go well today, Caroline.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-17T21:22:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71318>

Oh dear. Reminds me of those birthday cards that say "What we're looking for" featuring a major piece of eye candy on the front and then you open it up and its says "What's looking for us." and it's pretty much what you just described. And just to be fair, they are found on both sides of the aisle.

### caroline — 2013-01-18T00:03:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71337>

Aussie Nana - I think I would change beaches pronto! The eye candy here is great - all the young people look spectacular. There was an old guy in a budgie smuggler yesterday - dear Lord ---UGH! It is 44 degrees right now - I guess I will wait till later to go and see what's cooking - altho - in this heat it is likely to be people.
actually I had an avocado yesterday for the first time in a long time and I swear when I have an apple I am much hungrier ..... but why oh why do I crave apples????
It is just crazy! It took a long time for the diet coke cravings to go away - but apples - I always want apples sooooo bad.....
Darleen - I saw an older woman yesterday - in a bikini......she was very rotund ... lots of rolls of fat ...but she seemed quite content strutting her stuff.
It gave me the idea to get a bikini - I have never ever owned one by the way .... but I thought it would be great - when it gets cooler and I can go down to a more secluded area of the beach and take my top off. I figure if I throw a towel over my head no one will know who it is and I won't see them either! Perfect for the winter time I reckon...problem solved

### Inger — 2013-01-18T04:29:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71361>

So nice you have a journal now Caroline!

Go and buy that bikini NOW!

I can tell it is such a different feeling when you get some air and sun on your abdomen too, everything feels just much better! Get the tiniest bikini you can find, ok. The more sun you get on your body. Do not care about if people look, look at people instead! And the sky.. and the sea and the horizon.. and the trees and birds..Just think about how much healthier it is to get more skin exposed to sun and sand and air..huh. Have you found a secret place to tan naked yet, Caroline?

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-18T14:34:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71436>

Caroline!! You are such a hoot! That reminds me of one of our first New Year's eves out here at our current residence. DH was up watching tv and he heard a noise outside so he and Allie McDog went out to investigate. Foolish man. ANYway. The back of our house is one large, open room with engineered beams across the ceiling every few feet. They extend outside the wall. A raccoon had crawled up the faux rock fireplace wall and was sitting on the end of one of the beams trying to get to the bird feeder. When Hubby shone the flashlight on him the raccoon covered his eyes with his paws. "If I can't see you then you must not be able to see me." LOL.

Skip the towel over the head. If someone sees you just give 'em a thousand watt smile and wave. I can pretty much guarantee they won't be looking at your face anyway.

### endless — 2013-01-18T14:58:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71445>

> Skip the towel over the head. If someone sees you just give 'em a thousand watt smile and wave. I can pretty much guarantee they won't be looking at your face anyway.

Ha! Good point, Darleen!

I am a very self conscious person....I don't like showing my pudge, even to those who don't know me and don't care! I always admire people who wear what they want comfortably regardless of their size or shape! I'm working on being one of them

### caroline — 2013-01-19T02:29:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71558>

So Inger ... I not only have to buy a bikini ... it has to be really tiny too??? OMG
Darleen ... I chuckled all afternoon about that one! Have you ever noticed when you lay down you become slimmer and can suck your belly in!
Krista .... I love when I see someone on the beach that is a pudge and doesn't seem to care one bit ..... I don't know if I will ever get there tho. I think when your self esteem has been shattered - it is really hard to put all the pieces back together ..... at least in the rite order

I went to the beach last nite about 4:30 .. temp was 45 .... that is the teeniest bit warm! I walked for about an hour in the surf and then puddled around for another 30 minutes or so - and then laid under some bags of ice when I got home.

One thing that is great about going to the beach later ...... there is all these hot young guys in lycra .. with their surf boards ... very entertaining!
today it is about 21 - such a change from yesterday. I walked for about an hour and half in the surf - up to my waist.
I almost forgot Inger - I walked along way down the beach today - I think it goes for miles \[have to check that out one day\] and I found a spot that might be good for nude sunbathing. There is kind of a mound of sand and then more sand on the other side - it remains to be seen if I have nerve enough to do this!

I ran into an older women sitting on the rocks and got talking to her - I thought she might know what kind of rock it is. She told me she always comes and sits on the rocks because it makes her feel so much better and replenishes her energy. She used to care for abused women and she would bring them down to the ocean and tell them that she is there to care for them but they could do whatever they wanted. She said it was so heartwarming to see the change in them. Some would build sandcastles and some would swim and some would walk and they would leave feeling some peace and be replenished. I would guess that this lady was close to 80 - so she was doing this therapy all those years ago. It seems like we have been going backwards for the last number of years.

I had another fun converstion with a lady at the bus stop - we started talking about shoes - isn't that what you talk to strangers about?? somehow we got onto age and health - as you do - and she asked me to guess her age. She thought I was about the same age - I have 15 years on her. That was nice.
I usually walk home with groceries and I have taken to splitting them up in 2 bags and then swinging them as I am walking .... kind of kettlebells in motion. I was thinking of Gretchen today - she said she walked home from the bus top with her shoes off for grounding. My gosh - we are getting to be a strange bunch aren't we?? such fun - I love it

### Charlotte — 2013-01-19T13:18:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71605>

Well, Caroline, I actually wonder how old you are?
You seem so young somehow, but being a grandma you should be at least my age.
I would love to walk on the beach with you, get into the water and play. No such thing here, not even in the summer. The next beach is at least 800 km away from where I live and I love the ocean, always did. It was my nicest holiday as a kid when my parents finally decided that it was time for a change after 10 years of going into the mountains for vacation. Not that I did not like the Alps, but I craved for the ocean. I still remember every detail and some years ago I took my children to the same place and showed them where I had played, where we stayed. It still looked the same as I had remembered it. It was a small village called Diano Marina at the Riviera.
My friend is coming to dinner in a minute. She has been working all day today and is now coming to stay with me for the night. Her Mom died last weeks after many years of Alzheimer and having to be taken care of for everything. She turned 96 years just a few days before she died and I got to see her on Christmas for the last time. So I prepared a nice dinner, bone broth, stuffed huge mushrooms with minced meat and cheese and some strawberries with cream for desert. We will talk about her Mom and remember all the good times we had together when we were kids. My friend is a good narrator and she can mimick people that you laugh yourself silly,
So for me it is meat and greed in the kitchen now.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-19T13:51:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71610>

Excellent pun Charlotte! I wish my German were 1/4 as good as your English!

Caroline ... great stories. thanks for sharing them. How's your journey to optimal going?

### endless — 2013-01-19T15:03:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71631>

Your beach trips sound very relaxing....wish I had a beach nearby to visit! That makes total sense that she brought the abused women down to the rocks on the shore to replenish! See, sometimes we know, even if we don't know that we know.

### caroline — 2013-01-19T17:40:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71657>

Charlotte - I am pretty sure I am older than all of you.... don't know where all the years have gone....
It sounds like you will have a lovely evening remembering your friend' s mother.... it seems to me that you are a wonderful friend. It sounds like you have travelled all over? Melbourne? tell us more...
Krista - I think you are exactly right - it seems to me that long ago people used their natural instincts all the time ....when did we lose that I wonder...I remember reading an interesting story long ago correlating the rise of obesity to the introduction of supermarkets. All of a sudden food was available 24/7 and we were sold a bill of goods. Marketing genius - we are like lambs led to slaughter...
Darleen - I don't know how my journey is going actually - I feel crappy today for some reason. I have this constant ringing in my ear which is driving me crazy and I think maybe that is why I am nauseated. I stood on a small stool yesterday to fix a blind and I got really very dizzy. I did what Dr.K. suggested with the H2O2 but I have so much ringing now - I think I could interfere with planes flying overhead!
I seem to be having a problem with seafood now and the lamb heart last nite made me crazy. Ever since I got sick on seafood at Christmas I am feeling really squeamish and only want steak WTH
I forgot to tell you about another lady I met yesterday - I was walking home with groceries and all of a sudden I stopped \[I hate when people do that\] A lady behind me
said something about the darn hills never getting easier. As we talked she worried that I was okay and offered to help me - I told her I was okay but felt that I was stressing my mitochondria. She actually didn't blink an eyelash! and then we got talking about a great naturopath that she has ....

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-19T17:47:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71660>

I don't know if this is any help but when my ears start ringing it's my allergies acting up. You might also have picked up a bug. There's a nasty one going around here. big surprise ... it's winter here.

### diane — 2013-01-19T19:00:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71674>

My ears ring constantly - they have for about 7 years now. Sometimes louder, but it's always there. I notice that it gets louder when I eat things that I'm allergic to. But I still haven't been able to get rid of it. That's my goal - talk about annoying!

### ashryn — 2013-01-19T19:06:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71676>

Hey Caroline,
Fwiw, I think if you want steak, eat steak.. Maybe your body just needs something in your steaks right now. You can get back to the fish and raw later, but I reckon you should listen to your body when it is sending you such strong signals and you know steak isn't bad... Not like craving bread or something.

### Destiny — 2013-01-19T19:40:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71680>

Hi Caroline!
It will be nice to see your progress documented with tiny bikinis and all { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ! Ashryn is onto something with the steak. I also have days when I want something else than fish.

### janagram — 2013-01-19T20:22:47+0200

Hi, Caroline! So...a bikini is in yr future? You go! But I sorta like the idea of a tanned abdomen. Gotta be an improvement.

### caroline — 2013-01-20T03:13:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71765>

I just lost a big post ... hate, hate, hate that! Is there a way to retrieve a post?
I guess I will go looking for a bikini - I can't really imagine it - pretty scary! I will have to break out the shake and bake- at least you look better with some colpr!
I don't think I can trust my body to know what it needs - isn't that kinda a fallacy from all past diets?
I feel really crappy today - muscle ache, nausea, freezing, the runs - I even took a warm shower - don't tell anyone1
I am lying on the couch under a big duvet - so no stories ......
I am feeling overwhelmed by the blogs -gotta get busy.... Is everyone else keeping up okay?
IWANTADIETCOKESOBAD..... don't tell any one that either .... I am swearing you to secrecy...

### ashryn — 2013-01-20T03:32:24+0200

Oh man! I hope you feel better soon.

### caroline — 2013-01-20T03:40:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71770>

Thanks - me too. It sounds like the flu doesn't it? I don't know where I would have picked that up. It is going to be hard to get back to them especially since I forced myself to eat them - stupid!

### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-20T04:11:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71772>

Caroline I feel for you. Its terrible when you are trying so hard to do every thing right but somehow one isn't getting the results. ### caroline — 2013-01-20T05:02:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71773>

thanks AN....should be on the mend soon!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-20T09:04:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71782>

It does sound like the flu or some serious detoxing going on. I hope you're feeling better soon. I know what you mean about turning yourself off from a food. One night a few years ago we were having a BBQ with friends who are vegan and I ate a vegan sausage. I starting getting sick that night and was miserable with the flu for a few days later. I can't even look at those sausages to this day. Which isn't a bad thing I guess, considering the crap they're made with. But hopefully once you're feeling better you'll probably want the seafood again because it's good for you body.

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2013-01-20T09:48:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71787>

I hope you feel better soon Caroline!! Just do like you feel today, no pressure with food or Ct. Like Paula said, it might be so detox, which is good!!

### Destiny — 2013-01-20T09:55:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71791>

One day at the time, Caroline. Don't think about the food for later when you are sick.

If you feel that you need soda bubbles to help you with your upset stomach perhaps club soda with a bit of lemon would help? Have you tried a fennel tea? It really calms down your digestion. I hope you feel better soon.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-20T10:09:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71798>

Caroline, hope the worst is over and you feel better now.
About the bikini. So get one, you will enjoy it. I never dared wearing one, but many years ago I was on the psychology trip and did a semiar in Brussels. One of the "tasks" we had to do was thinking about an outfit, that was portraying what we disliked most. So I chose to dress up like an esotheric freak wearing a long pink dress, purple blouse, yellow scarf tied around my head. I made little Tarot cards and hung them on my belt together with a ball that I had covered with aluminum to make it look like a crystal ball and some other little tools these guys use. I looked terrifying, oh, yes, and I had red high heels. What I did not know was, that the seminar teacher would send us like that into the streets of Brussels. We all found that out, when we gathered in the meeting room. OMG, I should go outside like THIS?
I had no choice as the other alternative would have been to chicken out and that was not acceptable. So off into the streets we went, everbody by themselves of course, as part of the task was, to observe the reactions of the people around us and our reaction to theirs, and not to feel safe because being in a crowd of other freaks. So I walked down the streets in my outfit, at first very insecure and boy, you should have seens the reaction of the people. They looked at me as if I had just landed from another galaxy and the guys started whistling. They actually liked what they saw!!!
So I was picking up confidence and guess what, I was the last one to return.
I still like this story a lot, because one of the lessons for me was that you never know what the outcome of our actions are and often what we expect does not happen.
The begging did not go down so well, but that is another story.

### Inger — 2013-01-20T13:42:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71848>

Oh dear Caroline.. you will be well soon. I have those moments too when I feel not so high.. and then I might just take a loooong hot shower and then go for short dip in my ice hole, if I want to. I make no pressure on me at all. Sometimes we just need to rest and sometimes.. I need a lot of meat too!!! One day at work, it was a day I was feeling not so good.. and then I had one pound of raw elkburgers with coconut oil for breakfast, one pound of raw lamb, minced and with balsamic vinegar and EVOO added.. at work, and then almost one pound of raw salmon our chef made for me at work for dinner with marinated mushrooms. Oh.. I had so much energy in the end I could not believe it! It was a massive amount of food but I just needed it! I bet I was depleted with electrons and those magic foods gave me more than enough...lol So make you a HUGE steak now if you feel like it. But leave it as bloody as you can inside...

Sometimes I just want something cooked, and the I eat it. But I always cook very gently. It is not often but it happends..

You can buy you a tankini and when you lay down and your stomach looks so tiny, you can pull it high so your body gets more sun

I am confident the sun makes fat into muscles Caroline.. somehow.. at least that is how it feels to me in summer...

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-20T14:24:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71857>

Caroline I hope you're feeling better today. There's not much fun when my "twisted sister" is on the sidelines!

### caroline — 2013-01-20T17:57:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71899>

> **DarleenMB said**
>
> Caroline I hope you're feeling better today. There's not much fun when my "twisted sister" is on the sidelines!

### caroline — 2013-01-20T18:27:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71904>

Actually Inger - I have kind of made myself a tankini - well not made - but put together. I have a couple of pairs of very cute swim shorts and then I wear a compression like tank top and now I have an orange tank top on top - quite cute actually. I think as women age, not all of course - traditional swimsuits don't do us any favours.
I am feeling loads better today - thanks everyone. I guess I will just stick with eating whatever I can manage. I had some raw steak this morning and will cook a steak tonite - rare for sure. I don't understand tho - before - all I wanted was seafood and now it is very hard. I wanted to try really hard to bring my inflammation down before my next set of labs.
Destiny - soda with lemon would have been a good idea. I had a couple of big glasses of OJ that was left from when my grandsons were here not the best idea but I was getting really dehydrated. Soooo today is another day back to the plan stan!
Charlotte I love your stories! the other day when you talked about your life with that horror of a man you were married to - my heart was breaking for you - but in spite of everything - here you are - a HELLOFAWOMAN - adversity makes us so much stronger in so many ways but It is so sad that you didn't have an amazing love - but then again I don't know that do I????? You will have to tell us more.....
I still haven't read all of EMF1 - holy cow - I am so far behind........... Am I the only one struggling with all of this? .... it is so overwhelming...I have to stop going to the ocean and just sitting. I always bring stuff to read - but never seem to....
I was wondering if it was detox - but I am not sure if that makes sense - from what? ... have to think more about that...
I hope everyone has had a great day

p.s. I had another dream about Disney \[the place not the person\] last nite - it was a very fun sexy dream. The universe is trying to tell me something!

### diane — 2013-01-20T18:44:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71908>

Glad to hear you're feeling better! I've gone through phases as well where all I want is beef - then that changes and I want seafood. I think that if we are feeling for something, the body is telling us something and sometimes we need to listen to it.

I would stick to the ocean and work yourself through EMF 1 at your own pace! You're so lucky to have the ocean!

### caroline — 2013-01-20T19:10:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71913>

Thanks Diane - I have always adored the ocean - It feels like this is where I belong. We used to go to the Bahamas all the time and I can remember so clearly - getting off the plane and feeling like I was home. Funny tho -after we had made reservations - I would be really mad at myself and think that there are so many wonderful places in the world to explore and here we are back in the Bahamas - but as soon as I was on the tarmac I knew I was home and there was such a sense of peace and joy. Being in a swimsuit all day and barefoot and the soft caribbean breezes and the ocean and the calypso music and the Bahama Mamas\[drink\] - no wonder we would feel so much better - so in tune....I guess the only thing that wasn't optimum was the wonderful caribbean cocktails - but they were soooooo good! When I look back these beautiful people led such an unhurried life. They ate tons of seafood, loved music - there was always music going on and they danced very sensually. They always had a big smile on their face and love children. They didn't have much material stuff - but - really - they had everything. I can remember one Christmas a family we knew came to visit us at our hotel - they had kids he same age as ours. It was 2 in the afternoon and they still hadn't opened there presents. They had been to church and to visit relatives. I learned so much from these simple people....

### Destiny — 2013-01-20T19:34:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71921>

I copied that, Caroline, for myself, to remind myself what to strive for. It is so beautiful. Thank you!

### caroline — 2013-01-21T05:09:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71978>

I hope I am not boring everyone with my stories - but I guess it is a journal. I thought I was just going to enter my C/T etc. surprising turn of events! I just remembered another story from the Bahamas. We used to have the most lovely maid on our floor at the hotel and my girls used to love to spend part of the day helping Lorrie make beds (crazy I know) sometimes she would take them home with her - she had a whole houseful of her own plus grand kids. My girls thought it was heaven - there would be all these people singing and playing music and the baby slept in a dresser drawer!
I had a major long walk in the surf today and then had a cold shower and then laid in the tub under some bags of ice. I am still shivering.
I felt lousy yesterday and today I feel terrific ....
Gotta go and read....

### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-21T05:46:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71983>

I'm glad you're feeling better today.

And I like your stories. This is your journal, so story tell as much as you like.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-21T06:38:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71988>

I can relate very well, Caroline. This maid must have been a very warm hearted woman and she probably loved kids very much. It was probably like a fairytale for them. And the baby sleeping in the drawer. My oldest son slept in a woven washing basket.

### caroline — 2013-01-21T06:53:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71990>

I find the simple things in life are the most important ... keeps us grounded ..... where have I heard that before????

### Charlotte — 2013-01-21T07:00:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-71991>

yes and another said: all we need in life is food, clothes and shelter and everything we want beyond that is insanity

### caroline — 2013-01-22T02:53:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-72256>

This has been such a huge day ..... I came on the forum and JK had just posted his blog ... but I couldn't log on - this has happened a few times lately - no matter what I did I could not log on and I could see the blog sitting there. I don't know why but I went and got my ipad and got on right away and of course then sat down with a BPC and read and read and read. Pretty massive stuff with such huge implications for a lot in this community. So it looks like I am pretty good here - for me - the ocean has always intuitively been where I wanted to be. It is funny tho - today some friends came over to play with me in the ocean and have lunch sitting out on the grass - I had a great seafood salad by the way - I am definitely going beck to this place! anyway I am always completely mesmerised by the ocean every single day and I have been here for seven years. I was jumping out of my skin - so happy to share the day with my dear friends and - you know what - they completely take it for granted. They have lived in this area all their lives and take all this beauty for granted. When I asked them about it and said how I was completely over the moon about this place and they said - well isn't Canada like this??? There is a lot of beauty in Canada of course but it is this beach lifestyle 24/7 that has me mesmerised. My husband was born on Cape Breton Island in Nova Scotia and there is so much beauty there - I love it but there is something much different here - at least for me ....

So I played in the ocean for quite awhile and walked quite a bit and I will lay under some bags of ice in a bit
I seem to be getting very sleepy by 8:30 or 9:30 and then wake up at 4:30 - are we supposed to be getting more sleep than that? I wake up refreshed - not tired at all so I guess it should be okay.
I guess everyone's head is buzzing today - some are going to have to make massive changes to regain their health - it is going to be amazing to watch all of this unfold and see people get their life back. I always knew that this was a journey of a lifetime and we are lucky enough to have a front row seat .... Thanks Dr. Kruse .....you are the man.....

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-22T12:06:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-72296>

Cool...that is where I live, Cape Breton! I do love that we are surrounded by the ocean, especially in the summer, I spend as much time as possible camping at an ocean campground. I wish I could live there all year round!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-22T13:06:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-72305>

Caroline, I'm gonna give you the bad news Jack gave me last night. The iPad, iPod, iPhone ... all have to go. I don't have an iPhone and both my iPod & iPad are wi-fi only and I turned those off a while back. The iPad will now no longer be used for fun and games but for what I originally bought it for. I loaded all my bead-making tutorials on it as PDF's so I can keep them next to my torch as I work. Otherwise I would have a stack of printouts literally 3 feet high waiting to catch fire, LOL. I just am not ready to give it up altogether although I suspect that is going to have to change.

You guys are lucky to be next to the ocean but I get to live on top of a super volcano, LOL. See, I told you all I was hot!

### caroline — 2013-01-22T17:41:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-72352>

I know Diane - I can't think what to do about all that - I on't talk on the phone much - so not a problem. I have never liked reading books on my ipad - so not a problem. In fact if I read on my ipad before bed - I can't sleep. I told JK that at last consult a couple of weeks ago and of course you know what he said! BUT - the computer- I LOVE my laptop and I am spending more and more time here - what are we all going to do?? I think you could get thru all the new posts on the old forum much faster - am I remembering that correctly?? you know what a computer wiz I am!
I am kinda confused by all this tho .... I thought cold was the most important - but it seems like the ocean is???
I find since I am spending so much more time at the ocean I am really slowing down ... not a loss of energy ..... but a slowing down .... a peacefullness and a mindfullness I guess...... still like to look at hot guys on the beach tho { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### SeaHorse — 2013-01-22T21:08:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-72404>

Hey ladies...I've spent many a night in North Sydney waiting for the ferry to Newfoundland! Sometimes the wind would keep us there for a few days. I miss the ocean, but as I've recently realized, the weather in Newfoundland kept us indoors way too much...still it was my home for 25 years and I miss our friends and my husband's family. Paulalynn...you don't work for Marine Atlantic do you???

Caroline...so glad you've found your spot by the sea!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-22T21:48:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-72407>

Wow...so let me get this straight. Seahorse...you're from Newfoundland? All my grandparents are from Nfld! And no, I don't work for Marine Atlantic but drive by every morning on my way to work at the Northside hospital. I've been to Nfld several times, all my old university buddies were newfies! Been to Eastport, Grand Falls, and Clarenville. Bet you were expecting me to say St. john's and Cornerbrook? Well, we did go to St. John's too of course. I've had the best times in Newfoundland. Screeched in while in Grand Falls. Spent a week in Joe Batts Arm one summer. Awe the memories!

Caroline...your husband and I are practically family...we're Cape Bretoner's...when can I come visit...lol...just kidding.

. Where are you from Caroline? This is becoming a really small world!

### caroline — 2013-01-22T22:59:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-72437>

Paula - I was born in Toronto and lived a few years in Hamilton and then Guelph for 35 years and now here - in paradise!

Yes - you are family - the guest room is ready and waiting! Paula - do you know the B&B that I mean in Sydney Mines? I can't remember the name of it. It was owned and run by 2 men - but one was quite sick when we were there. We have directed many friends there and they loved it as well. I think I remember my husband saying that it had been the local Doctor's house. The house was beautiful and they built some lofts behind the house. They made the most gorgeous food. The first night we were there they were booked out for dinner \[locals\] so they set up a table in front of the fireplace for us..... just lovely men...

God I love Newfies!!! I could barely understand them - there are a lot of them \[ downhomers\] around Guelph. nicest people in the world - would give you the shirt off their back! There is a movie made about north Sydney and mining - staring Helena Bonham Carter - one of my all time favorite movies - OMG - I just remembered the name .... Margaret's museum - have you seen it? Maybe I will come visit you! Does Rita MacNeil still have herTea Room? what about - The Men from the deep?
we used to go and see all of them when they passed thru - great memories! Thanks for reminding me!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-23T12:21:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-72514>

Not sure about the B & B but I know if I asked my boss heâ€™d give me the answer in a split second. He was born there and knows everyone, and I mean everyone. Iâ€™ll let you know.

Margaretâ€™s Museumâ€¦I completely forgot about that movie but I did see it years ago! Did you hear of the movie Take This Waltz with Seth Rogan and Michelle Williams? They were here two summers ago to film a few scenes at the Fortress of Louisburg. Michelle Williams slept in our trailer (Iâ€™m surprised my hubby washed the sheets LOL). Rita MacNeil still has her Tea Room and it is always quite busy. She doesnâ€™t do many concerts anymore, I donâ€™t think her health is that great but donâ€™t quote me on it. Men of the Deeps are still active as well. I remember seeing them when I was a teenager when they played at the Farmerâ€™s Exhibition in North Sydney.

Maybe you need a visit to Cape Breton again??? Come in the summer though

. Have you ever been to Inverness? We camp in Dunvegan at the ocean, right before you get to Inverness. I mention Inverness because if you blink youâ€™ll drive right through Dunvegan without noticing!

### caroline — 2013-01-23T17:37:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-72565>

Inger - you know very, very well that you are welcome here anytime....
Paula - I feel so excited - my husband talked about Inverness all the time - he loved it there. I must have been there . I remember very clearly the Bra'Dor Lakes - don't know if I am spelling that correctly? so beautiful. There would be lots of my husband's relatives there - but I am not remembering names right now only a whole lot of funny stories. I will go back there one day. The last time we were there - we kinda drove around the whole island. On the west side we stayed at a Scotch distillery. One evening we went for dinner and this really old guy came in with his fiddle - he was 95 - and I thought - oh no ....but he was amazing - you know - all the old "down home" music. I will never forget that evening plus I had a lot of scotch that they were distilling

We have friends that have a fishing camp in NFLD - I will have to ask them where it is. God I have always loved newfies and always wanted to go.... I guess I love the simple things in life .... I really am just starting to put all that together. Maybe I better start containing the woo woo to my journal .....

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-23T19:03:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-72567>

The Glenora Inn & Distillery!!! Not far from Inverness. In case you're interested, I just read they have 12 bottles of their 20 year old scotch left, for a reasonable $500 lol!!! And you were bang-on with the Bras D'or Lakes!

### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-23T23:16:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-72619>

Your appreciation of the beach and where you've come from makes me smile. I don't know if its something to do with the diet we eat, but I'm no longer subject in the same way to hedonic adaptation and it seems if you might be the same. Hedonic adaptation is a psychological construct which says we stop appreciating something when it becomes commonplace. Well I seem to have lost that since I started eating paleo. I appreciate simple things so much more now and am so full of gratitude at having so much and so much opportunity that I sometimes feel its a bit OTT. it certainly makes life more pleasant.

### Inger — 2013-01-24T03:20:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-72631>

> **caroline said**
>
> Inger - you know very, very well that you are welcome here anytime....
> .

Thank you Caroline. I like your home!

What Aussie nana said about hedonic adaptation.. that is it! Wow, I never heard about that before. but it makes so much sense.
Feeling it so often.. how blessed I am with many things. Feeling so rich, so satisfied and enjoying what I have - the simplest things - so much! It is weird, really.. Maybe it is the epi paleo diet..lol
This is actually very weird! It would be intresting to know the science behind.. maybe it has something to do with our brain? Or electrons? Hmmm.. If one knew.. and could explain it clearly and easily understandable how it works to people that suffer and are longing for happiness. It would be so good!
But maybe we do not need to know more than.. come on with me to he beach, and lets eat some seafood there..

The sauce to happiness!

Now.. I wonder why Jesus were hanging on the beach all day long with his fishermen..lol He must have known.. weird that no one speeks about this angle in church?
And this Naaman that had to go and dip 7 times in Jordan to get well.. huh. He must have needed some electrons? Because of my religious parents (and no, I am not religious) we read a lot in the bible as kids.. and now all those stories pop up in my mind with a totally different meaning..

### diane — 2013-01-27T03:13:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-72804>

Just highlight all of the copy once you're done typing, and hit either ctrl+c (PC) or command+c (mac). Then it's copied, and if you lose your post, open a new reply and hit either ctrl+v or command+v. This through many frustrations of lost posts. LOL.

### caroline — 2013-01-27T04:07:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-72825>

I met a friend the other day who I haven't seen in about four months. She said I looked phenomenal and twenty years younger. - reall nice. - but they really have no interest ,in what is happening and why. They know that I am low carb. If I saw someone with these changes I would be all over them like a rug and trying to pick their brain .......that is what I think is different about all of us - we have been looking for answers for years and pretty much nothing is too crazy to give a go (within reason of course). My friends think you need carbs to be healthy and we eat too much protein and they think as you get older your hormones decline and that is how it should be and you don't mess with mother nature.
All my changes are coming from the inside out and I do feel phenomenal. I wish with all my heart the same for all of us.
It has happened slowly for me I think - everything seems to come on line little by little - so you don't really notice.

I just noticed a few weeks ago that the outer third of my eyebrows has grown back in - when did that happen? My upper thighs had that orangey dimples crepey look for probably the last 15 +years - that is mostly gone. I am sitting here in a pair of quite short shorts and a tank top - that definitely has never been something that I would have worn before - mind you- I am not going out on the street but I look okay .... So much to be grateful for.....but it is the feeling of calmness and the joy in the simple things in life that are really turning me on....

### caroline — 2013-01-27T04:17:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-72827>

I forgot to add. ...... Drum roll please..... I took my tummy for a sun tan on the beach!!!

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-27T15:11:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-72851>

Caroline and Paulalynne...sorry not to have kept up the Newfoundland end of the conversation...I couldn't post until today properly.
Anyway, I grow up in the US but first went to NFLD when I was 18 and then immigrated to Canada when I was 28. I met my husband there and we spent 20 years in St. John's with some years over in Prince Edward Island. Turns out my ancestors had made a stop in Newfoundland in the late 1600s, right where his mother's family is from...so we figured it was fate that I ended up returning 400 years later to find him! His mother's family is from the Southern Shore...you might as well be in Ireland spending time there...his father's family is from Holyrood area. Perhaps you are related Paulalynne....

We moved to New England two years ago...we both miss our friends and the millions of relatives that my husband has in NFLD....but we also love the Northern Woods a lot and my work with the horse therapy and his work as a musician thrive in this part of the world too. I'm loving the winter here so much this year....CT makes all the difference. Our youngest daughter is planning on going to Memorial U. so we'll be visiting regularly in the future. I can't wait to go back in the summer and swim in the ocean.

Also....Caroline...great news on the bare belly!!

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-27T15:31:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-72857>

Hi Caroline. I just randomly saw your journal and talking about Newfoundland. I have never been to NFLD but I have been to Halifax Nova Scotia. It was one of the best trips in my life.

Also I was very lucky with the weather in April: sunny and 15'C. That is like a miracle for out east.

Anyways, I'd love to visit NFLD sometime. I have met several newfies and it's true they are all so big hearted and warm people. Wow!

You lived in Toronto Hamilton and Guelph. Well T.O. I don't like, Hamilton I lived for 8 years (and might be moving back). My parents live in Brantford and I am back staying with them for a while even though I have a place in Ottawa. But I was missing family and friends too much up there (even though I'm living with a couple friends up there). So lucky to live with good people, but I end up needing to be back here.

Hope you are finding it well in NFLD (if that is where you are now?)

### caroline — 2013-01-28T08:57:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-72977>

I have been so hungry the last couple of days - it is like I can't get full - very crazy! I ate half a lamb roast for dinner!! maybe I have too many electrons????

### caroline — 2013-01-28T09:02:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-72979>

I spent a long time today going over EMF2 and making notes so I can explain some things to my 9yo grandson .... it worked for me too!!! Maybe I can take that quiz now!

### caroline — 2013-01-29T10:58:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-73128>

It has turned out to be a beautiful day - cooler but lovely. I have been thinking about food today.
I have stopped eating apples and chocolate and My weight seems to be much more stable. I ate so much yesterday - half a baby lamb and a bunch of carrots and tons of coconut chips etc. and my weight was down 1lb this morning.
I went shopping today for tank tops and shorts - who is this person??? I seem to be living in an alternate universe - I like it a lot - but it is lonely.
I seem to be able to understand what my body is telling me now. Befor \[preJK\] I was always hungry and never had a clue about what my body needed or didn't need.
I think all of our instincts and gut feelings have been remarkably on target. I am pretty darn proud of us ....... waddayathink???

### Inger — 2013-01-29T20:15:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-73185>

Yeah.. I think too. I am pretty darn proud of you too Caroline...!!!
Sometimes it can be a bit lonely.. but loneliness feels great at times too.. didn't it? That is when one can suck up all the beauty around 100%! It is quite renewing me thinks. And Caroline.. remember you are never lonely as you have us all! We are all on this beautiful path with you!
I have it too, some days I eat quite a lot, somedays not so much. But I eat when I feel I need it. Maybe onbe needs extra electrons then from food, when this huge appetite hits.. Alternative, if there is a need to save money.. one could just ground a bit and eat a bit less? hmmmm..

I have been eating snow. I wonder if it has good stuff in it? It tastes so good. Kids do it too. They must know? I often put some snow in my glasss of water before drinking. It cools it so nicely too. I imagine it has the sun energy in it, because the snow was up and touched the sun too. You could make you sun water Caroline. I do that in summer all the time! I put a glass jug of water into the sun, and I add some wild herbs like nettles or dandelion or such into the water too. I let it stay all day in the sun. It tastes so wonderful and I bet it is really healthy!

So cool, Darleen! Guess what! My parents sheep eats snow too all the time!!! Even if I give them fresh water evey sigle day from our own well. And the chickens! I always bring them fresh snow from outside and they love to pick on it and eat it. So I just know it must be some magic to it!

### caroline — 2013-01-30T08:29:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-73313>

I waited most of the day for DHL guy to pick up my 23and me. It is still pretty cool but the sun came out. I spent about 2hours in the rock pool and walking in the surf - hardly anyone at the beach. I am still really hungry ...Tonite I ate a16oz can of salmon and 4 strips of bacon and some goat cheese and stilton cheese . I think I should nock off the cheese but I really want full fat stuff to fill me up. I am craving stilton cheese and carrots.....does anyone else have these crazy cravings?? To go from chocolate cake to carrots is pretty weird. I haven't wanted any carbs for ages.......so now I have turned into a rabbit!
Darleen - I love your last dog's name - am I to deduce that you loved Allie McBeal??? one of my all time favorites! We have a show in Oz that reminds me of that show in so many ways/ It is my very favorite and right now I can't remember the name - it is shot in Melbourne and stars Asher Keddy. I sit and drool over her clothes....and the dreamy men she becomes entangled with. Our new season is just starting this week and next and I can't wait.

I feel really really good and the scale was down 500gm this am - so I am sure not complaining! But it is really over the top hunger. I wish I could stop with the cheese. I am not going to buy any more for awhile ......but I think goats cheese should be okay?
It is an absolutely divine day today ...... Heading to the beach now ..... Been missing all the hot bods!!! But why do older men look so disgusting at the beach?? They just don't take care of themselves at all ... But they love looking at young girls with big boobs hanging out .. Other than that they seem half dead!

I met the most interesting woman at the beach today - she does aromatherapy and I could talk to her about all of this - I was so excited. ### caroline — 2013-02-04T10:14:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-74046>

I had a lovely day at the beach today - I met friends for lunch - my new favorite shrimp salad and wound up talking for hours - amazing how women always have so much to say!
I did see one incredibly hot guy in spandex - it sure is fun to see guys surfing......
It has been very cool with big waves and very windy - hopefully things warm up tomorrow. I still am very hungry - I had shrimp twice today and salmon as well and god knows how much butter and garlic.

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-05T05:32:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-74237>

Hot guys are few and far between on our beaches where I can get to easily. Mostly its old people with guts much larger than mine ever were doing a two minute dip. Perhaps it would be more fun if I drove another half hour or so where I could watch some better looking people. But then I couldn't take the dog - I'm restricted to dog beaches with her.

Shrimp always leave me hungry, salmon not quite so much.

### caroline — 2013-02-05T10:01:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-74256>

It was an absolutely gorgeous day today - I swam for awhile and then walked in the surf for about 90min and then just sat and stared for another hour!!! I just feel like I am bursting with joy.....It is so much fun watching people learn to surf - I don't know how they keep from killing themselves!!!
As I was walking I passed two black guys and they said hello - they smelled so yummy - holey cow!!! It reminded me of the Bahamas .... the men were always so well groomed - especially when going dancing - and they always smelled divine .... and have you ever danced with a native in the Carribean????? Gives dancing a whole new meaning ..... now I really can't wipe the smile off my face!

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-05T20:46:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-74306>

Aussie Nana .. try having some avocado with your shrimp. I have to eat it that way or I'm hungry again an hour later. Need to add that fat. Or make it into a salad with mayo.

Caroline I am so jealous. I don't mind all our snow, what I don't like is it's been overcast so much the last few weeks.

### caroline — 2013-02-06T01:07:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-74349>

I am sitting on my patio freezing for some reason - got to go and put more clothes on I guess. I am taking my new bikini bottoms to the ocean today - should be interesting. I started taking oxytocin yesterday - so this could prove really interesting. When my Doc gave me the script -he had never written one for it before - he was reading his info on it and said he had better share the side effects with me ..... you could become very emotional and very attached and quite amorous. So I asked him if he was worried I was going to jump some poor unsuspecting guy at the beach! Worse things could happen I guess.......
If I could only run into that gorgeous guy again.......I figure he must have been here on vacation and is now back home with his wife and 12 kids........

### caroline — 2013-02-06T13:05:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-74407>

Okey Dokey - let's turn this journal into "fifty shades"

Actually - maybe it is the oxytocin - but the scenery at my beach is getting a whole lot better ... yahoo!!!

The two life guards today were totally amazing looking young men ... in their very, very tiny speedos -WOW

BUT........ it gets way,way better .....

I was walking on the pathway that runs along the beach but a bit above and I happened to look down at the rockpool where I usually go.
there was a totally gorgeous young man - think amazing surfer dude with the chiselled body and the long golden curls - well - let me paint this picture for you.....He was in his undies - very, very skimpy bikini undies and just about to step into his shorts. He was perfect in every single possible way. \[and then some\] on a scale of 1 to 10 - he hit it out of the ball park!
The thing is - if I had been a minute sooner - he had just gotten out of his swimmers.......

I will leave the rest to your imagination - and why oh why don't I take my camera to the beach?????? stupid, stupid, stupid!!!!

Altho - my imagination is running away with this one....

Ladies \[and Martin\] - how was your day?

### caroline — 2013-02-07T08:29:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-74597>

Today was another stunning day - I have this beach bum thing down pat! All I want to do now is go down to the ocean and walk in the surf and sit on the rocks and watch the waves. I can't remember the last time I dusted and vacuumed etc. etc.

Nothing too exciting today , the usual gorgeous bodies, the usual gorgeous bodies- the usual gorgeous bodies - and doesn't get the least bit boring.

I am going to be pretty sad when it starts to cool down - but I can come all year -except all the gorgeous bodies will be covered up! Altho - I must say - in winter - everyone just wears shorts and a t-shirt and a hat and a scarf and flip flops and on a really cold day - ugh boots.
Perfect don't you think??

I wish I could send some sunshine to everyone - it makes you feel so much better...

### caroline — 2013-02-08T10:24:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-74804>

Another stunning day - we have had an amazing summer. I picked some sea weed today so if you don't see me back here you will know I poisoned myself! The tide has been really far out the last few days- I must remember to ask why - anyway there is tons of great looking seaweed. I will rinse it and put some sesam oil etc.

There was a Chinese lady in the bathroom today. She had her swim suit pulled all the way Down and she was trying to rinse herself off under the tap. No one should have to witness that! Those puppies should never see the light of day!

As usual - all these awesome looking young people. I swear no one looked like that when I was that age. I guess in a warm climate everyone is outdoors all the time and more active but as they get to middle age they really fall apart. I think they say Aussies are the second fattest people in the world now?

I shouldn't even tell you the reall stupid thing I did last night. It was very windy at the beach and I was covered in sand so I thought I would just brush myself off and shake out my hair and go to bed. I kinda like to leave the sea water on me - that is my story and I am sticking to it! Anyway I was going to wash the sheets today and take a shower this morning (hair considerations)

When I woke up this morning I thought I was in a gravel pit - it was totally gross. This has to be our secret!!

### janagram — 2013-02-08T18:48:58+0200

### caroline — 2013-02-09T10:14:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-75006>

Well - let me tell you....
It was a very weird day at the office today....

I was laying on the beach on my tummy and I heard a guy kinda ranting and raving on the pathway above. I looked up and saw this fat guy in red swimmers
He continued ranting and then I realised he was coming down the stairs nearby. All of sudden he hollered "you fat bitch!!" so my head jerked up - you talkintome??? He said - oh no Darlin - not you- my fat bitch wife. So he put his stuff on the sand not far from me and went into the ocean. Coming back he is grinning at me and telling me I looked so brown and really good and he keeps calling me darlin and then he smiles at me .......he is missing half his teeth!!! This is the kind of beach action I get!!

But then....... I am still perplexed over this....
I was walking in the surf and noticed a bunch of people standing around on the beach and a bunch of white banners etc. As I got closer it became apparent that it was a wedding!! I thought - way cool - how lucky am I!!!! so I sat on some rocks and got to see all the bridesmaids \[6\] walk onto the beach and then the bride on the arm of her father - chairs, music celebrant children of the bride and groom?? etc. - the whole nine yards. I was in heaven - I was at a wedding - yeah me!!!

After the ceremony - BTW are all ceremonies so long these days? it was as long as a Catholic wedding mass -I was walking on the rocks and turned around to see a scrawny man in a suit coming hell bent toward me. I thought cool - maybe he knows me and he is inviting me to the reception altho I don't really know anyone here. He says to me "are you one of the organisers?
I thought - what a bizarre question since I am in swimming gear and I says - No.
He proceeds to holler at me "did I realize that I was sitting behind the bride and groom and I was in EVERY SINGLE wedding photo. He re iterated this about 3 more times and asked if I was happy??? I thought he was going to go into cardiac arrest!!!

He finally turned on his heel and marched away totally disgusted ......you know how little scrawny men can get...He really had his panties all in a knot!! I did holler after him ....ASSHOLE \[I really used to be a lover not a fighter - honest\]

I have been replaying all of this in my head....
first of all - I was about 50 yards behind them sitting on the rocks and I couldn't see the bride's face - I was kinda behind the bridesmaids and it is a public beach - there was all kinds of people coming and going - I just thought I would stay for church - what do you normally do on a Saturday afternoon at the beach???

Anyway - apparently - I am in every picture of this bride and groom \[they haven't heard of photoshop?\] I think they are pretty darn lucky!

The flower girl wore an ankle length white dress with a crown of fresh flower on her head and her and the little ring bearer were bare footed and played in the sand thru out the ceremony.

The 6 bridesmaids wore pale pink long dresses and were barefooted and the groomsmen were in dress pants and white shirt and tie and they had shoes on - strange?
A lot of the guests were barefooted too.

So - how was your day????

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-09T19:30:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-75040>

well for pete's sake. Why couldn't that asshat march his nasty backside over during the ceremony and ask you to politely move. That would have been the decent thing to do.

Sorry you had two icky run-ins.

So you're getting nice and brown? That's great. You must be churning out vitamin D like mad.

Hope today is better. We're supposed to get more snow.

### janagram — 2013-02-09T19:37:40+0200

Enjoy the beach, keep smiling!

yeah - the whole time I was watching I never saw the scrawny guy once. There were plenty of people taking pics from every which way and there was lots of people walking back and forth. It was very funny that he was so pissed off. I have been chuckling abut him ever since.....

Anyway - I had a great day. I will see what trouble I can get into today!!! can't wait....

### caroline — 2013-02-11T03:17:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-75331>

yesterday was pretty uneventful. I thought I would walk down the beach to the scene of my crime ... hoped to see a Christening or a Bar Mitzvah or a circumcision or something fun - no such luck - maybe next time.
It was another beautiful day - we have had an amazing summer. It was so windy tho - I feel as if I have been sand blasted. I am sure there is sand in places I will never get out.
I came home and took a cold shower and then did C/T but sure can't get the water any where near cold enough - I just lay there with the bags of ice on top of me - not optimum I know but better than nothing I hope

Today is overcast and cool so I guess I will see what trouble I can get into at the shops...

Hope everyone has had a great day!

### janagram — 2013-02-11T19:59:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-75416>

I remember spot CTing.....been a long while now....feels great in the right climate. Love hearing about summer anyway!

### caroline — 2013-02-13T10:23:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-75678>

Can you believe I saw another bride and groom last evening at the ocean! They were walking on the rocks - very strange. Because the tide has been out for so long - there is masses of seaweed on the beaches and really smells a lot in some spots especially where they were.

BTW - I did keep my distance - I am going to have to read up on my Beach wedding etiquette if there is going to be brides and grooms underfoot where ever I go!!!

I figured out today that I am kinda doing a very mini movnat at the beach - emphasis on very mini - I clamber around on the rocks - they aren't very big or high but it is fun all the same except there is no hunky guys to do it with! Life isn't perfect -sigh
.
Aso last nite there was about 30 girls doing exercise stuff. They were in two rows and alternating pushups and hitting each other and sprints etc. As I was passing I whispered to the last two girls that they should make a run for it. They started to laugh and face planted in the sand! I guess I should have been more careful - the trainer was a brute of a girl. She could have blown on me and knocked me down!!

Back to serious journalling - I seem to be sleeping pretty good but not really feeling refreshed like I was and have kind of a low level headache when I wake up and it kinda hangs around. I can't even remember the last time I had a headache. I seem to be getting more sensitive to stuff - has anyone else noticed that? I have a feeling that I shouldn't watch TV at all. I really can't figure out what to do with myself in the evenings \[I know what I would like to be doing in the evenings!\]. Is everyone managing to cut their computer time???? I also have really stiff feet - they feel like blocks at the end of my legs - don't know what that is about either. I think I will ask that question in the forum but can't decide where. I would guess I am missing some minerals or something?
I hope everyone had a great day!

### ashryn — 2013-02-14T15:48:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-75818>

Do you know what NGF is that dr K said you have a problem with? With all the beach walking you do I'd say you get your barefoot walking time in.

### janagram — 2013-02-14T19:24:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-75834>

what to do in the evenings....? yes, this is an area that needs to be developed. I, too, spend much time on electronic devices in the evening....OK, reading is good, and if I could read by candlelight that would work. Haven't tried.
Believe it or not- my exhusband's family liked to sing, and more than one party was a whole evening of singing along with the piano, which my BIL played. (best thing about the ex!) Could easily be done by candlelight.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-14T21:15:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-75850>

Are you taking D-ribose? Because last Fall I was complaining that my legs sometimes felt positively leaden. I could hardly walk. Since I started the D-ribose that hasn't happened.

TSK girl you do love to stir up trouble at the beach, don't you. You'd better find some honey to keep you out of trouble. Or maybe get you into more.

Evenings I find that I spend very little time on the computer any more (good thing) but I do watch tv. The blu blockers help and I sit back from the screen about 10 feet as well. But I don't watch much. Usually an hour and I'm done. That's about one program off the DVR. Bedtime is about 9:30 so I have to start getting the cats meals ready about 9. So from dinner to bedtime there isn't a lot of time left to get into too much trouble. I'll read for about 10 minutes before lights out and I have a 25watt bulb (the old fashioned kind) in my reading lamp AND my blu blockers on as well.

I've been thinking about investing in a decent "kerosene" lamp for reading at night. Problem is getting hubby to get off the computer and turn out the lights as well.

### caroline — 2013-02-14T22:19:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-75861>

> [ashryn said:](/goto/post?id=75818)
>
> Do you know what NGF is that dr K said you have a problem with? With all the beach walking you do I'd say you get your barefoot walking time in.

NGF = nerve growth factor. I have been googling my ass off but most of it is really technical. It is actually really scaring me - for the last couple of days my feet have been very numb - much, much worse all of a sudden and I don't have a clue what to do except for the supps that Dr. K. recommended. I am always so confused when buying supps - how do you know what brand to buy? some brands are much cheaper than others. I don't want to buy an inferior product just to save a couple of dollars but it doesn't work as well.
Anyone have any ideas?
Dr. K. said ..... ACL and magnesium and tumeric

2 nites ago - I didn't sleep all nite because my feet were so numb and it was going up my legs - I really don't like the sounds of this - and yes - I have been walking barefoot for months and months and spent 2 to 3 hours at the beach everyday....WTH

this is a neuro problem - at least i know a good neuro Doc!

### freesia — 2013-02-14T22:42:33+0200

I think I am remembering this correctly Caroline - functional brown adipose tissue stimulates NGF.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-02-14T22:58:00+0200

### caroline — 2013-02-14T23:04:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-75871>

How can I go from feeling absolutely fantastic to having trouble walking in the space of a couple of days???

Just goes to show that we need to live every single second of every single day to the very best of our abilities and to let our imagination run totally wild ... and dream big - very big - we don't get a do over........

The last week and a half has been amazing on a personal level. Ever since I met this young woman on the beach my life has shifted in so many subtle and very positive ways. It is so very true - when your hands \[and heart\] are open you do receive gifts. When I reflect on this last while I am astounded at how my brain has rewired on a couple of important issues.

When I think back to this gorgeous man at the beach that smiled at me - such a simple thing - I can't remember anything about him but I can remember everything about the two men he was with. I only remember this amazing aura of kindness and gentleness and the gorgeous eyes and smile.
I saw my counselor yesterday and she said she thinks it means that I am letting go a little and maybe a little ready to move on....... very scary stuff....

I also met my new friends mother who has been grieving for 5 years for her husband and that really scared the shit out of me ...... I came home and took my wedding ring off. It seemed that I needed to do that if I want to move forward. Now- I did replace t with a beautiful ring that Doug surprised me with one xmas when we weren't supposed to be exchanging presents because we were going on a big trip - so I feel like I haven't abandoned him.

In the last couple of days I have reconnected with my daughters in subtle ways - I realise now that I was putting up little barriers because of the grief.
This was a revelation because of my new fried as well. When her father died - a very gregarious and fun loving and devoted man - the whole family splintered. Grief does terrible things to families in very subtle ways that I had never imagined before. You do tend to shut out people - but it isn't intentional.

It is unbelievable how this young woman has changed my life in so many beautiful and unexpected ways .....I feel so grateful for so many things... but I guess it is a progression of all the experiences i have been having lately and really over the last couple of years - I have had an amazing life....

There should be a warning at the top of this post - ENTER AT YOUR OWN RISK .......sorry about the soppy post.....is that a word???

### caroline — 2013-02-15T11:25:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-75957>

Can you believe I saw another wedding party at the beach today? OMG they are everywhere! I was very discreet.
I met a friend there for lunch - gorgeous day.
My feet are still swollen and the left one much more so and looks jaundiced - very perculiar. I got an email from my doctor and I will see him Tuesday. However he said if it gets worse to go to the hospital. I think I said already that my friends Son in law is a neurologist and my friends daughter is a doc and works at the same practice - I dont know if that will help and I did buy their baby several nice gifts!! My doc said there are several very good neurologists but it will still take several weeks to get an emergency appointment. This is going to cut into my beach time!
My friend gave me a foot massage with some essential oils but it didn't help at all really.
Life can sure change on a dime.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-16T04:27:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-76068>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=75885)

Well the same can be said for some doctors as well. Our chiro has a master's in sports medicine and he really REALLY knows the biomechanics of the body. He spends most of his time doing deep tissue massage and he doesn't do the "cracking" style of chiro that some use. Ask around.

### ashryn — 2013-02-16T06:00:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-76079>

Caroline, how are your feet today? It might be worth seeing if you can get in to see a sports massage therapist (just google sports injury management) they often work weekends, and they might be able to give you a clue about what's going on, or perhaps fix it.. I have some training in sports massage, but not enough to guess what to do.. If I were near you I'd start by checking out your lower back and glutes to see if there was any unusual tension there. Then i'd check the deep ligament that you can find by laying on your back with your knees up and pressing down just inside where your hip juts out at its highest point. once you have your fingers on that muscle, straighten and bend your leg 10 times and the muscle should release. (Caveat: it is a long time since I studied this and have forgotten all the names, I just go by feel on a person, but I know this works well for relaxing the lower back and legs)

Often nerve related issues stem from muscle/ligament issues, and if it is as simple as that it would save you some cortisol and discomfort.

Good luck, I hope it is a simple solution

### caroline — 2013-02-17T10:54:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-76187>

Well - I have only been gone a day and missed tons here!
I did take myself off to emergency yesterday - I had decided to wait but between Dr. K. and my daughter really scaring the crap out of me I decided to just do it.
I was seriously on the verge of a melt down - this is the hospital where my husband died and generally can't bear to be any where near it - let alone in emergency where we spent so much time for so many years. My dear friend wanted to take me and my daughter did too but I couldn't see putting them thru sitting in Er for hours and hours.
So - I arrived with two bags - now I realize how ridiculous I must have looked! I thought they would probably keep me in so I thought I should pack accordingly, You need several pairs of nice pajamas - right? jeans and T-shirts \[sometimes nurses make you get dressed after your shower, a robe, lots of clean underwear and the Docs want to know what you are taking - so I brought all my supps and hormones. Then there is the hair care products - you need your own shampoo and conditioner and leave in moisturiser and mouse and gel and thickener and texturizer etc. etc. \[I have high maintenance hair!\]
I also decided that there is no way I would eat the food so I got some macadamia nuts and dark chocolate and cans of salmon etc.

Now you can see why I had two bags - One of the nurses asked me where I was coming from - I think he thought I had just got off a cargo ship!

I wanted to ask for the concierge so I could check my bags!

I had a lovely young ER Doc who looked like he was still in high school. He did all the appropriate testing stuff and couldn't figure out was the matter and checked with his boss to see if he was missing anything. Nope - they decided it wasn't acute and I should just see my own Doc. I said - surely it isn't normal for your feet to be numb and he said no but.....
So the moral is - when you look really healthy and your blood work is good and BP, SG and urine etc. there is nothing really the matter with you.
They couldn't believe that I am not on any meds and have no arthritis etc. and asked me that several times.
He did think the problem might be that I was swimming in the ocean and the ocean is cold - 68 - couldn't believe that! I told him I take ice baths - I don't think he believed me!

Then the funniest thing happened that I can remember in a long, long time.......... Dr. kruse will appreciate this........the nurse asked me if I could be pregnant???? I laughed and laughed and laughed until there was tears running down my face I told the nurse that I loved her. My daughter is still laughing. My youngest daughter will be 41 next month! and there is this problem of immaculate conception. My daughter called this morning to ask if I have morning sickness?

And so - I took all my luggage and went home.......

The end....
