Thanks Ash. They are quite numb today - definitely something is up.

I can't get over how unhealthy ER staff look. The male nurse that did my BP etc. is a diabetic and was just on his way out the door for a smoke. When I asked him about it - he said - "smoking isn't any worse than all the other stuff I do" Then he offered me a sandwich and I said -oh no thanks - I brought my own food. I got some pretty strange looks for that one. All in all I must have looked like a travelling freak show!! God I love that!!!
A couple of years ago I would have been so embarrassed to make any waves at all - and now I am having the best time. Maybe I have more than a couple of screws loose??? I can blame all this on JK...

### caroline — 2013-02-18T22:40:40+0200

I also feel like I am electrically charged - I can feel currents running thru me. Does anyone else feel like that??? I don't really know how to describe it ...like a continual buzz....

### Inger — 2013-02-19T08:43:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-76426>

To be with kind and loving people is healing for sure...

Is your leptop wireless...? If... very bad... (I have been using wireless leptop for months this last year so I know.. I just got it wired!)
The sensitivity could be also a good thing, I am much more sensitive to artificial lights after dark now, it really bothers me! But I see it as a good sign. It is like getting sensitive to tobacco again afgter quitting smoking.. as you smoke regularely you just feel great from it, but a non smoker will feel sick when smoking 1 cigarette. Sensitivity can be great if you better feel what is good for you or bad..

### Inger — 2013-02-19T08:57:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-76427>

A member on rawpaleoforum once wrote (he is Australian BTW and lives there too..) that when eating a all raw paleo diet he was much more sensitive to having he's laptop on he's thigh reading, he got almost burnt! When he ate cooked the sensitivity disappeared to a great degree. I really wonder about this, if it was a random thing or if it has something to it.
I was speculating why on earth is that happening, and if it is bad to eat all raw because of that, or if it is a good thing to sense dangerous radiation. If thinking about it, it actually is, because then you more easily protect your self. If you do not sense anything you go on and think it is fine. But I really wonder if it also does more damage (because it feels like that for sure)? But.. If comparing it with other poison like tobacco it should be a good thing to be sensitive, as the tobaccosmoke does big damage to a smoker even if he does not feel a thing...
I am more sensitive to wireless leptop than hubby too. He does not feel burnt on he's thighs when he has the leptop there reading, but I soon feel really hot and have to put something in between, like a thick pillow, and still I can feel the heat through! A weird stinging heat..

### Inger — 2013-02-19T09:06:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-76428>

I just read the thread about our sixth sense posted today.. and I got this idea what if your increased sensitivity about life and greater happiness goes together with increased sensitivity for everything... the other way to go would be going numb.. eating crap or cook everything to death (lol) and feel no happiness and increased awareness but also not suffer so bad so soon from the dangerous EMFs.. (but sure you would suffer later to a great degree you just would not realize why)

Audrey I am speculating wildy today..lol but I actually have been pondering this stuff for long, and why I so love raw. And why I feel it increases my sensitivty and awareness. Because it does make a difference, that I can tell. Maybe it has to do with bacterias and viruses? Hmmm.. Because they do die when we cook..
And it is not always a easy ride because you sense stuff around you that are sad too.. you sense more that you can bear at times because the reality is sometimes not pretty and it makes my heart cry.. but then, the increased joy and happiness, satisfaction and peace adds to it too so in the end it still becomes a win. A great win actually!

### caroline — 2013-02-19T09:37:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-76430>

Inger - I feel like I have this electrical charge going thru me - have you ever felt like that? I am extremely sensitive to light now - even if I close my eyes - artificial light comes thru some how - it is very weird. And this joy/bliss factor is so crazy - I just spent a couple of hours at the ocean just staring at the surfers and swimmers and kids playing etc. and I am perfectly content ..... but I am not making good use of my time I fear. I seem to be anesthetized by the sun and ocean. I just got myself a shrimp salad and sat and stared for ages at the yachts and all the people walking by and the sky was so beautiful....
I have turned into a beach bum for sure ......

### Inger — 2013-02-19T09:46:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-76434>

Audrey.. maybe to stare at the ocean and beeing perfectly content is perfect use of time..?! Look, you have to heal now Audrey! That is exactly waht you need and IMHO the most perfect use of time ever!!! Beach bum is the most perfect thing to be, I assure you.. Now give yourself 6 more years beeing a beach bum and then you have healed like Dr Kruse. He needed many years too, and he was much younger that you!
Just let the sunshine in, that is the only perfect light for you, that is what your body starts to feel for sure!

IDK about the charge going through me... but I do feel light and I do hate artificial lights more and more.. they makes me feel not good! Especially after dark. But I do not like them at daytime either but I must sit in front of the computer to learn all this and I hate that Jack wont make a paper book!!! Argh but that's life..lol

### Jude — 2013-02-19T12:16:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-76443>

Hi Caroline,
Hope the old suitcase and your own food trick to hospital, somehow fixed your numbness ? ! I believe all my bad palpitations and dreadful "thringing thru my body" attacks last year were /are emf sensitivity. I certainly stopped them with my huge intake of sardines! but had a mini repeat performance in Jan.
Believe mine are a result of a build up of exposure to wireless internet, 2 cordless phones, microwave, electric gardening equipment--handling the long cords for yrs, plus the family's cell phones. Can stop the feeling/event with large dose of ubiquinal--love that stuff. So am timing myself on the internet, I can feel it start up if I stay on too long. Am stuck with wireless as we are so far from civilisation but have all the electrics off and ditched the cordless. It's ironic that basically this area can be really void of these extra influences and I've managed to get sensitive. So it's EMF free zone here, think I'll advertise ! use the shearers quarters for those needing a country spell.

> Hi Caroline,
> Hope the old suitcase and your own food trick to hospital, somehow fixed your numbness ? ! I believe all my bad palpitations and dreadful "thringing thru my body" attacks last year were /are emf sensitivity. I certainly stopped them with my huge intake of sardines! but had a mini repeat performance in Jan.
> Believe mine are a result of a build up of exposure to wireless internet, 2 cordless phones, microwave, electric gardening equipment--handling the long cords for yrs, plus the family's cell phones. Can stop the feeling/event with large dose of ubiquinal--love that stuff. So am timing myself on the internet, I can feel it start up if I stay on too long. Am stuck with wireless as we are so far from civilisation but have all the electrics off and ditched the cordless. It's ironic that basically this area can be really void of these extra influences and I've managed to get sensitive. So it's EMF free zone here, think I'll advertise ! use the shearers quarters for those needing a country spell.

An EMF zone - such a great idea! I am thinking that must be at least part of the problem - I am spending more and more time on my laptop. I swear I can feel electrical charges running thru my body. What kind of ubiquinol did you get? what dose do you take?

My feet are still numb - but that doesn't seem to be the right word. I can feel sensations and all the pulse points are good. My Doc is sending me for a nerve study. I am just waiting for the mag etc. that JK told me to get - but I have no idea of dosing. Do you know anything about NGF ? That was a thought that JK had.

I'm taking Healthy Origins 200mgms x2 daily. If I have had a bit too much wireless I take another 200mgms. Dose up before I see grandkids so as I don't steal their electrons! and play on wet grass with them-no seaside here. Take 4 mag malate daily, am waiting on the orotate and threonate. Have 15m cord to my laptop hoping that distance may lessen the effect...just an n=1 for me..as I don't understand all this EMF stuff. That Dr Klinehart(not sure if I have his name correct) says if you have a sensitive/autistic child you can reverse the condition but...absolutely no smart meter, dec phone or wireless in your home!!!!!That bit has been proved by my DIL as we speak, so what am I doing here? Need more info to do better,it's life threateningly important, so am risking my ignorance for now till I know better.Your funny sensation could be your way of exhibiting sensitivity? Enjoy the beach.

### caroline — 2013-02-21T09:56:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-76855>

I don't really know why I wondered in here tonite - I got nothing.....

Still can't figure out my feet issue. My Doc wants an xray for one foot so I guess I will do that tomorrow altho xrays worry me now.

I sure wish I knew about nerve growth factor - keep googling my butt off .....

I got rained out at the beach - at least I got an hour in - but not many people today ..... not a bride insight! I will have to make up for it tomorrow. I feel so blissed out - like I am comatose. I pulled the vacuum cleaner out to make sure I did something - but nadda .... in fact I tripped over the vacuum and cut my leg!! how is that for bad juju??? and my apartment still isn't clean - I have sand everywhere so someone may accuse me of bringing the beach home with me!

### caroline — 2013-02-22T10:03:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-77009>

Soooooo - my life just keeps getting funnier and funnier. I went to get my foot xrayed today and guess what the young radiographer asked me...... could I be pregnant????? how does that happen twice in one week???? Anyway - this young man has all kinds of serious nerve problems so I told him about JK . As I was talking to him about all of this I realized where we were - talk about EMF overload! WOW - I was flabergasted!. How could this young man be doing this for his career? He said his problems were irreversible. So I made him promise to go outside at lunch time and walk barefooted and hug a tree. He said he swims every nite in the ocean. He also said he knew about NGF and perineural charge etc. I wanted to stay and talk to him all afternoon - I wish I would have given him my email - I would love to keep up with him and how he is doing. Before I left he asked me to write down this web address and I told him to read Alex's story.

### caroline — 2013-02-22T10:47:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-77010>

Now that EMF 7 is live. - I am feeling the quantum effect of forever being entangled with this lovely young man. He has such serious problems. I hope that he pops in here and can get some help and new ideas to help himself. I would guess that he is about 25 and has so much life to live. He has such a beautiful, kind and generous spirit.

I guess that is how I am feeling about all of this EMF stuff - we can only do what we can do. I don't feel panic like some here do ........but we have to do the best we can. We can't bury our head in the sand ....... But we are forever entangled and I love that. ....we have a Personal responsibility to each other and that makes me feel calm and light and energized.

### caroline — 2013-02-22T10:49:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-77011>

I guess this will be extreme and silly woo woo to some here .......

### caroline — 2013-02-24T08:21:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-77393>

There is nothing much happening today. The sun did come out in spite of the terrible storm in Sydney last nite. The ocean is like a washing machine - I don't remember seeing it like that before. The beaches are closed - no swimming. I did go to the baby beach today - it is sheltered and gets deep fairly quickly so I can get immersed up to my neck and paddle around - hopefully soaking up lots and lots of electrons!

I figured out this morning that I have a new protocol - well it really isn't knew but I just realised what I was doing! so here is my new morning routine - TA DA
#1 Get up and look at the sun - I first have to put my pj's back on - I seem to fling everything off in the nite
#2 S/O
#3 C/T

So I have this deal with myself - I have to S/O and then I have to C/T and only then can I use my laptop while I am in the tub!
It is working like a charm - I really want to C/T after S/O and I really want to get on line ...... so ...win, win, win

This is kinda backwards to what you all are doing - but - what the heck!

I saw an older couple at the beach today - in their swimmers - getting some sun and then they got fully and completely dressed using their towels. It was fascinating to watch. The lady was struggling to get her bra on - so she eventually dropped her towel and just let it all hang out!

I would never do that in a million years \[I am old enough to know better than to never say never\] but I was so impressed that she couldn't have cared less.
wish I was like that.......

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-24T19:22:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-77462>

Where's your BAB in all that? Man I do NOT get into the tub or shower without having had my BAB with all the fat that goes with it. I usually wait 30 minutes to give myself a chance to get the digestion fully underway THEN I climb in.

Hey ... the older we get the less we give a fig for conventions. What're they gonna do? Arrest her?

Hope you had a good weekend!

### caroline — 2013-02-24T23:07:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-77495>

BAB was always my stumbling point. By the time I eat and have a couple of BPC etc and go on line I didn't want
To get in the tub. I have only been having a HAB for a long time now anyway and not hungry until around 9am. Then I eat dinner around 4:30pm. It works great for me anyway - probably would suck for someone else.

### caroline — 2013-02-25T09:11:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-77530>

It has turned out to be a beautiful day. Not much happening at the ocean - not a bride insight........ But ....... I did meet a sister of a bride. She is from Yorkshire in england and came for her sisters wedding yesterday. She saw me in the water so she waded in - she had a dress on! She said it looked like I was having fun so she would have a go! She wanted to know if there was any fish?? I wondered if she was epi-paleo??? She also said she was quite hungover!!

My left foot is killing me so I have decided to stop walking in the surf for awhile - although I tend to walk in circles just to keep busy so I might just as well be walking in a straight line! LOL

### Inger — 2013-02-25T10:04:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-77537>

Caroline, do you have wifi on your laptop? What about an Iphone?
My testing shows they are really bad for us..... if I had health issues I would not use them at all.... do you have an old fashioned computer too, completely wired? Do you live in a apartement with neighbours that use wifi too? My bro said it radiates around 100 meters or so... you gotta buy you a cottage somewhere..lol

### caroline — 2013-02-25T10:12:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-77538>

Inger - I do have an iPhone but I hardly use it and I do have a laptop. The trouble is I can get about 15 other signals from neighbors. I would love to move down the coast and build a small cottage ....... My dream......

### caroline — 2013-02-25T10:15:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-77539>

I am trying to picture in my mind what I would build. I want to learn about all the natural materials to use etc. in Canada we had a stone cottage and I loved it there. Our restaurant was Also in a heritage stone building. It was very calming...

### endless — 2013-02-26T05:13:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-77722>

Stone would be great! Especially if the floors were stone, too. Grounding all the time.

How on earth do you manage your laptop while you CT? I'd be afraid I was going to dump it in the bath!

### caroline — 2013-02-26T10:07:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-77738>

Krista - I use my ipad mostly if it has a charge. I have a laundry hamper that is the right height and I put it beside the tub. I do worry about dropping it in tho! I can put my laptop on the hamper and angle it to see it.

My bathtub is low tho and narrow ..... I am always worried about getting out of it. I am afraid I will crack my head open and not be found for a month!!

Stone floors would be great - what about slate? A lot of our floors were slate - I loved them so much. There were kind of that soft aqua color.

I guess we would want the bedrooms on the lowest level? And would every room have a Faraday cage?

I met an elderly gentleman at the beach today he swims every single day. Apparently there is a whole mob of 30 - 40 people that swim every am at 5:30.

I had originally thought that is what I would do - but now - I want the sun. And there would be no brides underfoot at that time of the morning!
But I think I will wander down and have a look if I wake up early enough. Later in the day does have its advantages - surfers etc. etc. can't underestimate all the half naked men!

I don't wake up these days until 6:30 - 7:00 - don't know why that is happening but it is a good thing. I seem to be getting about 9 hours of sleep - that is amazing for me.

I had some nerve testing on my feet and legs done today - get results next week. Just realized the guy didn't ask me if I could be pregnant!

I feel so saddened about what is going on here. A couple of people are being so rude and insulting to JK and all of us really ..... where is that coming from???? I keep resisting the urge to jump in - it will just perpetuate it and won't help I guess. I don't understand the mindset and the ridicule is beyond belief ....talk about missing neurons......... EMF is such a huge problem and great minds have to come together...isn't that basically what JK is saying????

Caroline, isn't all that sleep wonderful? Mine is still a little hinky after months of having it be so great. Still haven't quite figured out why. I have my suspicions but don't want to give Jack a chance to thump me on the head and say "I told you so."

When will you hear about the tests? Are you any better?

and yeah, +1 on what Jack just said.

### caroline — 2013-02-27T10:29:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-77984>

pretty uneventful day at the ocean - altho I did talk to a chinese guy with a dog. I couldn't figure out if his partner was a man or a woman - pretty strange - but he was lovely and chatty and the partner was quite surly. The dogs were gorgeous. There are always a ton of people walking their designer dogs! I often see people trying to make these little tiny dogs go into the surf - there is almost always really big waves and crashing surf. These poor tiny dogs are terrified! Last week i talked to a woman who was trying to get her little handbag dog to go in and he was simply terrified ... the dog had these big pleading eyes looking at me to save him. This dog was desperate to come to me - kinda sad. Why does a dog have to go in the water??? Will have to ask Diane about that...

I had a hard time sleeping again last nite because of all this forum stuff - I kept trying to think of gorgeous surfer dudes but always wound up back here. Not good.

Does anyone know much about magnesium? I finally got mine yesterday but can't remember when to take what and how much. I suppose Jk would say big doses at first anyway to see if that will help my feet. I thought Malate was the one to use before bed - but in my notes I also have orate before bed....
Does everyone have notes all over the place like I do? Sometimes something comes up and I just grab anything to write on and sometimes I can't even read my writing! When I was at the Doctor's last week I couldn't read half my notes - I think he was better at it than me and he reads really well upside down!

I hope everyone has a great nite's sleep....

### diane — 2013-02-27T16:35:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-78009>

I use Evernote for my notes (computer app) which makes it easy for me - so they're all in one place.

No, dogs don't have to go in the water. Some like it, some don't. But you can teach it to go in the water, and I believe it's good to take your dog out of their comfort zone to teach them, build confidence. Same as people.  Lots of owners don't understand their dogs - and when the dog meets someone who does, you can get the "eyes". LOL. I see lots of dogs that would like to come home with me.

Animals also sense "energy" (or maybe it's electrons??) way more than people do. So i think that many animals are attracted to people who are more connected, and perhaps this is part of why some animals choose others over their owners. I don't have the answer to this - am thinking more and more of it though.

### endless — 2013-02-27T18:01:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-78023>

My dog likes the water, but she is a terrible swimmer! She sinks quickly, so if she is carrying a ball or a stick she can't breathe. I bought her a lifejacket

Carolyn, I take the orotate at noon and the malate at night, but in the sleep prescription they are reversed. I guess it depends what you are using it for. The Mitochondria Rx has malate at bedtime.

### diane — 2013-02-27T18:17:32+0200

Haha- Krista, my dog needs a lifejacket too! I took him to a dog pool (yes that exists, and used it last winter when my female dog recovered from ACL surgery) and he figured out his back feet could touch the bottom. So he would walk on the bottom and paddle his front paws like he was swimming. I almost died laughing.

### caroline — 2013-03-01T10:51:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-78481>

so - nothing new really. I stayed with a friend last nite and went to book club. There actually was stuff I could eat! I ate so many shrimp I was almost embarrassed!! I did eat a rice paper wrap with veggies which I guess I shouldn't have - but - I did. Also some marinated mushrooms and some carrots and cucumbers. I was going to say I was proud of myself - but that isn't the case anymore .... I am just not going to have something just because it is there ... it isn't even a will power thing - I am only going to eat things that are electron/nutrient dense - period - end of story.

My dear friend did make me a glass of fresh squeezed orange juice this morning - It was only a small amount so I did drink it and said thanks so much.

I really simply don't fit in any more - I have moved on or whatever that is. I am not interested in talking about and drooling over food. I cut the cake and served it to everyone with a custard tart - I was the only one who didn't have any.

Everyone has gained so so much weight since I saw them last - maybe 4 months ago and all the ailments are distressing. I was pretty proud of myself - my weight is at an all time low - at least for quite a number of years. When I jumped on the scale in the morning I was 57K and feeling absolutely great \[ except for my stupid,stupid feet!\] Two people didn't recognise me at first! But honestly - I think when you look healthy and slim - people/women don't know what to make of it - it is very strange. It is like there is an elephant in the room.
Has anyone else experienced this? It is definitely me that has changed - I want much more out of my life - I don't want to read the book club books .... I just want to read and UNDERSTAND EMF 7 & 8 and what ever else is coming. I want a simple life full of love and kindness and joy and smiles and laughter and blue sky and stars and flowers and beautiful music and I am lucky enough to be able to do it at the ocean. I don't think it gets much better than that.

I had a very interesting chat with a man his morning - he has travelled all over the world and had some really interesting and smart thoughts on alot of things. I missed my bus but decided it was much more fun to talk to him! It is so true - when your heart is open - amazing things keep happening......you seem much more approachable to people I guess. I had a lovely chat with a young black man at the bus stop ...and he smelled so darn good!! how do they do that??? But the thing is - it is my life that is so much more enriched. It is so much fun getting entangle with everyone you meet!! I wouldn't trade this for anything. I guess if this is what happens when you are a cult member ...so be it....I am all in....

### Inger — 2013-03-01T11:53:40+0200

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-01T18:53:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-78514>

Caroline, I know exactly what you mean about the food thing. I have a friend here who had a lap band years and years ago (before they called 'em lap bands ... can't remember the term because my brain is still offline) ... she nearly died from it. She is still morbidly obese and about the only thing she posts is pictures of gooey cakes. That and guns. She'll post 4 or 5 of those a day. I've almost blocked her posts because it just makes me crazy.

DH SIL continues to insist that she can eat whatever she wants as long as it's in moderation. She is 5'1' tall if she's lucky and she weighs 200 pounds. She had a heart attack 2 years ago, has been on 3 different anti-depressants for a good 17-18 years and is now on statins. Her father had colon cancer and last year they removed the last of his colon. His body is here but he is long gone.

My sister's health is in the toilet. Her husband is a T2 diabetic. They eat lots of grains and low fat. and they both continue a downward spiral.

DH's oldest niece just had a double mastectomy at age 50 because she had two exams that might have shown cancer. MIGHT.

BOTH our neighbors have developed cancer.

Dear friends in arizona have both had cancer. Another of hubby's friends is beyond morbidly obese and had colon cancer. I don't have to tell you what all of them are eating and doing. But they don't learn. They don't question. They just continue to trudge down the road to failing health, dementia and a sad death.

The friggin' list is endless.

If I let it get to me I would be a sodden heap every single day of my life. Instead I keep plodding down the path Jack has laid out. And like you, I find myself feeling calmer, more at peace with every passing day. More in tune with who I am supposed to be. No amount of psychobabble or therapy has ever given this to me. It's a sense of at-one-ness and it grows stronger with time. I LIKE it. And I see that you do to.

so enjoy this path. don't let the trolls get you down. They have their own path just as we have ours. I like mine. They can stay back in the dark and try and drag us down but I refuse to see them.

### caroline — 2013-03-05T05:46:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-79019>

I am sitting at the ocean ( stealing someones wifi) it is the most spectacular day. It has been cloudy and raining the last number of days but today ........WOW! Some one is playing opera and the surf is crashing - it just couldn't be more perfect.
This might be one of those posts. - ENTER AT YOUR OWN RISK!

Of course JK's news isn't unexpected - I am very happy for him (and his family) to get off this treadmill for awhile .....but I am sad for me.... Selfish I know.... That is how the cookie is crumbling right now.

I have been thinking a lot lately about the last two years ...... What an unexpected gift. Talk about being in the right place at the right time!

Actually all this started for me - I am realizing - 35 years ago (I think this is going to be a long story so abort now!)

We were putting together a cafe
. I knew nothing about design/layout but I wanted the kitchen to be open and in the middle. As usual Doug thought everything I did was perfect and so off we went. It worked wonderfully well except functionally we had table placement issues big time. Almost all of our tables were large ones - no tables for 1 or 2 which Can be a big problem for people just wanting coffee, in a money making scheme of things this isn't good! You do have to figure out how to pay the bills!

Soooo- since I was in the middle of all the action and knew everyone anyway ......I just started introducing everyone to each other. So when we were really busy and there was no tables - they could just sit together - worked out perfectly- especially since we saw a lot of amazing and interesting people. Right from the beginning I realized this and I was always hugely interested to see who was coming thru the doors and I wanted to know their story.

So - guess where this is leading??

It turns out I have been entangled for years and years..... And JK made me realize this....

### Inger — 2013-03-05T08:40:07+0200

Audrey.. I loved what you said about we are all entangled!!! That is how I feel too! It is a beautiful feeling, I know you know what I mean. We can never separate us from others. Even if we do different things. I think when we feel like that people will know.. and they will feel good when spending time with us because even if we are different we are together and that is why we must always respect others choises too even if we clearly see they are wrong.. but we can never give up. We just go on loving and caring. And the power to do all this we get when we have plenty of electrones...

I almost exploded from energy yesterday at work..lol I served a privat event in the Cabinet, it was a business meeting and dinner with 15 men and 1 women.
The boss asked me all of a sudden; Inger, are you always so happy? (all waitresses have small signboard with their name on) I said.. no. He said, when are you so happy, then? I said, when I have rolled in the snow (because I did in the morning..) and he said.. Do you roll naked? I said, yes of course... and lol.. all the men was staring at me and it was a really funny moment I bet I got red in my face.. The boss was just amazed and could not stop commenting about Inger..lol
I almost told him the whole story I had done that morning.. that I had been sleeping wired to the waterfalls.. been looking in the sun, had an orgasm, ate raw fermented meat, raw oysters too... and seaweed water.. but I could not, it would have been too much so I choose the snow. A waitress should not take any place BTW but just be there without any noticing and take care everyone get their needs filled and stay happy. I always try to do that but what to do when you get such questions..lol

I am egoistic like you too Audrey.. I will miss Jack terribly. But we can take it as a opportunity to really work on us because now we have the information! And 6 months are going fast! Remember he is doing a biohack.. and I love those I bet you do too. Maybe we can all do our own biohacks at the same time and then share, that would be so cool!
And then.. we can stay connected with Jack and each other without internet too.. I know it works because of the quantum theory.  We have each other in our hearts.

The more I think of it the more scared I feel .....obviously I am losing electrons - but why isn't all the beach time and epipaleo helping to restore some electrons?

It seems to me at this rate - I could be looking down the barrel of dementia or a heart attach or both.

or is EMF so big for me that I can't replace the electrons fast enough??????

### caroline — 2013-03-06T09:00:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-79270>

We had another gorgeous day today. I sat on the beach and read the last blog......that would be just great if I actually understood it! I guess we all get the overall gist of them but it would be nice to fully understand!!!

Off to see my Doc tomorrow to get all the new testing ordered - sure hope I don't miss anything.

When I started this journal I wanted to keep myself on track and be fully engaged so I could get the best possible lab results when I retest. With all this foot stuff I suppose my inflammation will be way up and who knows what else.....

I think my sleep is a lot better tho. I am just realising that I fall asleep pretty quickly but since I unplugged my clock and leave my watch in the kitchen I have no idea what time I wake up - but I suspect it is around 5:00am and then I seem to fall asleep for a bit. Maybe tomorrow I will get up and actually see what time it is!

We have some beautiful weather coming up - so - no reading tomorrow! I am just going to have fun! .... I wonder what twist that will take??

Have a great day everyone!

> Fitness - thanks - I have a great Doc. He listened to my first consult with JK and is totally on board. He is just so very busy. I can stretch an appt to 25 or 30 min. But hate to do it because it just puts him farther behind. I can email him if I have forgotten something.
> Enjoy your nice weather! So when it gets really hot. - you don't go out much? Have you always lived in that area?

I lived most of my life in Michigan, which has four distinct seasons. Moved south to Houston, Texas in 2007 and there are two seasons - mild and hot/humid. Right now it is mild. This morning was upper 30s and it reached to about 66 degrees today.

I stopped at the park before work and watched the sky get light, then went back again at lunch to take a break and earth. I take my shoes and socks off and plant my feet on the ground

When it is really hot, I avoid midday heat (no lunch outside at the park). I still stop there in the morning and spend time outside at home after work. On weekends I can swim in the pool, but the water isn't very refreshing when it feels like a warm bath. Soaking in a tub of cold water and ice feels lovely then!

### caroline — 2013-03-07T09:56:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-79436>

Another beautiful day today - I just sat at the beach and did absolutely nothing........I am getting so I can do that really well....an art form I think? could be just laziness?

I went to see my Doc today. On the way - for some reason - I decided I wanted to talk to him about autism. I don't have a clue where that came from - anyway - I forget. I was getting blood drawn and it turns out I knew the lab lady from long time ago and we started chatting. She started talking about my Doctor and how he was so great and really into alternate medicine and does a lot of work with autistic kids! I was stunned that I had made that connection in my mind before getting there. There was no earthly reason for me to want to talk to him about autism - especially since we had so much of my stuff to get thru. Is that a quantum leap do you think???

Her grandson is autistic so I was telling her all about EMF's and the Schumann resonance etc. She took notes and said she will google later! I also gave her the recipe for Paleo Banana Bread and shared some of mine with her.

Did you know that you can be in a fasted state too long before a glucose test? I never knew that - apparently it is 14 hours max and I was at 16 hours if not more so I have to go back tomorrow.

I also discovered that my Doctor's wife is a naturopath .... I find that so interesting. A lot of Docs I knew thought naturopaths are a blight on society --- let alone loving one!

Have a great day everyone - wish I could share the beach and sunshine with all of you...

Ever since yesterday I have been wanting to email him about your EMF series \[at least he has a shot at completely understanding it!\]
and I am going to send him the link to the podcast you did last week. Any other things I can tell him to listen to?

I also sent him the recipe for Banana bread!

I love this deja vu stuff ....this I can get a handle on! I feel amazing today!! \[except for my stupid feet!!\]

### caroline — 2013-03-09T01:33:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-79747>

My day yesterday took a very weird twist.....
I was having a great day - I live close to an open air mall and they have a market on Fridays and sometimes they have an ukelele group that plays. I just love them. They are a mixed bag of older and younger men and women who just get together for fun and play.
They all wear Hawaiian shirts and crazy hats and have a ball! They play alot of oldies but also Elvis stuff and beatles stuff etc.

I started talking to the lady next to me and it turns out her husband is in the group. So I spent about an hour talking to her and then her husband also. He has no musical ability at all and just bought himself an ukelele and showed up for their get togethers. If you go early there are people that will help you learn and they have tons of music.

I had gotten their info before but couldn't figure out how to get there - or at least get home. So my new friend Trevor went and spoke to the guy that is kind of in charge and he said there is all kinds of people going my way and I would get a ride home.

Sooooo - I am buying myself an ukelele and going!!! I am so excited - I have always loved banjos and ukeleles. Apparently they do weekends away and people come from all over.

So now I have to buy myself a Hawaiian shirt and a crazy hat!

That was the great part..... I went to buy some bottles of water and was going to cross the street - and don't remember anything else. When I came too I was sprawled face first on the street and people were standing around me. I heard some one say that they had called an ambulance.

A really lovely man and woman kept talking to me and waited with me for the ambulance to come. I totally remember nothing - I don't remember stepping off the curb - I don't know how long I was there and I can't even remember how the ambo guys picked me up.

Anyway I am fine - just a little beat up. There are a couple of cuts on the inside of my mouth and my nose and my knee. They had a specialist look at me and decided it was better not to stitch anything. They did all the regular tests and EEG etc.

The ambo guy said the payment was all broken where I was - so I must have just tripped - but funny that I don't remember stepping off the curb or anything at all. I don't know how long I was out for because no one saw me go down.

For the longest while I have wanted fuller lips...... well I achieved that yesterday!!! I am kinds scary to look at tho!! It would be just my luck to run into the handsome man on the beach today when I look like I have been in a bar brawl!!

I ripped my good jeans - but then again - I have been wanting a pair that are ripped and broken in - so job well down!

I have blood all over my favorite linen t-shirt - but the nurses gave me tips on getting that out.

My Chinese Doctor gave me a head trauma sheet to tell me what to do later. It says to call an ambulance if I become unconsious - we laughed so hard over that. pretty hard to do if unconscious.

Now I have to figure out what I can eat - it has to be very soft and cold. I am thinking cottage cheese and chocolate nothing else has come to mind yet.

overall - a very good day - I could have done so much damage. Nothing broken and since I landed on my face - I am surprised no teeth are broken or my nose. I am very lucky!

Before it had happened I had bought myself a gorgeous bright orange T-shirt which says in large white letter .."Just Warming Up"

I was thinking of what JK had posted yesterday .... about emptying your pockets of miracles before it is time to check out.

I am very, very lucky in so many ways it boggles my mind....

Now I have to figure out this wifi thing ..... so doing C/T and using your ipad - doe that help at all???

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-09T01:39:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-79750>

CAROLINE!!!!!!! OMG.

If you hit your head, which it sounds as if you did, that's probably why you don't remember what happened. (TV medicine ... you know) If you tripped and landed on your face that probably would cover it. I did a one=point landing in my nose 2 summers ago when I tripped on the hose. One second I was walking, the next I was flat on my face in the grass wondering if I'd broken my neck.

BE CAREFUL!!

And NO on the CT and using the iPad. Unless it's several feet away from you. Jack would say no altogether but hey, baby steps.

For crying out loud... I cannot believe you hurt yourself.

### ashryn — 2013-03-09T02:54:21+0200

Do you think it's your tingling feet that made you trip? I've stood up when my feet were asleep and fallen straight over before.. It really does sound like you need to work out what's going on there. If you can't let go of the wifi, can you shield yourself when you use it? Lessemf.com sell all kinds of shielding clothing, maybe their site will give you an idea about what you can use?

Glad you're not freaking out about the whole experience.. I blacked out once shortly after starting this lifestyle and still have a scar on my knee from it.. I freaked out big time, but we never did work out what it was all about. Nothing like that has happened since either.

Soft cold food ideas for you:
You could make paleo ice cream..
How about a MHS?
any other kind of smoothie or shake you like..
Cold soup (bone broth cold?)
hard boiled eggs?
do you eat oysters cold?

Make sure you rinse your mouth with salt water a couple of times a day whilst you have cuts in it.. Seawater would probably be good

### Inger — 2013-03-09T08:40:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-79785>

Oh Audrey.. dear.. I am so glad nothing worse happened...! OMG. Your life really is full of action..huh
I do had to LOL when reading about the "just warming up" T-shirt.. too funny! Maybe it was a sign to you too..? to take it serious with the wifi issue and your numb feet.. or things will start happend.. hmmmm..
That might be a constructive way to look at the whole thing too!
So,
now to the wifi issue.
Can you get your internet all wired? That is the road I would go. Wired, or non. I would not use wifi at all. If you cannot get wired at home (but you should be able to, if you have corded phone and otherwise too I guess, at least you can here) then go to the library and use the computer there only. It is wired (at least here) and it would limit your time on it too nicely..lol
Check your Iphone that the wifi and bluetooth is off! Better, sell it and buy a very old cellphone that you only use for sms and limit calls as much as you can. For the money you get, buy oysters.

### Inger — 2013-03-09T08:50:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-79787>

Cottage cheese and chocolate? Hmmm..lol I would let the cheese out.. no good for healing I bet. If, do some nice roquefort from raw sheeps milk! Or some other raw cheese from goat or sheep.

You can make you nice fishsmoothies, MHS? Or just do the raw fish in the mixer with some spices and raw onion or such, it tastes nice! I do this all the time and eat raw "fish-cakes" from cheap fishes I do not like too much raw. Sashimi from salmon is very soft.
Tartar too. You do not need to chew raw animal foods much, as they digest fine even if you swallow bigger pieces. Do a seaweed-smoothie! Make it with soaked wakame or other mild tasting seaweeds, chocolate powder, coconut oil, maybe an egg yolk and a tiny bit raw honey or stevia maybe if you want it sweet.

If you want something cooked make a fish soup and puree it.

Do you have a grounding wire yet Audrey? If not, make you one soon! It will help healing too, and you need it badly to protect yourself from all the wifis from your neighbours! Can you dig the grounding rod (just a thick copper wire) into the earth behind your house and let the wire run up in your window? Then tie yourself onto it as much as you can daytime, and all night long too.

### caroline — 2013-03-10T00:18:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-79865>

Thanks everyone for the well wishes!
I don't think it was my feet......I was hurrying to get a bus and carrying my bags of water. I was holding my debit card and I suspect I was looking in my bag for my wallet to put it away and don't remember anything after that. When you fall - you usually remember going into the fall and trying to save yourself.
I remember absolutely nothing except coming too and people talking to me ...and feeling like an idiot!

I really don't understand why/how I knocked myself out - I have no bumps on my head or anywhere - just all the trauma to my face and inside my mouth and my knee. I was fine before hand - no feeling faint or anything - I felt great.....so why was I out cold - with no memory????

Anyway - I had just picked up my cortisol test at the post office before it happened and was going to do it today and then send it back tomorrow.

My sleep has just been awful the last 2 days and I lay awake most all last night \[and no - S/O didn't help!\]

I finally realised it was pretty stupid to do the ASI in this condition.... I screwed up the last one and sure don't want to do the same this time!

Anyone got any ideas????

I don't understand why I can't sleep - I am quite tired and wrung out - like I really have been in a bar brawl!

### caroline — 2013-03-11T09:03:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-80032>

more quantum entanglement today!!
I was on my way into the ocean when a young woman said Hi - she liked how I was laying on the wet sand and decided to do the same! so I stopped to chat.

It turns out she is a fellow Canadian - from Manitoba. She went to teacher's college here but has to go home in 2 weeks because she is 31 and can't stay any longer - however she does have an Aussie boyfriend! - more on that later!

The last job she has had here was working with a family who have twin 9 year old autistic sons ..... so I guess you know where this is heading......She told me that she loved that job and has discovered a real affinity for working with autistic kids and wants to continue doing that. She is currently working on a masters.

I gave her Dr. Tim Jackson's name and Dr. K. of course and told her about the interview and how to find it and all the stuff here.

She is a lovely young woman looking for excellence and I am so proud to have sat in the sand with her and talked for a couple of hours.

I dropped into a walk in clinic to get my mouth looked at like the ER Doc told me to. No one seems to know how I could knock myself out with no bump.

I have been back to the scene of the crime twice now - can't figure it out. My blood is still all over the pavement!

I am so bummed that I need to wait a month before doing the ASI ....but the good thing is that I thought to ask!!

When I started this journal I wanted to be 100% in so I could get some good lab results - so we will see.

One good thing ..... I had all my bloods done the day before .......

### caroline — 2013-03-12T08:54:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-80162>

Nothing new today - I am feeling really flat and not much energy...... so not attracting any Quantum anything! very sad state of affairs - I will have to pick up my game tomorrow - that is for sure. I guess my body had a lot more trauma than I realised and now telling me to take it easy. Okay already!

I forgot - I ran into a friend of my daughters who I have always loved. She has been transferred to a bank where I live so I will get to see more of her.
We sat in her office and pretended we were doing business!! She is such a lovely young woman with the biggest smile and kind heart.

She told me she has lots of wealthy clients so she will keep her eyes open - I can't believe I even went there.....

I am not eating very much but my weight has gone up by 2 Kilos - very strange. I think I am not drinking enough water - it is so hard to open my mouth...

I finally had enough nerve to try to look in my mouth to see what is going on......I did chip one of my front teeth and there is this huge flap of skin just hanging. I look really and positively attractive - let me tell you .......

I guess no bride is gonna want me in their pictures now for sure!

### Inger — 2013-03-12T09:08:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-80164>

Oh Audrey.. I am so sorry about your mouth! Good thing is it heals very fast in the mouth.

Audrey.. whenever you like you can come and stay by where I live for a while! In summer here is so nice! Not too warm, and we can go to the coastline often when i get my drivers license and lay on the warm rocks and CT in the Baltic sea.. and you will get an own bedroom too or sleep outside on the grass here are many places to do that.. we would have so much fun! Here are no wifi signals as of yet so your feet would heal I am sure too!

### vkiernan — 2013-03-12T13:52:34+0200

I have been checked out by ER but I don't think I asked any questions - just answered what they where asking....I need a do over....

### caroline — 2013-03-14T09:46:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-80478>

A pretty uneventful day - not much energy at all. I thought I would bounce back faster.

When I was in the ocean today a big wave knocked me down and scared the crap out of me!! It seems like I can't keep upright for more than 5 minutes!

I did find out something tho that I have been dying to know .......all these gorgeous young women wear the tiniest bikinis and I always wonder how they keep their tops on .....doesn't everyone want to know that??? The waves are always huge. They can re arrange you pretty fast.
I finally asked the gorgeous young woman I was standing beside before the wave decked me.
This is what she told me...... "when you see a wave coming ... You grab your breasts - easy"
So there you go - problem solved!

Have a great one everybody.......

When I was in ER the Doc said she was having a specialist look at my mouth re - stitching etc. So - it was a woman and she seemed very knowledgeable and was explaining to the Doc why I didn't need stitches. I thought to myself - okay she is a specialist - knows what she is talking about.....
Yesterday I got the discharge notes in the mail and it turns out this woman is a nurse practitoner - not so happy about that. I have all this skin flapping around and the Doc I saw the other day said it should have been stitched.

All of us here definitely know to ask a lot of questions and have more knowledge than average but when it is just you and you are in shock .....what the heck can you do??? I have a million questions now......

### caroline — 2013-03-16T06:22:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-80736>

It is another stunning day at the beach. I am sitting in wet sand - hope that helps somewhat......

The waves are really huge today and the sound is magnificent. I am listening to Bethoven.........

I am having the most amazing summer........funny how things turn out. I could never imagine that at this stage of my life
I am sitting on a beach in bikini bottoms (I will leave you to figure out what else if anything!)
The only thing that would be better is someone to share it with....but I needed to get to this point first. I have crossed paths with so many amazing people the last few years. Talk about quantum biology at work! I am really grateful.....

I was looking for a blog yesterday......and once again.....I swear there are tons I have not seen! I really do think I am keeping up and JK is sneaking more in on us!

When I read one again - I am stunned at how all the answers are right there! Could I have been that fogged in??
The answer is yes, yes, yes!
We must drive JK crazy with our questions when it is all here - we just have to dig for it....

when Dr. k. goes "walk about" For six months or however long ......I am going to re read all the blogs once again and all the comments. Sometimes the comments are just as or more important

I also need to re read some threads. There is so much gold......

Now - back to the ocean and the waves and trying to keep my clothes on.........

### caroline — 2013-03-16T06:25:27+0200

I understand what you mean about rereading the blogs. The amount of information that is available is overwhelming. I also have read them all but need a photographic memory to retain it!

Your day at the beach sounds grand. All the sunshine, the smell and sound of the surf, and people watching. You seem to draw people to you with all the conversations and interactions. Who knows? One of them may be an interesting man the next time

We spent our day and evening at the Houston Rodeo. Lots of good people watching there! Blake Shelton was the entertainment tonight and I love his music. We snuck out about 15 minutes before the show was over. There were 75,000 people in the arena. Fortunately that early start got us out of the parking area and on road before the mass congestion started. Time to turn in. I'm tired.

### caroline — 2013-03-18T07:29:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-80940>

Another great day - not terribly warm but the sky is a brilliant blue and someone is playing opera again. It just overwhelms me......

I dont have much energy but I guess that is to be expected. Yesterday I crawled back in bed at 12:00 and covered up with a duvet. When the seasons start to change I seem to be cold a lot. Really don't understand and can't remember what JK said. I remember he did say it is because of the light cycles changing.

I am starting to get really tired around 6:30 and head for bed around 8:30. I seem to be falling asleep pretty fast. I got up twice for the bathroom but seem to go back to sleep. I then wake about 5:30 but fall back asleep again unti about 7:30 or 8:00. That seems like a crap load of sleep.

Does anyone know how much spring water we should be drinking? Or just to thirst?
All I do is think about water - yikes! There is a morning shower if I have to wash my hair.... Then C/T.....then shower after the ocean and then trying to drink my body weight in spring water.....

I am trying to do everything I can before doing my saliva/cortisol test in 2 + weeks. I hope it isn't too soon after the fall.

I have been having lamb heart and tons of shrimp and salmon. I know the best would be a boatload of oysters. Just the thought makes me gag.....gotta get over that somehow...

Anyone got any other ideas that could get me back to where I should be?

I hope everyone has had a lovely day - spring should just be around the corner for y'all...

### janagram — 2013-03-18T08:05:09+0200

### endless — 2013-03-18T16:52:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-80975>

I would just enjoy the sleep...your body must need it now. Sounds to me like you are doing a lot already...hopefully including emf reduction. I think the best thing you could do to prepare for your cortisol test is not worry about it.

### caroline — 2013-03-21T00:53:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-81237>

I had the loveliest day yesterday. I met a friend in Sydney who I haven't seen since we were in Uzebek last October. I just love her.....she is always fun to be with. She always speaks to everyone she sees and is so positive and we wind up in the most amazing conversations with strangers. We spent the day in the Northern beaches - so beautiful. Janet's house is a federation style - over 100 years old. We went to a restaurant/cafe on balmoral beach. I have had their cookbook for 10 years and have wanted to go there for so long.

My lunch was great ..... Grilled baby octopus and shrimp and scallop ceviche with some greens - just perfect.

Two days ago I saw my Doctor - fist time since I fell He is concerned because I can't remember going into the fall and wants me to wear a holter monitor for a day and maybe get an MRI. As he was walking me out of his office he was kind of musing about me being fasted that morning and probably dehydrated as well.

After I left him I realized I had been fasted the day before and they had taken 5-6 vials of blood and I had felt really faint and my head was spinning. The next day I fasted again for the glucose test. When I fell it was about 6 hours later and I still hasn't eaten or drank anything.....so I think problem solved ....my blood sugar must have really dropped and I fainted.
That accounts for why my blood was so close to the sidewalk and why I don't remember stepping off the curb ...I didn't!

That makes me feel so much better! I emailed my Doc and he said very likely - but wear the holter monitor anyway just to check.

It is going to be another gorgeous day - heading to the beach......

I am working really hard on limiting EMFs. - trying to only spend 1 hour per day .....

### caroline — 2013-03-23T07:58:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-81516>

A friend shared this with me and I want to share it with all of you.
I think this is quantum love and caring ......J.K.Style

The empty chair
LETTER FROM A MOTHER TO A DAUGHTER

" My dear girl, the day you see me getting old, I ask you to please be patient, but most of all, try to understand what I am going through. If when we talk, I repeat the same thing a thousand times, don't interrupt to say "you said the same thing a minute ago"....... Just listen, please. Try to remember the times when you were little and I would read the same story night after night, until you would fall asleep. When I don't want to take a bath, don't be mad and embarrass me. Remember when I had to run after you making excuses and trying to get you to take a shower when you were a little girl? When you see how ignorant I am when it comes to new technology, give me the time to learn and don't look at me that way......remember, honey, I patiently taught you how to do many things like eating appropriately, getting dressed combing your hair and dealing with life's little issues every day ......the day you see I'm getting old, I ask you to please be patient, but most of all, try to understand what I am going through. If I occasionally lose track of what we are talking about, give me time to remember, and if I can't, don't be nervous, impatient or arrogant. Just know in your heart that the most important thing for me is to be with you. And when my old tired legs don't let me move as quickly as before, give me your hand the same way that I offered mine to you when you first walked. When those days come, don't feel sad....just be with me, and understand me while I get to the end of my life with love. I'll cherish and thank you for the gift of time and joy we shared. With a big smile and the huge love I've always had for you, I just want to say, I love you....my darling daughter.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-23T13:53:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-81521>

Do you have a path in life you follow or do you make your own trails? The choices you make make you. Nothing else does. Bad choices can unmake you. Choices are the paths and trails that our thoughts follow to take to make a decision. Choices exist because we have options and every option is an opportunity. Since we become the sum of our choices, what are the best ones to make? What of these are truly important? It's easy to decide when we know what our values are. There is never a wrong time to make the right choices. Life is made up of so many options, there is bad, good, better and best...but the choice is yours! It is your responsibility to make the correct ones. Today begin to blaze a trial to your optimal self.

### caroline — 2013-03-24T00:25:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-81583>

Thanks Dr. K. For dropping that in here .......some days it is hard to blaze a trail ....but if not now ...... when? The clock is ticking....my optimal self awaits.....so many really huge possibilities.....I can't wipe the smile of my face!

### caroline — 2013-03-26T07:35:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-81823>

It is really cloudy today and very humid ....I will go to the ocean later this evening .....see if the scenery is any different!

I bought a salt lamp today and some stones and found out about meditation classes. The woman that owns this shop is really lovely. She is moving her shop and so classes will start in a couple of weeks - can't wait.

I spent a couple of hours in a shop that has the most gorgeous stones set in jewelry - the shop ladies were just great and we tried all kinds on - fun.
We wound up talking about Tibet and the Dalai Lama . One of the woman is making a pilgrimage with her friend who has had cancer. She used the Dalai Lama as her focus for meditation while fighting cancer ....very inspiring stuff.
I think I have decided on a cross on quite a long chain - Tibet changed my life and This cross I think symbolizes that.

I am trying to stay cold, dark and boring until next week when I do the ASI .....keeping my fingers crossed that I have recovered enough altho my sleep is terrible and I don't know why ....I was sleeping so well before. I need to get up for the bathroom multiple times - don't understand what has changed. That means I am dehydrated - right? So that means the spring water isn't getting into my cells - but what has changed? I will have to examine everything I am doing and see if I can figure it out - not sleeping is such bad news.

### Inger — 2013-03-26T09:22:10+0200

Audrey,
you could take a wide glass bowl with you to the beach, lots of springwater bottles and put the bowl in the sand and pour the spring water in.. and let it stay as long as you tan! Sprincle maybe some natural salt in the water too? Or put some wild herbs or seaweed in it? I bet the water gets way better then and maybe your body can absorb it better too?
Have you gotten rid of your laptop yet? Or buyed a trifield meter to check it out so you believe how bad it is...
Remember to tan nude too Audrey. It is so good. It helps healing so very much. They must be a lonely spot somewhere for you.
Could you try sleep at the beach or somewhere in the nature just to test if you sleep better there, a place with no wifis? Maybe you have a tent you could use, do some camping!  If I had the money I would come right now and camp with you Audrey! One day. We must get your feet better. Maybe they are already better Audrey?
Oh.. and I guess carbs is not a good idea living in a high EMF environment. No apples Audrey. But maybe you quit them already? Maybe you could buy tinfoil and cover the walls in your bedroom? If you cannot move anytime soon?

### caroline — 2013-03-27T00:59:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-81900>

Thanks Inger for all the suggestions. I have quit apples - about 2 months ago and have hardly any carbs at all - almost none. I have ordered broadband - should be live in about a week. I did sleep must better last nite ...I walked in the surf and then went home to bed. I think it is coffee that bothers me - at least cheap coffee. What I am drinking now is 1 decaf in the morning but I think it has alot of mould. When I drink my regular coffee - I am fine. I will go to Sydney next week and get some.

I would love to go camping with you Inger - someday - when we both have money!!!!

I am going to go down the south coast and share a little house on the beach for a week with a friend. I cant wait - no wifi etc.
I havent seen Patricia since we were in Tibet together. We shared so many amazing and life altering experiences.

### caroline — 2013-03-28T05:07:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-82024>

I wonder if this is happening to all of you guys...I keep getting into conversations about EMFs and the SR and grounding etc. etc.
I went for Physio on my foot today. I haven't seen Justin for about 2 years since I have been pain free because of CT etc.
He is the loveliest young man - very kind and gentle and he is a musician as well so of course I had to talk to him about The SR etc. I wonder if we bore people to tears?? Anyway - we had a great conversation.
Next on my agenda was the hairdresser - I haven't been for about 6 months. Tracy didn't recognize me ( I have been going to her for about 5 years). So of course we talked about hormones etc. her assistant was washing my hair - she is a young girl - but I learned so much from her! She is into natural food and the ocean and gardening and her boyfriend is a surfer.
I didn't know that surfers are into meditation and yoga because they need to get in a zone......so interesting. Here all this time I was just looking at the great bodies ....how shallow am I??

### caroline — 2013-03-30T08:17:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-82259>

I sat at the beach today and read The Hormone 101 blog . I have read it a few times. I just can't connect all the dots. My Vit D is great ... my hscrp is good. but my f T3 is low \[it has come up\] and my r T3 is high but it has come down - so I am still just not converting properly. I wonder why this is more problematic when the seasons change? I am cold a lot now and sleep has gone down the tubes and I am getting up a few times for the bathroom.

I can see how this would be a huge problem if you weren't controlling your exposure to artificial light - so it must just be an internal clock thing?

I bought a corded phone today - I can't even imagine how long since I have used one! Thank goodness the sales guy was from the U.S. and took pity on me when he realised I was from Canada .....so much to learn ...so little time!

I talked to a lady at the bus stop - we are about the same vintage and on our own. We talked about surfers- made perfect sense to me and how they are in the zone when surfing. Surfers never drown - but they do get eaten by sharks occasionally!

She was going to see the vintage Jaguar rally at the north beach - I was going to go but decided if there was someone interesting there they would just have to come to the south beach to find me! ... I needed to read hormones 101 more than looking at old guys with old jags!

I'll probably regret that decision ........

I was all set to go and looked out and the sun was shining - so - once again - took off all my clothes and put on swimmers and tank top and headed for the ocean. It was really nice - just not hot enough but I am so glad I went........and.........
I saw a bride!- it has been ages ...I thought they must be avoiding me!

Why oh why do brides travel around in those ridiculous 2 block long hummers????

You can get the most megawatt smile from a little kid - makes my whole day.....

### Hope — 2013-04-07T02:38:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-83159>

Audrey, was thinking of you today....as I was sitting in 10 degrees C and wind by a lake, barefoot....and freezing my feet off after I walked a bit in the lake. but I eventually warmed up....and got to read EMF 6 for her 4th time (and hopefully will start EMF7 tomorrow!).

Jack told me in October, winter is a great time to do CT and be reduced. It really made me change the way I look at winter, dark, and even nature in the winter on my walks. I found a beauty in it, the barren-ness, the dead-ness...there is a purpose for it. I never saw it like that before, and now I do. Not sure that makes sense at all....

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-04-07T03:45:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-83172>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=83159)
>
> ack told me in October, winter is a great time to do CT and be reduced. It really made me change the way I look at winter, dark, and even nature in the winter on my walks. I found a beauty in it, the barren-ness, the dead-ness...there is a purpose for it. I never saw it like that before, and now I do. Not sure that makes sense at all....

When I was 19, 20, and 21, we used to do winter skinny dips in the ocean and in glacial rivers in the Pacific Northwest. At that time it seemed like kind of an extreme thing to do, but we were never in the water more than a few minutes. After all these years, and thanks to Jack and this website, I've embraced the cold deeply. All winter I've been soaking in the cold water, and I am particularly fond of being in natural cold water, outside, in the environment. I agree that there is so much beauty to be seen when one is willing to try something that most would consider strange or extreme. I find solace, peace, and exhilaration in the cold water, outside. I am surprised by the benefits and how much I end up craving it. Now that it is getting warm where I live, things are changing, and they are still beautiful, but there is something about the cold starkness of winter that just feels right within my soul. Plus, I could do without the water beetles that seem to think my legs are something to find food in! Ack!

> Going to order a bikini, havent owned one in about 18 years. Wont wear it in front of anyone yet, but I want to soak up some rays w/o getting caught stark naked by some poor soul that happens upon me...lol.
>
> Sooooo .... what color bikinis are we getting? I actually bought bikini bottoms some months ago and then just wear a tank top and then roll it up to tan my tummy! Firs time in my life I have ever had a brown tummy - love it!
>
> I find Bikini tops so difficult - they are always so padded - I don't need or want that!!!
>
> I would love to sunbathe nude tho - haven't figured out how to make that happen yet. I love to see older women on the beach who have far from an great body comp and they could care less...I love that! They have all these rolls of fat and they are just strutting their stuff - it actually makes them attractive ... I guess because of their own positive body imagine.
> I need to get some of that!

### Inger — 2013-04-12T09:41:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-83868>

I love pink or blue bikinis.. go so well with tanned skin.. I love white bikinis too.. I have a white, a pink, a blue, a romantic beige/flower, a black and white stripes one, and a black bikini. I think it really depends on your color what fits and looks nice.

I do not care if the top has some padding in it.. makes it look sexier..lol But I hate those metal things and never buy those with it in, or I take them out. Audrey you can take the padding out too, mostly! There are many without padding too... makes you nipples shine through really much though.. ha! But maybe it is hot?

I would love if everyone would only wear bikinis all summer long.. so liberating! And I have no perfect body comp either! I need some more positive body image too.. But I am quite happy with my body today - I have learned to love myself better and better - and I think those confident ladies on the beach with fat rolls have hubbies that really love them with the fat and all... I think that is so important to feel loved.
Audrey, remember we can just love ourselves as much as we want  so it does not matter if we have no partner to tell us we are hot..lol To me it helps to make some pedicure, use some oils on the skin and stuff. To just take care. I think we women need that somehow. And tan. I think I always look better with a tan. I could bet tanning is slimming..?!

I love stretchy clothes too! Almost all my clothes are... I have gained some this winter (I always do - I bet because being so much more disconnected from earth than I am in summer) and I do not realize it so much when everything is stretching..lol so makes me not stress about it at all!

### caroline — 2013-04-12T10:49:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-83869>

Inger - you always make me laugh.... but sometimes you make me cry too - because you are so insightful and inspiring.
I love how you think about things - you have a way of reaching into your soul and examining everything and coming to terms with stuff ... some pretty big stuff .....and then you see all the positives and wonder and joy that life has to offer.

We have been on an amazing journey this past year - can you see how far YOU have come? It is truly extraordinary all the progress we have made and how we all are helping and supporting each other on this trip to optimal.

### caroline — 2013-04-12T11:05:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-83870>

Okay back to bikinis - I think Black Bikinis are stunning ... but there was a young woman on the beach in a soft orange one -
She looked spectacular! I am not sure about the nipple action tho - will have to think about that! I have bought a few things this summer that are a soft orange or soft melon colour - I so love it. I have never bought those colours before - but - I have never been this colour before!

We have had a couple of lovely days - around 24 C and clear blue skies and tomorrow is going to be the same. I am trying to soak up every possible sun ray while I can.

yes - we do have to love ourselves .. so someone else can love us. I have discovered essential oils and love them so much. I would love to have the money to spend the day at a beautiful spa and be pampered - what fun!

### Inger — 2013-04-12T11:37:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-83874>

Soft orange... wow!!! That sounds amazing! I bet it looks awesome with a tan... I actually love soft orange too...

24 C? But Audrey.. that is summer temps? Warm summer temps! I think I am jealous now..

I do not have money for spa or stuff either. But I make my own spa for free when I feel like it.  way better! Actually I have a fabulous spa out in the river..lol I bet way better than any commercial spa?  100% natural. Earthed.

### caroline — 2013-04-13T01:14:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-83945>

Inger - I can always count on you to see the positives in everything - I think that is your gift....
Yes - what could be better than laying in the sand at the ocean - and swimming too ...with beautiful sunshine and clear blue skies and some days there is opera coming from somewhere .... I could totally combust from joy!
no spa in the world could compete with that for sure and .....it is free!

I hope everyone has a terrific weekend!

### caroline — 2013-04-14T09:38:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-84047>

I am still at the ocean .....it has been the most remarkable day. It has been about 28 degrees with a stunning blue sky. Lots of yachts out today and skydivers and surfers. I love to see the little tiny kids playing in the sand and in the water..it is just magic to see them get so much joy out of the very simplistic of things. They are so happy and have huge grins and lots of giggles.
For the first time ever ...I wore bikini bottoms and a sports bra top! The world didn't end! I only stayed in one spot - but baby steps lol. ...... I have the most gross middle - kind of flab city.. I guess - at this late stage - it is what it is. After a couple of kids and a surgeon who split me open from stem to stern and then trussed me up like a pig - what can I expect?

It really doesn't matter at all in the scheme of things. - I am a very, very lucky girl - and that is an understatement-

I hope everyone has had a great day ........

So far so good, Audrey. Enjoying P3 immensely. Had my lunch of salmon and an avocado at the park today. There is a squirrel that climbs up on the picnic table and visits with me regularly. I shared a slice of the avocado with it

My appetite is good, but two meals per day is all I am eating on most days. Don't seem to need three meals right now...

### caroline — 2013-04-17T05:31:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-84312>
