I also need to keep it very simple - I learned that from doing HCG. I eat really simply and the same things pretty well all the time - don't mind a bit - I actually love it this way and really enjoy what I have.

So glad you are doing well! Your weight is stabilizing okay?

### caroline — 2013-04-17T09:30:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-84320>

I had a fun day yesterday. I decided to take the train to Sydney and just muck about. It was a rainy day so took a day off from the beach. We have a really gorgeous newish mall in the CBD with all the designer shops like Channel etc. that I love to browse thru. I was passing a very elegant jewelry shop and paused to look in their windows. I figure if I were ever to remarry I need to know what kind of ring I want ....right? Saying that just added another layer of guilt...Sigh

Anyway - as I was admiring all the gorgeous diamonds - the sales assistant came out and started talking to me about all the them. They had a lot of pink diamonds ...I Love pink diamonds! She explained a lot of stuff to me and we kept going from display to display. She kept asking what cut I liked and what setting and kept pulling out each one for me to try on!
What fun!! Then I sat done with her and she was able to pull out a bunch that I liked to try and compare.

She kept telling me that they had a six month lay a way plan .... I loved her tenacity! So......the one I chose is $25,500.00
My second favorite was $24,000.00. .....they really were amazingingly beautiful and they all fit perfectly! The thing is - I would never be able to decide between them - so I would have to get both!

So - I have her business card - with her personal phone number - in my wallet.....just in case!

Other than that - I really shouldn't go into my favorite store - especially when I am this broke. I definitely have champagne taste and a beer budget ....Always have had and guess that isn't going to Change anytime soon......

I bought myself some fresh figs and smoked salmon sushi as a treat to have on the train home. I love it how we make great choices now and it isn't even a question ....we are so in control......
Now - if I could just control my love for cashmere sweaters .......

### Inger — 2013-04-17T13:18:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-84325>

Lol.. hubs once bought be a diamond ring, 1 carat - it was a little yellowish-rose colored, in white gold. I loved it. But when we got in financial trouble 2 years ago we had to sell it so I have it no more.. I do love diamonds!

Fortunately all the best things in life are for free Audrey..LOL so no need to be sad we have no champagne and diamonds yet!

No idea about losing because I do not weigh. But I am not fat and I eat always 3 meals so far, and they are big. must be very electron depleted..lol I do think when comes summer and I get much sun and earthing I will cut down on food. But it might be because eating almost only raw though. I guess that is the difference. Or the age. Being younger might be why I can go with more food..? Hmm. Mom (61 yo) eats way less that me. She is also thinner than I though, almost too thin, but full of energy. But she says she cannot eat more! And she never diet, ever. Eats a fairly healthy diet. But not Epi paleo. She does goes every morning in the ice hole..

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2013-04-17T17:44:30+0300

Inger you always are right: best things in life are free! If everybody could think like you!

### caroline — 2013-04-20T04:33:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-84529>

We have had very high winds and a lot of rain all nite and today. I keep thinking I should do housework etc. but don't seem to be very motivated.
I am going down to the south coast on Monday - to meet a friend that I haven't seen since we were in Tibet last year. I jokingly said we will relive our Tibet experience - very rustic - to put it mildly! We had such a great time .....and now the weather has turned so nasty here - we really will be reliving our experiences! Be careful what you wish for!

Does anyone else hate packing as much as I do??? I can never decide what to bring - I always want to take all my favorites....but, of course, when you get away ...you totally don't care .... and I know that......

My bedroom looks like a cyclone hit it ...clothes everywhere. I guess the real problem is that the weather has changed so abruptly - fall clothes are really called for ....and I can't remember what I wore last year. I am pretty sure I wasn't naked last fall/winter ...Your old stuff always seems so tired and unappealing.......

So - these are rustic cabins on the ocean amongst 4 acres of bush ....no wifi,TV, telephone - sounds like heaven. Thanks Corriander! ... you promised there would be surfers there tho.........
There is a pub I can't wait to go to - have wanted to for years ....it is an RSL club - so very rustic I am told - but on the ocean...... there should be lots of fresh fish - I am hoping.

I am so disappointed - no consult on Monday ... and my labs are even more of a mess than I originally thought. I just got more results yesterday. My cholesterol is really thru the roof and other stuff too - WTH
I guess I won't think about it for a few days....
I need a lot more brain power to figure this out - everything is so interdependent ......

well - I am going to pack now ...something - anything ..doesn't really matter does it.
perhaps I should leave the cashmere sweaters and silk shirts at home ..... in case I get in a bar brawl......

### Kiera2 — 2013-04-20T05:53:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-84535>

I'm so happy you were able to organise this! Can't wait to hear what it's like, and of course, the surf report  I hope the weather improves for you.
You've picked a great time to experience the RSL club; it's ANZAC day on thursday.

### Inger — 2013-04-21T08:16:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-84569>

Yes Audrey.. I had this issue with packing before too! Now I own so little clothes that I always use the same..lol so no issues with packing anymore. That is the good thing about having little stuff lol.
I would say, a bikini is the most important. And something sexy pretty comfy stuff, a sweet short dress where your beautiful tanned legs show up... who knows who you will meet there!

So sorry about your labs Audrey! But do not worry! If you do the right things, they will slowly improve I am confident! I am 100% sure in fact. Just remember to tan a lot naked, the best you can ever do for your hormones, IMHO. Just do it all and stop worrying about the labs, I promise you the worrying will just destroy your hormones even more.. You know when we do what nature designed us to do, all will be well.

What a nice place you picked for your trip!

### Hope — 2013-04-24T03:03:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-84716>

Audrey,

how cool that you are going on a trip. I have had the same issue with clothes. I now write down what I take. Since they make you pay for checking a bag if you fly I have learned to pack as little as possible. I am also seeing, I usually only wear half what I bring. You have inspired me too when you said recently, if it's warm you don't need much. So true. I have always taken too much everywhere and now i want to take as little as possible. I always admired people who take little and now I am growing to be one of them. I am confident you will figure it all out....and will have a blast!!!!!!!!

Loved your story about the jewelry store. I have a beautiful diamond, but not big. We got it at a good price years ago before the precious metals boom here. And, I have to tell you, I have spent much more on my health than I have spent on jewelry, ever. (wish I didn't, lol) Perhaps because my grandma's stuff was sold after she died young by the woman who then married my granddad and I hated that - you can lose it for whatever reason. A friend of mine recently dropped hers into her Vitamix (it slipped off her finger while using it), and it was smashed to pieces. Though apparently her store will help pay for the replacement.

You wanna hear a funny story? I am going through our whole house, decluttering and organizing every waking moment I have 'free' (and more so since I"m online less, yaye! I am LOVING getting things cleared and organized and looking beautiful just by being more spacious) So yesterday I was trying on clothes to see what to give away etc and was dancing to my favorite 90s pop music (I'm a pop girl, LOL). Something I hadn't done in years (I must be healing, I used to do this every day as a teen LOL). Also played with fake necklaces and one looked great on me. So I stood there, dancing, singing along, wearing an old sweater and the necklace but looking in the mirror, laughing at myself - I had the best time. But the best part was, I could see my beauty. My own, personal, humble beauty. I used to never see it. Ever. And I can see it despite my belly and big thighs. It's in my eyes, my heart. LOL. And you know? I felt like a million bucks. I felt free. No money can make me feel that way.

As to the labs, I hear you. I am in the same place. You so much want to understand what is going on, what it all means. Why things fluctuate or change as they do. You have them, paid for them and want a real explanation. I am sad I couldn't get one either. But, truth is we both will be OK. I am learning I am actually stressing too much about all this, the labs, EMF. I need to loosen up a little and not want it all to be perfect - though I will never understand why HS CRP can be less than 0.2 and I'm still LR ugh (outside of EMF)......

And I agree with Inger...worrying about them will only make things worse. But I know you know this  I am glad you shared your frustration and hopefully by now you feel better about it.

Have a great time away!!!!!

### caroline — 2013-04-28T10:37:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-84985>

Thanks Inger and Hope ...worrying is so counter productive for our health and we know that for sure. I only wish I was smart enough to understand at least some of it - I seem to figure one thing out and then go on to something else ..... I need to keep better notes ... but I have pages and pages of notes......Today I was thinking that I should make graphs of all the key hormones and how they relate to each other and then how that relates to me.

We have had beautiful weather this week but today - WOW - 28 degrees and not a cloud in the sky - pretty darn perfect.

Our little adventure down the coast was great. The cabins were very basic - probably a little too basic for us - we would have liked a little comfort. We would just wind up going to bed to be comfortable and talk.

I thought I would sleep great but didn't - my fault mostly .... too busy drinking port and eating too late and leaving the lights on. I sure have a problem controlling my port intake - that will have to be a no from now on. I knew that but Patricia doesn't really like red wine much - but loves port.

I did really well food wise - well mostly .... a bit of white chocolate and a few gummy bears - who eats gummy bears!!!!!!

Patricia has a real sweet tooth - but I am not tempted at all ..... I don't know what I think is in port????

We went to a gorgeous bakery for coffee and Pat OD'd on cake and cream ...I wasn't the least bit interested.

I did keep forgetting to drink enough water and was getting pretty dehydrated - consequently had huge problems with leg cramps at nite .... lesson learned - and alcohol is dehydrating too.....

The beaches were gorgeous but there wasn't many people ..... and only the odd surfer - in fact the whole place was very quiet. As we were leaving 3 families arrived with a couple of toddlers - so beautiful. There was one little 2 year old girl with red hair and her name was ....Scarlet - perfect!

We had such a good time reliving all our experiences in Tibet.... Pat brought a bunch of pictures and I had to guess when and where they were taken - surprisingly hard ...she is a typical teacher!

We have this friendship that we can share anything - things we have never told anyone else before - that is gold.

So .... Fall is here .....definitely can't be a beach bum for too much longer .... very, very sad about that....Now I need to get a life.....

### diane — 2013-04-28T22:49:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-85039>

I think that the life of a beach bum is a fabulous life.  Sounds like you had an amazing summer!

### Inger — 2013-04-29T07:30:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-85089>

I love port too, Audrey. But it has to be a good one.. omg I love to have some for dessert. But I rarely have anymore because it is so sweet. Kind of way too sweet to me now. I am not a sweet girl at all... I actually never was? Little comes a long way for me.
Sounds like you had a blast! And Audrey.. no need to be sad.. you can be a beach bum all fall long, and winter too! Remember we are the Kruse klan... and we do things a bit different than mainstream...lol You are going to have the most interesting time in your life, believe me
Now I want to come and visit you... and we could go to the fall-beach together.... and I just know it would be so much fun

We have +10 degree C here now, the river is +3 degree C and it feels quite like summer! At least when sunny outside.. ha. Think about that and you will enjoy next 2 months as summer beach time even more..lol

### caroline — 2013-04-29T07:56:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-85092>

Inger - I don't like sweet things anymore either - but I love Port ... go figure.

We would have so much fun if you came to visit! Yes we definitely do things differently ..... I love that!

So - if you guys are saying it is okay to be a beach bum all winter ..... that is exactly what I shall do! I don't think I will find many brides to become entangled with tho ...... I think they will be out of season!

I forgot to say - when I was down the coast - we popped into a tattoo parlour! I would have had one done - but couldn't decide what and where exactly. I should have just done something .... the place was very new and very clean......
I would guess that is a prime requirement......

My friend was quite anti tattoo - because they are so permanent ..... but after 30 minutes of talking to them and looking at various ones - she was up for it too!

### caroline — 2013-05-02T13:58:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-85294>

Just thought I would pop in and say "Hi y'all". My life seems very boring right now - I got nothing. I am thinking I need to do something outrageous ...but what????

I definitely am missing summer - don't like wearing all these clothes - quite a departure for me. When you are fat you tend to cover everything up - at least I did.

I am struggling because of all that port I drank and was eating apples too and gummie bears - as I said - who the heck in their right mind eats gummie bears????
my weight has gone up 2 kilos - how disappointing and I can really feel it. i need to get back on track - fast.
The disappointing thing is ....being away from home - I wasn't vigilant - but why - I love what I am doing and how I feel ...why did I let it slide? Lesson learned i hope ....the thing is - it was dead easy to eat what I wanted to - I was in complete control ....I guess I just relaxed too much. I could blame my friend and take the easy way out.... but I made the stupid, stupid choices. What would happen if I was away for a week or more? not good.

maybe I should get a tat that says ..."idiot".... it would be so nice to be able to blame circumstances....but that certainly was not the case......

I hope you guys are all enjoying beautiful spring weather and out running around naked!

### Inger — 2013-05-02T15:54:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-85298>

Nah.. Audrey. Next time make a trip with me. I will feed you MHS and other great stuff and I assure you you will feel like a queen after the vacation...  (of course we would be tanning nude too)

I really was thinking about this today BTW. There are few that lift us up in that way.. I have the same, yesterday was grill party here and everyone indulged in sausages filled with shit (sorry but it is true) potato chips.. and other crap. Because it is tradition. What can I say? It hurts my heart every time I see it.. my mental ill brother sitting there full of apathy.. and no one really cares. It is killing me. I was shopping today and it stuck me how sick people looked all of a sudden.. so gray.. terrible body comp.. and everyone was buying some "healthy" stuff... low fat stuff.. low fat milk crap.. bananas.. I looked at their groceries.. and I looked at their faces.. and it said more than 1000 words.

You know what I tell my dad now? "Dad, oh well you choose whatever you do. Actually why worry at all anymore about your health, what only you can change BTW - if you want a shorter life and go on eating crap.. then I'll probably get this house sooner.. HA!"
I have reached a point where I believe religious people are extremely resistant to change. Real change, not only one or two days of eating a bit healthier, you know? I am talking about extreme religious dogmas, because they always go on living like they always had. As they have arrived.. and the other has not because hey, they has no holy spirit. And Jesus helps them and food does not matter that much at all. uh? I do think Jesus cries up in heaven when they talk like that but hey, what can I do. I can do right only me. And I am. I never touch their foods either.

Oh.. I think it sounds really bad what I wrote above. lol Must be because it is so stormy today?  I got all stormy inside too..lol

And Audrey.. I think you are not one bit boring... I think you are so uplifting and funny and I love reading your stuff. I give you a tip. Next time you want to indulge in port, take only a little.. = 8-10 cl, maybe first some good red wine if you want to drink more.. (but this is of course not good for health..hmm) and then last glass as "dessert" a small port wine.. and together with it some nice raw sheep or goat cheese! A huge piece cheese. But raw, and from goat or sheep. I bet you will feel better and get no weird cravings after. Drink plenty of spring water the whole evening too. Maybe munch on some organic cucumber with the cheese too. No apples and gummie bears.. ugh... :-o

Have you found a place for nude tanning yet Audrey? ;-) (I was on my balcony for two hours but had to come in as I was about to go with the wind..huh SO stormy here today!)

### caroline — 2013-05-03T00:58:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-85346>

Oh no ..... no gummie bears! Inger - I know so much better - I don't know what I was thinking - well - I guess I wasn't thinking. I was very dehydrated - they told us we could drink the rain water - but it looked very dirty and we didn't want to drink it. We did buy bottled water - but I just forgot to drink! very stupid. I think that is why I started eating apples - for the hydration. That is a great idea to have a glass of red wine and then a bit of port after. I was eating stilton cheese - so I got something right LOL. I just hate how I fall apart when I am away from home - even tho I make the necessary plans for what I need. I think because I am very strict at home - I just wanted to relax and enjoy my friend's company ...which I did.

It is very funny to me - I now consider eating apples and drinking port as going off the rails..........
If it wasn't for the extra kilos - I guess I should consider that somewhat of a win in the broad scheme of things.......

### caroline — 2013-05-03T10:13:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-85374>

We are still having lovely weather - altho cooler - probably around 72 degrees but sunny and beautiful clear blue skies.

I talked to a young woman on the beach today - she was born here but lives in SteamBoat Springs Colorado. I didn't realise what a tough winter they have had ... just tons and tons of snow but the resorts have closed now so no skiing.

I am going to Sydney tomorrow....TEDx is on at the Opera House. Tickets were all sold out by the time I realised but apparently there is lots going on outside - around circular quay .....so I will check it out ...sounds like it will be great.

I hope everyone has a great weekend!

### Hope — 2013-05-04T02:56:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-85410>

Dear Audrey, I haven't been here too much....too much enjoying being OFF line and OUTside these days as I feel like a HUMAN BEING again. Every day this week I pinched myself - except one night I had a nightmare, dreaming my in=laws were coming to visit showing up at our door, I freaked out in the DREAM I woke up and had trouble going back to sleep.

You sound amazing. Yaye for not needing the sugar and cream as your friend did

As to gummy bears - I am GERMAN and LOVE gummy bears. THey have these beautiful ones there, organic, still sugar but all natural (there you go, oxymoron). I have a bag from last summer. Still sitting there. Will give it to DH. No sugar for me LOL. But I wanted to empathize as I love them too. Course would be nice to be perfect but you are human. I have realized trying to be perfect in all I eat is way too stressful.

I LOVE love love Inger's idea of having a vacation together. My dream is having a EP no EMF no artificial light vacation somewhere in the world, with all of us CTing and eating and swimming together.

And, oh, definitely keep going to the beach any non stormy/rainy day. The water and air is still great. Water here has gotten warmer now but when it was freezing I put feet in until they hurt (no swimming allowed in lake). My other dream is to do a polar bear plunge next January 1. Would be so much fun.

Have a lovely time out in Sydney. Thanks for sharing all you do!

I have the same, yesterday was grill party here and everyone indulged in sausages filled with shit (sorry but it is true) potato chips.. and other crap. Because it is tradition. What can I say? It hurts my heart every time I see it.. my mental ill brother sitting there full of apathy.. and no one really cares. It is killing me. I was shopping today and it stuck me how sick people looked all of a sudden.. so gray.. terrible body comp.. and everyone was buying some "healthy" stuff... low fat stuff.. low fat milk crap.. bananas.. I looked at their groceries.. and I looked at their faces.. and it said more than 1000 words.

(but this is of course not good for health..hmm) and then last glass as "dessert" a small port wine.. and together with it some nice raw sheep or goat cheese! A huge piece cheese. But raw, and from goat or sheep. I bet you will feel better and get no weird cravings after. Drink plenty of spring water the whole evening too. Maybe munch on some organic cucumber with the cheese too. No apples and gummie bears.. ugh... :-o
>
> Have you found a place for nude tanning yet Audrey? ;-) (I was on my balcony for two hours but had to come in as I was about to go with the wind..huh SO stormy here today!)

Inger - just wanted to say.....it is tough for me too to watch people. I see lots of pregnant women - even older - eating all kinds of crap and am always shocked. But, they got pregnant. Others too of course but I see pregnant people everywhere LOL.

As to religious people - I have never fully gotten that either. I think or wonder if they allow themselves to eat whatever because when they are legalistic, they are not free. Jesus was all about freedom, complete freedom. THey should know that he died for that but these religious people have all these extra rules in their heads that makes living so hard, so they jerk off on food, or tell themselves it's OK. Their brains can't be very healthy, if you think about it, with all they eat and how they live. It breaks my heart. Also, I think that true Christians - not religious people - they realize they are NO better than anyone else. They are all the same, trying to improve themselves, be more loving. That's how I see it. That's what I observed in some of the nuns that taught at my Catholic high school (though I'm not Catholic)......I saw a sweetness in some of them, a true spirit of freedom that I rarely see in anyone now. It is so weird because of course they had lots of rules. But there was something special about it. So sad it is so rare. I mean look at Jesus, he turned water into wine as the first miracle. I think too he must be sad that all these religious people that use his name in vain eat all this crap and take all these drugs instead of looking at their lifestyles. They allow GMOs in this country, no one talks about that, all the drugs, vaccines...they just say yes and Amen to it all. God never meant for that I am sure. Well, at least I can't imagine LOL. I wouldn't be surprised he meant for all of us to eat more fish. That was another miracle, after all...if they ate fish then, we should now too. I have met some pretty legalistic people and it is very very difficult to witness them. But oh well. I do feel like I can have more compassion when i consider how the brain is so broken, in mine too.....though boy, I am making some amazing progress.

ANyway. Must stop now but had to say this.

### Inger — 2013-05-04T07:58:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-85427>

Hope you bare so right there are some Christians that are amazing and loving people looking for development all their life. The true Freedom is amazing. Weird enough it is the opposite what many thinks.. ;-) I bet you are right about the only thing to enjoy for many religious people are the food.. sadly. Because they have so many weird rules. To me everything must make sense, the rules must make sense always, there must be some benefit! Or I do not care for it. Like having to use heavy clothing to not show your body. What benefit has that? No IMHO. To get sun on your skin has huge benefits.

### caroline — 2013-05-14T11:11:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-86009>

I love you guys spamming my journal ....I seem to be pretty flat lately.

I had a dream about JK last nite ... I dreamt that he got VERY TALL and looked 20 ears younger and very ripped. He was so tall that I couldn't look in his face even. I spent a lot of time talking to Sandy about what he had done to achieve this .....can't remember what the verdict was tho!

I talked to a 73 yr old woman at the bus stop today for about 30 minutes - the bus was very late.
It is amazing to me that older people seem to have much more on the ball about their food choices. She eats lightly and real food and doesn't eat late. She gardens in shorts and a tank top. I convinced her to get her vit D checked.
She watches Dr. Oz and has been learning the importance of Vit D,

She said she avoids butter - but loves it and misses it so I convinced her to go back to eating it - that it was very good for her. She said - yes - you need good fat for your brain!

Such a great conversation with a complete stranger - and she was in very good shape. I continually find that older people understand all this more than younger people - they remember how their parents ate real food and only three meals a day and no junk food and she was raised on mostly seafood. She was so interesting and her brain was working great!
She was an inspiration to me ..... and she drinks spring water!

I went to lunch today - it was a friend's birthday. I didn't know anyone really altho I had been hearing about some of them for years. When everyone was getting ready to leave a man came over to talk to me and said it was nice to meet me etc. and I was asking him who everyone was - I thought one of the women was his partner. He said no - he is on his own.
I really became uncomfortable and realised that I am not ready for this.

A couple of days ago I met a woman at the market. It turns out that she is a healer and reads cards and is a psychic ... we talked for ages - I was at a booth where they make beautiful handmade copper jewelry. I am still uncomfortable about taking my wedding ring off and was going to get a copper ring. She told me that I wasn't ready ... and I kept saying - I think I am - I would be really scared but I am ready. She said several times - no - you are not ready. Now - after today - I realise I definitely am not ...... back to the drawing board....

I hope everyone is enjoying great spring weather and shedding all their clothes!

### SeaHorse — 2013-05-14T14:26:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-86012>

Caroline....remember being scared feels a lot like being vulnerable...and becoming comfortable with new things happens when we allow ourselves to stretch out into new things and be vulnerable. I'm not talking about safety of course, but emotional vulnerability. You are not in any real danger by exploring new (healthy) relationships, but it can feel like that to your nervous system.

Perhaps this experience is just a taste of what this will feel like and as it happens again (which it will) you can stay with the uncomfortable feeling, recognize it and see if change over time...kind of an advance and retreat approach. Eventually you will stride out into the newness and you will be ok with the "jumping off a cliff" feeling that happens with all big life shifts. You know yourself best, so trust yourself and stretch yourself at the same time....all the best to you

I talk to strangers all the time and never feel threatened or vulnerable ... but there was a shift this time......
Thanks for taking the time to read my craziness and respond. xo

### caroline — 2013-05-20T05:43:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-86513>

Hi y'all! I Hope everyone is having an amazing spring and living life to the fullest.

I have been very lack lustre lately - I hate it - lost my mojo for some reason.
I have been remembering the comment lately about how if you want your life to change - you have to do something different.
The meaning of insanity - doing the same things and expecting a different outcome.

I have had internet problems the last three days and spoken to I don't know how many techs.
Today it was a young man located in Auckland.
He was so fun to talk to - While we were waiting for things to boot up - we got talking about EMF's
I asked him where he keeps his iphone .... young men get that connection very fast!! he whipped his phone out of his pants pocket right then! He was interested in everything I was telling him - we talked for over an hour. He asked me if I was a Doctor or a nurse .... I must have sounded like I knew what I was talking about!! Kudos Dr. K ....

I gave him so many websites - he wanted to know everything and promised to email me after he reads Jack's EMF series.
I also gave him magnetico and less EMF and MDA and Dave Asprey.
He was opening the links as I was telling him - I don't know who was more excited ... him or me!!!
As we were talking \[and opening links\] he was emailing them to his whole team.

He told me that at his company they are wired in - no wireless ...is that strange for a phone company?
I told him to take the clock radio out of his bedroom and turn off the power. He has a really old fashioned TV and a 1996 car. He is from India and working on a PHD. He is into accounting and spreadsheets etc.

I asked him to go to the ocean on his days off this weekend and lay in the sand and contemplate his future - I can't imagine looking at spreadsheets the rest of my life - but to each his own. I was trying to convince him to switch to engineering and help save the planet!

He was so open to all of this exciting information and it seems - he solved my internet problem.

It is very exciting to talk to someone - especially a young person - who isn't mired in all the dogma. So his not having funds for all the toys may save his life. We talked about diet too - he doesn't eat a traditional Indian diet and was very interested in what to eat and he sleeps on the floor. I know nothing really about Indian culture - will have to learn.

It is wonderful to have a conversation with someone who is so open and so full of energy and promise and can't wait to dive in with both feet .... totally made my day....

As I am writing this - I am so excited .... and the spelling is awful etc. etc. because the thoughts are tumbling out.

I have always thought about this when people complain about Jack's spelling etc. - I can understand that he is jazzed about all this and the ideas are spilling out and he can't get them down fast enough and he has so much he wants to share .....major plus in my mind.....

okay - now I have to go make something happen ....stay tuned to this space....it seems like it just isn't good enough to want something desperately - you have to take steps ...that's the hard part - figuring out the steps. I think I was trusting in the universe .... and that isn't good enough...

### fitness@home — 2013-05-22T04:33:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-86632>

Loved this post ^ Audrey. Your conversation with this young man produced ripple effects...he was sending those links to his friends. Wonder how far those ripples will reach?

So, have you made anything happen yet  ?

### Inger — 2013-05-22T10:52:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-86644>

Yes.. taking steps. That is actually the interesting part I think...  Challenges are so much fun, it kind of makes the life so much alive! I am so curious about what steps you take.. please share  Sharing is so fun too, very different to the habit of complaining only, that is quite common. Looks like you got your mojo back Audrey . Isn't it amazing how just one human out of the blue can helps us regain it in seconds? This always feels magic to me!

### caroline — 2013-05-22T11:18:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-86649>

Inger - I haven't come up with any steps yet - gotta think more on it .... I have a habit of just thinking that stuff will happen and I do definitely believe in the randomness of life - everyday is a new day and you just never know what bizaar and wonderful and magic thing could happen . I started to have coffee at a cafe at the beach..... I hate paying $4.50 for a coffee but decided that I should do that - so far the only one I have talked to is the Barista! but he makes a great cup of coffee.

One person can change the whole course of your life ....look what has happened to us here......

### Inger — 2013-05-22T11:28:10+0300

lol you are too sweet Audrey! Maybe you are a dreamer like me..haha. But I like to be alone. I like to be in the nature, with the animals.. I feel not alone at all. I need peace to figure my stuff out..lots of peace. I meet so many people as a waitress I might need the balance? Maybe you should start waitressing!

### Inger — 2013-05-22T12:59:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-86656>

Oh... Audrey...
I do think there must be time to grieve too.. I do that myself still I think. But it is not as strong as it was it is getting less intense slowly... Give yourself time Audrey. You must follow your heart. We can have lots of beautiful and fun moments alone too or with friends without a partner. I think time heals too. Someone needs maybe a bit longer time? I am quite slow..lol But maybe you could go out and sit down and look for people and talk to them? I like that too, I love to go alone on a coffee or tea or a glass of wine / food and just look around me. Very often someone comes to talk to me. I like that too, I like to talk to people a lot, but I also love to just be alone. I suck in the energy around me it is like a part of me too. So many experiences! I like to think; there is a time for everything.. a time to be silent.. a time to talk.. a time to grieve, a time for joy... that feels just perfect!

Wanted to give you one tip if you are going to indulge on fruit...  I know this from my high fruit rawfooder time. Always try to eat wild greens with them! I can tell you you do your body a big favor if you do that. Dandelion + flowers, ground elder, linden leaves, yarrow leaves.... anything you know is edible, change them up and take the ones that taste the best to you. They have huge amount of minerals and other stuff good for you. I would do maybe a mono meal with only greens and fruit, that way I digest them best. You might be different but try it out.

### caroline — 2013-05-26T04:19:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-86871>

I have decided that having coffee in a cafe is my new step - for now anyway - until I can figure something else out!
A young man at the beach cafe told me about this cafe down where the shops are - It is in a very out of the way place - I have never noticed it before. It reminded me so much of our place - everyone very friendly - people waiting for somewhere to sit - friendly staff and an on site owner that was great. He was helping me choose a hand grinder and then showed me how to use it properly - a really lovely man. He briefly touched my arm when I was leaving and said he was glad I dropped in - a very sincere man. This brief touching thing really interests me so much ... my Doc does it too. It conveys so much - but is so such a simple gesture - it is quite lovely and it always blows me away. It makes such a connection between strangers.

I was asking the owner about mould in coffee \[ they roast there\] He didn't really understand what I was talking about so I was talking to him about Dave Asprey ... he was very interested and said he will look at the website.

I feel vindicated today.... I was listening to Dr. Sarah Gottfried at the sexyback summit. She talked a lot about the book 50 shades of Gray - did anyone else see the summit? Of course - it isn't a literary masterpiece - but it is fun. The comments she made are exactly how i have felt about these books from the beginning. To me they are about love and telling someone what you want/like/need and going outside the box as well. It isn't about sadism. Some of the stuff sounds like a lot of fun actually!
and knowing what each partner wants and needs and is willing to think about doing. It is about caring and exploring your limits and spending the time to make your partner feel treasured.

Sorry Gretchen - I know you think it is trash!

I am heading to the beach - we are still having mostly sunny beautiful days - but cool. I am sitting here shivering as I type. I know we are supposed to want to be cold, dark and boring ...... but honestly - I felt so much better in the summer - much more alive and vital. Dr. K is in a much warmer climate now - I am confused about that ....any thoughts anyone?
I definitely prefer hot weather - never knew that before! There is something amazing to me about needing only a few clothes on and having the hot sun on your skin. I felt so healthy and more energy......

Have an amazing weekend everyone .......

### Inger — 2013-05-26T07:17:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-86878>

Audrey, maybe go slower? Tiny steps.. do not let yourself too cold and shivery all the time. Put on some more clothes but let as many body parts out for cold exposure as possible? That is how I do in winter.. I am not very sadistic..lol so I always keep a degree of comfortability.. except of my ice hole dips but I keep them short! Please Audrey do not go around and suffer all the time.. sounds too sad. I also sometimes warmed up in a hot shower after my icy dip. It is maybe a bit humiliating to not be able to take as much cold as someone else but we are all different.. I really believe in slow adaption. Do it fun, do it as slow as you need! But just do it.  You will always adapt a bit.. and a bit more.. and in the end you will be surprised how well you can tolerate the cold.

Maybe think about how fresh and crisp the air is now.. I always loved the autumn! I get so much energy, and the air is so totally refreshing.. and it goes into winter where all is getting so peaceful and time to contemplate... I love the silence of the winter.
Maybe think, this winter is going to be a very special for you, you are going to get some deep emotional healing.. I think winter is the best for that. It is allowed to step back.. slow the pace. When spring comes you are ready.. to blossom.. and it will be so beautiful because you needed the winter sleep for that to be the most amazing

You know Audrey.. I really like that turning inside me thing.. I think it is because my heart is still a bit broken? I like to be alone.. I like to contemplate.. to figure out how things are.. and just be in this beautiful place I have created inside. It is so safe and beautiful there. It is very connected to nature. That is also why I like winter so much, it is more allowed then. Comes summer life is so busy.. I am sometimes thinking if I am totally weird... maybe I am but then it will go away slowly when I do all these healing protocols.. I am sure! But maybe you are not broken anymore and then you feel this need to step outside? Hmmm.. then I guess the coffee place idea is a great one! And please do not go around freezing all the time Audrey.. baby steps

Oh.. not that you think now I am a total lonely girl..lol I do love to meet people and I do at work, a lot. And I also date at times. It is always very nice. But I need it not much now. Just.. a few times in year. No more. Maybe because there is so much "noise" around me through my family. Makes me seek the peace even more. I just cannot get enough.. huh

### caroline — 2013-05-26T08:31:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-86882>

Inger -?you just made me realize some things........ well I know them but now. I understand them a little better. I crave the vitality of the warm weather and all the motion and activity. I feel much more Lonely now. Introspection is not good for me right now. I am overwhelmed by guilt and sadness and I have to stay away from thinking about things ..it just snowballs out of control if I let it. That is why I love the sunshine and the long days and everyone outside doing stuff.

### Inger — 2013-05-26T10:40:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-86884>

Hmm.. I see Audrey. I am built kind of that way that if I feel grieve and sadness I really have to "feel" it and go through.. I need to feel them too. I know this sounds weird.. but I feel not so good if I try to "escape" my feelings. I always think, if I cannot feel peace and happiness alone.. there is something I need to heal and I then do that. It has been a tough way, sure. I had so many fears. So much anxiety. I feared so much to be alone, that was my main fear. But little by little it got better and now I rarely feel those things. I do need the nature though. Just like you.. they are so healing! Maybe you can explore the stormy weather to be healing too? I think all kind of weather are very interesting.. sure lovely sunshine wins, but when it is not there what does it heÃ¶lp to wish it was..  I then have learned to like any weather.. they all have their own charm. Audrey.. xoxo .. I see you on the guilt thing.. I have had that too and I still get touched by it at times but now I have my weapons.. Strong weapons! I then think the right thoughts - and it goes away. Like, what I have done in the past I cannot change, only what I do today, and so I do all the best I can. And back then I managed to do no better either! It is good to see the mistakes but I think guilt is really not a good thing. It really helps me to live here and now, 100%. That is my best weapon and it gives me so much joy and peace! To live 100% like this was my last day on earth.... and I forgive myself a lot when I do mistakes.. and I forgive others too.. very easy. I have promised myself to do that. Forgive me, and forgive others. I love to live like that. I do not want to get stuck.. I hate stuck. I want to run run forward

Sorry if this is all confusing Audrey.. wish I could be there and we could share our stories. oh man I fear it would never end..lol

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-26T16:50:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-86889>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=86871)
>
> I have decided that having coffee in a cafe is my new step - for now anyway - until I can figure something else out!
> A young man at the beach cafe told me about this cafe down where the shops are - It is in a very out of the way place - I have never noticed it before. It reminded me so much of our place - everyone very friendly - people waiting for somewhere to sit - friendly staff and an on site owner that was great. He was helping me choose a hand grinder and then showed me how to use it properly - a really lovely man. He briefly touched my arm when I was leaving and said he was glad I dropped in - a very sincere man. This brief touching thing really interests me so much ... my Doc does it too. It conveys so much - but is so such a simple gesture - it is quite lovely and it always blows me away. It makes such a connection between strangers.
>
> I was asking the owner about mould in coffee \[ they roast there\] He didn't really understand what I was talking about so I was talking to him about Dave Asprey ... he was very interested and said he will look at the website.
>
> I feel vindicated today.... I was listening to Dr. Sarah Gottfried at the sexyback summit. She talked a lot about the book 50 shades of Gray - did anyone else see the summit? Of course - it isn't a literary masterpiece - but it is fun. The comments she made are exactly how i have felt about these books from the beginning. To me they are about love and telling someone what you want/like/need and going outside the box as well. It isn't about sadism. Some of the stuff sounds like a lot of fun actually!
> and knowing what each partner wants and needs and is willing to think about doing. It is about caring and exploring your limits and spending the time to make your partner feel treasured.
> **
> Sorry Gretchen - I know you think it is trash**!
>
> I am heading to the beach - we are still having mostly sunny beautiful days - but cool. I am sitting here shivering as I type. I know we are supposed to want to be cold, dark and boring ...... but honestly - I felt so much better in the summer - much more alive and vital. Dr. K is in a much warmer climate now - I am confused about that ....any thoughts anyone?
> I definitely prefer hot weather - never knew that before! There is something amazing to me about needing only a few clothes on and having the hot sun on your skin. I felt so healthy and more energy......
>
> Have an amazing weekend everyone .......

LOL! personally I think there are better books that express asking for what you need and exploring your boundaries than 50 shades... this is one of only 3 books I've never finished - Hemmingway's The Sun Also Rise; DH Lawrence's The Rainbow & 50 Shades... I guess its in good company!

### caroline — 2013-05-27T03:00:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-86915>

It has been a tough way, sure. I had so many fears. So much anxiety. I feared so much to be alone, that was my main fear. But little by little it got better and now I rarely feel those things. I do need the nature though. Just like you.. they are so healing! Maybe you can explore the stormy weather to be healing too? I think all kind of weather are very interesting.. sure lovely sunshine wins, but when it is not there what does it heÃ¶lp to wish it was..  I then have learned to like any weather.. they all have their own charm. Audrey.. xoxo .. I see you on the guilt thing.. I have had that too and I still get touched by it at times but now I have my weapons.. Strong weapons! I then think the right thoughts - and it goes away. Like, what I have done in the past I cannot change, only what I do today, and so I do all the best I can. And back then I managed to do no better either! It is good to see the mistakes but I think guilt is really not a good thing. It really helps me to live here and now, 100%. That is my best weapon and it gives me so much joy and peace! To live 100% like this was my last day on earth.... and I forgive myself a lot when I do mistakes.. and I forgive others too.. very easy. I have promised myself to do that. Forgive me, and forgive others. I love to live like that. I do not want to get stuck.. I hate stuck. I want to run run forward
>
> Sorry if this is all confusing Audrey.. wish I could be there and we could share our stories. oh man I fear it would never end..lol

Lots of stuff to contemplate Inger - I have done many stupid things in my life - I guess as everyone has - but I can forgive myself ... when you know better you do better ..... that phrase changed my life and mindset quite a few years ago.

But this survivor guilt is something very different - God knows I have talked to enough people about this - I actually think that it will never go away completely and that I will learn to live with it. I need to keep very busy. That is what I love about travelling - there is so much to take in - so many amazing people to get to know - experience life and different cultures etc. ... but the guilt always comes back. My new Doc is going to have a go at trying to rewire my brain when we have time - it is all about letting go - knowing that you did the best you could at the time. That seems pretty easy when you read it in black and white but it is not. A lot of people have been thru much horror in their life and you just have to put it aside. Past horror can't rewrite my future - I won't let it .... so I am a work in progress.... but sunshine sure helps a lot!

We need to have a face to face chat Inger ..... How many bottles of wine would that take????

### caroline — 2013-05-27T03:29:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-86921>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=86889)
>
> LOL! personally I think there are better books that express asking for what you need and exploring your boundaries than 50 shades... this is one of only 3 books I've never finished - Hemmingway's The Sun Also Rise; DH Lawrence's The Rainbow & 50 Shades... I guess its in good company!

Not to belabour the point .....but - for me- "fifty shades" was an affirmation about how to live your life. Jack talked about a lot of the same stuff. Sex is sex - but how two people approach it can make it wonderful or not. Making the person you love feel cherished and the most important person in your world and being willing to go the extra mile - putting their needs above yours when that is very important to them - sort of what you can live with and not compromise your own beliefs.
Allowing your partner to be who they are and supporting them .... and maintaining your own personal self......

Sorry - I seem to be on a soap box about this ...stepping off now

### Inger — 2013-05-27T08:40:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-86931>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=86915)
>
> Lots of stuff to contemplate Inger - I have done many stupid things in my life - I guess as everyone has - but I can forgive myself ... when you know better you do better ..... that phrase changed my life and mindset quite a few years ago.
>
> But this survivor guilt is something very different - God knows I have talked to enough people about this - I actually think that it will never go away completely and that I will learn to live with it. I need to keep very busy. That is what I love about travelling - there is so much to take in - so many amazing people to get to know - experience life and different cultures etc. ... but the guilt always comes back. My new Doc is going to have a go at trying to rewire my brain when we have time - it is all about letting go - knowing that you did the best you could at the time. That seems pretty easy when you read it in black and white but it is not. A lot of people have been thru much horror in their life and you just have to put it aside. Past horror can't rewrite my future - I won't let it .... so I am a work in progress.... but sunshine sure helps a lot!
>
> We need to have a face to face chat Inger ..... How many bottles of wine would that take????

I do think your survivor guilt will go away Audrey, and pretty soon! Now I tell you what I think could make it happend, ok.
Eat raw. I know I always go on about this but from my own experience, when I did that something changed in my mind. It became bolder.. I was able to think and act in ways I had never thought before.. crazy stuff! I stepped so outside my box.. and it made me fly! Now.. if you want that, that is the medicine. Courage to think new thoughts.. new ways. It kind of lifted me up in a weird way! Sure I still had hard times but it was different now! It was never hopeless.. it could be excruciating painful but it was sweet at the same time.. just different. Because I was looking at the pain and mess from like higher? Like a bird? Does this make any sense..? lol sorry I am not good in describing this stuff at all..

Next thing.. could you possibly think magic? I do that often.. no idea if it is crazy or not, but it helps! And that counts to me. Because if I am not feeling well and have dark moods I will not affect people around me good at all... So, the magic thinking goes like this. I imagine the loved one in my mind, that the person is inside my heart, and I think about all the beautiful things with this person and it feels like so much love. I tell myself this person is always a part of me and that is so enrichening and beautiful! It work with people that have passed away, and also with people still alive that are just not reachable. There is no place for guilt at all anymore. There is just love. So much love and it feels so good your heart gets so big so big and you cannot stop smiling and you feel no lonely at all anymore!

If you have issues with the magic thinking you really must try raw because that helps huge with this  (and it must be all.. because just a part does not work that well.. only a cup of coffee, tea or spices and such minor stuff is fine not raw from my experience) I do not care if it is crazy or something, I know it sounds so weird but it works!

When I get a camerea for my computer and skype we will chat and chat Audrey, I promise... it is going to be so fun!

### caroline — 2013-05-27T10:58:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-86935>

Inger - you have amazing intuition. I do love raw too and I do see all the beauty around me ..... maybe I have come farther than I thought. I feel very sad at times but never hopeless .....I do hold the love in my heart but sometimes it is very lonely. I am, by nature, a very positive person - so I do look forward to the future - actually can't wait.....maybe that is it......I am just impatient!

I spent the afternoon at the ocean with a friend - so much fun. We talked about the light that we are drawn to in people and how sometimes we just simply outgrow our old friends. JK talks about that on facebook today- people pass thru our life for a reason ... some stay for a short time, some stay for a long time but there is always some purpose no matter the amount of time.

I was telling her all about the sex webinar - what a hoot! She is very dialed in to all this stuff. People around us must have wondered what was in our coffee!!

### caroline — 2013-05-29T11:04:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-87065>

Oh my gosh people ...... there is an amazing light show in Sydney harbour .......... The Opera house looks amazing. I would expect it will make news worldwide. I hope you get a chance to see it!! just WOW!

I have lived here seven and a half years and never tire of the Opera House and circular Quay - I still wonder around in awe.

I think it is called ... The Vivid Festival ... I suppose it would be on you tube or somewhere ..... hope you get a chance to see it.

### caroline — 2013-06-01T07:52:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-87239>

I spent the evening with people I am not comfortable around any more and forgot my blue blockers. I had the worst sleep.....very, very disturbed. It just isn't worth it -life is too short and I have too much I want to do. Old friends tend to treat me different - I don't really understand at all.....one woman said to me you look very fit - where you are living now is really agreeing with you. End of conversation...
Another friend is having double knee surgery and the pain is excruciating. I suggested that she ice her knees before surgery and everyone looked at me horrified. They said that would be the worst possible thing to do and it would do so much damage.....end of discussion.

I want so much more out of the rest of my life.....

It seems the better I feel about myself the more I have to jettison old ways etc. I have Been buying some new clothes that are very different from anything I would have worn before. If I feel much better about myself I am going to be totally broke!

I have to keep working on that swagger tho......doesn't come naturally at all!

I am sitting in the sand but it is starting to rain - so time to go home.....

Have a great weekend everyone!
On a positive note ....a really good friend is asking me more and more about what I am eating/ doing and actually listening.

### cantweight — 2013-06-01T14:12:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-87246>

Its that one person that listens, that you help, that makes all the other nay-sayers not matter

I bet you've got your swagger on more than you know....probably why those other ladies are so different to you now....can you say jealous! Enjoy it! The better you feel/look the more likely they will want to join you going down this "crazy"path. And if they choose to remain blind...that is their choice.

I am off to the beach in a week and I can not wait!!!

### fitness@home — 2013-06-01T18:30:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-87258>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=87239)
>
> I spent the evening with people I am not comfortable around any more and forgot my blue blockers. I had the worst sleep.....very, very disturbed. It just isn't worth it -life is too short and I have too much I want to do. Old friends tend to treat me different - I don't really understand at all.....one woman said to me you look very fit - where you are living now is really agreeing with you. End of conversation...
> Another friend is having double knee surgery and the pain is excruciating. I suggested that she ice her knees before surgery and everyone looked at me horrified. They said that would be the worst possible thing to do and it would do so much damage.....end of discussion.
>
> I want so much more out of the rest of my life.....
>
> It seems the better I feel about myself the more I have to jettison old ways etc. I have Been buying some new clothes that are very different from anything I would have worn before. If I feel much better about myself I am going to be totally broke!
>
>
> I have to keep working on that swagger tho......doesn't come naturally at all!
>
> I am sitting in the sand but it is starting to rain - so time to go home.....
>
> Have a great weekend everyone!
> On a positive note ....a really good friend is asking me more and more about what I am eating/ doing and actually listening.

There is nothing wrong with jettisoning old ways (and sometimes that means old friends too). It happens when you are open to learning new things and experience a lot personal growth. I've also had to move away from some friendships that I simply "outgrew" over the years. Sad when it happens, especially when you shared so much in the past. On the positive side, new friendships develop and these people are more in tune with the person I am NOW.

Glad you are having fun shopping for new clothes! One item I have not been able to bring myself to purchase is a pair of really "skinny" jeans. They just don't look good on me and are not designed for my body type!

It's great that one of your friends is asking about what you are eating and doing.

I stopped over to see my neighbor after work yesterday and she commented on how I looked like I'd lost more weight. She was cooking a pasta dish for dinner and of course offered me some. I politely declined, telling her that although it smelled wonderful - pasta just does not agree with me and I avoid gluten in general. That opened the door to a conversation of what type of foods I eat, etc. Her husband seriously has all kinds of health issues going on...diabetes, high BP, overweight, and needs knee surgery. She has been taking him to a series of doctor appointments over the last two weeks. Sad...he is the same age as me.

Have a great weekend!

### Inger — 2013-06-01T21:20:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-87270>

I want to see pics from your new different clothes Audrey!  and the opera too!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-02T04:25:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-87304>

Sex is about your perception........you can alter it if you choose.
This comment is on my FB wall now........."When I was young I thought I had to wait for the perfect woman for me. So egocentric in my youthful thinking I was......To say that one should wait a lifetime for his soulmate to come around is a paradox. People eventually get sick of waiting, I decided to take a chance on someone good at her core to heal my badness in my soul, and by the art of commitment and hard work we became soul mates and this will take a lifetime to perfect. So far 20 yrs in we are ahead of schedule. When I found her, it was at the wrong time of my life. In the midst of getting my ass kicked in neurosurgery residency. And I made time for what I instinctively knew was correct. My "smart friends" told me I was nuts. They are divorced now, ironically. Thankfully I was inspired to listen to my gut. In all the world, there is no heart for me like yours. In all the world, there is no love for you like mine. For me, love is a temporary madness, it erupts like volcanoes and then subsides. And when it subsides, you have to make a decision. You have to work out whether your roots have so entwined together that it is inconceivable that you should ever part. Because this is what love is. Love is not breathlessness, it is not excitement, it is not the promulgation of promises of eternal passion, it is not the desire to mate every second minute of the day, it is not lying awake at night imagining that she is kissing every cranny of your body. No, don’t blush, I am telling you some truths. That is just being “in love”, which any fool can do. Love itself is what is left over when being in love has burned away, and this is both an art and a fortunate accident. Yes, I love you that much, my wife.

### nonchalant — 2013-06-02T04:50:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-87309>

Just beautiful, Jack.

### caroline — 2013-06-02T07:23:12+0300

I was fortunate enough to have that kind of love .... trouble is - how do you move on from that .........

### caroline — 2013-06-02T08:23:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-87317>

I've also had to move away from some friendships that I simply "outgrew" over the years. Sad when it happens, especially when you shared so much in the past. On the positive side, new friendships develop and these people are more in tune with the person I am NOW.
>
> Glad you are having fun shopping for new clothes! One item I have not been able to bring myself to purchase is a pair of really "skinny" jeans. They just don't look good on me and are not designed for my body type!
>
> It's great that one of your friends is asking about what you are eating and doing.
>
> I stopped over to see my neighbor after work yesterday and she commented on how I looked like I'd lost more weight. She was cooking a pasta dish for dinner and of course offered me some. I politely declined, telling her that although it smelled wonderful - pasta just does not agree with me and I avoid gluten in general. That opened the door to a conversation of what type of foods I eat, etc. Her husband seriously has all kinds of health issues going on...diabetes, high BP, overweight, and needs knee surgery. She has been taking him to a series of doctor appointments over the last two weeks. Sad...he is the same age as me.
>
> Have a great weekend!

I really don't quite get this moving on from old friends - It seems to me if a friend had new interests and doing some things differently - I would be very interested to know more. Why can't we change and still be friends? I find what people are doing is endlessly fascinating and I love to hear about new things/ideas........

Dawn - if you want skinny jeans - believe me - there is a pair out there with your name on them! You may have to try on a 100 pairs to get there - but you will!

I shop on line at ShopBop .... clothes are very expensive here. It is an excellent site. Go thru the brands and read the reviews. One woman in her review talks about J Brand doing trunk shows in store.\[U.S.\] It sounds like she has the same body type as you. They really helped her by telling her which styles looked bad and she wound up with a pair of skinny jeans she would never even have tried on. I think she wound up with JBrand cargos - I can check if you want. The other thing is - you have to pay good money for nice jeans - no way around it!

The department store I shop at in Sydney has a wonderful personal shopper for us poor folk and she is so helpful. The other day I got her to try on a sweater that I loved but wasn't sure about - it was really helpful to see it on her. I just can't afford to buy stuff that - once I get it home - I won't wear for whatever reason. BTW - I trust her judgement - she always steers me in the right direction - sometimes kicking and screaming. lol ...... and the reason I shop at this store is - I can return things at any time. You know how you can love something in a store and when you get home you think ...what the heck was I thinking????

Maybe if JK is doing a cruise or something - I can come a little early and we can go shopping..... there is a heck of a lot of "ifs" in that last sentence!!! I may have just spent my cruise money on skinny jeans and cashmere sweaters...lol

Have a wonderful weekend .... and I know you will be skinny dipping with your DH .... lucky girl! xo

### caroline — 2013-06-04T11:03:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-87433>

The last week or so - I have noticed my body shape is changing again..... My newish jeans are too big and I have gone from buying XL tees to small tees. I am back down to the weight I was before the gummy bear debacle so I would not have thought that my body comp is still changing. A saleslady told me today that I have lost weight when in fact I am the same weight I was last time I saw her.

I think that Jack's Leptin R/X should come with a warning - beware - you are going to be clothes down sizing often. Great if you love to shop - and I do - did you know that you get a great oxytocin rush when shopping?? So .... shopping is a great thing ..... should be part of the R/X I think .... all you need is money!

I actually do look "fit "..... unreal to be happening at this stage of my life!

I get a big kick out of shopping on line and I usually am just about the same size as the model - That has never ever happened! ..... of course the models are usually 5 inches taller and 40 years younger!

Happy to take what I can get!!!

### caroline — 2013-06-06T10:58:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-87606>

Yesterday I met a really interesting woman at the beach .... she has been trekking in the Himalayas. There was no support van or anything - they just walked ...pretty impressive. It turns out there is about 30 women that swim every morning around 8:00 am and She invited me to go for coffee with them - can't wait. I prefer to go to the ocean later when the sun is out but I might have a walk down and check it out tomorrow am.

Today I met a women at the train station in Sydney - it turned out we were waiting for the same train. She is a herbalist and Bowen therapist and a naturalist plus, plus, plus. It was so fascinating. Her daughter had just moved to a new house in Sydney .... she turned down one because she saw all these power lines. She called an EMF expert to check the house and the EMF's were off the chart! What are the chances I would run into someone that understands all this stuff!!!

She said she was so happy to meet me because she has all this knowledge and no one to talk to about it! Match made in heaven I think. She has a lot of stomach issues .... celiac and maybe crohns etc. We discussed leaky gut - as you do - on the train with a stranger. She has been eating gluten free bread - but I convinced her to read the leptin reset - and give up the bread ... and of course crank up the fat!! We talked about C/T - she has arthritis... and she didn't think I was some nut!

I was so excited! - we exchanged emails and made plans to meet again...... you just never know what each day might bring ...... many gifts..... things always happen for a reason ...you just never know who is going to cross your path....

### Inger — 2013-06-06T20:35:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-87644>

Audrey, what amazing experiences! Now you got a new friend  one that share the same view. Starfish you

Hope you are not freezing so much anymore and feeling great overall!

### caroline — 2013-06-17T11:55:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-88224>

Just popped in to say "Hi" everyone. I seemed to have turned into a very uninteresting and boring person lately......

I miss the hot summer sun so much ..... at least it has been mostly sunny. I haven't been to the beach for 2 days - have to fix that tomorrow. At least I have the lifeguards mostly to myself!

I met an interesting young Chinese woman on the train yesterday - it turns out she lives pretty close to me. She is going to China tomorrow to visit her parents. She had bought all this vitamin stuff to take to them and was asking me what I thought ...... It was weird stuff someone had talked her into and I had no idea. She was bemoaning the fact that you can't depend on what they are telling you......when you have a horse in the race.........

I love the strange names that Chinese women give themselves ..... this woman was probably around 40 and had chosen to call herself .... Candy..... cute ..... silly but cute!

I am always amazed at the interesting conversations you can get into when you tell someone that you like their shoes! But when someone has fusia shoes/boots - you have to say something - right?

We got talking about food and it turns out she loves to eat fat! I was pretty surprised by that ..... everyone generally thinks I have gone off the deep end if I mention what I eat! So - I directed her to Grassfed beef with Fat. Some people are so open to to new stuff......

Candy is a toucher .... I love that. She would touch my arm ever so briefly when making a point or excited about something. It is such a lovely connection that doesn't cost anything......and it makes you feel valued....

We were sitting in the quiet carriage and an old cranky man kept telling us to stop talking! We told him we are allowed to talk quietly - he said - fine - but every once in a while you get excited and your voices raise!!! We probably should have leaned back and touched him!!! zap!

I have been noticing more and more lately when I am out and about ..... people don't look at one another at all - if you glance at them they avert their eyes ...... so sad that people are so disinterested in one another........

I am sitting here in my usual condition these days - shivering ....... wondering how many calories that burns???

I hope all of you are enjoying being outside and lapping up the Vit D ... in your bikinis of course.....
I tried on a bikini this morning and I might just be open to this ...... If it hadn't been for a surgeon doing a real number on my mid section .... I would be good to go.........see - miracles do happen when you least expect them!

The real miracle is that I would go skinny dipping in a heart beat ........for sure .. a change in self esteem issues. Better late than never I guess....

Have a great day everyone!

### Hope — 2013-06-17T16:15:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-88241>

Hi Audrey,

love your stories about meeting people!! I feel sorry for that old man, he was likely lonely and miserable or tired and not wanting any noise around.

But how cool about this woman from China...and how sad in a way because they have the amazing Chinese Medicine......with herbs and so on.

I agree with what you said about people not looking at each other. i often try to strike up conversations, just naturally. Especially when there are doggies or babies involved. My DH sometimes thinks I'm crazy because I don't know these people and he is way too shy. But I've actually made friends like that. And I LOVE little babies. They always seem happy, or mostly, when you pay them some attention. I love to make them smile. I guess because I don't have my own (yet!) I have to live vicariously through other's babies' smiles. Today I am meeting a friend's baby, born in April, for the first time. He has blood in his stool and digestive issues. Oh how I want to talk like a waterfall with his mommy but I must tread carefully or she won't listen and turn me off. Ugh. She is 43 and got pregnant naturally, easily, but had to take antibiotics three times. Broke my heart when I heard that....

Anyway, enjoy that beach. I so long to have a beach available to me and I'd visit it no matter what the weather. I am dreaming of finding a local polar bear club!!

When you say shivering - because you feel cold generally or from CT? Does your thyroid function well?

### caroline — 2013-06-18T03:40:11+0300

Hi Hope!
Yes my thyroid is sluggish. I have started the iodine protocol - hope it helps. I try not to wear too many clothes - just a t-shirt usually .... hence the shivering! I don't put any heat on either.

I am sleeping so terribly - well not terribly - just not sleeping .... I am not tired at all and just lay awake most of the nite. I am wondering if it is the iodine - I certainly feel energised. I am not taking any today and see what happens tonite.

I think talking to strangers adds so much richness to your life - all the shades of gray - and beyond....you never know who you are going to stumble upon and what fantastic story you are going to hear......

Hope your sleep comes back into line. Nothing worse than lying awake all night long!

Oh and you must try nude swimming Audrey. You can do it now at the beach when no one else is there. I can tell you it is magic! I do it every day in the river.. oh man it is lightyears ahead of the feeling you have when swimming with a bikini I can tell!!!

### Inger — 2013-06-18T07:58:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-88329>

I just washed my hair.. and I go on thinking about you Audrey and how you freeze all the time. Oh man I could never do that. Audrey I always put on something warm if I have it cold in my apartment and i am freezing. I have those lambs skin shoes that are so warm, I got them from hubby for Christmas. And I have a pullover on. But I never turn up the heat. And if I feel to chilly to go into the river, I even take a hot shower before! But I go. I go every single day. But I always take care I feel well. I do not go around and freeze and feel miserable all the time at all, I could never do that. I am super strict with my food instead and I get down the weirdest stuff that makes me feel great. But I just cannot go around and freeze all day long.. that would be just too sad. Why you do not do short bursts of cold, like dips in the ocean, and put on some more clothes at home instead? Your head and face still gets exposure to cool air then. But you are not going to freeze all the time

### caroline — 2013-06-19T06:55:31+0300

Hi Hope .....I didn't sleep well again last nite and I didn't have any iodine. It is very cool at nite time now so I just lay there like a bump on a log! I just seem to have a lot of energy - not nervous energy. In the summer I was always getting really sleepy around 8:30pm but now I don't seem to get sleepy. My melatonin must be crap.
I am very cold at nite time - but once I get in bed I am hot and take off all my clothes. I usually start off with trackies and last nite I even had a hoodie on plus assorted t-shirts!! Within 30 minutes I am peeling everything off - so when I get up for the bathroom it can be a bit chilly when you are starkers! but not that bad really.

My self esteen is so much better - so I guess I would swim at a nude beach.......maybe.......with you and Inger....

I guess that is one unexpected success from the Leptin RX!! I wonder if Dr. K. had factored that in........

The bigger downside .....I must fall asleep way later and then wake up way later ..... this morning it was 9:00am.

### caroline — 2013-06-19T11:39:00+0300

I have terribly neglected my apartment all summer - so I need to do some serious cleaning ...ugh, ugh, ugh!

I almost forgot ..... I got some books on Einstein out of the library - so I have lots to do until summer comes back!
