### caroline — 2013-06-23T11:33:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-88726>

My grandsons spent the weekend and I just wanted to share something that I learned from them....
I had taken them to the movies .....BTW - I really can't stand sitting in a movie theatre these days - it seems like such a waste ... even if I like the movie...
anyway - we were walking home and talking about Einstein \[as you do\] and Tommy said that he knew about Einstein ... and so I asked him what? He said , at school, there is a magazine and it asked the question .... What do really famous people .... Einstein and Justin Beiber ... have in common...... the answer ...... they are left handed!

You just never know when you are going to learn something new!

We talked about the winter solstice - they knew nothing about that .....what do they learn at school these days????

We googled it and they learned all about the winter and summer solstice and tonite we have the biggest full moon of the year .... which their Mother won't let them stay up to see! At least they were very excited to tell their Mother all the stuff they had learned.
We did manage to spend a couple of hours at the ocean in spite of the drizzle and cold. It always amazes me all the people that swim every single day of the year. We talked at length with an older gentleman who has lived here his entire life. He is in really good shape. It is really something fascinating to me that they know intuitively that it is so good to be in the water every day. The water temperature is really dropping now - last week it was still about 66 degrees but going down now. He only swims in the pool ... but so many of these people swim daily in the open ocean for miles ...... he called it - "The Shark Run"

### fitness@home — 2013-06-23T16:23:05+0300

### Albert83BCN — 2013-06-23T18:45:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-88748>

Today I was at my grandparents home (I guess I played the opposite role of yours hehe) and my mom started to talk about something she brings up from time to time, related about where she was pregnant (of me). She says that one day she just realized I was going to be a boy, even without an ecography. She says she just 'heard my voice' one night and from that day on she had no doubt I was going to be a boy. We sometimes made fun of her for such ideas. But today I thought something different. I remembered about Jack explaining in the book (or some of the blogs, don't remember for sure) about the connection between the fetus and her mother, how we receive info about circadian rhythms on the outside via our mother, etc etc.

... and then I realized it might be something true in that 'connection' she felt. For the first time. She also explained that one night she had a epic need for mussels despite she never liked them a lot and her doc told her not to eat seafood during her pregnancy. I thought "damn! I can understand it now, we were looking for DHA and EPA that might be missing and was very much needed for my brain to develop!".

Don't know for sure if there might be some truth in all this or she really imagined it but now seems plausible.

So you see, sometimes we do better even when we don't know better! like the man swimming on the sea everyday. Instinct at play?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-23T18:52:48+0300

### Inger — 2013-06-30T09:18:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-89230>

Audrey I wish I could help. I am sending you a hug through the air. I am a lonely chick too! But I do not really suffer from it. I take care to experience many sensual moments through my day and I guess that helps feeling not deprived? Maybe try to have fun with yourself.. like kids do you know? And suck in beauty from others? Many different kind of love, not only between a man and a woman. But I know it is hard when you are used to have a partner. Last spring when I was thrown out from my home I suffered so badly. I felt so terribly alone and hurt, even if my marriage was not great at all. So weird. But I bet they are the habits and what we are used to. I just went through it all, letting the grieve roll over me. I cried my heart out. It lasted a few weeks, and from there it was uphill! That was when I left the old behind. Maybe you need to do that? let go? Hmmm.. I am sure you know when you dig deep what is the issue. Winters are perfect for that. Being alone in the dark, without anything.. you will be able to see what you have to do. I think you really need this winter Audrey, even if you do not feel like that at all. Just do not be scared, because you know you are on the right path! Even if it can feel terrible for moments.. but I rather think it feels more terrible when we are fighting it and do not want to change.
I just want to tell you, life can be amazing even without a partner. And it can be like hell with one..lol So do not get fixated about needing someone. I have given those thought up, and it makes me way more peaceful and happy. At the same time I am totally open, as you know.  I often think... kids are so happy alone too - and they do not look for a partner. They have this magic mindset. I have decided to learn from them.

I am proud of you too walking around with little on.. wow. I bet I have lost my cold adaption somehow by now. I do only natural CT and it is not very cold anymore. But soon enough it will be again.

(((((hugs)))))

### caroline — 2013-06-30T11:35:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-89234>

Thankyou Inger for all your kind and thoughtful words. It is not the partner thing really .......it is when you have caused the death of someone you loved so much ..... no matter if it was the right thing to do .....it is impossible to get past - at least for me ..... no one should ever have that responsibility ... it is too big a burden to carry...

But - everyone carries burdens of one type or another .......it is how we deal with them that counts. One day a light bulb will come on ....... and I will forgive myself....

Mostly I am okay - but today is the anniversary and I chose to think about stuff .......tomorrow will be better....

see - in this case .... thinking is not productive. It is like the definition of insanity - I keep thinking the same things and expecting a different outcome....

Someday I want to have one of those ceremonies and clear my mind and feel liberated.......I think we should do one together..........whaddyathink Inger?????

I think maybe one day we will sit beside a river - when there is a full moon - and have someone help us get to a beautiful peace ........and I suppose we will be swimming naked ..... now there is something to look forward too........LOL

You know what part of the problem is??? - actually I think it is Dr. K's fault \[sorry in advance!\] All of this has turned me into a much more sensual being ... hormones etc.etc. and all of this very open dialogue about everything under the sun!!
My body and mind have really responded as never before. Dr. K's willingness to share so much and talk about everything in a very open and natural way has left an indelible impression on me ....

WOW ..... sorry - I don't know where all that came from..... I could still hit the delete button ...thinking, thinking, thinking...

I feel alot better now ..... thanks Inger ....hugs to you too! xoxo

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-30T15:50:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-89237>

I don't dwell on anything from the past because the past is moment already gone. I only focus on the present moment because it is the only moment that I can have an effect on by what I choose to do. At any given moment in our lives, there are certain things that could have happened but didn't or won't. The real magic moments often go unrecognized in the present, and then suddenly later it dawns on you that the hand of destiny changes everything in the path you are on. The past can never do that to us unless we allow it too. Caroline, your thoughts about the past are a drag on your future destiny. Past thoughts are just old gambles or risks in the game of life. Past performance has no bearing on future gains in anything in life contrary to how people think. If you are not willing to risk the 'usual' in the present, you will have to settle for the ordinary in your future. It is time to cut the rope and focus on what you can control..........the now.

The mindset, that we are a compilation of our past is flawed. The past never equals the future…….Biography is not our destiny, decision however, shapes our destiny, but decisions and choices are what determines achievement. Resources do not limit us, but our resourcefulness is the the issue of our failures.

### caroline — 2013-07-01T02:55:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-89266>

Thanks Dr. K.

"it is time to cut the rope and focus on what you can control". I am imprinting this on my brain. I definitely don't want to settle for ordinary ..... Nothing about this journey has been ordinary - in fact it has been pretty spectacular!

### Inger — 2013-07-01T07:41:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-89277>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=89234)
>
> It is not the partner thing really .......it is when you have caused the death of someone you loved so much ..... no matter if it was the right thing to do .....it is impossible to get past - at least for me ..... no one should ever have that responsibility ... it is too big a burden to carry...

Audrey my dear. I am 100% sure you have not wanted anyones dead. If we want the best, but make mistakes because we do not know better, we are not guilty at all. How could we be? I am 100% sure you did the best you could in given situation. How could you do anything else? I forgive myself for all the mistakes I have done without knowing, and instead I try to learn soooo much, that such things do not happen anymore. That is all I can do. Always learn. Never settle. Stay humble. Previous mistakes can add to that, which is good, but never let it break you down, or dwell in self pity etc. Just use it to be so very merciful to every other being on this planet.

### caroline — 2013-07-01T10:54:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-89286>

You are a wise woman Inger ........I think I have been indulging in self pity for too long now .....and yes - never settle.
Dr. K.has been telling us that for a long time!

Time to turn the page.......

I was doing some shopping today and a sales woman told me I was very artistic ..... that totally shocked me. I had to ask her to repeat what she said. I have never thought of myself in that way ...... it greatly pleased me. I so admire people who are artistic and don't really understand why she said that to me. I have seen her many times before so she must see something that I don't ........That was a magic moment actually......

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-01T15:31:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-89292>

Our life seems to be a series of events and accidents. Yet when most of us look back we do see a pattern to our life. One day in your life really is a microcosm of what your life has been up until that point. Maybe that lady was trying to give you her perspective of the reality she saw for you?

With reflection, you realize everything you can imagine has its own new reality if you begin to live it and give it life. There is no greater agony than carrying thoughts of an untold story inside you. When you begin to sense your real heart emotions in life, you begin to sense it is never too late to be what you might have been. This introspection makes you see your heart has always had problems, which your mind could never understand. The difficulty is trying to rebuild yourself with this insight, piece by piece, with no instruction manual, and no idea as to where all the important parts of your life fit. It's the possibility of changing who you are with your imagination that ultimately, makes life interesting.

### Hope — 2013-07-02T01:36:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-89329>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=89286)
>
> You are a wise woman Inger ........I think I have been indulging in self pity for too long now .....and yes - never settle.
> Dr. K.has been telling us that for a long time!
>
> Time to turn the page.......
>
> I was doing some shopping today and a sales woman told me I was very artistic ..... that totally shocked me. I had to ask her to repeat what she said. I have never thought of myself in that way ...... it greatly pleased me. I so admire people who are artistic and don't really understand why she said that to me. I have seen her many times before so she must see something that I don't ........That was a magic moment actually......

Audrey I am swamped with work and exhausted from sleeping badly away at the beach last week (not sure why) but was thinking a lot of you.....and how you get to live by the ocean. How that is my dream.  And you have that dream...and I was wishing and setting intentions some day I will too.

What you said.....why do you think you caused your DH's death? I agree with Inger. Have you acknowledged your feelings? My acupuncturist who is very into Taoism and living with nature and accepting whatever is our life situations before we move on and change them, says that until you acknowledge your feelings, they will keep trying to get your attention...via your thoughts or your feelings or body symptoms. This is not easy but I want to encourage you to think about that. Perhaps it's part of why you had trouble sleeping before? IDK but I am sending you a big hug....I understand how you feel, and though it wasn't a different family member, I have a sad anniversary coming up too next week, and that is always hard. One thing another family member encouraged me with about this great loss of mine was, that this person loved me very much, and would not want to see me sad and unhappy, but to make the most of my life, and still find joy while I hold the feelings and memories and love for them in my heart.

And someone who lost their baby to SIDS many years ago said grief of this kind is like losing a finger on your hand.....the scar will heal, but your hand won't ever look exactly the same, but you can move on and the scar serves as a reminder of your great love for them...and the hand, like your heart, is still capable of a great many things, joys, experiences.

Do you have friends who you can connect with at a deep level, to support you, locally, and give you platonic hugs? I was single for a long time and desperate to meet someone.....but when I finally got over myself and created a life of joy for myself, filled with good friends - guys and girls - and things I loved to do and let go, that's when I met my DH. You sound like you desire another partner, and I would be surprised if you wouldn't be found by someone again or find someone yourself.

And lastly, they say it is better to have loved and lost, than never to have loved in the first place. I know you know all this. But I wanted to encourage you nonetheless.....I hope you find meaning in even one word of this attempt....

I know you will come through this...and on the other side will create a great life full of art and beauty and optimal health...and swimming naked with amazing Inger! You are entering the season of darkness and winter and nature dying...and getting ready for spring and new life and beauty. Have you looked at any trees or shrubs lately? I am not sure about Australian plant life, but here, in October, after the leaves are gone (or later), you can see the buds all ready for spring even while they now lay dormant - even on the acorn tree in front of our house. I realized that a few years ago and that really helped me accepted the seasons. Not sure it will help you - and it may not - wherever you are now, is OK....just feel the feelings. Don't wallow but allow them and then you can move on...which you will.

Love to you ~

### caroline — 2013-07-02T05:56:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-89351>

Thanks again Dr. Kruse - you always have a way of putting things in perspective with a minimum of fuss.

Thankyou Hope for taking the time to write all those heartfelt thoughts.

When my DH was very ill - the Doctors and hospital admin convinced me to take him of Dialysis .... that it would be the best thing. In retrospect I realise that I was manipulated and not strong enough to fight back and , of course, now I know so much more.
I have had a wonderful and rich life ....for sure - not always easy. I have spectacular memories It kind of scares me now because I think maybe I want too much - I don't want to settle but I have had so much already. Most days I move forward but sometimes - the odd day - I just think this is all so hard.

I can't imagine losing a child ... But I have heard people say that the child wasn't theirs to keep - only on loan and they are so grateful for the time they had. I couldn't be more grateful for the time we shared.

I have been indulging in too much self pity .... It is kind of funny ....But when JK said to cut the rope to the past - that is what I am going to do. I have had 3 therapists the last couple of years and they all say to examine my feelings and then let them go. To keep examining these feelings isn't productive and I think I have always known that - so - no more of that for this girl!

Okay - enough of this........ as I said above - I am turning the page ...... thanks to all you lovely and generous people.

Hope - I hope you realise your dream of living by the ocean some day soon ..... as you all know - ad nauseum.....I am completely in love with this place - well except for this cool weather! and maybe because there a lot fewer gorgeous young men in speedos!! I noticed the lifeguards were wearing trackies yesterday!! Life isn't fair!!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-02T06:22:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-89353>

In my opinion, talk therapy about past feelings is an epic waste of time and very counter productive. I believe in bungee cord therapy. Sometimes you have to make your leap when you're not ready, and build your wings on the way down to Earth. All you need within you is waiting to unfold and reveal itself. Focus on the now. Your present moment is the ever moving shadow that divides your yesterday's from realities of tomorrow. In that sliver of time lies our hope.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-07-02T09:33:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-89361>

^I couldn't agree more. It may sound harsh or rough but it's the truth. I did a form of therapy with a very experienced and intelligent psychologist when I was 16, and my fiance went to different psychologists on and of from 15 up until earlier this year. We both found them unnecessary and relatively unhelpful. After taking some courses in psychology it made me come to terms with a lot of the techniques they used and it isn't worth it. You can even look up studies that show CBT/various talk therapies have no benefit over other therapies, yet people have a lot of faith in them. Face your fears, even if it takes awhile to build up the psychological/emotional strength to do so. You are doing that anyhow in CBT, only you are paying someone to teach you how. I do this now as I still battle my anxiety. I used to get anxious out of the blue for no reasons, especially when I would go on jogs (which I know longer do - substituted with walks). Now I purposely go on long walks, far away from home without my cell phone or any cash on me, no water, anything. I push the limits and challenge myself. The worst that could happen is I get anxious which then actually offers me a chance to practice techniques in calming myself and mindfulness, so it can actually be beneficial. You know what also helps...? Grounding and sunshine. When I go out into the field nearby my house and do foundation training with minimal clothing on in bright sunlight, I feel so free, liberated, and energized. Look up some very simple meditation techniques on youtube (look up neurosoup's channel or Brandon Gilbert) and go out in direct sunlight while grounding and do 5 minutes of meditation, and you will quickly become a new person.

That being said, I would be interested in trying more 'fringe' therapies such as EDMR. Even hypnosis, but I quite honestly don't trust people enough to hypnotize me and put things into my subconscious mind. I downloaded some hypnotism tapes but it was a waste of time, I'd rather just learn self-hypnotism, lol.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-07-02T17:34:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-89379>

Thank you Caroline, I really appreciate your kind words. It is actually going so-so. There has been an extreme amount of stress lately, I'll spare everyone all the bad news, but in light of all the negative, I did get my brother to buy some gluten-free products and luckily my mom caught a good deal on shrimp so the past few days he's eaten a lot of that. We cooked shrimp together one night, and that was fun! Luckily I'm starting to get longer hours this upcoming week so I'll be ensuring he eats some more shellfish and I'm going to try just throwing out all the bad oils in the house and buying butter, even if it isn't the highest quality. I'm also thinking about asking my dad to read the preface and first chapter of GCBC. Thank you again for your kind words.

To be honest, I just started reading your journal/thread. So I apologize if I came off harsh, I didn't know what you lost. I hope you don't mind asking me where you reside, do you stay in Poland? You sound very similar to My fiances' ciotka. That being said, my respect for you is tremendous. I don't want to ponder the thoughts of what you have been through and how you're adjusting. Speak to your family and reside in them, they are apart of you, an offshoot. Maybe find therapy in your grandchildren? I don't know how young they are, but I know I can even feel the innocence radiating from children when I see some.... young children and animals seem to have the innocence and natural 'life force' still residing in them that many people seem to lose as they age. Besides fighting aging the way Dr. Kruse does, one of my goals is to never get caught up in the fast paced, future oriented nature of the modern world. Stay in there, Caroline.

### caroline — 2013-07-03T06:53:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-89426>

Thanks BigPapa ..... actually - I have always wondered about the name you use here - is it significant? or just a name?
You didn't come off harsh at all and besides, sometimes things need to be said. I am in Australia - but strangely - from a Polish and Austrian background....that is funny that you mention Poland. Why was that? what is similar?

The one thing I have discovered about loss ..... it often splinters a family. Everyone handles grief in their own way. I always thought that grief brings a family together - but definitely does not .... sorry to say.

Yes - being around young children is wonderful ..... I love to see them playing in the sand at the ocean - they truly do radiate love and happiness and innosense and when you get a smile from them it lights up the world.

I am so sorry that things aren't going so well with your brother - but sound like you have made some progress.

You are such an old soul in a young body ....I love how you are desperate for knowledge.

I am definitely hanging in there - thanks to everyone for their comments and suggestions and encouragement here. It means a lot.

### caroline — 2013-07-03T10:59:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-89436>

Yesterday, as I was walking to the ocean, I was losing my shorts......today - I was losing my swimmers in the waves! I don't understand what is happening to this body.

Someone I know at a shop was asking me how I stay so slim......this is quite a revelation to me for someone who has spent a lot of her life overweight. She said - obviously you have been slim all your life ... how do you do it? and what exercises do you do? I usually just say - I have changed how I eat \[people don't generally want to know more\] and I always say I don't exercise. People just don't believe that you don't.... in most people's mind you have to exercise like crazy to even stand a chance of getting your weight down. When I explain that I think exercise - other than walking, swimming and just being outdoors is much too hard on our body .......I lose them. The CW is so ingrained - I wonder how long it will take to change? I see so many overweight, older people at the beach jogging - it is so disturbing.

I have been coming to the realisation the last day or so - because of something Dr. K. said....... that I have been working on myself the last year. I was kind of feeling like I was just wasting a lot of precious time doing nothing much.
I sort of lost myself the last ten years and I was in need of a makeover ... from the ground up!

I have been learning so much here and I have loved every second of it ...and I have been learning so much about myself or I guess I should say - rediscovering myself.

I have been beating myself up because I don't like large groups and avoid them like the plague. I understand now it is because I feel invisible and I hate that feeling. I don't want to be invisible anymore.

Every new day is a chance for some more magic to happen.........how lucky we are.....

Goodnite everyone!

one other thing ...... I have a bottle of wine I can't open ....any ideas?????

A thought for you to ponder: Embrace your darkness.

Have you reached the pinnacle of success yet? If not, why not? To get there you must use your present moment to act. The best way to predict your future is to create it with the moment you have now. Whenever you begin to ascend this mountain, you will occasionally fall into the darkness of its shadows as you climb. Those who succeed embrace the darkness because they know there is extraordinary opportunity for the light to eventually burn brighter at the pinnacle of the mountain. We all must remember the present moment is the ever moving shadow that divides yesterday from our tomorrow's. In that small slice of dark, lies our hope. Without that dark, we'd never see the stars to guide us to our pinnacle of success.

And I open bottles with out a cork screw by breaking the tops off with a fell swoop on a counter top.......you tube it and you will see it cleaves the glass in one shot. I have saved many an old first growth bottles this way.

### cinnamon — 2013-07-03T16:02:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-89442>

Caroline - Losing your shorts and losing your swimmers - sounds like a butterfly emerging from the chrysalis!

Here's an excerpt from the [wikipedia page](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pupa):

A chrysalis (Latin chrysallis, from Greek χρυσαλλίς = chrysallÃ­s, pl: chrysalides, also known as an aurelia) or nympha is the pupal stage of butterflies. The term is derived from the metallic gold-coloration found in the pupae of many butterflies, referred to by the Greek term χρυσός (chrysÃ³s) for gold.

When the caterpillar is fully grown, it makes a button of silk which it uses to fasten its body to a leaf or a twig. Then the caterpillar's skin comes off for the final time. Under this old skin is a hard skin called a chrysalis.\[4\]

Because chrysalides are often showy and are formed in the open, they are the most familiar examples of pupae. Most chrysalides are attached to a surface by a Velcro-like arrangement of a silken pad spun by the caterpillar, usually cemented to the underside of a perch, and the cremastral hook or hooks protruding from the rear of the chrysalis or cremaster at the tip of the pupal abdomen by which the caterpillar fixes itself to the pad of silk. (Gr. 'kremastos'=suspend) \[5\]

Like other types of pupae, the chrysalis stage in most butterflies is one in which there is little movement. However, some butterfly pupae are capable of moving the abdominal segments to produce sounds or to scare away potential predators. Within the chrysalis, growth and differentiation occur. The adult butterfly emerges (ecloses) from this and expands its wings by pumping haemolymph into the wing veins.\[6\] Although this sudden and rapid change from pupa to imago is often called metamorphosis, metamorphosis is really the whole series of changes that an insect undergoes from egg to adult.

On emerging the butterfly uses a liquid, sometimes called cocoonase, which softens the shell of the chrysalis. Additionally, it uses two sharp claws located on the thick joints at the base of the forewings to help make its way out.\[7\] Having emerged from the chrysalis, the butterfly will usually sit on the empty shell in order to expand and harden its wings. However, if the chrysalis was near the ground (such as if it fell off from its silk pad), the butterfly would find another vertical surface to rest upon and harden its wings (such as a wall or fence).​

Once you have fully emerged, you just have to harden your wings and you will really soar!

### Inger — 2013-07-04T09:04:38+0300

### caroline — 2013-07-07T11:00:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-89638>

I finally got my bottle of wine open....... I chickened out and asked my neighbour! I did contemplate doing it the JK way - but I don't have the skills of a surgeon .....and didn't want to clean up the mess if it went bad!

The wine turned out to be a really nice French Merlot that I didn't pay very much for ...quel surprise!

It has been a beautiful sunny day - about 16c I think. I took my tummy for a suntan .... it seems to be ridiculous to be laying at the beach and shivering ..... I'll take what I can get

### caroline — 2013-07-09T12:11:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-89754>

I ran into some interesting people in Sydney yesterday. A sweet little lady who had taught in the Gobi desert for years .....at the College of Cabbage. Absolutely true story! It was so fascinating to hear how they lived.....they had not much of anything - but yet they had everything.

I also talked to a man from California .....he was giving some lectures at the University in Melbourne and then had hired a campervan and is driving to Cannes. I was trying to give him some shopping tips in Sydney .....he was hilarious. I was telling him about David jones (upmarket department store) he agreed to go and take a look .....but then he said to me - David Jones is the one that started the "Monkies" right?? I really don't think so says I ......but what do I know......

A lady at a shop said to me that I have a really good figure...that is really a lot for a former fat girl to take in.....I really want to know if men have these same self esteem issues.......Dr. K.?

You never know what wonderful things each new day holds.......every day is such a gift......

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-09T14:33:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-89758>

I am not sure men really care about the facade........and if they do it might be pathologic.

### caroline — 2013-07-12T12:54:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-89946>

I had another interesting train trip to Sydney ... or maybe I am easily amused!
I chatted the whole way with a young man who is a graphic designer and on a computer for 8-10 hours a day. I asked him if he was concerned about EMF exposure .....he said he hadn't really thought about it. I asked him where he keeps his iphone .....you got it ....in his pants pocket. It really did shock him into thinking about where to keep his iphone. I asked him if he knew who Dr. Charlie Teo is .....I said that he is really concerned about EMFs - that made an impression on him. I asked him how he is feeling and sleeping etc. I almost asked him how his erections are ....but I realized that that was beyond the pale!!!! If I am not here some day - you will know that someone has had me committed!

He promised me that he wouldn't keep his phone in his pocket anymore!

As I was getting ready to get off at my stop - I went upstairs.....at the same time a lady was coming downstairs and we sat down together. Talk about Quantum magic .... it was as if we instantly bonded. It turns out we are in a similar
situation and we felt exactly the same about things that have been happening. She is so lovely. We sat on a bench and talked for about an hour and exchanged addresses and phone numbers. Her daughter has just started medical school here so she will be coming down lots ....and she loves the beach! She had never taken the train down this way before and was in awe. It is a beautiful trip along the coast - coming down from Sydney ....I never tire of it.

When in Sydney I had been trying on bathing suits.... there is tons of great sales right now. That is such a horrible, horrible, discouraging experience....ugh, ugh, ugh \[to quote Hope!\]

It is one thing to look okay with clothes on - but it is quite another to try to stuff yourself into various and assorted bathing suits. I came to the conclusion, pretty quickly, that I look better naked! ....like - way better ....how is that possible??

I also discovered, in the shoe department, that high heels are fun and sexy ....I had forgotten! All the staff was trying shoes on with me....what a laugh!

Now I want a pretty dress.....

### Inger — 2013-07-12T22:18:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-89991>

LOL Audrey, you make me laugh.....
Maybe you should swim naked then. I really suggest you to try it out, a very beautiful experience! High heels are sexy, sure. But so uncomfortable to me at least..huh. I am too unwilling to suffer..lol I can see in front of my inner eye how you and the shoe store stuff have fun..lol You look like you enjoy your life very much Audrey, makes me happy!

### caroline — 2013-07-14T05:43:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-90091>

I had another magical day yesterday. As I was getting ready to get off the train last nite at my stop ....I said to the young woman beside me that it had really turned cold ... and I just had a sweater on and sandals. She asked me if I was going home and I said yes ......She said - If you weren't going home I will give you my jacket \[a lovely puffy one\] That was so beautiful......who does that?

I also met a gorgeous young woman in the shoe department. She was trying on Jimmy Choos for her wedding. She was so lovely and intelligent and gets all this stuff. I feel like I am an ambassador for JK's book. She really thinks about EMF's etc. and we had such a great discussion on everything.......including the best jeans!

The rewards of talking to strangers is huge.....your life becomes so enriched .....and there certainly is quantum entanglement on so many levels.......

I was actually going to Sydney to meet a woman I have become friends with that I met on the train about 6 weeks ago. She has multiple health issues and is very interested in all this and following the leptin reset etc.
Unfortunately - She had to cancel because of a medical emergency and wound up in surgery.

### fitness@home — 2013-07-14T20:03:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-90117>

Audrey, I love interactions with strangers too. People are so interesting!

Yesterday I made myself go shopping for a Mother of the Bride (MOB) dress. Have really been procrasting on this because I dreaded spending hours looking for one. Went to a local shop named T Caroline's, filled out my card, and was assigned the most gracious elderly saleswoman . They have actually been filming some kind of reality show there on MOBs.

She was such a sweet woman and HONEST. Very tiny and petite with an eastern European accent. If the dress wasn't right for me, she would shake her head. She even took pictures for me so I could email them to my daughter -- who has final approval on the dress.

One hour later, I walked out of there with the gown, shoes, and scarf and even came in under budget. The formal gown is a silver with some shimmer to it and it flows with graceful lines. No taffeta for me!

Later that afternoon we stopped by a Starbucks and found some comfortable seating. It was a very full shop so people watching was good. What stood out the most was this family of five. The children ranged in age from about 5-9 years old. The five year old was playing with hot wheels cars and the rest were playing a card game together! No electronic devices! (Bravo for them) They were laughing, talking, and having such a good time together as a family. 90% of the other people there were on laptops, tablets, cell phones, etc...

I turned to my husband and commented, "Imagine if there was some kind of electromagnetic pulse and all their devices went down. These people would be forced to interact with each other."

### caroline — 2013-07-18T02:33:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-90302>

I have been reading the "cosmic serpent" that Erwan talked about in the Q&A. I am loving it ....so I thought I would listen to the Q&A again. There are so many really thoughtful and insightful comments from both Jack and Erwan. I got a ton more out of it once again - almost like some things didn't really register the first time or the second time. That seems kind of embarrassing ....am I that dense?? Erwan's thoughts can seem so simplistic on the surface and yet - so deep.

I loved how he said "Integrity is everything" he said a couple of things at that point that I just loved but can't quote accurately so I will have to listen again........

I think all this resonates so deeply with me because my DH was this type of man. At his core he was honest and kind and integrity was everything. It was never something overt - it was just who he was and who he always was and he never swayed from those values. He was a very quiet man but had amazing integrity and people always responded to him in spades.

Listening last nite again - I got so, so much more from what Jack said as well. There really was a ton of gold and it seems to have affected me so deeply and on so many levels......quantum magic for sure....at least for me....

### caroline — 2013-07-18T02:44:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-90305>

When I was in the rock pool yesterday I waved at a couple standing and watching. The gentleman was probably in his seventies and in really good shape. He grew up a few blocks from here and spent all his spare time at this beach. This is a historic area and the old bath houses have been preserved and the old men's shed is now a cafe. There is a lot of old photos from the40's and 50's showing what life was like on the beach then. This man remembers it all and told me some great stories. It is always completely amazing to me when you take the time to talk to a stranger....magic definitely happens and you are so enriched.....

Audrey... please tell more if you can. I loved what you shared here. Integrity is such a beautiful thing.. and I miss it so terribly here. I have big difficulties to relate to stuff.. in a wise way.. and suffer quite a bit. Soon it is over, just one more month. But man is it hard.. just because of the lack of integrity in this place. 4 "sick" people and me.. so lonely.. as the other all live in a weird state of spiritual brainwash. Dad just said to me as I told him that we divorce now with my hubs; If you ever get a new man before your DH is dead you will never enter this house anymore, just that you know. I said, yes dad. That is why, if, I need to find that kind of man that it does not matter really I cannot visit this place (like your DH ). He did not said anything to that.

You know, it helps me so much now, those words of wisdom. I need to stay cool. Just cool. But something in me is about to explode all the time. It is so stressing. Must be the lack of privacy that drives me nuts lately. I k now it all comes down to my thoughts, and I know there is a way to stay cool and not let that stuff touch you but I really need some help to see clear now. I am going to think about your post today and about your DH.

I love how you always talk to strangers.  I like that too. I just have not come away from this place at all lately, as I have to take care of the animals and they need so much grass now as it is so dry here. I am kind of stuck. But soon enough I will talk to strangers too all the time.. I cannot wait

### caroline — 2013-07-18T10:35:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-90315>

My dear Inger.....please be strong. I personally think that integrity is something that one has ....or does not. But - I have never known another like my Doug. He was innately kind and generous and loving. He never wanted anything for himself - he only cared if I was happy. His core beliefs were solid ....he could not be swayed. He was like that his whole life. He had seven brothers and sisters and was mostly on the outside of the family because he was different. He was fiercely independent and was often at huge odds with his father who tried to keep him under his thumb.

As I said above - Erwans views seem quite simplistic but yet so deep and so spiritual and he and Jack share the same values....they just approach things from different ends of the spectrum.

I will watch it again and take notes for you ...but, really, you have the same beliefs and values and intuition. Please just believe in yourself. Maybe put together a mini movnat for yourself and go into the forest and extend yourself to your limits...so it pushes everything else out of the way for a little while ....push your boundaries to the limit.

You are a survivor ....but sometimes it is hard - I know. Think of my arm around you... supporting you when you need it.
I was given so much ...and now I will be there for you. It is my absolute pleasure to pass on what Doug so selflessly gave to me .......xoxo

### Inger — 2013-07-19T20:23:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-90387>

Audrey...!!! Guess what happened! I turned it all around    The turn started with some real good sleep.. after those itchy sleepless nights.. and after I went to a night dip in the river that took away every single itch on my body. And then everything changed?! I also decided to skip my breakfast meat, and do fish instead, only fish/seafood at every meal. No idea if that helped but at least I have been able to have some amazing days! WITH those people that I thought was killing my spirit! I just... squeezed all the love out of me that I was able to.. and I really worked on speaking wise words.. so they could understand and no reason to fight or stuff. Instead we have had some very productive talks.. with love and understanding and change to the better. Now.. it is almost magic?
I once more got to feel HOW much it means that I am taking good care of me. Like sleeping well, doing all that great stuff. It gives me an amazing kind of power, really. It does not matter if the whole world does it totally different, and I am even able to explain stuff in a way easy to understand, that makes people listen then. It makes me not scared. I thought I was losing... but I won.  In a way that made everyone happier

Oh man whats up with the sheep, they are screaming outside?! I need to run and check whats up.. but I just needed to tell you this.

### caroline — 2013-07-26T08:19:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-90893>

I ran into a woman today that I hadn't seen for quite a long time ..... Holy cow ....she has so many health issues.
She broke her Achilles tendon just from getting out of bed one morning and has bleeding into her muscles? And some other bleeding and they can't find where it is coming from....and on and on and on.

Where do you even start with some one like that? She was drinking a diet coke and listening to her iPod. She is very overweight and has osteo.

I wound up saying nothing....hard to believe I know......

### caroline — 2013-07-26T11:04:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-90897>

I am so over the top excited ...you might say that it doesn't take much to excite me....

I feel, at this moment, I am so grateful to you Dr. Kruse for this journey I am on ..... because of you.

I got on the bus tonite to come home and the bus jerked and I fell into this woman' lap. I, of course, joked that it would have been good if she had been George Clooney! We wound up standing on a street corner talking for an hour.

There is so much to absorb - I can't take it all in. She works as an auditor \[not sure that is the right word\] for hospitals - to raise standards ....The U.S. is the gold standard apparently. She works for a swiss company - out of California.

Anyway .... she accidently met a 92 year old woman at the beach and has become friends with her. This woman's father was a doctor in Russia and he developed a special technique for treating asthma with a series of breathing exercises and she worked with him for years. She wants to work with aboriginal people because asthma is very prevalent.He didn't believe in using drugs

Her son has forced her into a nursing home and she needs to keep her brain active - so she is going to organise an asthma clinic for aboriginals. So, Helen, the woman I met is going to get her a laptop and hook her up with Jackkruse.com

Helen is totally on board with C/T .....she told me that Audrey Hepburn \[my hero\] used to take cold showers every day!

We were both so excited - we were jumping out of skin! I can't wait to meet her for coffee....

We exchanged so much information ... it blows my mind....

Does this happen to you guys?????

### caroline — 2013-07-27T03:20:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-90981>

I had an appointment with a foot surgeon yesterday. I was on my iPad for a long time and when the doc came to get me - my feet were numb and it took me a few seconds to be able to walk properly. He really didn't seem to care much ( he is a foot surgeon for the love of God!) anyway - I thought I would tell him ..Because he should know....that EMFs make my feet numb. He really wasn't interested at all.....so I always use my trump card ..I told him that Charlie Teo is very concerned about EMF exposure. ....he still didn't care! This foot surgeon is highly regarded here - he really should have been interested in what I was trying to tell him.

My friend called me to see how I had made out with the surgeon so I was telling her about my chance encounter. She absolutely knew all about this Russian doctor and his 92 year old daughter! She knew all about the tecniques they had used and that this Doctor had been ridiculed for years by his peers although the average lay person used these exercises.

How can you possibly doubt that there is such a thing as quantum entanglement.....especially since I seem to bore you on a daily basis with my silly stories....

### Inger — 2013-07-30T20:02:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-91178>

Audrey, I ment I have not felt them you see! I always had it way too warm in my home, way too much light.. I fought the winter. Last winter was my first whole winter when I let myself feel the cold and the darkness. Oh my it was magic! I miss it. Lots of healing to do still for me. I have had so many summers.. but winters I have always escaped. Because I did not know. But now I do, and that is why I am awaiting the winter so much again.  I especially wait for the lonely time at my brothers cottage.. away from civilisation... no electricity.. nothing. I am so freaking exited! That is kind of all I crave..lol Pretty weird! I bet it will boost my creativity in a big way. For my art work.  You should come there too, Audrey! You are welcome anytime for sure! I could pick you up from Oslo airport, I will have a car shortly after Christmas for sure. An own car. Maybe a little old but who cares..lol

My bro is so kind, he has told me I can live there for free, if I just do some work on the house and yard for him. It is pretty old 2 room cottage.. he said I must be careful I do not break through the floor.. haha. Or he said I better not jump on the floor, that is when it could happen! Thankfully the roof is in good shape! That would be not so fun to get the roof over me..huh. I am going to make it so nice and cosy there. And collect and cut some wood for the fire place. There are two fire places inside! If I feel I need to warm up, I can. If you come I will make a nice fire for you so you would not freeze too much for sure!

### caroline — 2013-08-01T03:10:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-91306>

I have had some very interesting conversations with a dear friend the last couple of days. We sometimes feel like we aren't having any impact on those we care about......but yesterday my friend was asking more about iodine and we were talking about GMO foods and wheat etc. etc.... and it turns out - she really has been listening. At one point I started laughing and she asked me what was so funny .....I told her - you are turning into me!! and she totally agrees. All of this has been impacting her in subtle ways that I didn't realise.

I was talking to her this morning and she said she is worried about her electric meter on their bedroom wall ......It was weeks ago that I had asked her where it was .....so today I was talking about her microwave etc. and I told her that I have unplugged mine and that I turn off the breaker to my bedroom.

It seems, absolutely for sure, that actions speak louder than words! You really can't deny how healthy I look and feel and that is what people notice. Words really are hollow unless you are living them every day...people do notice!

I seem to notice lately, even more than normal .....that I am treated with respect and kindness and a sense of playfullness where ever I go. It is so much fun.

There definitely is this quantum energy ....and quantum magic that passes between people - I see this every single day...

### caroline — 2013-08-03T09:22:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-91412>

I have been such a PITA the last couple of days. It has been my birthday and I really hate surprises - it makes me very nervous and unsettled and miserable - I don't really know why. I definitely have to work on that.

A couple of days ago some friends took me out for lunch and they really took care to take me somewhere that would suit what I would like to eat.
Today my daughter organized a surprise lunch with a bunch of friends. everyone notices that I am particular about what I eat and at gatherings I often eat before I go in case there is nothing I would eat. There is never much dialogue about this.

One of my presents today was dark chocolate. They said they knew I would prefer dark. We have never talked bout this ever...Everyone has just been noticing subtlety What I am doing and paying attention.

Our actions speak much louder than words....I have always known that....but now I am seeing it in action.
Quite a revelation.

I hadn't seen one of my friends husbands in quite a few months and he gave me such a big hug and said I look so healthy.

It doesn't get much better ....now if I could just turn back the hands of time....

### caroline — 2013-08-04T04:21:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-91462>

There are more ripple effects from my lunch. One friend has had double knee surgery and I talked at length about making sure she was well hydrated with spring water and taking magnesium. Her nurse in rehab was mad at her because she was drinking so much water but she knew instinctively that she had to. I have just emailed them a huge reading list with Jack's book at the top of the list! They are hippies from way back ... lived on the land in a commune and really embrace alternate health stuff. I don't know why I didn't recognise this before tho.....Jenny has always been a really lovely, kind and generous person but for the longest while now - she has been quite aggressive..... so I am thinking E dominance. She has a very pronounce apple shape.

The friend that gave me chocolate also asked me about coconut oil - she said she was sure I would be using it. So we talked about raw meat and raw fish and she was very receptive.

This is all so surprising to me because all my friends think that weight watchers is the best diet plan out there but they seem to get that I am low carb etc.

Actually - died in the wool weight watchers are the perfect example of insanity .....they keep doing the same thing over and over and over for many years - and hoping for different results - that they never get.

Weight watchers is quite a business model ......clients keep coming back for more and more and more without any lasting results. If a neurosurgeon was losing half of his patients on the table - I would guess he would be out of business pretty fast.....
I have always wondered what "acceptable loss %" is okay? Here is Australia - if it was Charlie Teo - I would guess 10% success on the table would be great? because he takes cases that no one else will touch and are mostly terminal....

Another friend has had breast cancer and so when I emailed her this morning I asked her to get "The iodine crisis"
and of course Jack's book!

It seems I have come out of the closet ...... when you walk the walk - people want to listen to the talk.....

### caroline — 2013-08-10T10:45:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-92011>

We have Master Chef on TV here the last while - I don't know if you guys get it or not? I generally just barely look at it - so much tension and pressure that I remember all too well - but ......Do you guys know who Heston Blumenthal is????

I have decided to kick George Clooney to the curb .....and I am now mesmerised by Heston! He is such a cutie

I love seeing some one who has so much passion and gets so much joy and has so much sheer fun out of what he does.

Being a Chef is something you do because you have the passion .... there is impossibly long hours, incredible amounts of pressure and not all that much money. In an interview Heston said that the week he got his third michelin star he couldn't afford to pay his staff.

He is doing okay now of course - book deals and TV etc. He is just a joy to watch - like a kid in a candy store.

How can you go past a person with so much passion for what they do? I know I can't - it is magic .....

### caroline — 2013-08-10T11:12:35+0300

goodnite everyone - I am going to go and be a slug for awhile \[ with a glass of wine\]

I wish so many wonderful things for all you lovely people .........

### caroline — 2013-08-11T08:14:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-92086>

It is a glorious day here .....about 23 C ....and a beautiful clear blue sky. The beach is littered with bodies! I no longer have the life guards to myself!

About half the people are in long pants and jackets and the other half are in bikinis/ board shorts/ budgie smugglers. Things are definitely looking up....the scenery has really improved

I seem to be really looking forward to another year of being a beach bum .....I figure as long as I am laying in the sand and reading the quilt everything is good. I am getting an education and a sun tan and grounded. - what's not to love....and the added bonus of the surf crashing a few feet away from me.

I hope you all are having a great summer....

### caroline — 2013-08-13T08:41:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-92345>

We are having a gorgeous week .... Yesterday it was 25C but very, very windy. It felt like my bare legs got sandblasted - it really hurt! Today it is 22C with a beautiful clear blue sky and only slightly windy.

I was talking to a young woman I know in a shop today. She is 12 weeks pregnant and so excited. She did that test where you hang a ring on a string and put it over her belly - and she is having a girl! I told her it was quantum magic! I did check that she is healthy and so is her mom and grandma.....

It is so refreshing to talk to her. She loves going in the ocean, doesn't like technology at all, hardly ever uses a computer, and tries to eat very healthy and loves being around her family. She is 21 years old.

I also talked to a young man at the apple store - he was helping me and I asked him about printers .... I told him I didn't want one with wifi. He was completely baffled by that - it just made no sense to him at all. I asked him where he keeps his iPhone - and you guessed it. He was totally and completely unconcerned. In my experience - young men always get it and become agitated about what to do with their iPhone. - like it could possibly be contaminated.

### caroline — 2013-08-16T09:35:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-92761>

I have had the best day today....but keep in mind that I seem to be easily impressed!

The farmer's market was on today. There is a couple that go very early in the morning and hot smoke fresh salmon.... it is so delicious. I also got some hand made veal sausages - really yummy too. I asked for some Lamb's liver and she said it always sells out because of all the people on a paleo diet!! I was pretty surprised by that.

Then the best part - there was a bunch of musicians playing aussie bush music. The lead singer played a traditional bush instrument - a lager stick I think it is called. It is what it sounds like - a stick with beer caps on it!
They taught us a bush polka and all the hand signs for "Give me a home amongst the Gum Trees" so much fun - as I said It doesn't take much!
They were teaching us other dances too ....... a fat guy with a huge belly hanging out \[his t-shirt didn't cover\] kept asking me to dance ....OMG - I do have some standards....
The only action I get are fat old guys with no teeth.

I have a horrible head cold - don't have any idea where that came from ....it is not as if I have been sharing germs or bodily fluids with anyone ........

I should have bought myself a bottle of brandy and put myself out of my misery....

### caroline — 2013-08-16T09:56:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-92762>

I forgot .... I saw a sign today that I really liked .........Avoid - plant a veggie garden....
and another sign that said ...... Visionary, Dynamic, Passionate.....I think that is what we all should be - even when life gets in the way, even if life isn't perfect ... It would be wonderful if people would say that about us - and why not?

### caroline — 2013-08-20T09:02:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-93201>

I just went down to the shops and bought myself a bottle of brandy ........hopefully I can get rid of this cold/thingy. Is a tumbler full too much??? My neighbours had the same thing and it took three weeks to go - I can't wait three weeks!

Martin has created a monster ...... I keep looking in full length mirrors ....WTH. Today I looked like this scrawny waif with wild, wind blown hair, sunken eyes and pale. For some reason I was dressed all in black with a very nice pair of track pants etc. I kept thinking who is this skinny person??? That is what happens when your clothes are too big!

I spent years asking my poor DH ..."Does my bum look big in this" to now having to think about looking too scrawny - WTF
This journey is one crazy ride ........

All the times I didn't want to go out because I felt so fat - even tho my DH was fine with what ever size I was. Just goes to prove ....feeling good about ourself is so important and it comes from within. It doesn't even seem to matter that the person who loves you more than anything doesn't care what size or shape we are ....we need to be on the outside who we know we are on the inside......

I am going to sit and have my tumbler of brandy and contemplate life.......

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-24T15:37:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-93602>

There are random moments - like sharing food with loved ones, walking out to the deck at sunrise, ironing the crease on your pants, standing at the kitchen window and looking out at the clouds, hearing children laughing at the beach when I feel a wavelike rush of joy. This is my true passion now: arbitrary moments of nearly painful happiness for a life I feel privileged to lead. This is where real joy is found everyday. Your past is now done, so forget it. Your future is yet to come so dream it. Your present is now, so live it in joy. After all, even a fake joy is better than real depression.............

Your future is yet to come so dream it. Your present is now, so live it in joy. After all, even a fake joy is better than real depression.............

Those moments. I was thinking about them yeseterday. They make me think I am bi-polar, but they are not manic, nor are they accompanied by severe lows. It is well being and far too many people experience this. Nice, Jack. Gratitude.

### caroline — 2013-08-25T09:55:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-93678>

I went to Sydney today shopping. I was in a fish shop and saw this..... Sea Bass $120.00 Kilo....sourced from Patagonia
I could eat 1 kilo by myself ..... clearly a pretty expensive dinner!

I bought myself some sashimi for the train trip home ... $13.50 ..... I could have eaten 4 more of them.....It just whet my appetite and I have been eating ever since I got home...I keep going back and raiding the fridge! I can't seem to fill up.

### caroline — 2013-08-25T11:10:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-93682>

This is my true passion now: arbitrary moments of nearly painful happiness for a life I feel privileged to lead. This is where real joy is found everyday. Your past is now done, so forget it. Your future is yet to come so dream it. Your present is now, so live it in joy. After all, even a fake joy is better than real depression.............

Does this wisdom come with age? altho, I guess, some people never get there..... and, come to think of it, this is how children live ...totally in the moment...sucking the heck out of life.

Have gratitude for every single day....

I am really privileged to see children, every single day, playing in the ocean, building sand castles, laughing for no apparent reason, running with sheer joy on their face, chasing birds and just simply in awe of a handful of sand.

How lucky am I ......

### prAna303 — 2013-08-25T11:24:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-93683>

I think it is hardwired in us. We start our lifes with all these things inside us but thru time we shut down many of the above abilities. Today we destroy ourself and others with human made rules so we can fit in to the society, one of the biggest one is how we see time, we live in a linear timeline but i thinks it is more circular. We set up rules that by 25 you should have a house, car, work and other things, and so on. When we can not get it we are failed humans... If someone feels lost i think you must look back to the time when you were happy and free and start all over again.

### caroline — 2013-08-27T05:03:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-93874>

The best news ever .... spring is almost here!

I went shopping yesterday for a bathing suit ...... WOW .....when you have a positive shopping experience - there are also oxytocin benefits! not the most ideal way - but, I guess, I can't be picky!

The last number of years, when shopping for a bathing suit, it has been heartbreak city. I always come home empty handed.

Yesterday - I came home with 2 and couldn't decide between many! I was looking for a tankini - I thought it would cover a multitude of sins. I did get one ...but the other one.....The one I love the most - is backless and very low cut ... a lot of boobage going on \[is that a word?\]. This is so unlike me - so far from who I used to be. I can't wipe the smile off my face.

I also came home with a pair of black lace board shorts .... who buys black lace board shorts???? me I guess. They are so adorable! I really am too old for all this silliness .... but WTH ....I am having so much fun.......

I sincerely hope that y'all are having as much fun as I am.....but - I guess - simple mind - simple pleasures......

I think I should re arrange that saying ......peaceful mind .... simple pleasures ... joyfull life ....That's way better.....

### caroline — 2013-08-29T11:13:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-94143>

Well, it seems that living in the northern Hemisphere is preferable to the southern hemisphere............

I thought I was golden here - on the ocean and willing and able to spend vast amounts of time ogling surfers, working on my tan and tracking brides........

I guess I am going to have to come and live with one of you guys......

just remember - I don't cook anymore .......and I don't do housework..........

I do like to drink Port and Brandy and , of course, Malbec

please keep gummie bears out of my field of vision as, apparently, I can't resist those......

and I seem to have a penchant \[please say that with a french accent\] for surfers and men with long lashes and in skinny jeans........

### caroline — 2013-08-30T10:35:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-94318>

It is the most glorious day today - 27C with very clear blue skies...... and it is still winter! Apparently we have had the warmest winter on record for the last 150 years - I think I got that right.

My tummy got lots of sun today - and various other and assorted areas ........

Why is it that only fat guys with no teeth stop to chat??????? I am definitely doing something wrong.....

I am really getting worried about spending long hours in the sun with this hole in the ozone layer. Any ideas? suggestions?

I was assuming that since I don't burn I would be okay .....but maybe that is a false assumption ....maybe I have my head in the sand? literally and figuratively....

Why did I always burn when we lived in Canada and never got much of a tan? We used to go to the Bahamas all the time and I would lay out in the sun for ages and usually burn and and barely get any tan and it was such hard work!

Now I love the sun and heat - can't get enough of it .......so something must be a lot different in my labs. I was finally able to bring my Vit D up \[163 \] a year ago. I thought that was crazy high but Dr. K. was good with it - because of my age he said ....ouch!!!

I think I will get some regular tests done next week and maybe a free T3 too. I am dying to know what my Bun/Creat is.
Can anyone think of what else I should maybe test for?

My glucose seems to be fine - and I don't crave carbs......

There is a relationship between Vit A and Vit D ....am I right about that? what blog was that? - I better go back and read that one. It was the connection we all missed ......and obviously I am still missing.....

I hope everyone is having a great weekend .... when do the kids go back to school?

Audrey.. looks like you are doing so great!

> Why is it that only fat guys with no teeth stop to chat??????? I am definitely doing something wrong.....

LOL

About too much sun. I do think it cannot be wrong to be cautious. Look... you are not an aborgine, they have darker skin, you are not adapted to lots of sun like they are. So to live fair skinned in a country close to equator definitely is different. Even I, in Finland (!) felt it was not good to get too much sun, so if there was many sunny days after another, and I was out every day, I felt I got enough, and tried to limit my sun exposure at midday. You will feel it if you listen to your inner voice, trust me.  You will know when to seek the shade. Do not worry.

### nicld — 2013-08-31T20:42:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-94500>

I have started to notice too that I am able to stand being in the sun a little bit more without burning and I am white as white. I never tan just get a darker shade of pink. For years I was paranoid about being in the sun without sunscreen due to the risk of skin cancer. I had a couple of really bad, bad sunburns in the past and they say that makes me more likely to get skin cancer. Now that I am doing CT, after a long cold session I am CRAVING getting in the sun. I have increase my pinkness but have not really burned yet. I too seem to know when I am done plus rotating like a rotisserie chicken helps too. I can't wait till fall comes. I can stay out way longer without worrying about burning.

School started for some districts last week but got cancelled due to some extreme heat we have had but most start on Tuesday. We have had heat indexes up to 110 and that is way way unusual for where I live but it did make rewarming after CT a little better.

### caroline — 2013-09-01T10:43:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-94583>

Today is the first day of spring...Yahoo!!!

I took one of my new swimsuits and all the resulting boobage out for a spin today.......amazing who I have turned into...

I went to the rockpool to get my C/T in \[altho it is too warm - 18c\] I sat on the rocks for a bit in the sun to dry off and then thought I would go down to the beach. I walked up the stairs and turned to look back down and TA DA ...there was this great looking older guy that had just arrived. He appeared to be wearing boxers - I kept walking but decided to turn around and have another look......sure enough - he had a towel around himself and took off his undies and put speedos on!
I am liking speedos more and more. I just stood there staring at him - I thought - WTH - why not get a good look

But I gotta say - my timing is really off ...I am going to have to work on that.......

So - this is shaping up to be a stellar summer ........

I am trying to work up the courage to ask the life guards if there is a no topless policy. I have gotten to know them over the winter and now feel embarrassed.... you may say that it is a little late to be embarrassed....

I don't know if I have said before ....one day when i was coming to the beach - a bunch of them and their supervisors were standing around having a meeting and as I was passing I made the comment that I had never seen them save anyone. They simply said.... that they don't save Canadians!

### caroline — 2013-09-03T06:36:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-94728>

I have just been to the dentist and then cam to the beach. It is an amazing day. My mouth is really hurting and I am feeling sorry for myself. I am sitting here eating strawberries and chocolate and I am pretty positive that my brandy will not be safe tonite!

I went to the store before coming home and got myself treats.... Blue cheese and jalapeÃ±o cheese and sweet potatoes. I need soft stuff - oh - and I did buy some fish. There was some whiskey smoked salmon but I figured it would be lite on the whiskey and heavy on the sugar...........

It is amazing to me that these are the things that are treats these days.....before it would have been cinnamon buns.....

### caroline — 2013-09-04T07:13:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-94781>

I got an email from a friend in Uzebeckistan today. I had asked him how he is. His reply ....."I am really good....I am flashing as a rat with gold teeth!". I really like that....

### caroline — 2013-09-05T09:17:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-94858>

It is a really gorgeous day ...27C.... perfect blue sky and hardly any wind.

I am pretty comfortable walking around in this barely there swimsuit......and.....the girls were out for awhile! That was one of the challenges I gave myself.......I realise that is pretty lite weight stuff ....but it is pretty huge for me!
Turns out - no biggie........everyone seems to have nips these days.....and who really cares anyway.

After the fact - I asked the lifeguards about topless sunbathing ......they really didn't seem to know what the rules are for sure - but they did think that the girls shouldn't be out between the flags ......fair enuf ....I can live with that
They also said - despite what ever the rules might be - they don't mind a bit! I am positive tho they had in mind all the gorgeous young things hanging about

The hot sun feels so good...and I am hoping my hormones get a bump up.....

It would be totally awesome to swim in the ocean naked.....like I said .... who is this person???

### caroline — 2013-09-05T09:33:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-94860>

I know, I know - you are trying to get us where we need to go and stimulate discussion and make us think and problem solve and just plain step up....

So today, when I was walking to the beach I was so preoccupied, I almost walked into a car...it was parked - but still.....

### caroline — 2013-09-09T07:17:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-95326>

I went for blood work this morning. It turns out it is the same lab as last time (when I had the fall). And then I went to the same grocery store. I was so paranoid - that fall is costing me thousands in dental work. The first think I did was grab a block of chocolate and had eaten almost all of before I got to the checkout. The girl ws laughing at me ... I said - well - last time you had to call an ambulance for me .... She said ....keep eating!

### caroline — 2013-09-24T01:39:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-95858>

It has been one year since the first time I skyped with Dr. K. And what a year it has been ...well almost three years all up.

I am sitting in a tub of ice ....I thought I needed to be in the appropriate location for this!

I remember so vividly reading JK's comments on those other forums - so much has happened since then - all of it mind blowing ....what a ride it has been and continues to be.

All of this has been so intuitive....and I have to say....sometimes when JK uses that word I get really ticked! He will explain something really difficult and then say "now it should be intuitive" and I go ...actually no...no it isn't intuitive - for me anyway!

But - when I first started reading his stuff .. It was very intuitive. I knew that this was the journey I wanted /needed to be on ......and what a spectacular journey it has been and for all the right reasons.

I never thought if all this in a time frame ...this was for life ...and I never had any doubts what so ever. It isn't a lose 50 pounds in 30 days scenario.

This was all about helping myself - not depending on Docs to fix me.

It is tough and exhilarating and mind bending and I wouldn't want it any other way.

Dr. Kruse ....you have made me question everything - although I was never a slouch in this department! You have made me take responsibility for my health ..although I always knew that as well....but you have made me own it.

I now know it is okay to insist on what I want ...no matter if it is in the Doctors office or in a restaurant or just in daily interactions with people in my life.

So many remarkable things have happened to my body and ny spirit and my soul and I can't wait to see what the next years brings for all of us.

Thanks Dr. K and to everyone who participates in this forum....I am so very proud of all of us.....

### caroline — 2013-10-04T09:33:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-96853>

It seems I have turned into a real girl.....Today, at the farmers market - I bought a copper bracelet with a heart on it - I love it. It goes with the magnetic bracelet I bought last week with hearts on it. Then I bought a piece of Rose quartz in the shape of a heart and a sterling silver long chain. I was told that this crystal is about giving and receiving love etc.

I also ordered a copper medallion with the saying in my sigy.......I don't know what has come over me........

This market stall is owned by a lovely young couple and they make all this stuff. They use a lot of old coins and incorporate them in their designs. It made me remember that I have jars and jars of coins that my DH had collected over the years. So I am going to use these coins to make bracelets or necklaces for my grandchildren as a remembrance of their grandfather. It gave me goosebumps and tears talking to her about what we will do.....
