### caroline — 2013-10-06T08:18:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-97000>

It is a gorgeous day today - and has been all week....31c.

I am getting worried about myself .... I have stopped paying attention to guys in speedos.....that is not good! I need to stop reading and do more ogling.....got to get back to the eye candy...

I have a new T-shirt today.....it is lime green and has a huge heart shape in silver studs .....and it says ....LOVE BARE....

I really don't know who this person is anymore.....But I like her....the old me was okay too...but I am not quite as quiet and I feel great in my own skin .....that has never happened before.....I wonder what is next on this journey of a lifetime.....

I was so stressed out yesterday about the new blog ....how Can I Possibly think that I could get all the detail.....I always think if I try hard enuf....

Honestly - how dopey.....I had a pounding headache, sick to my stomach and stressed to the max.....I am not doing that again anytime soon....

From now on I am just going to lay at the beach ....perving at guys - no matter what age.....so you guys are going to have to give me a synopsis of the blogs.....

### caroline — 2013-10-06T08:33:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-97001>

I just realized - it is almost 5:00 pm and the only thing I have had today is 2 BPC.....and I am not hungry in the least.

Sunshine and the ocean does amazing things!

However - for some reason I have ants crawling all over me ..... Not a good look....

### caroline — 2013-10-07T05:59:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-97142>

I seem to have a new man in my life.......the most adorable little 5 year old boy. I bought myself a grilled calamari salad and was sitting on a bench at the yacht basin. This gorgeous little guy rides up on his scooter and wanted to know my name and what I was doing...I told him I was reading Jack's Blog. He kept saying Bye and riding off on his scooter - and that he would be back and letting me know where he was going. I did check where his Mom was.

Every couple of minutes he would come back and he would sit down beside me and we would chat some more. He asked me why I was alone ...and then asked where my husband was. I wasn't sure what to say ...I finally said - he went to Heaven..... so he says "today" and I said no - awhile ago ....this little guy was so sad for me.

But the best thing ever - he asked me to take my sun glasses off and so I did - and asked him why? He said "I want to see your eyes" how intuitive - it blew me away.

I told him I don't normally wear sunglasses but they are reading glasses - that is why I was wearing them....and so he said "it's okay then - you can put them back on"

He told me about his baby sister ..and I asked him if she was a cutie pie ....he didn't understand ...he said a pie is something you eat.

He asked me about my banged up knee - it has huge, thick scabs on it. I told him I fell so he wanted to know why. I said that I wasn't being careful and was preoccupied and thinking about stuff. He wanted to know what I was thinking about and I told him ...Quantum physics.....

He wanted me to play in the sand with him and asked when I would come back to the beach so he could see me again.

He had a really lovely Mom and aunties and an adorable baby sister....

He had trouble saying my name - so I said he could call me Caroline because that is what my DH called me - that made him happy.

So - the only men I attract are fat men with no teeth .....and 5 year old boys............

there must be a message in that .........

No clue what the universe is trying to tell me ..... I need a decoder ring.........

### caroline — 2013-10-13T07:48:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-97686>

I was thinking this morning that I haven't had any silly beach stories lately ..... no tripping over brides ...no photographers to berate me etc. etc. still lots of fat guys with no teeth .......

Why do some guys wear budgie smugglers? they truly look like they are smuggling budgies ...don't they look in a mirror??

Anyway I digress.....

Today it was very hot - 36C + ...... after about 30 minutes I started feeling really crappy - my heart was pounding out of my chest, nauseated and weak so I thought I had better get out of the sun. There is a cafe so I asked if I could sit in the cool and have a bottle of water - btw it wasn't imported. I just couldn't get feeling better - in fact I was feeling worse and worse and really didn't know what to do. The young man checked on me a few times\[didn't want me to die on the premises\] and then a young girl came over and told me I had sun stroke and she brought me a glass of ice. The young man came back again to check on me and I finally had to realise I needed help and also go to the bathroom. He said just leave my stuff and he would walk me to the toilet. I stood up and just then my bowels evacuated totally.
I looked at the floor, He looked at the floor ...we looked at each other.....talk about wanting the ground to swallow me up

Anyway he was truly lovely and said don't worry about it and helped wrap my towel around me. He walked me to the toilet and then went to get a lifeguard.

It turns out I was their first rescue of the season!! cool......Here I am covered in poop - smell horrible - feel horrible - flies are having a field day on my body ....not my finest hour.......

and - to add insult to injury....One of the nicest looking men I have seen in a long time walked by........

I hope y'all have as entertaining a Sunday afternoon as I have had - you might have to work at it some to top mine...

### caroline — 2013-10-24T10:40:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-98821>

I had the most interesting conversation today with a lovely young woman ... she is a hairdresser where I go.

In all this time - years - I have never realised that she has exotic birds. She has 6 in her house in a really tall separated birdcage that reaches to the ceiling..and then she has an aviary in her back yard with about thirty more.

when they breed she hand raises them .....feeding every 4 hours etc. etc.

All of this is so fascinating ....the care she gives them is unbelievable. There is this quality of caring and kindness That took my breathe away. If they are sick she doesn't leave their side and she has a remarkable vet that she can leave them with and he will take them home so they get 24/7 care. He charges her next to nothing and she is always taking him a bottle of wine They both rescue pets in distress.

I asked her what they \[birds\] ate .... we have had food conversations before.... and she said you know that I eat very healthy and that is how I feed my birds. She buys them fruit and veg.

She knows all about them - how their digestion works, how their body works and on and on. She knows what to do when they are sick.

They need to be socialised and so she takes them out - 2 at a time - on her shoulder when she goes to Bunnings \[large building supply store\]

When she takes them out like that - she puts a jet pack on them - sort of like a body suit......

They talk and are house trained and will ask for paper - to go to the bathroom!!! They live for 60 years or longer.....

I was blown away by all of this. She also makes all kinds of bird stuff and sells it on line....

She is 26 years old ...she bought a house a few years ago and her and her partner are renovating it by themselves.

She grows vegetables and cooks from scratch always - never eats out....

This is one vital, amazing, instinctive young woman....

### caroline — 2013-10-24T10:46:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-98822>

ran across a saying today that I love.....

Chocolate Men, Coffee
some things are better rich!

and my friend told me this one....

Women say that they can multi task ..... but they certainly can't.....because they can't have a headache and sex at the same time...

### caroline — 2013-11-01T09:01:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-99618>

When I was at the ocean yesterday ...I was paying more attention ... well - to things other than hot surfers!

All along the seawall there are many, many conifer trees ..... and a lot of grassy areas and rocks.

This is a heritage area and I was looking at all the photos again yesterday .....dating back to the mid 1850's. There was hardly any trees then. That surprised me - I kind of thought there would have been more back then.

I also was surprised to learn that the beaches were segregated back then! The women wore quite formal long gowns when going to the beach....and for the guys it was "neck to knee" .... no buggy smugglers back then... LOL...so sad...

The scenery is much better these days ...and the trees are massive and gorgeous.....no wonder there is so much positive energy here! I will have to take a picture one of these days...

### Lava — 2013-11-01T16:30:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-99636>

I think we should trade homes for a week or so...LOL Your area sounds so wonderful and beautiful...on the side of Big Island where I live we really don't have a "beach" to walk along..Lot's of lava. rough on the tootsies. Have a great day!!!!

I'm with Lava...we ought to do a swap. I've never been to Australia, but it's on my bucket list.

### caroline — 2013-11-08T07:41:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-100171>

I just have a few ramblings today....

I met my neighbour at the farmer's market this am. She told me I was so lovely and thin - How do I do it - I must eat hardly anything!!!

I told her all the usual stuff ....epi-paleo etc....."you don't eat bread?? you don't eat fruit???? I could never do that - I would die .....I would sooner be fat!!"

I was at the optometrist ordering new glasses and we were chatting ...as women do...and she asked me the same thing - so I told her the usual.

She tries so hard - exercises every day - really watches what she eats etc. and she can't get rid of the big spare tire around her middle. She even eats a lot of fish - impressive - and can't understand why she can't lose weight.

She asked me what I eat etc. and I told her about kettle bells....She was very receptive - I gave her some websites...

She asked me what I eat for breakfast - I told her. I asked her what she eats for breakfast.... very, very healthy she says. She eats yogurt and muesli and fruit.....

### nonchalant — 2013-11-08T15:37:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-100186>

And when that optometrist visits her doctor and complains about her weight, the doctor will just assume she is lying about what she eats, because everyone knows fruit, lowfat dairy, and healthywholegrains are what she needs!

But that medical skepticism is what it took for me to start to realize that the answer is elsewhere.

### nicld — 2013-11-08T16:41:41+0200

I get told that too when I say that I don't eat any wheat products. It is alot easier then people think isn't it

### caroline — 2013-11-09T04:19:16+0200

### sooperb — 2013-11-09T16:09:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-100262>

I think people's resistance is a composite of having been conditioned by years of misleading nutritional advice, not actually being ill but not recognising that their issues, whatever they are, can be caused in part or whole by their diet and/or lifestyle. We are conditioned to expect a pill for every ill, to agree mutely with everything we are told by doctors and to follow blindly. It's only when you question that you start to find the answers, when you become proactive in your own health equation that the scales fall from your eyes and you begin to see that you have been misled, lied to and manipulated by big business which is hand in hand with government. Even when you begin to realise and understand, breaking out and setting yourself apart from the mainstream is never easy, it opens you up to ridicule, criticism, disbelief and pillorying that you might never have expected and find hard to combat. It doesn't surprise me that although awareness is being raised that some prefer the path of least resistance.

I am in a very small forum where a member with type ll diabetes who smokes and has poor cardiac health even following surgery, talks about his large bars of chocolate, eating most of his meals (along with his wife who also has poor health) in supermarket cafes, another who routinely eats out and does so with the children in MacDonalds, KFC and other such places, another who is losing weight by calorie counting and won't countenance other than skimmed milk and crap margarine etc. and readily admits she does it because she's scared if she eats any other way the weight will fall from heaven onto her body as punishment lol because that's what the diet club tells her. I am a convert to the better path and I feel like a religious missionary as I regularly try to "educate" them which amuses them greatly. Not one of them has said "Wow, that sounds worth a try", they are all happy to remain blinkered and continue slowly ruining their health. I honestly think that goes for the vast majority. Until our government health gurus become switched on to what they should be telling people nothing will change.

Omg, who put that soapbox there lol?

### caroline — 2013-11-10T00:04:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-100293>

I love it when you are on a soapbox sooperb!! very well said ...... you are always so articulate.

I seem to sense more resistance these days ... or have people give up? How can someone say to you that they could never give up bread - that would sooner be fat? and yet they are very envious of someone else.

everyone is becoming so damaged.....it blows me away to see a fat Mom and her fat children in a food court eating french fries and KFC and drinking a coke. I feel so icky just around all those smells. I can't understand how you wouldn't want so much more for your children....isn't that ingrained in us???

Why are we different?

### endless — 2013-11-11T06:00:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-100383>

Addiction is very powerful...I've given in to the wheat addiction a few times and then been shocked at myself afterwards. Imagine being that fat mom in the food court and not knowing how to make changes, or feeling like it's out of reach. We are in the minority here, it's hard to remember that MOST people don't know anything and often don't have the circumstances they need making them uncomfortable ENOUGH to make a bold move. I think many of the people that wander in here do so because they are desperate, they are finally willing to try something different....a lot of people just accommodate their worsening health, like a frog in a pot of water. They don't notice it getting hotter and hotter until it's too late. We are the lucky ones who jumped out of the pot!

### caroline — 2013-11-11T06:10:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-100388>

when we know better ..... we will do better

### sooperb — 2013-11-11T14:21:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-100417>

Thanks for the compliment Audrey, mucho appreciated.

I love the analogy, I reckon there are plenty of frogs that just sit there until they are done for

I see many people who are grossly overweight and I never cease to wonder why they aren't doing something, anything to change their lives for the better. We have a chap in our village, he was a policeman, a traffic officer. He is enormous now but has always been fat. The same man used to run the boy scouts, imagine what a message he was sending to the lads. He gave up when he could no longer physically manage it. He is under the GP's care but he's no thinner than he was years ago. He can't walk even a 100 yards without breaking out in a sweat and panting. He has a wife who is podgy but not that fat and 2 now grown up children, the son is built like a racing snake so he must have left home lol but the daughter is shaping up to be like her father so that's at least another fat adult in the making. I know you should change yourself first because that makes the message much clearer but I can't sit and watch my children slowly swell without trying to make them aware of how bad it is for them. How do you make them listen? (Kicks soapbox to one side and sneaks out lol)

### veelansu — 2013-11-11T19:56:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-100439>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=100417)
>
> I see many people who are grossly overweight and I never cease to wonder why they aren't doing something, anything to change their lives for the better.

I know what you are saying. My whole family, except my dad, on both sides have been obese. I was a chubby kid, but in 6th grade someone called me fat, and I vowed that day to never let myself be fat. I would jump rope and exercise every day as a kid. my mom though I was going to be anorexic but I was just healthy. I was never one for crappy food. I loved veggies, and meats, but I did have a sweet tooth. I also could not understand how people could let themselves go, but then...it happened that I started to gain and could not lose, no matter what I did, it was not for a lack of trying. Some of these folks just don't know enough to do something about it, like endless said. It is not that easy to jump out of the pot. We are the lucky ones that have taken the extra effort to find our way here. Yes, there are those that don't care, but I think that is a minority. Financial and other life pressures keep folks from being healthy until they are faced with possible death. It's crazy but death is a pretty good motivator for most.

### caroline — 2013-11-15T08:45:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-100764>

After re reading C/T #7 yesterday - I am going to start the leptin r/x again and the C/T protocol again. I am going back to basics.

I have been hating C/T lately for some reason - maybe because I can't get the water cold enough.

I am also noticing that the sun is burning my skin. When I sit on my balcony at 9:00 am. It feels as if the sun is burning me - I don't understand that. My face got burned the other day when I was out - it was only 22C and I covered it pretty quickly. My face isn't getting brown altho the rest of me is. Does this all mean that my biology/physiology has changed?

I was talking to my Daughter in Canada this morning and she was telling me she is going up north with a friend to a place that has an ice pool and hot pool. Her friend had been to one in Quebec and loved it. She is looking forward to it! When I first told her about it - I think she thought I was mental....as daughters do....LOL

She asked me if I was still doing ice baths and I had to admit that I am a wuss lately ... that was embarrassing!

In C/T 6 and C/T 7 Dr. K. really lays it al out

### sooperb — 2013-11-22T11:46:45+0200

I eat very little fruit these days compared to when I used to start every day with a full bowl topped with Greek yoghurt! We still have that about once a week, composed mostly of berries, maybe an orange, an apple and a banana (that's between us) avocado, grapes and whatever else we have. I add cinnamon and coconut oil too. My downfall is having a coffee while "out", resisting the cakes is hard. I've started having a toasted tea cake instead but that's no better really because of the wheat but hopefully less sugar.

### caroline — 2013-11-23T01:33:53+0200

I look forward to your journal Sooperb ...you are a very articulate and interesting woman!

### caroline — 2013-11-23T07:38:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-101445>

This a silly little story about thinking outside the box .....and only for girls .....

I am going to a big party this week and bought myself a lovely new outfit. As all women know ... Lovely new outfits often need lovely new underwear. It is definitely true!

The top of this outfit is rather low cut ....so I spent hours looking thru what I have and then hours in a department store trying on bra after bra after stupid bra! I hate bras anyway and at the best of times the words bra and comfortable cannot be used in the same sentence - for me anyway.

I usually shop online at Shopbop and I was looking thru their selection and a bandeau caught my attention ... So I went and read the reviews. One woman said that she loved it but found it worked much better if she wore it ...upside down!

I thought to myself .... If that isn't thinking outside the box - I don't know what the heck is!

Sooooo - now - I am going to wear my shape wear back to front ....TA DA.... Problem solved.....

### Inger — 2013-11-23T09:55:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-101457>

Lol... that's cool! I am like you... I hate wearing bra too! Always have. I never wear at home... but I passed the pencil test so maybe I do not need to?

Now we need a picture of you in that new beautiful dress......

### caroline — 2013-11-23T10:43:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-101463>

I don't think I could pass the pencil test Inger....LOL actually I just tried ....and I can!! .....great news!

### caroline — 2013-12-01T07:00:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-101931>

I seem to have survived three very late nights this week .... barely. It is fun but I don't like how it makes me feel.

I wound up having I glass of a delicious red wine and one half a glass of bubbles - I was more than happy with that - and lots of water.

It is amazing to me how my mindset has completely changed ..... I don't feel like I am missing out at all ...on the contrary - I love being in control and make my own choices ..... everyone knows not to mess with me!

I wound up staying over at a friends - I would usually bring coconut oil with me .... so I asked for butter for my coffee. She was pretty surprised and asked if it was a Canadian "thing". Friends really do become concerned over the high fat and medium protein thing .... I try to not even go there now.

I did become mixed up with a bit of cake ..... a good friend is an exceptional pastry chef and she had made the 50th wedding anniversary cake. There was three different cakes and I did have a big bite of two of them. Ugh! I hate how I feel after sugar.....very lethargic and yet wired in a bad way. I lose clear thinking and feel simply awful. Absolutely, and no way, is it worth it too me. You certainly lose your edge.

I am amazed at how I now feel the chaotic affects of EMF ... and the wonderful power of the right fuel for my body. The power of electron dense food and sunlight and grounding. I want it, I need it, I love it!

I was sitting next to a woman who is five years older than me .......and men are always hitting on her. She is quite wrinkly and her boobs are always hanging out - and she does have a flamboyant personality. Her son came to her house the other day and found her up in a tree - cutting off the top branches!. She has been known to take an axe to a wall - to later find out it was a retaining wall! It is funny tho - she is quite self centered..... no accounting for men's taste I guess - or it is only about big boobs?

And then there is me ....... I really have gotten too intense ....I think I scare people. Someone I hadn't seen in a couple of years asked me twice if I was okay. I guess when you change sizes people think you might have cancer or something....
So - as people age they just keep getting fatter and out of shape and unhealthy........and that is more acceptable....normal it seems.....

George Clooney wasn't there ...... Altho a lady I was sitting beside said she actually ran into him at an airport in the U.S. and he had to grab her shoulders so she didn't fall over.......

### caroline — 2013-12-01T08:44:06+0200

### Inger — 2013-12-01T10:18:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-101938>

Sounds like you had fun  It is fun to be with people! I never touch cakes tho. I think I would lose credibility and why would I want to suffer..lol especially when I am with others I want to stay clear and feeling great or I have the feeling I miss out of something... I do take some alcohol at times but I kind of have gotten so sensitive to it lately as I drink so very seldom that I don't enjoy too much how it makes me feel just after a few sips..lol maybe with a lover or a very close friend it would be different...?
Too sad George Clooney was not there..... any other magic males? I met the fisherman again a few days ago when shopping. He is special. We talked for quite a while at the parking lot. He has such an energy... it feeds me! Always makes me crazy happy to talk to him. I bet he has this connection to nature... being out on the sea with his boat fishing year round. I am in love with him. But not in the way I need to possess him..lol he has a beautiful wife and kids! But in a very... special way, IDK how to describe? It is the same way like I am in love with the sky and the moon and the wind.. the same magic.

Any children on the party? Children are so magic too....

I think... with boobs.. some men like big boobs and some likes small boobs..lol I have small. When I have talked with men about this issue it really seems some do not like those big boobs at all. Rather big butts..lol

### sooperb — 2013-12-01T11:29:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-101945>

I have had big boobs nearly all my life, I don't like them much at all. As a bloke pulling device, who wants to think your bosoms are doing the pulling and your head could be empty it wouldn't matter? Unfortunately OH is a boob man! I feel embarrassed by all these "stars" who constantly have them on display, the Kardashian clan, Nicky Minaj, Rihanna and countless others, why do they feel the need to do that? Is it poor self esteem somehow. Be proud of your body but really, keep it covered lol.

Cake, I'm doing better and now when I have some I often get indigestion which is a useful reminder of how bad it is. Alcohol I can pretty much avoid, and like Inger, I much prefer to be in control when socialising. I am usually the only sober person there and it's educational watching the other guests slowly slipping into slurred speech, muddled thinking, giggles etc. I find that a bit embarrassing too.

### caroline — 2013-12-01T11:40:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-101947>

Ohhhhhhh ..... big butts????? ........ I really lose out then! never had a big butt and never will I guess! pretty happy about that tho. I would have to give away all my skinny jeans!  I do love my skinny jeans ...LOL

Pretty funny about the big boobs tho...... I am not lacking in that department - but when one is wearing shapewear ....... I think it would be like unwrapping a present...........

I thought all guys like big boobs? I realise - I have never had that discussion......Maybe we should take a poll??? I don't know if that is true .......guys can't keep their eyes off big boobs as far as i can see!

Inger - there was lots of children at the party .... my grandsons included. Children are magic - they have so much fun and unlimited energy!
There was a bush band playing and I was doing some barn dances with my grandsons. There was a chubby young man - he had so much musicality and enthusiasm and very lite on his feet - very surprising.

There was also a pipe band ...... they were wonderful. It was really amazing when they played Ole Lang Szine and everyone forms a circle and holds hands.

Nope .... no magic males....... I think they are in very limited supply .....

Maybe one was all I am entitled to.........I shouldn't be greedy

I just finished reading your journal! Wow! I love reading about your encounters with people you've never met before! You're so lucky you can go to the beach everyday!! Are you a chef??? What did you do in Tibet? How exciting

### caroline — 2013-12-08T07:11:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-102807>

I can't believe that you read my journal of silliness! It started off serious but kinda disintegrated pretty quickly!

I travelled in Tibet for a month with a small group - 4 wheel drives and Tibetan guides. It was the trip of a lifetime for me.
We went to Everest base camp etc. etc. no tourist infa structure, no epi paleo, no electricity, no running water, no heat, no toilets of any kind only bushes. I had the time of my life.....

I yearn to visit such a place for it's panoramic beauty, the stillness and a view of heaven on earth. Big sigh. I didn't bookmark it, how remiss was that!

### caroline — 2013-12-18T00:59:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-103699>

I have been discovering how hard it is to change things/ routines sometimes - they are like a security blanket.

I finally made myself go to the ocean early in the morning last week - I have been wanting to do this for ever and it just didn't happen .... so silly, ridiculous, and just plain stupid and absolute nonsense - \[to coin a phrase from someone we all know and love!\] - It certainly isn't a hardship .... That is the understatement of the century.

It is amazing to see the sun come up over the ocean.

I love seeing all the people just taking it all in - enjoying the present moment - basking in it really.

There is such a positive mood - everyone chatting with friends and laughing. It is all so dialed down and yet so very dialed up on life.

Now - I can't not do this every single day - what took me so long.....

It is still hard on your own - believe it or not - I am really pretty quiet ... but I do have nice chats and it will get better!

all these folks who come out to swim so early seem much healthier to me. They have such a positive, relaxed presence.

I don't know why it is so hard to make changes sometimes and get out of one's comfort zone ..... I jumped into all of this with both feet and never look back!

ice baths - okey dokey - I can and will do that ..... no problem.....

I've been thinking of you....it's winter here soon and you are enjoying the heat of summer eh? I assume your tan must be amazing. Have not been here - so busy with travel and desperately trying to find a better place for us to live...but I"m thinking of you.

### endless — 2013-12-19T06:19:32+0200

### caroline — 2013-12-21T07:35:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-103952>

It is the most glorious day today at the ocean - but very windy. I was laying down sunning myself ... I took the straps of my swim suit down so as not to have tan lines.

I was just laying there day dreaming - probably of hot surfers ..... Or maybe thinking about QED - I dunno ....take your pick

Anyway - I looked down and it seems the wind had blown my swimsuit all the way down! The girls got a nice bit of sun and ....I am laying between the flags!

I would have Been a tiny bit embarrassed if a lifeguard had come over and told me to cover up! C'est La Vie!

My life sure has taken some remarkable twists and turns these last couple of years.

I wore a sundress out to lunch yesterday ..... I have never even owned a sundress before. That is just a really little thing I know ..... But it is huge for me..

Quantum magic is all around me ..... Absolutely no question about that....

I was buying a dress today for a garden party... All the staff and some customers. became involved in my choice. What fun. I haven't worn a dress in 20 years!

These are such simple little things.....but I have the hugest grin on my face ..... And the dress is an xsmall - I really can't wipe the grin off my face. It all seems kind of trite I guess ....but it is a measurement for me of how far I have come.

I couldn't have done it without JK's inspiration and gentle prodding ( did I just say gentle?? More like a freight train at times!)... I know I did the work - but it hasn't seemed like work in the least .....it has been a hugely rewarding and fun journey and we have so much more to look forward to....

Bring it on.....
.

Don't forget to dance as if no one is watching....

Merry Christmas. xo

### caroline — 2013-12-21T23:48:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-103995>

Honestly - this quantum magic is contagious .... it is like serious euphoria - everybody becomes entangled - the space you are in literally changes. The positive energy is palpable. Everyone sharing electrons, laughing, smiling and the most important thing ..... fully engaged and sucking up life.

I wish I could say that my life is perfect .....that is nonsense \[I will say it before Dr. K. does - lol\] - nobodies life is perfect.
I have decided to take a stand this Christmas .... it probably won't go well.... but how I feel about myself is more important and I need to be treated with respect and love and kindness.

I will probably spend Christmas alone - but that is way better than being invisible .....

I am going to have the most perfect picnic at the beach .....and just take in all the energy and beauty - and wind up in a much better place.... I am looking forward to it.

### nonchalant — 2013-12-22T03:04:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-104017>

\*hug\*

I bought some white aged Stilton with cranberries - it is a probable no no .... But I couldn't resist the red wax it is covered in! So it will be coming to my picnic.....and some cherries..... And some banana bread maybe....

### Inger — 2013-12-22T11:19:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-104032>

I love summer dresses too Audrey! I use them all summer long, short feminine cotton dresses and often just bikini for underwear so when I get out doing work in the yard or picking berries around the neighborhood I just pull my dress off and get sun on my body - very easy and practical. You need to post a pic!

I need to come down there and learn you fishing! Oh man... that would be so much fun... To be honest I would rather be alone Christmas too. The more free I become in my head the more weird family appears to me.. even when they sing songs for Christmas and otherwise it is in a stiff... weird way without joy or compassion.. makes me suffer instead of enjoying it - it is like all have to be dead? Because that is how their religion is and everything has to be done like it has always be done, from clothing to food to music... all weird rules without reason (so it appears to me at least) i can tell you honestly it is not easy for me! Christmas is also about gorging on foods here and feel not well after. What a waste of time. Why do not use the time creative and figure out solution for issues together instead? That would be so much fun! Because there are so many unresolved issues... I do love my family but something is happening inside me.. a weird feeling that i am letting it go. Maybe my real family are people that want to evolve? Maybe relatives must not mean that much at all. Since birth I have been a crazy "family holds together" girl... but it is loosing its grasp on me. The weirdest thing is, I kind of like it?! IDK where I am going... but interesting it is - because something is happening.

and Audrey.. talking about oxy..
I woke up early with boiling hormones once again today, huh! I dreamed I was close to a young man.. his body was so manly and strong and there was this magic "energy share" between us. we just stood there and hugged, without moving or kissing or anything, he was in the military so had clothes on and so had I. But the energy was so strong it made us not want to move. And it increased and increased... I could hear by his breath he felt the same... the energy became so strong it woke me up, darn!
But nice.. so nice  IDK why I have those dreams so often lately? I am not complaining

### sooperb — 2013-12-22T17:19:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-104041>

I have no time for religion Inger, I can't see how to belong you have to follow a set of rules and behaviours dreamt up by someone who then exacts often terrible punishment if you don't! A lot expect that you pay to belong too, should that be a prerequisite to worship any god? I do believe that the Bible's teachings were often founded on some common sense such as those relating to foods, I also believe in the idea of a church serving the community and that we should all help each other, that it's our Christian duty, but worshipping a deity, hmmm, I can't get into that. As for relatives, well, we don't get to choose them do we but as with children, we don't have to love them or they us, neither should we hurt them either. Oh dear, sorry, soapbox again lol.

sooperb - Thanks. Your one phrase ...."neither should we hurt them either" That is the crux of my dilemma. Some days I feel so hurt and want to lash out ... but that isn't the right thing to do - so I wind up saying very little. But - suffering in silence is nonsense too!

### endless — 2013-12-22T23:23:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-104074>

I think Inger is onto something....it's painful to evolve away from people you are emotionally attached to and don't want to hurt or lose, but at the same time the desire to live authentically is overpowering and must be acted upon for one's own best interest.  Sometimes presents a bit of a dilemma....

I think your picnic plan sounds wonderful!

### caroline — 2013-12-22T23:23:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-104075>

Inger .... fishing would be fun!

I do feel much calmer and not all tied up in knots ......I will suck up every bit of energy at the beach.

It is very hard now to be around people that don't want to or can't evolve. I always feel very calm but very full of life/joy except when I am around people like this .....I feel the need to protect myself from all this negative energy and I grow very silent and introspective ..... and have to get away. Being by myself is infinitely better and then you feel the true energy of others.

People with energy are drawn together in so many weird ways ..... I love that so much.

It is hard to get past the notion that family should treat you like gold, be there for you, love you unconditionally ......but where is that written??? That is a nonsensical, distorted view of life that I have to get over.

Nice dreaming Inger.......

### caroline — 2013-12-22T23:37:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-104077>

I think you hit the nail on the head Krista! life is a dilemma, wrapped up in an engigma etc. I think Jack just said that somewhere.
"to act in one's own best interest" I definitely have to do that more.... sometimes seems selfish to me ..... but then the power of self preservation kicks in big time and then I go WTF - this is my life, my journey and I need to do it "my way"
cue Frank Sinatra in the background...

I think this forum is made up of a wonderful quilt of people ...... all on our own personal, special, amazing journey of a lifetime.

this has been a great year of learning and interaction and introspection and inspiration ....

Thanks everyone ...

I use them all summer long, short feminine cotton dresses and often just bikini for underwear so when I get out doing work in the yard or picking berries around the neighborhood I just pull my dress off and get sun on my body - very easy and practical. You need to post a pic!
>
> I need to come down there and learn you fishing! Oh man... that would be so much fun... To be honest I would rather be alone Christmas too. The more free I become in my head the more weird family appears to me.. even when they sing songs for Christmas and otherwise it is in a stiff... weird way without joy or compassion.. makes me suffer instead of enjoying it - it is like all have to be dead? Because that is how their religion is and everything has to be done like it has always be done, from clothing to food to music... all weird rules without reason (so it appears to me at least) i can tell you honestly it is not easy for me! Christmas is also about gorging on foods here and feel not well after. What a waste of time. Why do not use the time creative and figure out solution for issues together instead? That would be so much fun! Because there are so many unresolved issues... I do love my family but something is happening inside me.. a weird feeling that i am letting it go. Maybe my real family are people that want to evolve? Maybe relatives must not mean that much at all. Since birth I have been a crazy "family holds together" girl... but it is loosing its grasp on me. The weirdest thing is, I kind of like it?! IDK where I am going... but interesting it is - because something is happening.
>
> and Audrey.. talking about oxy..
> I woke up early with boiling hormones once again today, huh! I dreamed I was close to a young man.. his body was so manly and strong and there was this magic "energy share" between us. we just stood there and hugged, without moving or kissing or anything, he was in the military so had clothes on and so had I. But the energy was so strong it made us not want to move. And it increased and increased... I could hear by his breath he felt the same... the energy became so strong it woke me up, darn!
> But nice.. so nice  IDK why I have those dreams so often lately? I am not complaining

Oh Inger, what a nice dream. Not to rub it in but that's how I feel sometimes when I am with the hubby. I tell him that I need an "electron share" and he just laughs but I think it is real and I do feel something. I have not had much time with him the past couple of days so I think there will be some energy sharing tonight.

### caroline — 2013-12-23T02:16:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-104095>

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=104086)
>
> Couldnt agree with you more Audrey

### caroline — 2013-12-28T06:57:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-104332>

We have been having really beautiful days here. It is so great to see all the children running and playing and building sand castles and sucking up Life ... Nobody does it better.

I was walKing to the beach early this morning and Started chatting to this old Rusian man walking his dog. We continued walking together towards the ocean. About 10 minutes in he said he might have to marry me. We continued walking and talking and then he said something about kissing me. He had a pretty thick accent so I was sure I hadn't heard that right!

He kept asking me how long I was going to be ..... He had showed me where he parks his car and was telling me a different way to get home and said he would show me later.

I was really over this so I turned to go down to the beach ... And I turned to say goodbye ...and here he is all puckered up.
I go ... You must be kidding me ... I am not kissing you ...WTF...

The only action I ever get is fat men with no teeth and now a 79 year old Russian with a wife .....double WTF!

Now I need to know ..... How do I get quantum unentangled???

He knows more or less where I live .....I will have to go out Much earlier now.

I have to double up on the gorgeous surfers now to erase those puckered lips... Triple WTF.....

I Need a hot dream like Inger has.....
.

### sooperb — 2013-12-28T12:21:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-104345>

Audrey I wonder if you are now giving off some sort of aura that attracts needy people to you? I have experienced something similar, being attracted to one or two people. One was an alternative practitioner at a weekend retreat I went to. I'm happily married but I would have surrendered to him if he'd offered lol. I was really drawn to him, he had a gentle nature and just made me want to be close to him, moved me to tears in fact. I am never attracted to other men usually.

So, maybe you give off the same vibe now, you resonate with health and well being and self assurance? Younger people probably don't recognise that sort of signal, they are full of self confidence and self assurance. It's only after you've lived a life and battled through some hard times and experiences that you start to understand that there must be a better way.

### prAna303 — 2013-12-28T16:28:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-104348>

> So, maybe you give off the same vibe now, you resonate with health and well being and self assurance? Younger people probably don't recognise that sort of signal, they are full of self confidence and self assurance. It's only after you've lived a life and battled through some hard times and experiences that you start to understand that there must be a better way.

Feeling better, living a more analog life, ending the constant thinking about illness makes a person more living... This is what they feel, they see that this person in front of you is not sick compared to the others around, this has many names, Qi or prana or simply... A LIVING HUMAN that radiate energy.

### caroline — 2013-12-28T22:09:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-104367>

"A Living Human who radiates energy" I love that Peter! That is exactly right. I often do find that young men do stop to chat - they seem to appreciate the energy.

Older men often have their eyes cast downward and are not giving off any signals of life!

except the fit people I come across every morning now - swimming .......they definitely radiate energy. There is a kindness and joy and life force....

It is extremely attractive ...... nothing to do with the exterior tho.

Peter - I am realising that I have always watched people's gait. I have been wondering for years ...... why does a young boy have the same gait as his Father? I guess I might not ordinarily notice unless it is quite a peculiar gait .... and then to see a young son the same......

So many people walk around here - there is miles and miles of walking along the ocean ....... and I notice so much about a person by how they walk. My gait is certainly much healthier!

### prAna303 — 2013-12-28T22:29:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-104369>

> why does a young boy have the same gait as his Father?

So many things that do that... Genes to some degree but i think the social factors and the environment plays a bigger role. I mean, if you have a dad that is ill and lumping around you watch and learn, and repeat. Today i see many children forced into a living exactly the same as their parents, we watch and copy the gait.

I think todays world of sitting and over use of computers, iPhones and iPads makes us start to loose a lot of senses from our motion, we do simply not know how to move around natural anymore...

I was really drawn to him, he had a gentle nature and just made me want to be close to him, moved me to tears in fact. I am never attracted to other men usually.
> So, maybe you give off the same vibe now, you resonate with health and well being and self assurance? Younger people probably don't recognise that sort of signal, they are full of self confidence and self assurance. It's only after you've lived a life and battled through some hard times and experiences that you start to understand that there must be a better way.

I definitely have a different aura now ..... maybe a calmness and peacefullness but definitely a lot of energy under the surface bubbling away. I think I am even more quiet now tho ...... I am really content to just sit and stare at the waves/ocean. My dream job!!! as long as you throw in a surfer or two!

Sooperb ... this old man was not needy - he was a very confident man actually...

I always remember Jack saying to someone - one day you will meet someone and you will realise who you were waiting for all along. Lightning has struck me once ... but will it strike again ....

I am old enough to know better than to never say never ..... everyday is a present waiting to be opened and then enjoyed....

It is funny how some people affect us .... a quiet and kind and confident nature would get me every time. It has only happened very rarely ..... one was a Japanese man in an elevator in China - how weird is that!

### caroline — 2013-12-31T03:55:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-104491>

I am having the most awesome day at the beach this Mew Years Eve. I was In the pool early this morning and a woman I had spoken to yesterday asked me to tell her again about C/T.

She had told me yesterday that she always comes to the pool and also does water exercises for her really bad arthritis.. I had been telling her how my pain is all gone.

She said she had been thinking about what I said ....and her pain is not better at all - so she wanted to know more!

I started telling her about giving up grains etc. Another woman I had just started talking to chimed in . and said oh yes ....you need to eat paleo . I spent the next couple of hours having coffee and chatting about epi paleo.

Happy New Years everyone!

### caroline — 2014-01-02T04:51:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-104585>

I did something New Year's eve that I thought I would never do. I went to E Harmony.....

A really good friend has been trying to convince me to do this for 2 years .... so I finally did.

This is extremely out of character for me ...... I have always said I would never, ever do that - I could not do that out of respect ....

It actually was kind of neat to fill out the questionnaire ......It makes you focus on what you are all about.
I was very proud to say that I am very healthy, slim, energetic, focused, love to read QM etc. etc. etc. I don't look too bad on paper ...lol

So yesterday - my inbox is full of all this junk from eharmony .....holy cow!

And now I have come to realise why I never did this before ...... you have to deal with all this stuff - religion, music taste,
food taste and on and on and on ...... and what happens if they don't like QM?????

This is pretty darn unnatural I think ...... anyway - it is not going to happen.

It all made me feel really bad yesterday - well - feeling sorry for myself .... so I kind of went off the rails. A bunch of yogurt and mangoes and peaches and cream and chocolate and almonds and smoked salmon and cheese and then all washed down with some Malbec ...tres stupid!

I can't believe what a funk I was in on the first day of 2014 .... I had all these great plans to get my life on track. All these positive thoughts really went down the tubes ..... but only briefly - thank goodness!

This morning - when I went on line ... I had all these wonderful New Year greetings. There is one greeting that is extremely special/spectacular to me - and he will know who he is. I will never, ever forget that greeting - it means the world to me. Thankyou .... and right back at you!

It is really something how some lovely and amazing words can turn your day/thinking/life around. I need to always remember this.
and I need to pay this forward every chance I get!

Coincidently - I went to see the movie ... the secret Life of Walter Middy. Has anyone seen this one?

Hi Flora! Imagine meeting someone through a newspaper ad! but that is how it was done .... I remember those days well.
We must be around the same vintage! lol

This e-harmony thing ......when I see someones's profile and they say they are an atheist .........

Of course - I haven't really signed up - paid money ......just did the profile questions. That was pretty interesting to have to think about all those things about yourself.

The really hard part ..... people around my age look soooooo old! You can only see a somewhat fuzzy picture - but you can see it sorta good. That isn't good English is it?

That is the thing with quantum magic/entanglement .......it is about the connection - not the looks. Well tho - if they have a huge pot belly and ass - I am probably outa there! But that is a health issue.... they gotta dig QM.

It just seems unnatural .......I really like Quantum magic and quantum entanglement and maybe throw in a little quantum tunnelling as well! I think that is how we roll ..... makes the most sense to me.....

I really have to understand that I may be alone ..... and deal with it I guess. Forget I said that .... that is a waste of time!

We just have to keep dancing until the music stops! and even then - we should keep on dancing!

### caroline — 2014-01-03T23:33:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-104676>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=104659)

I used to love days like that when I lived in Canada .... seemed to take the pressure off!

I used to find when there was a huge storm and they were telling everyone to stay off the roads - our cafe would be full and people would be in a great mood. There would be so much laughing and camaraderie and it would fill your heart. People would all help each other to dig out etc. I have such fond memories.

### caroline — 2014-01-04T08:45:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-104714>

I just have to come here and say ..... I am back to walking around with a silly grin on my face!!! I love it when you pass people and it triggers a big smile ... so fun! and then there are people that don't respond at all - not many - but they seem so sad. I know you never know what is going on in someone's life ... but a smile always makes everything so much better.

It is the funniest thing - even when alone - I can't contain this ridiculous grin. It is like I have this huge secret - but I forgot to tell myself what it is!

However ....Next year I am avoiding Christmas and New Years at all cost .... I gotta get a plan ....

Maybe I will go to the jungles of Africa and cavort with the lions and tigers ......

Suggestions???

### nicld — 2014-01-04T23:47:56+0200

> I used to find when there was a huge storm and they were telling everyone to stay off the roads - our cafe would be full and people would be in a great mood. There would be so much laughing and camaraderie and it would fill your heart. People would all help each other to dig out etc. I have such fond memories.
>
> Do you remember when the power went out along the eastern seaboard? same thing.
>
> Think of the huge C/T benefits!

Uhmmmm, the east coast is getting slammed with snow, we just have the sub-sub zero cold weather (I live in Wisconsin, upper Midwest of the US). No snowman's here, the snow is too cold to pack. Right now, I think I would like 25" of snow if that meant it would be 25 F.

Tomorrow at the game the wind chill is projected to be between -50 to -60 F BELOW!!!! When I bought the tickets the high was suppose to be 7 F. That would be a heat wave now.

### prAna303 — 2014-01-06T12:33:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-104864>

> It just seems so unnatural. I always think/have thought that if something was meant to be - it would be. Quantum entanglement

Me, have never got to understand that form of dating. For sure you could find someone that suits you but how many dead ends must you enter before that?

Either it is meant to be, just like you said OR you make it. I think that in the game of flirting you should look for the ones that have that special energy, that special presence that make you go, hmm.

Think that how we do make connections today is dead wrong, it is as bad as the food, the arts and our modern living has gone. This is up to us to change because i do not see the others doing it.

### sooperb — 2014-01-06T17:49:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-104879>

Ah the Dating Game. In today's world it surprises me that anyone finds a mate once they are past their teens. Take my son, lovely lad, stepped out with a girl in his late teens which lasted nearly 3 years then they fell out and she was "dumped" rather unkindly. After several months he decided he'd made a mistake and spent the next couple of years trying to woo her back, unsuccessfully. By this time he was in his early twenties, too old for discos and in our part of town that leaves night clubs for the yoof and pubs. The pubs, well, believe it or not, most blokes go there to drink (it's what British Northern blokes do), if the girls go to pull the lads then they have to be stunning in order to make any headway past the beer. The town has its own University so the pubs are generally thronging with beer drinking "poor" students who don't need to notice the girls because they can chat them up at Uni. So, not a happy hunting ground for romance. The night clubs charge to go in, they charge eye watering prices for their drinks too so those who go tend to congregate in someone's home first, drinking cheap supermarket vodka and the like in order to keep the cost down once they get to the club. Alas this ensures that most are bladdered by the time they get there and are only likely to "pull" someone equally tanked whom they may not wish to see again after they've woken up next morning, it's never the handsome stranger you find in such circumstances.

So, what is left? Once you are past the golden age then you become very restricted, my son has found this out alas. He has met a girl who works where he does, it's not perhaps ideal because they fall out regularly which must impact on their work time. He is back with her for I think it's the 4th time. His only 2 interests are birdwatching and football, I can safely say he's not going to meet a girl at either of those pursuits so what does that leave him in the way of opportunity? As you get older I'm sure that unless you are hugely gregarious or have a wide social circle that opportunity is slim. So, after a long ramble, I have arrived where I could have started lol, online dating must surely have its merits for those not in the first flush of youth. If you have to work your way through a few hopeless cases well, is that much different to when you were in your prime? At least you don't have to be in a rush, you're not looking to start a family and you can have fun looking. At a later stage in life, most of us will be wiser (not all alas) so dodging the no hopers and those looking for a cosy billet for their old age should be easy. Go. For. It. Audrey, what have you got to lose?

### nicld — 2014-01-06T20:23:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-104890>

Yep could have cuddles if sat down but needed to keep standing to stay warm and they basically would have let anything go through security. The only "security" was a sheriff with a wand looking for guns.

It was chilly but tolerable thanks to CT.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-10T00:29:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-105229>

Audrey, I finally started following your lead. I purchased some frozen sockeye salmon (I had plenty of other seafood in my freezer but I wanted something fresher) and I now have it on a board in my fridge. I took a slice earlier and was amazed at how good it tasted. Even better than high meat! ;-) I went back for a second and then a third slice. This is easy! The skin was hard to eat so I took it off. Perhaps slicing it into small chunks would help.

Thanks Inger for showing the way and thanks Audrey for urging me to try it. I bet that gut biome survey would have better results for "paleo" meat eaters if people ate more raw meat.

### caroline — 2014-01-10T02:27:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-105237>

Sally - I have never tried it with seafood. What a great idea! The wooden board is the important part! Does it smell at all?

Have you done it with steak? Last nite I had some Roast Lamb - and honestly, I find cooked is so much harder to digest. Plus you have to wash up!

I am going to start calling you the "boom" girl ... you are rocking it!

### endless — 2014-01-10T02:54:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-105245>

I have not taken the high meat plunge yet....but I did keep a beef roast on a bamboo cutting board in the fridge for a week and just cut some off when I wanted....it developed a dry outer skin but the inside seemed the same the entire time. I'm still a bit leery of the high meat.

### caroline — 2014-01-10T03:01:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-105248>

> [endless said:](/goto/post?id=105245)
>
> I have not taken the high meat plunge yet....but I did keep a beef roast on a bamboo cutting board in the fridge for a week and just cut some off when I wanted....it developed a dry outer skin but the inside seemed the same the entire time. I'm still a bit leery of the high meat.

Hi Krista .... I have been eating it for a really long time now - and no problems at all. I think of it as salami! I used to love salami - I am sure I still do ... but all the stuff in it really bothers me now.

The high meat I made was so vile - I have a book on fermenting and he really cautioned about eating high meat. I think Inger has been eating it for a long time and can handle it.

Last night when I was eating roast Lamb - I just could not digest it and I got very bloated. I was really surprised by that......

### nonchalant — 2014-01-10T04:15:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-105255>

It seems that high meat is a lot like my compost pile. If it isn't turned very often in the summer, it can develop an offensive smell. But if it is turned regularly, it doesn't smell. Love that aerobic bacteria.
Audrey the more air my high meat got, the better it smelled. A lot like aged cheese. I like to have a few layers of cheesecloth between the jar and the lid, to allow even more airflow.
I did forget an early batch for a while and it smelled pungent and vile. No way I could eat it.

### caroline — 2014-01-10T05:38:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-105259>

Did you just eat your salmon as is? Lemon juice? I love raw salmon with honey - but I often get carried away with the honey!

I see you are on fire in the comments!! Are you eating more oysters? Or what's the deal Lucille??

### caroline — 2014-01-10T08:18:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-105267>

Good luck with that I say ........ Dr. K. has more chance of teaching me how the brain works!

### Inger — 2014-01-10T21:11:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-105303>

high meat roquefort style and raw fish and oysters - oh man I need nothing else to feel vibrant

my high meat always smells so I air it on the balcony (or it will smell like someone had farted sooo bad in my whole apartment huh! sorry for this explanation but it is the truth) but the taste I love! But I always loved the weird tasting foods like aged cheeses and stuff.

I do think when meat ages it gets easier digested? Like it already is pre digested in a way. Many animals do that, they hide their pray and let it ferment and eat it then....
But I do think some people do not do so well with old meats, maybe if they have histamine issues or such, I am not sure why.

How are you doing Audrey? So glad it is less hot where you live, lets hope it stays cool

### caroline — 2014-01-12T02:59:18+0200

is the correct terminology. Anyway..

OMG. ... I love the beach...

### caroline — 2014-01-12T03:06:41+0200

### caroline — 2014-01-12T04:24:21+0200

I got a little off track because of the pouch guy ..... Is there a technical name for a pouch?

I was sitting on the rocks this morning watching people swim laps ....it mesmerizes me. They are all in great shape. They seem to take much better care of themselves and are much more interesting to talk to. There is a zest and vitality that I love and I guess that is why we always end up talking about paleo and C/T etc.

A lot of guys seem to shave off all their body hair these days....is that common?

A classic one... This together with making my own skateboards, out skating all days formed my early years.

### caroline — 2014-01-18T06:46:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-106099>

We are having a spectacular week ..30C today and a beautiful clear blue sky. I am sitting on my balcony and it is spectacular.
The birds are singing .... amazing. There is a gentle breeze blowing thru the trees.....

I had to tear myself away from the beach today ... needed to get groceries and water etc.

There are great sales so I decided to pop into my favorite store and see what's up. What a mistake that was. I promised myself I would not buy anything for 30 days - that was 3 days ago!

I came home with three dresses .... I am turning into such a girl! The lovely young woman who was helping me decide said I looked so cute. I told her - at this age - I don't think cute is what I should be going for ...lol

One young man had a huge armful of clothes that he was buying his girlfriend .... I asked where I could get one of those.....

I am going to the ocean now that it is cooler .... and see what trouble I can get into.....

### caroline — 2014-02-01T03:27:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-107658>

Some Canadian friends have been here in Sydney for a few days and they went to the Opera House yesterday for the very first time ever. They saw a circus type show with a completely nude magician and raunchy jokes and songs!

How did they get so lucky???? They had a ball......

So I told Brian - from now on he will be my entertainment advisor and I will run everything by him first!

I am sitting here reading EE12 .... and trying to improve my redox potential .....and they get to have all the fun!

Brian is a Doc who is retired and now assists in Surgery a few days a week. They don't own cell phones and he won't wear a pager for the hospital - if they need him they have to call on their landline.

I thought how smart are you...

I had asked him long ago why he retired quite young .... his answer ..."I told my wife if I had to tell one more person they were dying I am quitting" and he did.

He is the most lovely, kind man with a generous spirit and a huge smile and even bigger laugh .... all those years of being a GP and not being able to help much really takes a toll.

He told me - all those years ago in medical school they were told ... if you don't have a diagnosis after taking a history and doing a physical exam ... it will be really tough.

Those were the days when Docs actually talked to you and wanted to know how you really are and actually had hands on....

### Inger — 2014-02-05T15:01:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-108450>

Hi Audrey! I can see you now, yay!!!  those smileys are weird..hehe

Your friend Brian sounds like such a lovely person. How lucky you are to have such friends.

Ok, I wanted to ask you about your fat fast, how did you do it? What exactly did you eat/drink? How was it how did you feel during it?

I am experimenting a bit now, I dropped my BAB and do just a fat coffee for breakky now and eat at lunchtime and then a early dinner. That way I will be fasted from 4-5 PM to 11 AM most day (if I do not count the fat coffee..) It is fun to experiment a bit here and there! I was thinking about dropping my protein a bit or I will probably die early LOL or not. I feel so great even with high protein but I am just checking out how I do with a bit less, maybe great! If not I just go back to eat lots of protein no problem. I am not doing this to lose weight really.. as I feel pretty perfect at the size I am now... I do not want to get skinny either. I do not like when my ribs stick out too much huh
But just to see how I feel with even more fat and a bit less protein.

### caroline — 2014-02-06T03:26:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-108636>

Hi Inger ...it is so much fun to have pics! but I want a glamour shot like yours - I am very envious!

I adore my friend Brian .... he always had enormous respect for Doug and treated us both like gold - how could I not love them? I was very upset when they were leaving.....

anyway back to the fat fast. I wish I could understand this better - I tried to ask Dr. K. .... but I couldn't formulate a question properly - I have too many!!
Part of the reason I did it is to get back on track after too much wine and cheese etc...... but also There is still too much fat on my body and I am curious to see what weight I should actually be at now that my body comp is so much better. This morning I was at 54K - so it seems I have quite a small frame that I never knew about. Sounds silly doesn't it???
what I have done is have a couple of BPC with CO and butter in the am and then another around 12pm and then another around 4pm and some more butter if feeling hunger. The odd part is I am not really very hungry at any point and feeling great. Great energy and clear thinking not to the point of getting QED tho ...lol

I don't know how to stop it tho - Dave Asprey says you need carbs the next day - Jack says you don't. The first time I had some rice but I think a lot of seafood would be better.
What do you think? I always feel better with less protein ..... my n=1 I guess.

Much more fat seems to be the key for me...... I seem to be really thriving.

I would love to hear your thoughts Inger......

I know you have said before that you have to eat simply and that is exactly right for me too....is it because we were binge eaters before? I think it must be our biology?

If I ate like others here I would be so bogged down and not thrive at all - I don't really understand why....
What do you think?

### cantweight — 2014-02-06T04:28:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-108648>

I listened to a podcast by Ameer Rosic and I really liked his plan. It has more IF than I have done but I am hoping to help my sleep get better again. I think it was around noon that he had fatty meal...not huge though, included bone broth and espresso and fatty meat then around 5 another keto meal and that was it till the following day. Those are the times and things I feel naturally inclined to eat, often I am forcing things at times my body rejects them. My body needs to get the hell on board with what i am trying to do..lol and stop giving me trouble!!

### caroline — 2014-02-06T08:04:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-108657>

Christi - which podcast is that? I can't remember. I say, and Jack has said, trust the Doctor in your head. Go with your instincts? that is what was happening to me - forcing myself with all the protein. It did help with the cravings - kicked those to the curb! so grateful for that!
Fat saved my butt - I just can't tolerate all the protein - but I can eat a boatload of fat .... I really don't understand - but I am going with it. I was concerned about muscle wasting - and I asked JK that on the Q&A ...and he said it wasn't a concern. I feel like a lot of us are missing something .........

What say you????

### Inger — 2014-02-06T10:14:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-108667>

Thank you so much for your detailed answer Audrey, very interesting indeed! I have been eating a BAB for 2 years now so I think it might be time to try leaving it out for a while, just to see how I do without. I am not sure having only fat for breakfast is the best thing to do, I might do a little protein too with lots of fat. But right now I try just the fat coffee. Weird I do great with the coffee now. Maybe because I make it very mild. And have just 1 not too big cup. I add organic, whole cardamom seeds and about 2 tbsp french butter and 2 tbsp raw coconut oil. Oh man it is crazy yummy!
I have had very little coffee last weeks, many days totally without. No issues at all. Maybe a break from the coffee did me good and now I can tolerate it better? My body gives me very clear signs if I have too much of it.. I feel it right there and have to toss the rest as my body says NO..lol Maybe also to drink enough cold good water is a key to tolerate coffee better. I have been drinking more water lately. I realize if I have a bottle and a glass on my desk as I work it is way easier to drink much!

Jack has said with much EMF high protein is not good. Maybe that is it Audrey. If there is still many wifi signals around you your body wants much fat not much protein... I really do not think we need that much protein if we eat it raw, I think the body gets more of it that way? I feel it is more bioavailable? So I am sure you do not need to worry! Just take care the little protein you eat is mostly raw seafood and raw meat/organs and you will be fine! Raw nust have some protein too so if you eat them they will add to the protein content too. I like to have raw nuts for dessert! Oh man I love nuts.... but they have to be very good quality or I clearly feel they do only damage to me. I just ordered some Hazelnuts... they grow not far from here so pretty natural for me to eat I guess. They are crazy yummy! I rediscovered Hazelnuts this Christmas. I never knew I love them that much. Are there any Macadamia nut trees around where you live Audrey? Those are great on a fat fast too I think! You might be able to get them really fresh.... in their shell. I have a macadamia nut knacker. It is very handy as they have crazy hard shells.

The thing with simple eating is so true for me too Audrey! From too much cooked I feel not good at all. Little here and there is ok but for me an all cooked diet just does not fit... I love the yummy crispiness and juiciness from raw foods too! So I do not feel deprived at all. I like the taste of pure raw foods way better.. and my stomach too! We are weird..hehe

Around 54 kg is what I weigh too, I guess. I never weigh tho... so it could be more or less lol I am pretty small framed too, I weighed 46 at my thinnest but that was too thin I think. I used to weigh 65 as a teenager it was way too much! I am 166 cm but really my bones do not weigh too much. I am so glad about my once a week training, I really like how it makes my butt and proportions get better even if maybe the same weight. It really is not too much about how much we weigh but rather how we carry it I guess... I now, weirdly, really prefer to have some fat on my body... I used to want to be skinny.. but lately I am just finding it way better with nice muscles and some fat on the top.

You should experiment a little Audrey (I think you do lol), you could break the fat fast with lots of protein, like a protein refeed? or just go on with your normal foods, low protein high fat and then see how you feel about it.
Don't forget to share with us how your experiments go! I love to hear about others N=1

### caroline — 2014-02-10T04:47:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-109404>

I had such a special day at the ocean yesterday ..... quantum entanglement in spades.

I don't know if y'all remember me telling you about an extraordinary little 5 year old boy - Jay.

I was sitting on a bench at the yacht basin - eating some calamari .... and Jay rocked up and say 'Hi Audrey"
He looked me up and down and said ... your knee is better? The last time I saw him, months ago, I had a big scab on my knee from falling.

This is the most special little boy. His aunt remembered me and my name too.
Jay's Mom is such a special young woman - I met her DH and her brother and SIL.

I can't ever remember meeting a family with so much grace and kindness and sheer loveliness. They just radiate love and kindness and genuineness .......to be in there presence is quantum entanglement at its very best.
They also all appear to be the picture of good health - just beaming and radiating energy. I am interested to know how they eat. I have never seen them with junk of any kind - altho everyone is this area is usually munching on french fries from the cafe. They are out power walking along the ocean.

I initially had sat on a bench with a couple - and of course we got chatting. A very lovely couple who moved here about 7 years ago from England - about the same time as us.
We chatted for a long time and they told me about their son and daughter who are Doctors and they talked quite alot about their daughter and how she is so focused and always wanted to be a Doc and became a specialist by the age of 26. It turns out that she is a renal Doc and works in the hospital here ........ and was my husband's Doc.
We had changed to her a few moths before he died.
I have said here -We have always have had good Doctors - they always listened and did their best and treated us with respect and kindness...... except this one! I truly despised this woman - her manner with me was offensive and she was ridiculous. The head Renal physician later talked to me and helped me with all the horror and said hopefully our Doc had learned a few lessons.... I guess no one is perfect!

Anyway - her parents are lovely.....

It was the most spectacular day - 28C and clear blue sky .... what's not to love! C/T in the ocean and tons of sunshine and then I sat under a conifer tree and read EE12....... perfect whatever way you slice it...

And I got to see my little "angel" boy again. He was wearing a NYY baseball cap - my DH's favorite team.

He asked if I was coming back next Sunday so we could play in the sand together ......heck ya!

How can you not think about Quantum entanglement and quantum jazz everyday and in every way ......

I am wearing a special barely there T-shirt for Nicole .... it has a huge black sequined skull and crossbones and my bottom half is sporting tiny lace boardshorts .... and no underwear ..... yikes.

I suppose the fewer people that know that the better.......
