I had a gum infection 3 years ago \[old root canal\] it went away with antibiotics and my dentist suggested to just let sleeping dogs lie for now. It is in a really difficult spot - near a bridge etc.
Last night it came back .....all nite long I worried about it .....trying to remember what the oral surgeon\[and Jack\] had said. After hours of pain I finally got up and rummaged around for some antibiotics - couldn't find any so had some brandy and went back to bed. This morning - no pain at all ......WTH!

I wish I could talk about my experience with the empath ..... I did tell my dear friend because she knew I was going and asked. She always worries about me ...... but she doesn't really believe any of this stuff could happen - she thinks if they are really bright they can pick up on cues from you ....she is open tho - in a way.

But when I told my friend some things it made it seem really trite and it definitely was not...... I am having a one on one with the empath on friday ........

### yewwei.tan — 2014-03-01T00:35:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-112739>

How in particular is your sleep getting disrupted?

For example, my Dad has a problem where he always gets up too early -- 3 or 4am, and then is unable to get back to sleep after that. Typical sleep period for him this summer is 10pm to just before 6am.

\---

Ever since reading EE4 I've been casually tracking solar activity, especially since the sun has been in a solar cycle for the last few months. (the 'Aussie Donut Hole' section will be the most relevant, and talks about solar EMF -- [http://jackkruse.com/energy-and-epigenetics-4-light-water-magnetism/](http://jackkruse.com/energy-and-epigenetics-4-light-water-magnetism/))

Another solar flare was recorded about 3 days ago (25-Feb) -- [http://news.discovery.com/space/sun-erupts-with-huge-x-class-flare-biggest-of-2014-140224.htm](http://news.discovery.com/space/sun-erupts-with-huge-x-class-flare-biggest-of-2014-140224.htm) . NASA site -- [http://sdo.gsfc.nasa.gov/gallery/potw/item/496](http://sdo.gsfc.nasa.gov/gallery/potw/item/496)

Could definitely spike up levels of EMF that cause dehydration, but obviously we can't say for sure if that's the cause of your problems. Just a point of interest I guess

### sooperb — 2014-03-01T00:37:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-112740>

I am very open minded about such things but apart from a few personal experiences of not much merit, I started attending a circle led by two mediums, husband and wife. We did various things, most of which had no impact on me and somethings where others reported what they were experiencing things but I wasn't.

The chap would generally chat to those who were there afterwards. He came over to me and just started telling me something that he really couldn't have known, I hadn't discussed it with anyone there because it was something I hadn't been too sure about. To say I was stopped in my tracks puts it mildly. It wasn't much but it was so accurate he couldn't have guessed! And he looked me right in the eye when he said it almost in a knowing way.

It's a subject I've been interested in for years and I'm willing to believe that there are those who have some sort of gift or ability, in fact I worked with a girl who was very matter of fact about it because she said she had the "gift" since she was a child. Who knows for sure.

I had a particularly horrible Christmas and New Years this year ..... I was rummaging thru a drawer looking for something - can't even remember what now ....and I came across my Husband's wallet. I cried buckets of tears - he would have had this next to him every single day.
I can't even begin to explain the power of this wallet - There is such strong vibrations it takes my breath away and my life has changed even more dramatically since then.

and then she kept repeating " you need to let go"

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-01T12:17:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-112815>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=112737)
>
> Good morning everyone! I had another really awful sleep last nite ..... I really need to change this. Actually, Inger. I am looking forward to winter ..... looking forward to life.
> I had a gum infection 3 years ago \[old root canal\] it went away with antibiotics and my dentist suggested to just let sleeping dogs lie for now. It is in a really difficult spot - near a bridge etc.
> Last night it came back .....all nite long I worried about it .....trying to remember what the oral surgeon\[and Jack\] had said. After hours of pain I finally got up and rummaged around for some antibiotics - couldn't find any so had some brandy and went back to bed. This morning - no pain at all ......WTH!

Sorry to hear it.

Root canals, at least depending on the material, seem to be potentially dodgy things. The most interesting stuff I've heard on dentistry from any source in the ancestral health movement was this podcast:

[http://www.blogtalkradio.com/drloradio/2012/01/18/holistic-dentistry-with-dr-stephen-lawrence](http://www.blogtalkradio.com/drloradio/2012/01/18/holistic-dentistry-with-dr-stephen-lawrence)

Stephen Lawrence certainly sounds well-informed and thoughtful, insofar as an outsider like me can tell. The only thing that gave me pause for thought what was he says about colloidal silver: it sounds like a bit of a scorched earth policy to me ... but I'm not in a position to know. He is interesting on root canals. Apparently, they used to use gutta-percha, which sounds like it was a bad choice. You can also get bacteria in the pocket under there ... and how do you get them out? That can be a problem. Might be worth a listen.

He impresses me, anyway. Wish he was my dentist.

He talks about water-picks. I've not tried one, though I've thought about it. They're supposedly very efficient at getting between the teeth. Flossing, though much promoted, seems to be a waste of time. Lawrence says with those you can also put essential oils, like wintergreen, in the water, and that helps kill harmful bacteria. There are a couple of other products that sound intriguing but that I think you can only get in the U.S. -- a "candy" that kills the bacteria responsible for tooth decay, and a "lolly" that kills the ones responsible for gum decay. (Or maybe it's the other way round!)

The host asks him about oil-pulling, and he says he doesn't do it, because he doesn't like the taste and the other strategies are very effective. Maybe you could try swishing organic virgin cocount oil round your mouth, though.

You are using a soft brush, aren't you? The harder ones can hurt the gums.

How about taking some CoQ10? That seems to be generally recognised as helpful for the gums. It's a supplement Jack recommends in the ubiquinol form:

[http://jackkruse.com/catalog/leptin-store/#supplements](http://jackkruse.com/catalog/leptin-store/#supplements)

I guess hunter-gatherers probably got a decent dose of it from eating animal hearts. AFAIK, it's abundant in heart muscle.

### sooperb — 2014-03-01T13:29:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-112818>

"He talks about **water-picks**. I've not tried one, though I've thought about it. They're supposedly very efficient at getting between the teeth. "

I have a water pick, I can't tell you the difference it made to my routine. Having neglected my teeth for years, I then took up the cause armed with dental floss in all shapes and sizes. My conclusion is that to floss effectively you need to be a contortionist with miniature fingers. I moved on to the miniature sweeps' brushes, not realising that there are different sizes for a reason so I just picked the pretty colour. Big mistake, there weren't many teeth I could fit them between and of those that I could it became a battle, pushing them in wasn't too bad, getting them out, well, let's say I had to resort to almost surgical procedures to remove them, similar to having to cut pieces of floss attached to plastic handles (make your flossing easier with these easy to use flossers). I ended up with three different sizes of brush, one so thin that it invariably didn't last more than a couple of goes before it bent and snapped.

Where was I? Oh yes, a water pick. I had discarded the idea, they are stupidly expensive. My OH, no doubt desperately wracking his brains for a Christmas present one year, must have remembered my considerably lengthy inspection of one, the humphing and snorting at the price and my response to his weary "are you actually going to buy one"? With an even louder snort and the declaration, not likely, he sighed and we moved on. So, he bought me one. It's amazing, coupled with an electric toothbrush it does a wonderful, fast job on my teeth. It blasts out even the most stubborn bits of food, it is merciless, ruthless even. My dentist approves. She says my dental hygiene is excellent. Sadly efficient as it is, my teeth refuse to stay in good fettle and root canals are becoming the routine fix. Without a doubt I will be listening to that link.

Do read The Book Thief, an excellent read. I can't wait to not see the film, I can't imagine they will capture the essence and beauty of it.

### sooperb — 2014-03-02T17:30:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-113067>

I looked at the oil pulling. I had to sit down and breathe deeply for a while! I had this vision, brought to me courtesy of some old TV programme that went down into the sewers. The biggest problem sewers have is the sheer volume of fat that is flushed down the drains. It builds up and forms fatbergs (very similar to the one I carry around I suspect) which then have to be hacked off. Well alongside these Titanic sized towers of lard runs the sewer and along the top of the water as it gurgles and burps on its way, is the foaming fat deposit, bubbling and frothing looking for somewhere to lodge and start the next fat monster. I'm afraid that's what I conjure up when I consider oil pulling, foamy, greasy bubbles that grow ever bigger and collect the old food deposits from within my buccal cavity as they swirl and seethe round my gums and strain through my teeth. Ugh.

Please don't ignore the tooth pain Audrey. When you feel that unmistakable throb, it's never nothing. It may fade but I can almost guarantee it will be back. I have a graveyard of old molars in my mouth, all have fillings, some are no longer with me and all started alerting me to the their impending demise by a twinge, a throb, an ache eventually concluding with a crescendo of pain that means you left it too late sucker, I'm a full sized, pulsating, ready to erupt abscess that will rob you of your sanity.

Just sayin'.

### endless — 2014-03-02T23:00:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-113093>

> I looked at the oil pulling. I had to sit down and breathe deeply for a while! I had this vision, brought to me courtesy of some old TV programme that went down into the sewers. The biggest problem sewers have is the sheer volume of fat that is flushed down the drains. It builds up and forms fatbergs (very similar to the one I carry around I suspect) which then have to be hacked off. Well alongside these Titanic sized towers of lard runs the sewer and along the top of the water as it gurgles and burps on its way, is the foaming fat deposit, bubbling and frothing looking for somewhere to lodge and start the next fat monster. I'm afraid that's what I conjure up when I consider oil pulling, foamy, greasy bubbles that grow ever bigger and collect the old food deposits from within my buccal cavity as they swirl and seethe round my gums and strain through my teeth. Ugh.

Haha! Wow, that's quite a description  Nowhere near that dramatic when I do it, I have to say....but a good reminder not to discard the oil into your drains. I spit mine outside.

No yurts yet, but I'm thinking of getting started on a greenhouse once the snow melts!

### caroline — 2014-03-02T23:49:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-113107>

Hi Hope and Soo .....You are right I think - root canal is not good. I am kind of stuck about what to do with mine. The tooth that has the root canal is cemented to another tooth that has a long bridge attached....

I do take threonate ..... I am not exactly opposed to supplements altho I like what Jack says about getting what we need from our food. I just can't afford supplements. But I do think the three legged stool is working for me!

when we were trying to make this happen ...Vee posted a great pic of a house in Destin that has 18 bedroom and bathrooms and a hot tub and on the ocean - it was gorgeous! Dr. K. thought - for the first get together - it wasn't a good idea ... we might kill each other!

### fitness@home — 2014-03-06T21:14:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-113820>

Audrey,

Your recent experience with the medium inspired me to make an appt. I saw her once in late 2011. Appt is tomorrow after work.

I was looking over my notes from that visit. Remember, she had never met me before. These two things stand out:

\- Sees promotion and financial prosperity at work (accurate)
\- Sees Florida as a vacation destination for us (first scheduled to go March 2013 - canceled due to funeral. Going later this month). Wasn't even on our radar until daughter-in-law brought it up.

There were a also lot of personal things which I will not post here

### caroline — 2014-03-06T23:02:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-113841>

Thanks Dawn for that. I am going today and I was wondering how I was going to remember ...did you just make some notes afterwards?
Last time is very fuzzy - but my friend \[the empath's sister\] has the same memories too.
I think I mentioned that the Empath \[Janine\] read her sister that night .... she never does that .... and it was spot on - stuff she did not know that had just happened that day.

I can't wait to hear more .... for both of us! It did a world of good for me I am just realising slowly......

How is Chuck?

### sooperb — 2014-03-06T23:24:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-113844>

In the old days, when technology was more mechanical than electronic, most mediums would record the session on a cassette tape (who uses those these days, a loss in my view) for the client to take away. I don't suppose they do it now as recording onto a CD isn't quite so easy or convenient.

### caroline — 2014-03-07T00:23:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-113847>

I suppose it will be easier this time - it is a one on one with Janine. It was so odd last time - she lumped four of us together - thought we were all friends. The two in front of me were friends and then Jaoanne \[her sister\] and I. It was the same message for all of us basically.....about moving forward. It was different but the same for each of us.

All this quantum stuff is so real to me - I live it every single day - and I have felt a great deal of peace since I saw her last.

I am so curious to see how she does it. In a group she very consciously raises the energy in the room.

I think - no - I know ... I have been living in another dimension for the last couple of years ...but especially since the webinar that Jack did because of Erwan ... and then the Q&A with Erwan.

I find that everything is so tangible and real and entangled ...I do love quantum Jazz!

I am on such a high on life .....you guys may have to come and scrape me off the ceiling!

I find it so mesmerising .... Dr. K. is a man of science and yet he is the one that has taught me all of this ...how is all of this possible?

Do you suppose that was always the intended message? but I do have to stop and think ... Dr. K. is growing too - that is what the webinar, influenced by Erwan, was all about .... an open mind.

So - Erwan opened Dr. K's mind to some things and then Jack payed it forward .....

I really am just getting it .... That is what this journey has been all about! I really am like a puppy going around in circles until I find a place to land .... for now...

I suppose that is why I feel frustration here sometimes .... people won't open their mind. They are so set in their own way, and , seemingly very proud to be so. They insist that no one is going to tell them what to do, and rightly so, but it is a far different kettle of fish to open your eyes/heart and have a little look around and see what the world has to offer.

When your hands/heart are open ... you do receive gifts ......I am freakin living proof!

Sorry, sorry, sorry .... I don't understand where all this comes from......at least you can abort at any time!

### caroline — 2014-03-07T09:08:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-113949>

This is going to be a soppy post - so abort now! I am really just talking to myself I think. I am having just the best day possible! On the way to the shops I was practically skipping............

I saw Janine \[medium\] this afternoon and can't even begin to express all the things that happened.

I wish all of you could experience this once...... quantum magic to the max.

My life/heart is full to overflowing .......because of my DH and JK and all you guys .......I am so incredibly lucky.

It is all extraordinary ....... I think Mother nature has really laid one on me!

All of this may sound a little silly or sentimental or ridiculous to some .....but it is very, very real to me.

There is so much we don't know, so much we are learning ......so many endless possibilities......

### caroline — 2014-03-09T10:19:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-114284>

I am on such a high - I don't know what to do with myself! I wish I could channel this energy into cleaning or ???

I was walking/skipping to the shops today and everyone keeps smiling at me and then they break out into a huge grin! I don't understand what I am giving off.

I feel very peaceful and contented and really, really alive. Today, a lady I haven't seen for a long time had this huge smile and took my face in her hands gently and just grinned at me.
and then ... we talked about her lovely dress!

I was standing at a stop street and a man in a lovely old merc was waiting to proceed .... This car was gorgeous - we were using various and assorted hand signals to communicate that his was indeed a lovely car and I appreciated it and he appreciated the fact that I appreciated it! weird!

I had to go to pay my credit card - I wasn't even going to go right into the store .....but before I knew it I was chatting away. Everybody smiles at me and calls me by name .....and I came home with a short dress and a skirt and a silk shirt!! obviously everyone has my number! But it is more than that - they ask my advice on things and what would look good on them and we always talk about what I eat and what I do......

I don't know where this is all heading ... but I can hardly wait .......

### sooperb — 2014-03-09T10:33:12+0200

I realised today that I am missing a beat at the ocean. I usually swim in the rock pool and then go to the beach and walk in the surf and lay in the sand.

There are always a few people at the rockpool ......and then they lay on the stones .... and I always thought why the heck do they do that? I guess I kinda thought they couldn't be bothered packing up and going to the beach.

### Sammy — 2014-03-26T05:15:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-118010>

Caroline,
Can you give me some guidance with ordering labs in Aust. Where do you get your labs done? Through a doctor?? I really want to do a Metametrix GI effects and a salivary cortisol(ASI). I gotta find a doctor who knows how to interpret this as well.
I know I have a leaky gut and fat malabsorption(which would lead to deficiencies in other vitamins...). I was even considering perhaps a consultation with Dr. Tim Jackson-I just want to get better ASAP! Tired of getting tossed around from one dr to another...I'm pretty sure I have some level of adrenal fatigue too. My GP did me a huge favour by doing a HS-CRP and rT3 so I am not going to push it. I am waiting for those results to be back. Meanwhile, I should really update my journal...
By the way, how are you??

### caroline — 2014-03-26T07:48:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-118013>

Hi Sammy! I am great - thanks for asking.

Dr. Jackson has an account with a lab in Sydney ...I will have to look it up and get back to you.

My Doc is great and will order all that he can via medicare. even an IGF 1 - they can't order that one very often without incurring the wrath of medicare - they open themselves up to inestigation.

I use ZRT labs \[canary club\] for an Saliva ASI but you can also get that in Oz thru Healthscope. They do a melatonin as well. your Doc can order pregnenolone - but be aware that it is batch tested and can take some time to get results.

The thing with ZRT is they email the results to you.........

Sammy - where are you now? back in Melbourne? I was given a name of a good Doc there - but I don't know how I would find it. It was from a young man who had lived in Melbourne and then moved to just south of Sydney - near Miranda... Do you remember him? He hasn't been on the forum for a long time...

### caroline — 2014-04-06T00:16:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-119966>

Good morning Lah! things are just fine down here - cooler now and a bit rainy. How are you????

I am so bummed this morning - I bought some stuff in Sydney yesterday - and this morning was going to try some on and I can't find it! One very bright yellow package is not here ....I can't even imagine what happened.

Stuff is expensive the first time around .....and sucks to have to buy it again!

I had the most peculiar day yesterday. One of the reasons I was going in was to get my laptop looked at. It was me - I was totally doing something wrong ...and then idiot mistake #2 - I asked him to look at my iphone because the ear buds didn't seem to be working \[I never use them\] and had wanted to listen to Pink Floyd on the train.

The volume was turned all the way down!!!

idiot #3 ..... I came down in the elevator with this grumpy looking guy and we were walking out of the building. It is a massive all glass front ..... and I was preoccupied - as usual ....and I hear this guy yelling madam. madam .....I came inches from crashing into the plate glass ....... I had missed the exit.

I haven't had a day like that in a long time ....no quantum magic yesterday - it seems.........

well, actually there was ......If I had walked into the glass I can't imagine how much damage I would have done to myself ...... and I got some beautiful french lingerie!!!

### Gracelind — 2014-04-06T06:24:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-120006>

Caroline,

Your computer issues remind me of some stories told by tech support guy way back in DOS days. He told the lady on the phone to first close the Window (on her screen, of course). He heard footsteps, then a slide and soft bang, more footsteps. She came back to the phone and said, "OK the window is closed, now what?"

Another person called to say the cup holder on her computer was broken. He finally realized she meant the CD holder--back when they slid out.

We've all made silly mistakes. Goes with being human, I guess.

And ... ooh la la! on your new lingerie. Enjoy.

We all have off days, it makes the good days look wonderful n'est ce pas? I hate losing things, my friend once spent £40 (I'm going back 20 years when £40 was a king's ransom) on shopping for her child who had all sorts of health allergies and needed special food. She then went into Mothercare and put her carrier down while she was looking at something. As you do, she wandered over to something else, when she went back her carrier had gone. She consoled herself with the thought that when the discovered what was inside they would be miffed to find it was all stuff they almost certainly couldn't use.

### caroline — 2014-04-07T05:40:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-120168>

I am just realizing that the universe is trying to tell me something ..... When I lost my bag the other day - I had three bags. One was the gorgeous French lingerie .... And the other was a beautiful silk shirt. Both of these cost way more than the bag I lost and I would have been devastated if I had lost the lingerie!

I always feel really safe in Sydney - don't worry too much about closing my bag/ purse etc. this has given me pause and to be much more careful. My DH always worried about me when I went off to Sydney and always wanted to come with me to protect me ..... But it was way too much for him....

I am now realizing that it was a pickpocket at work ... Lesson learned!

### Andrea — 2014-04-08T19:52:56+0300

So, at the workshop, I take my wallet out to discover... yeah. Wonderful!

Money is one thing, but taking someone's bag with stuff?! I'm so sorry about what happened to you.

### caroline — 2014-04-08T23:50:31+0300

thanks RR .... it was a good lesson learned - one I needed.....and oddly - I feel it is because my DH is still and constantly looking after me and he was giving me a wake up call!. I often have no filter and really need that.....

that must have been horrible for you - to lose a wallet is devastating ...so much to replace .

A friend told me yesterday that she thinks I am a witch! I am taking that in the nicest possible way and hoping she meant empath ...lol

Things just come out of my mouth - as you all know - that surprise mostly me, but really surprise others sometimes.

Later they will tell me I was correct ........

That isn't what I am going for .... just making an off the cuff comment on the fly.....

### kyrakitty — 2014-04-10T09:14:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-120755>

Hmm... Caroline I have had days like that and for me I am finding that it is all consistantly coming back to not enough nutrient dense foods on a daily and consistant basis or something else effecting my redox and my brain was being kind enough to tell me in a very curt way by producing very similar dramatic situations as you described. This would piss me off because I was working hard at being healthier and doing everything right. No wheat, high fat, blah blah blah etc...... And I wonder to myself why the hell does my brain have to be so passive aggressive why can't it just tell me exactly what my n=1 is instead of having to make me work so dam hard at it. I'm not saying this is what is happening to you but this has happened to me and the more I work on my n=1 the less that happens.

Being a witch comes with the territory because we are extra sensitive to everything inside and around us. It is an compliment.

### caroline — 2014-04-10T09:34:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-120758>

I got the most beautiful email today from friends in Canada. They had been to our old restaurant for lunch and I guess our names came up because they had just been here in Oz.

The waiter was telling them about a woman who had told him about being pregnant and her water broke in the restaurant - that prompted a lot of other people in the restaurant to reminisce about us. He said the atmosphere in the restaurant was totally elevated as people relayed stories about all the characters and craziness and wonderful people that used to be our life on a daily basis for 35 years.....

I had such a good cry ........ my heart is full to overflowing .....

I have had the most amazing life ..... and getting better and better....thanks to all of you!

that sounds really sloppy - sorry....you always have to enter this journal at your own risk

### caroline — 2014-04-12T10:22:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-121090>

I had such a great day today! I got up at 4:00am and took the train to Sydney ..... I wanted to go to an organic market. You need serious money to shop at these places! Lots of great people to chat too - and I basically snacked my way thru the market!
I got some delicious chorizo and sausages - wood smoked and no crap - just pastured pork .... and some very delicious cheeses.
and some bones for broth and osso bucco and rump steak

A woman that has a wonderful restaurant in Sydney has a stall there.........so this is what I had at 8:00am.....

Sticky rice parcels steamed in bamboo leaves with organic macadamias, goji berrries, local greens and ............
Roasted crickets and roasted meal worms ..... and home made Chili sauce.....yum, yum, yum

I had some great conversations at the table with people who wanted to see the crickets and worms!!!

### caroline — 2014-04-13T01:14:01+0300

I used to struggle with BAB too ....... too much food - so after the cravings and all the crazy hunger went away I just did a lot more fat ...BPC.
Can you do fat? I can't remember without going back and reading your journal.
Being outside more is a huge game changer for me .... when I am at the ocean I am not hungry at all. However I notice when I am in Sydney I am hungry the whole time - no matter what I have eaten. Inger just mentioned that she is the same in the city!

It is pretty clear ...... ocean vs City........

at my beach there are lots and lots of rocks and conifers ......

How could it be more clear .... rocks and conifers or non native EMF.....

well - once a neurosurgeon tells you ...... and you listen with "eyes, heart, mind"

### sooperb — 2014-04-13T14:40:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-121250>

Sounds like my kind of day, organic food, shopping, sunshine and to finish off by the ocean with rocks and trees. Sigh, I can't even start with a place that sells organic food and gives you samples. Regular sunshine would be good too. My walk along the beach this morning was with a coat and hat on, the wind was icy and I had to keep my hands in my pocket.

I read in Clinton Ober's Earthing book how when people go on holiday, they come back feeling so much better. They never seem to make the connection between sun, sea and sand and all the grounding they get  Life should be so simple, haven't we complicated it!

Hi Audrey

just had to tell you... I was shopping yesterday in the City. Last time I shopped was in fall in Norway in a second hand shop! Now, I wanted to buy me some dresses.... I ended up with no dresses at all, no one was perfect.
Instead I bought 3 sets of bikinis.. oh my... they had so many beautiful bikinis I could have had them all! Just super cute and feminine...
And I got me 2 shirts from Ralph Lauren.. they were both way too cute... soft cotton they felt like velvet on my skin... and very feminine too, I just could not resist! So I spent way too much money I had to take from my account I am saving for the second magnetico  I clearly need to stay away from shops!

I ate 100 g raw sheep milk (Roquefort) cheese and lots of organic Hazelnuts when shopping and had lots of water and I felt great

I got such a great history lesson the other day in Sydney ....I wound up waking along with a young man who was walking his dogs and he took me on a tour of his neighbourhood.
This is a very old area made up of old workers cottages ...they were called 2x2's ....... 2 rooms up and 2 rooms down.
I was lucky enough to stop and chat with a woman and her children too - they have lived in this little village for years.
That was such a fascinating day - I am still on a high! well - I do think this "high" seems to be permanent ...and all without drugs!

High on the three legged stool .......

I don't know how anyone would cope with me .... I seem to be running on high octane!

My iPad really needs its mouth washed out with soap! It is saying really erotic things ....I wonder what that means? I dont want to know ....I am having way too much fun.

I haven't read any blogs for a day ....don't tell JK.....

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-24T15:46:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-123307>

> [Rerun said:](/goto/post?id=123300)
>
> Sounds weird to me... Only 150 ml of oil to 3 egg yolks? Either the lemon is very small, or someone likes their mayo/aioli runny.

Thanks. That was the first recipe I'd found; I've now had a look around online, and I see what you mean. Jamie Oliver is using one single egg with 285ml of oil (and half a clove of garlic: maybe Jamie doesn't like garlic). I also found this on an site from Down Under:

[http://www.taste.com.au/recipes/7531/aioli](http://www.taste.com.au/recipes/7531/aioli)

That uses two egg yolks to a "cup" of oil. I think an Aussie measuring cup is 250ml.

So James Martin is very generous with both eggs and garlic compared to others.

### Andrea — 2014-04-24T19:18:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-123332>

Sorry, Caroline, for turning your journal into a kitchen...

> [Gagnrad said:](/goto/post?id=123307)
>
> Jamie Oliver is using one single egg with 285ml of oil (and half a clove of garlic: maybe Jamie doesn't like garlic). I also found this on an site from Down Under:
>
> [http://www.taste.com.au/recipes/7531/aioli](http://www.taste.com.au/recipes/7531/aioli)
>
> That uses two egg yolks to a "cup" of oil. I think an Aussie measuring cup is 250ml.
>
> So James Martin is very generous with both eggs and garlic compared to others.

Actually, the recipe I just found from Jamie Oliver states 2x 285 ml = 570 ml olive oil for 1 egg yolk. [http://www.jamieoliver.com/recipes/eggs-recipes/aioli](http://www.jamieoliver.com/recipes/eggs-recipes/aioli)
Courageous, but possible. I think it's usually said that one yolk can make an emulsion with 150 ml of oil. But, in fact, one yolk can emulsify at least 2 kg of oil. Yup! 2 kg!

As for how much garlic to use... Well, that one is completely up to you - and the company you'll have during the day. Personally, I can't do too much raw garlic without later suffering.

### Albert83BCN — 2014-04-25T14:21:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-123432>

Hey, [Aioli](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aioli) is a very typical Mediterranean sauce with a lot of tradition here in Catalonia, the region in Spain where I live! In fact, aioli comes from _all_ (garlic) _i_ (and) _oli_ (oil). It's darn delicious and an optimal sauce that we use very typically when doing red meat, roasted vegetables or fish at a barbecue. In fact, could you think of a sauce more optimal than olive oil and garlic? I have never done it myself but I've seen my mom do it quite some time ago. Eggs are not used in the original receipe but some people use them because if not it is way more difficult to make it emulsionate, you have to do it in a mortar for quite a long time and be very cautious or it can get messed up, then you have to throw it to the bin or add the egg, AFAIK, not an expert here.

The only problem here in Catalonia is that it has become very tied to the use of bread, because here bread rubbed with tomato and olive oil or "[pa amb tomàquet](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pa_amb_tom%C3%A0quet)" as we call it is a very typical food as well. I guess it's one of the few non-optimal from our tradition. I must confess is quite delicious although I do not eat it any more, maybe just once per year if its a familiar event and I don't want to mess with the charm of the moment. As of today I find that I can handle it pretty well but I prefer to keep gluten away as much as possible.

> Aussies dont tip.......neither do most europeans........LOL

Here in Spain is not mandatory, although it's seen as a good practice, and you give just what you feel like to, depending on the context and the service. With the current economical crisis and unemployment it's less and less common though.

### sooperb — 2014-04-25T14:59:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-123436>

I wonder if Jack has ever been in a London Black Cab, if you don't tip you can't get out of the cab lol. Tipping is still in practice in the UK, although some recipients of old are no longer in receipt of as much as they used to be, milkmen, dustmen etc. I'm sure there are still some who tip them but mostly tipping is reserved for good service in eating establishments and hairdressers. Indeed, in some restaurants there is still a service charge and the expectation of a gratuity too.

### caroline — 2014-04-28T16:03:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-123690>

Greetings everyone from Amelia Island!

Dr. K. Wanted me to say a little about the conference .... I don't think I have fully formed thoughts just yet. The jet lag finally caught up with me in a big way! We are presently checking for the nearest oyster shack!

The conference was a real eye opener...

There are so any amazing Docs here .. ... They are trying really hard to do the right thing for their patients. - and that is why we have to take everything on board that Jack is teaching us .. .. And go to our Doc appointments armed to the teeth with all this knowledge that Jack is so generously sharing with us.

I thought I somewhat understood - in fact I was sure that I understood..... But now I get it in spades.

Jack is giving us something so massive and extraordinary ... And we need to get it - way sooner than later!

### caroline — 2014-04-28T16:23:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-123701>

I wanted to say" hi" to the amazing Docs that we met .....Shari and Susan and Roger and Jerry and Martin and of course Bavesh who bailed early on us! And the list goes on and on.... All simply terrific Docs who were so much fun and generous and eager to learn...

Most of you guys don't really get what an incredibly generous man Jack is ....I have always instinctively known this and now I completely understand why I go off a cliff when someone says something really stupid about him ... Or to him....

When Jack says "pay all this forward". He lives this every single second of every single day.. Absolutely make no mistake about that ... And he needs to expect this of us - and we need to expect- actually demand - this of ourselves.

I forgot Dr. Achacoso .... And many others ...my brain isn't working so good yet! Dr. K. Can verify that unfortunately!

The fact that I forgot a guy in a leather jacket and a pony tail is proof positive! I never forget a guy with a ponytail!!

### caroline — 2014-04-28T18:51:17+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-29T03:40:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-123801>

Here is a treat for you: My tasting notes from 1951 Grange:
**1951 Penfolds Grange**
South Australia Shiraz Blend, Syrah
[Options](https://www.cellartracker.com/list.asp?table=Notes#)
**11/27/2008 - [I WROTE:](https://www.cellartracker.com/note.asp?iWine=521567&iNote=866733) 94 points ([Edit](https://www.cellartracker.com/editnote.asp?iWine=521567&iNote=866733))**
This wine is amongst the rarest that I own...........and I decided to open it for the surprise. My son has always wanted to taste this wine so I decided to give him his chance. the wine was opened for over ten hours and it really needed it . It was very oaky form the outset but opened nicely. The other finding of this bottle was the intensity of the purple color.............it was ink like. We could not see an LED light thru it when we examined it. The first smell was of heavy oak with berry licorice. Mid palated was softer and had hints of spice like cumin and fig with leather tanins. Very soft and velvety. The finish was strong but not long and had berry raisin confit with jammy black fruit and some dried apricot. It was a pleasure to drink but i think it is starting to decline................fun bottle.

### caroline — 2014-04-29T03:41:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-123802>

Okay .... We are back from dinner. Lots of oysters and assorted seafood.....

Now we are going to listen to the sex webinar Q&A ......God help us.....

### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-29T03:41:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-123803>

OK.........fast forward to tonight. I opened the second 1945 Mouton. My profile pic is the label.

[Red](https://www.cellartracker.com/wine.asp?iWine=4401)
**1945 Château Mouton Rothschild**
Pauillac Red Bordeaux Blend
[Options](https://www.cellartracker.com/list.asp?table=Notes#)
**11/27/2008 - [I WROTE:](https://www.cellartracker.com/note.asp?iWine=4401&iNote=866724) 100 points ([Edit](https://www.cellartracker.com/editnote.asp?iWine=4401&iNote=866724))**
The bottle was opened and it looked pristine. Total fill and no seepage or oxidation. The wine when opened smelled like a chemical smell that burned off after 90 minutes. The wine was purple with garnet at the meniscus. The first smell was of classic wet clay chocolate mint berry fruit and shale. The secondaries on this wine were otherworldly. The wine brought my tears to my mothers eyes, my son and his wife knew they were tasting greatness. The look on their faces before Thanksgiving dinner was worth everything. Second sip revealed crushed stone licorice cherry berry and more mocha mint chip. I also sensed some grass lead pencil and some calla lilliy floral bouquet. The ending was plus 70 seconds and was dominated by mint mocha and currants with more crushed clay..............this wine has depth and complexity that is present in great wines. It is a great bottle but it did not eclipse my TN on the 1900 or 2005 Margaux.........the wine is balanced and has perfect acidity............depth is special and it is a complex wine in flavor.......this wine far exceeded the first one we opened but I am left with the sensation that we should drink the rest of what we have.............I am unsure of how much time it has left. The company in which this opened was amongst the best of my life. Family and wine inclusive on this Thanksgiving.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-29T03:42:43+0300

Tonight's wine was sublime............and I listened to Pink Floyd the whole time...............so don't talk to me.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-29T03:47:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-123805>

My favorite thing about Australia..........

**1999 Penfolds Grange**
South Australia Shiraz Blend, Syrah
[Options](https://www.cellartracker.com/list.asp?table=Notes&Page=2#)
**6/1/2008 - [I WROTE:](https://www.cellartracker.com/note.asp?iWine=17104&iNote=717253) 99 points ([Edit](https://www.cellartracker.com/editnote.asp?iWine=17104&iNote=717253))**
Penfolds is a great wine. The history goes back longer than one thinks and it started with a vine in 1845. Penfolds takes its name from an English gentleman, Dr Christopher Rawson Penfold, who emigrated to Australia in the 19th Century. Like many of his time, Penfold held a belief in the medicinal qualities of wine, and his baggage included a collection of vine cuttings sourced from the south of France. In 1845 he and his wife, Mary, built a small stone cottage at Magill, near Adelaide, around which he planted his cuttings. This cottage was called...The Grange. This is where the legend began. Tonight i got back to Boston from HK and I hit a nice hotel and rdered two bottles of wine prior to bed and getting up to go back home in the AM. The 1999 Grange was my first bottle. The history of Penfolds was from 1845 to 1950 a wine that was fortified for close to 100 yrs. Then the family decided to hire a stud named Max Schubert and his life goal was to get out of fortified low end to the high end table wine. He felt the vines could hack the change. In 1951 he made a table wine based upon Shiraz and the family hated the wine. Thank God Max knew he had something. The 1951 is based upon what has become Grange in concept. I have been fortunate to taste that very table wine several times and own some bought in the 1970's. Max went on to make Grange for many yrs with out the owner family even knowing it. After about 5 Vintages they knew Max was up to something otherworldly. Grange officially hit the world stage commercially with the 1957 vintage released in 1960. As the initial vintages aged, however, their true value came to be appreciated, and in 1960 the management instructed Schubert to re-start production, oblivious to the fact that Schubert had not missed a vintage. The great 1955 vintage was submitted to competitions beginning in 1962 and over the years has won more than 50 gold medals. The vintage of 1971 won first prize in Shiraz at the Wine Olympics in Paris. The 1990 vintage was named 'Red Wine of the Year' by the Wine Spectator magazine in 1995, which later rated the 1998 vintage 99 points out of a possible 100.

Prior to 1997 the wine was made form many terroirs and called Grange Hermitage or Bin 95 for the old timers. In 1999, the wine was 100% Shiraz from the Barossa valley district for the first time. This is a historic Penfolds Grange. Moreover, it is the first Grange since 1963 to be 100% Shiraz (not all Barossa district however). Most vintages contain a small percentage of Cabernet. This wine is sublime and must be experienced to be believed.

The color was deepest purple and the nose is infreakingcredible. It is one of the greatest shiraz ever in my view. It is so complex that in blind tasting since its US release I have never nailed it in blind tasting with first growth Bdx. The aroma you get right off the bat is massive berry fruit of blue and raspberry with cherry anise and wet clay with tar. The wine's mid palate can compete with any great French wine. It is here where the Penfolds complexity comes in full force. I get herbs cedar and beautifully integrated oak with cherry cassis berry and licorice. It does not end there. The coda is massive. This is a wine you can never get enough of. the ending begins with fine leathers spice box cigar cedar vanilla and plummy candied fruit. The wine is massive and magnificient in construction.

This wine is one you must own. It is a wine to love and experience. My only regret is when it is all gone. I saved a glass to savor for tommorrow. This wine has decades to go and gain further complexity. It is one of the great shiraz's ever. Rating 99 and it will go higher. I like the 99 Vintage the best over the 98 which got a 99 rating. Try it...............my bet is you'll you die for this wine.

[Comment](https://www.cellartracker.com/editcomment.asp?iWine=17104&iNote=717253) (12,132 views)

### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-29T03:54:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-123809>

**1900 Château Margaux**
Red Bordeaux Blend [more](https://www.cellartracker.com/wine.asp?iWine=254137)
[Options](https://www.cellartracker.com/list.asp?table=Notes&Page=4#)
**12/6/2000 - [I WROTE:](https://www.cellartracker.com/note.asp?iWine=254137&iNote=704703) 100 points ([Edit](https://www.cellartracker.com/editnote.asp?iWine=254137&iNote=704703))**
This bottle was opened for the birth of my child and the notes were taken from what I wrote that night on a piece of progress note form the hospital. It was a very difficult day for me from the birth since i was an older Mom, but this experience washed away those memories of pain as soon as it touch my lips. My hubby brought this to our birthing suite with glasses and a picnic lunch with family. It was paired with a 1921 D'Yquem and shared with my Mom, Dynette, David and Jack. This is one of the stars of my husbands' collection that he acquired via a trade in BDX with a French surgeon for 61 Petrus in 1999. The bottle was decanted for 5 hrs. It probably needed more time but the wine color was ruby garnet with a yellow tinge at the meniscus. The bouquet was classic Margaux of violets and fruit. The wine contained only 12% alcohol so it was definitely different than 82 83 and 90 Margaux that I became accustomed too drinking. The complexity was, however, different than any Margaux I have had before. It was so soft with few noticeable tannins. I wrote, "no edges at all" and gave your palate and taste buds a "soft landing that made you think". This wine made love to one's mouth it was so good. It had the chocolate cassis, cedar, spice box, lead pencil, cherry, berry, tobacco, oak and herbaceous tones, from my notes. The most remarkable finish of a wine I have ever tasted up until that time. It went on until the next morning. I reviewed my TN on my laptop, and it said, " I did not want to brush my teeth on 12/7", that is how good this wine is. I doubt any wine will top this wine in my lifetime but it gave me so much pleasure that I remember the experience everytime I have drank any Margaux since. This bottle gave me experience that will alter my drinking habits for the rest of my life. I want to drink wine of this ilk more than not. Now, everytime my daughter's B-Day come ups in December, I drink another vintage of Margaux to celebrate her, but I selfishly return to that night of 12/6/2000 and treat myself to that memory. It made me forget the pain of her birth and completely overshadowed the D'Yquem, it was that good. It also started a ritual for me and Margaux. Now everytime I finish a bottle of any vintage I wisper to myself or to my husband, "until we meet again!"

bottle label posted to CT database as well

### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-29T04:00:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-123813>

Now you, Josh, and Stella had this with me: This was my TN on the first bottle I opened. I opened bottles 2-5 with all of you in Orlando since they were in my cellar there. I love this wine...........it dances with life. With all of you this was the first time since I wrote this TN I have opened it. With you guys I opened the bottles the vintner signed for me with his 24 kt gold marker..........I update my TN today. Our only mistake.......is we did not decant it. But I will confess........I let a glass sit out on the cook book you gave me and I had it after 8 hrs. That is when I stayed up til 2:30 in the AM and wrote my TN.............it was sublime.

[Red](https://www.cellartracker.com/wine.asp?iWine=525836)
**2005 Château Croix Mouton (Jean-Philippe Janoueix) 20 mille**
Bordeaux Supérieur Red Bordeaux Blend
[Options](https://www.cellartracker.com/list.asp?table=Notes&Page=3#)
**5/24/2008 - [I WROTE:](https://www.cellartracker.com/note.asp?iWine=525836&iNote=711686) 97 points ([Edit](https://www.cellartracker.com/editnote.asp?iWine=525836&iNote=711686))**
This is a wine you must pay attention too. The wine maker is a young superstar in the making. Only 300 cases made in 2005. Robert Parker likes him alot as well. How do I know? RP never talks about a first vintage wine in the Wine Advocate but if you look closely this wine and Jean Philippe Janoueix made #176 WA released in May. I first heard about this wine and tasted it 18 months ago at EP. This concept behind this wine is to improve the wine quality by increasing planting density to lower the yield to vine to gain grape quality and hence juice and then wine. Well, most great wine is planted via 12-15K vines per hectare. This wine has 20K in a hectare and that is how it got its name. That is so dense planting that no machine can get into this vineyard to work. The wine is made from 100% Merlot from vines 7 yrs old in the Croix Mouton. When i first tasted at EP I thought this wine was very good but I gave it a TN rating of 90. Nothing extraordinary. Well, I got to taste a bottle of this wine and this wine has evolved a serious backbone and evolved into a monsterous superstar in my opinion. Millesime 2005 was the first vintage and the wine just makes you take notice. I tasted this wine in the company of 2 other Firsts (Mouton and Cheval Blanc) and this wine hammered those two. The bouquet brought power...........raspberry blackberry and blueberry with cherry and incredibly fragrant. This wine was the wine of the night from the nose alone in my opinion. The first taste showed its age. This has robust tannins but the density and backbone present in its anatomy is so good you cant imagine until you taste it. This wine is off the charts good. The midpalate just explodes berry with cherry and cassis and currant and the wine is smooth and in total balance. The finish on this wine is the best I have had sine the 2005 Margaux which also is perfection bottled in my view. This is a wine that must be bought and cellared but it is extremely hard to find. I was lucky enough to buy a case of this wine and had Jean Philippe Janoueix personally sign each one and inscrinbe a different note on each one in a gold sharpie. This wine has to be tasted to be believed. RP, in my view, has given us all a clue by just listing JPJ and 20 Mille in the WA. This wine is a strong buy. Rated 97 up from a 90 in 18 months

[Comment](https://www.cellartracker.com/editcomment.asp?iWine=525836&iNote=711686) (7,471 views)

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-30T12:15:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-124025>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=124023)
>
> Huh???

I meant any artifacts left by the native peoples. Pre-Colombian is just a way of saying "from before discovery" (i.e. before Columbus). I bet they have some good collections on display in Florida.

Interesting history after contact, too, of course. The U.S. Army never succeeded in actually defeating the Seminoles of Florida. Andrew Jackson (who was actually quite a nasty character) was involved in that war.

> The clash that inevitably resulted from this policy finally began in 1835, and the seven years that it lasted frame the last, the greatest, and arguably the most tragic years in the history of US-Indian relations east of the Mississippi River. Known to history as the Second Seminole War, the US government committed almost $40,000,000 to the forced removal of slightly more than 3,000 Maskókî men, women, and children from Florida to Oklahoma. This was the only Indian war in US history in which not only the US army but also the US navy and marine corps participated. Together with the desultory Third Seminole War, a series of skirmishes that took place between 1856 and 1858, the United States spent much of the first half of the 19th century in trying, unsuccessfully, to dislodge about 5,000 Seminoles from Florida.

[http://www.semtribe.com/History/IndianRemoval.aspx](http://www.semtribe.com/History/IndianRemoval.aspx)

### caroline — 2014-04-30T14:41:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-124034>

>
> Interesting history after contact, too, of course. The U.S. Army never succeeded in actually defeating the Seminoles of Florida. Andrew Jackson (who was actually quite a nasty character) was involved in that war.
>
>
>
> [http://www.semtribe.com/History/IndianRemoval.aspx](http://www.semtribe.com/History/IndianRemoval.aspx)

Mike ..... I have been drinking rum and lots of wine and smoking cigars... And eating oysters....

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-30T15:13:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-124038>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=124034)
>
> Nothing Pre Columbina has crossed my path .... Not that I have noticed anyway!

You'll have to strongarm Jack into showing you while you've the opportunity. I bet he knows. IIRC, he's been a habitué of museums since boyhood.

I don't know about "culture". It's just something to fire the imagination of anyone interested in a paleo lifestyle. And the sheer thought of these people having moved across the landscape one's now in, living and moving in a totally different rhythm with different dreams.

If I'd known you were going, I'd have recommended Michael Wood's book on the Conquistadors, which also covers trying to trace Álvar Núñez Cabeza de Vaca's path across what's now the southern U.S.

[http://www.amazon.co.uk/Conquistadors-Michael-Wood/dp/1846079721](http://www.amazon.co.uk/Conquistadors-Michael-Wood/dp/1846079721)

The story of that journey and the different peoples met on it is a classic.

### Stella — 2014-05-01T16:40:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-124304>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=124253)
>
> Going to hit the mall today I guess ... Everyone has deserted me! It is raining.
>
> Not as much fun drinking wine on my own......I really, really miss my sister......But she is doing REALLY fun stuff! We hit a sex shop before Stella left.......never been before - lots of revelations for me. The staff was sooooo helpful - they answered a million questions for me .... They know almost as much as Jack!

Miss you too sista!! I had an amazing, unforgettable time making a quantum connection that will last thru eternity

It was really interesting going to that sex shop with a mature, experienced woman. I mean you were telling me that I need this and that. Oh and the furniture and swings, etc. I certainly have a long wish list after that visit.

I do agree that the staff at the store was very knowledgeable. They had their personal favorites in each department. What amazed me was that you knew what you were talking about and asked so many good questions. You rock sista.

As I'm home now, I must say hubby approved of the purchases. But I must comment on that DVD. Even porn stars can't orgasm vaginally. Wtf is that????.... Must be that she is seriously missing electrons!!

Off to eat more oysters and get more Oxy. And work on a summation of the amazing few days of Jacks teachings. More from the crazy Cuban from Miami a bit later.

### caroline — 2014-05-01T16:45:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-124306>

I can't even begin to tell you how much I love you and miss you.. .. talk about quantum entanglement! And then throw Jack in the mix ....

Quantum magic every single second .... What conference???

### caroline — 2014-05-01T18:48:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-124353>

Okay - my brain fog is lifting at least a little. Lots of sunshine and ocean and seafood and spending time with some amazing humans ....many, many amazing humans, and my dear, dear sister Stella. You have no idea what an amazing, smart, direct and kind human she is..... And she knows about wine and cigars! Although I did have to teach her about sex! You can't even understand how bizarre that is!

I have always "got" intuitively what Jack is saying ....and now I really get something that he has been working hard on to make me understand ...... I am so lucky - I have no Pre conceived scientific ideas and so I have to focus and work hard.

I watched Jack first hand and in living color bring all this to life for people that were interested and wanting to learn.

He was tireless in repeating the same messages he gives us - to Docs who wanted to learn. I saw him repeat over and over and over the same simple principles .....and truly, the people who impressed me the most were those who weren't afraid to ask him to repeat a very simple principle that he taught us long ago! Like really simple things that a lot of us got in a heart beat!

Quite a few times I would realize, and actually after the fact, that I had been surrounded by Docs and they were asking me questions and listening to me and then asking more questions ....and then they would go and get his book and spend half the nite reading and come back and ask me questions about what they had read.

I can't tell you how many Docs asked me for my card! I kept asking Jack .. ..why didn't you tell me I needed a card! We laughed like fools over that every single day!

It was amazing to watch some of these Docs like Shari and Jerry and the Cuban Doc and Susan ...hanging on every word and absorbing and turning it all over and over and over....that is my biggest takeaway I think - for today anyway!

One of the most lovely women I have ever met - Susan - who will be joining us on the forum btw..... Has talked to Jack before. Susan is incredibly on board and actually dragged her husband to the conference to meet Jack. He wasn't around much because he was at the pool studying for something .... And he was a big sceptic.

When this brilliant Doc finally had a chance to talk to Jack, and he was a great listener - he became so animated and all in .... I will never forget that.....

I am still trying to process all of this ....

### Hope — 2014-05-02T16:06:42+0300

### caroline — 2014-05-03T03:38:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-124817>

I have moved hotels.... This has been so interesting....

First I was at old key west at Disney .....a very beautiful spot - just perfect... So much fun! For so many reasons

Then Stella and I had an oceanfront suite near Amelia Island ....just gorgeous - beautiful beach and a lot of wine

And then a Sheraton at Lake Buena Vista .... Very, very nice and a lot more wine....

Now - an airport hotel ....oh my....no wine ... among other things....

I don't want to go back to Oz......suggestions??

I have had the most amazing time..... I have so much to be grateful for....I truly am the luckiest person alive. thanks to some very, very special people ... Their kindness and generosity and love and caring has been overwhelming.

I really do love and appreciate you guys more than you will ever know..... xo

I got so much more than I bargained for, so much more than was reasonable to expect...

I got an amazing sister for life (although she may be after my money!)

I got the education of a lifetime....in very unexpected and unusual
ways..... You have no idea....

I had an opportunity to experience Josh's very caring nature ....and hands...

And I learned I should never offer to buy Dr. K. Dinner.... In spite of your oyster fetish - you are the best!
And I would do it again in a heart beat....

This is all so emotional..... I am one lucky girl....

Going to be pretty interesting ...

### Jack Kruse — 2014-05-06T05:56:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-125422>

Avogadro's constant is a scaling factor between macroscopic and microscopic ([atomic scale](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Atomic_unit)) observations of nature...........

Small subatomic changes lead to wide alterations in energy. This is why the brain works on just 20 watts. It takes an electron and strips its mass and the result is a photon with no mass. So I have been trying all day to show how this effect of the brain scales in nature.........So I am invoking Avogadro's number and the problem has been bugging me for two days. The electron rest mass (symbol: me) is the mass of a stationary electron. It is one of the fundamental constants of physics, and is also very important in chemistry because of its relation to the Avogadro constant. It has a value of about 9.109... X 10 −31 kilograms or about 5.486×10−4 atomic mass units, equivalent to an energy of about 8.19×10−14 joules or about 0.511 megaelectronvolts.

The weight of one kilogram near the surface of the earth is 9.8 Newtons.

The weight of an electron would then be 9.1093829 x 10-31 \* 9.8, or ~8.9 x 10-30 Newtons.

The ball at the end of a Bic pen is ~1mm diameter tungsten carbide. At .52mm3 in volume and WC being 8.6g/cm3, we have approximately 4mg of tungsten carbide.

As of 4AM GMT, the ISS is flying at 413km above the surface of the earth.

If the ISS flew over your head right now, and you weighed 70kg, the force of the gravitational attraction between your body and the ball at the end of a ball point pen floating in the cupola would be (G \* 70kg \* .000004kg)/413000m2 , or 1.095x10-25 N, or roughly twenty thousand times greater than the weight of an electron.

FWIW, due to doppler effects in the gravity gravitational waves coming from the moving ball point pen, the force vector will be lagged behind the location of the ball by about 23m at 413km, or about .003 degrees.

It just occurred to me that since scientists just have re-estimated the electron weight figured out to 13 significant figures. If someone could actually measure the force of gravity from the aforementioned orbiting ball of tungsten carbide to just ten significant figures, you could actually determine the distance to that ball with a precision of +/-.1mm. Add one other detector and you could triangulate its position, providing you with the ability to read what the pen is writing.............while this is all going on.............assuming nothing else in the universe is moving.

And Albert thought he has crazy thoughts..............

### caroline — 2014-05-06T05:58:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-125423>

I had an interesting flight from LA to Sydney....
I was in an isle seat and at the other end was quite a lovely gentleman in the other aisle seat.

He told me the middle seat was free ..... so we could share it later. I said .... great idea - what do you have in mind?
What is the plan? He said we could just both lean in and see what happens .....

I wound up listening to Pink Floyd the last million hours .... big mistake ......total mess by the time I got home!

I had "wish you were here" and "shine on you crazy diamond" on repeat..... not such a great idea....

Did he ever............................hope this leads to some comments on existing thyroid nodules ( one article suggested 70% pop have varying degrees of them). My kindly GP has a sister who had a very nasty reaction to an IVP (iv iodine).
Does oral iodine set sensitive folk up for over reaction should they require iv iodine?
Basic patient filled questionnaires prior to these procedures do ask are you allergic to iodine...is there a different reaction between oral and iv doses?
Just how many folk have bad reactions and is it only to the iv stuff?
Our endocronologist reckons oral is just as bad as iv....wth
How does one ensure that one doesn't have pre existing states that will trigger a hyper active thyroid? Should an ultrasound of the thyroid be mandatory to exclude those folk who could have these emergency events?
That should just about cover all my questions

### Inger — 2014-05-11T12:44:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-126280>

Maybe eat Audrey -eat until you are no hungry no more! And a bit more!

You do not want to know what I did yesterday..  something very bad!

I was stuck... in my mind.. and desperate
so I bought me a Grange - the only one they had in our wine shop. And I had so much of it - 3 glasses... with lots and lots of grassfed beef tongue I had gently cooked medium on the wooden stove the day before. I sliced it up cold from the fridge, put French butter on it like a sandwich and ate. It was a huge tongue and I had half of it at 8;30 PM in the night with that delicious red wine. And a bit earlier I had a glass of the wine too with 250 g organic Roquefort cheese and French butter.
I broke all my rules.
But it was emergency. And I was out all day long fixing the fence barefoot in the wet grass, many many hours. and had 7 oysters au naturel for breakfast with raw salmon. Maybe it saved me because I felt great today morning...?!
Sometimes I just get the urge to eat. And I do! Maybe I lost so many electrons because of the stress, so I needed to fill up very seriously?

I would say load up on electron dense food and water and sit in the ocean 7/24

((((hugs))))

### Stella — 2014-05-11T14:43:30+0300

Don't sweat it too much. Just as the tide goes out, it will come back in.

Give time for your body and spirit to settle down as you try to collect electrons. Soon you will be back at equilibrium.

Good things and wonderful people are being readied now for your future. New family and new places. Just enjoy the trip and smell the roses along the way.

And happy Mother's Day mom! I'm glad you've adopted me as your daughter. Couldn't wish for a better mother!!!

### caroline — 2014-05-16T11:44:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-127198>

Holey heck .... I love his voice too ...and his body ...and his thighs......and his thoughts......

### caroline — 2014-05-16T11:58:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-127201>

We are having some beautiful weather ... around 23 - 24C ...but I am still cold all the time...... crazy.

I stood in the surf and talked with a woman for such a long time ..... about electrons and photons and seafood and how we come from a star and how important it is to smile and be positive and touch others and care and friendships and meditation ....and how good real butter is! We shared a huge hug - even tho we will likely never see each other again..... just simply the most gorgeous afternoon...

It was lovely to find a kindred spirit and chat for awhile.

I almost forgot ....... we are both crazy for George Clooney!!!

### caroline — 2014-05-17T09:20:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-127304>

another great day .... for very different reasons ...for reasons of vanity I guess.

I took myself of to a clinic because I have been feeling lousy and suspected a urinary tract infection.

I didn't sleep all night because of pain ..and a heavy heart. I fiddled around for ages - thinking I would feel better at any minute and wouldn't need to go.

Finally -the pain was getting worse and worse ...and so without even a shower or fixing my hair .... I walked down to the clinic.

The lovely Doc called me into his office and kept looking at me ...and then asked me my age...\[why do Docs always do that???? JK??\]

He said he had looked at my age three times and then said how I didn't at all look my age..... so now I am Stella's twin sister

can you image if I had had a shower???

Is it okay to take your Doc home?

### caroline — 2014-05-17T09:56:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-127318>

I forgot one thing ....I mentioned to this lovely Doc about anti aging ....and he said he didn't believe in it.

I was quite surprised so asked him about it .... he believes in aging gracefully etc.

I asked him about menopausal women etc ....and he does support BHRT etc. --but doesn't know alot but keeps reading and learning.

He gave me pen and paper to write down books for him! you just know what I wrote down!!!

### Stella — 2014-05-17T17:26:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-127356>

It's hard to tell us apart!!

Isn't it amazing how we can turn back the clock by paying attention to simple but important details. Like many people say we are meant to age and go through menopause - that's total BS!! Could you imagine how much better this world would be if BHRT would be standard of care for everyone. Oh, but right that would cut out big pharma profits and then I wouldn't have a job!

### caroline — 2014-05-28T09:22:42+0300

I am sitting at the yacht basin and eating oysters and watching the sun go down.... Almost heaven.....

### Lahelada — 2014-05-28T15:42:22+0300

### caroline — 2014-06-02T05:50:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-129317>

I am sitting in a Starbucks in Sydney ..... Contemplating life and some wonderful friends.....

I was going to say "new " friends .....but that simply isn't the case ..... Quantum entanglement and all that...

A voice inside my head has been telling me to go to Starbucks today ..... I am learning to trust the voice in my head and just do it....

I don't know where all this is going to take me....but I couldn't be more excited!

I love the new blog and I love where we are all going .... I love that I get the overall picture .....

I love that I can't wipe the smile off my face!

### caroline — 2014-06-02T05:53:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-129318>

I forgot to say that I haven't been in a Starbucks in about seven years. ..... Except for Orlando where I made a couple of coffee runs!

I also never stop for coffee when I am here.....

I am learning to just enjoy the ride ..... And go where life takes me........

I just need to hope it isn't a shoe shop!

### caroline — 2014-06-03T08:47:11+0300

I wonder when I am going to start actually liking these things???

At least now .... I have the life guards mostly to myself!

Every cloud does have a silver lining.....

### caroline — 2014-06-06T06:14:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-129918>

It has been raining and cold ......and I seem to be going thru this period of self reflection.... it is pretty amazing how mother nature/the universe takes our hand and guides us - if we are willing to listen and be open to all possibilities and just do and just be ...

I look back and see all this quantum entanglement and all this quantum magic and I am in complete awe of where life has taken me when I actively engage and also surrender to all the possibilities.

This journey - my life - has been so awesome .....and I can't wait to see what is next!

I am also learning patience ...and to just be....

patience is easier if you have a glass of wine and a cigar ....who knew!!!

more wine tonite..........

I am overwhelmed by all the magic .....

That word - "magic" - is no longer woo woo .... it is very, very real.....

I wonder what the universe has in store for me ......

I wonder who I will connect with tomorrow? ...what amazing person will cross my path? what will we share?

everyone has a story ...and I intend to listen .....

### Josh — 2014-06-06T21:28:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-130013>

It is just a picture to go with the "Books" joke....seems to say it all........perhaps we should change "books" to "blogs" then it would have more relevant implications in the current COE's.....

### caroline — 2014-06-07T09:08:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-130080>

I am in Sydney today .....the first thing I see is a huge van painted in wild hot pinks etc advertising adult toys!
It said, in very large letters, we deliver to your door discreetly!!! Really???
When I pointed it out to everyone at the light .....we had quite an amusing conversation!

The trains aren't running between certain points because a melt down today. - so I spent an hour talking to a couple of lovely ladies. People have come from all over to sing at the opera house tomorrow.

They have to pay $300.00 and then they are sent the music a few months ahead of time so they can learn it.
The men practiced this morning and the ladies this afternoon and then they get together tomorrow at the opera house.
People, literally, from all over the world will come and spend a few days in Sydney to do this.

Then I talked to a young couple who have moved to Melbourne from the UK. It is the gorgeous young man's birthday.
They are going to Watsons Bay for seafood tomorrow ...and sleeping overnite at the zoo.
How awesome! And I got to give him a birthday hug! Just a beautiful young couple.

And I got a Starbucks for the trip home .... With heavy cream.....

### Stella — 2014-06-07T20:41:42+0300

### caroline — 2014-06-10T08:01:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-130330>

really cold and rainy here .....not a fan.
I know it is early - but decided to have a glass of wine and just enjoy a lazy day....

my very own n=1 ...for today anyway.....

### caroline — 2014-06-16T01:01:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-130943>

Thanks Spin .....was that the webinar with Michael Neurt? or another one?

How are you doing?

### caroline — 2014-06-16T09:36:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-130993>

I have had such a lovely day.
I sat at a Starbucks and had a coffee with heavy cream ...I am learning to just ....be.

Then I took myself to an oysterbar and had some oysters and some shrimp.....lovely.
I have been told by someone special to treat myself more ...to do things for myself. I wasn't sure what that meant at first .....I still don't like oysters!!

I had fun talking to a lovely lady sitting at the bar as well. She is from Victoria and they have snow. They have a beautiful property with self catering .....She had so much positive energy.
She said she always sits at the bar and treats herself and there is always somebody interesting to talk to. I have never done this before ....until Disney and had so much fun that nite - definitely need to do that a lot more!

Then I was returning a nightgown and the lady beside me said ...oh that is so lovely maybe I will get it. She picked it up to have a better look and said ...oh no.... I couldn't get it - my tits would be hanging out!

This woman was such a revelation ...she lives in Bali ....and comes to Sydney to shop! That is completely opposite from Ozzies ..... They live to go to Bali to go shopping!! She said the quality there is shit! She was a pretty colorful character!
She asked me if I was a healer.....

I am learning to go downstream with the flow ......I feel very Abundant these days....

It is amazing to me .....some one sees you with their eyes and hears you with their ears.. ....but there is all this positive vibration we seem to give off .... Quantum magic for sure!

### caroline — 2014-06-16T15:04:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-131014>

so Spin .... I am not clear ......do you earn money racing? Have you followed Margo on the forum? She goes to bike races all over the world.
Yes - I guess I do think life goes in cycles .... what would you retrain as? a neurosurgeon? I hear there is good money in that!!

I am just trying to relax and, luckily, I have always been a positive person ....and enjoy everything that comes my way.

I definitely believe in quantum magic!

Do you know the book ....Ask and it is given: learning to manifest your desires .....by Esther Hicks

### Lahelada — 2014-06-16T15:24:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-131016>

Good morning from here!
Love reading about your people encounters. Here it is much easiee to get people to talk than in an Anglo environment. You must have great energy emanating from you.

### caroline — 2014-06-17T01:58:42+0300

### caroline — 2014-07-04T10:56:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-133125>
