malbec and cigars are part of the JK RX ...right???? I am pretty sure ..... I may have misunderstood......

I went back to the Fish market for breakfast before going home ....oysters of course!
and a mandatory stop at Starbucks ...making new memories - and remembering old ones .....

being conscious and aware and happy ..... people do notice in a huge way.

I keep getting mega watt smiles from men, women and children and lovely greetings ........ I am not invisible anymore!

Haha, I was feeling just fine in a t-shirt when I went to visit grandma (caroline) on Tuesday. I even jumped into the ocean and was radiating heat like crazy after that.

Then grandma gave me a chocolate bar to take home that Tuesday, then I had a coffee with cream on Wednesday with her at Starbucks, and that night I was feeling cold and still haven't warmed up completely today  Coffee and chocolate have been my enemy for the last year now, so again another n=1 case in avoiding certain stressors that do you in. (though the 12 hour overnight bus ride back from Sydney to Melbourne didn't help either )

I believe I was one of the first people who used the word grandma when I made that joke about Little Red Riding Hood following your several red speedo comments, caroline. It was meant to be funny and I sincerely appologize as I sense that you might have felt offended by it.

Aging is something that none of us can avoid, but we can embrace it by trying to do everything we can to be as healthy as we can. There is an old saying "Growing old is mandatory, growing up is optional." All that really matters is how you feel and how you see yourself.

### caroline — 2014-07-11T10:16:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-133909>

I realised the last few days that I have been in a little slump ..... I thought I was cocooning and taking care of myself. The reality is - I stopped all my forward motion/momentum .... and just got stagnant and stale.

Really - when all is said and done - I got lazy and was feeling sorry for myself..... not my finest moment!

Some days it is a little hard to put yourself out there .....when you are alone .... but what's the alternative?

If we want to be the best version of ourselves and keep the forward momentum - we need to work at it!

Consequently - I have had such a fun afternoon ... just silly things really - but lots and lots of laughing.

I stopped at the eye glass place because something has been the matter with my glasses for ages. The woman I know there said I should get tested again.... Do you want the man or woman? I said I have had both - which one do you think? She says - you need to see Trevor ... he is eccentric like you! She is a former hippie with the combi van and marihuana etc. I have never had that - It is on my bucket list ...... Stella???

I love, love, love being called eccentric!! we were laughing like idiots - someone had to tell us to be quiet.

Then I met the most lovely couple at the grocery store - They had a cart full of wine! we wound up standing outside in the sunshine and talking for an hour - and they live close by ...and I am going for wine!

They had been to Mardi gras last year and love it - and I told them that apparently you are allowed to wonder around on Bourbon street with just beads on! .... and they are going to Miami after xmas.....

Once you get momentum .... keep it moving ........it is totally awesome.....

It probably doesn't seem like much ..... and yet it is everything! and you make such an impact! people kept stopping and chatting and laughing and joking and we collected quite a crowd.

So - you can feel sorry for yourself .... or you can say WTF .... and dream of one day wearing only beads on Bourbon Street at mardi gras ....and drinking lots of bourbon

or some other crazy ass dream .... the sky is the limit ...shoot for the stars!

I am learning and getting better and better .... but I have had so much help....

### Stella — 2014-07-11T15:34:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-133931>

Glad you had such a different day. There's nothing like having contrasting experiences to help you figure out what you really want. Looks like you have decided to choose fun experiences involving interesting people. Good for you! Keep wanting those experiences. Who knows who you will meet. Like you say the sky's the limit.

Hey about your Mardi Gras plans, friends don't let friends walk alone wearing only beads on Bourbon St!! I'm there with you, twin sisters!!! But we've got to do it right. Got to get the body paint and a nice mask or headdress. Sounds like we will have loads of fun. But we've got to be careful or else we will end up on Jack's FB page.

Have lots for fun with your new friends, especially when they had a cart full of wine.

Keep those positive thoughts. You've got the momentum and remember to use your intuition, your heart will never lead you in a wrong direction. Can't wait to hear what happens next.

### caroline — 2014-07-12T01:25:46+0300

### caroline — 2014-07-12T01:29:58+0300

### caroline — 2014-07-12T12:02:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-134083>

I had quite an interesting conversation with a lovely young woman in my favorite store in Sydney today.

This young woman had the most gorgeous and spectacular boosoms I have ever seen ..... so I had to tell her!

In the course of this conversation .... I came to realise that this incredible endowment was 2 years old!

so we had a great discussion about surgeons and how you pick a competent one and costs and recovery times and pain etc.

she went from an A cup - to an E cup and wanted to go bigger........

I just can't begin to tell you how spectacular she looked in a T-shirt!!! It made me think about the thread about girls in dresses and jack had talked about a woman who had come into a restaurant and every eye - male and female was on her.

This girl had the most divine breasts .......

My mind has not been on the blogs today .....at all......and I have been getting a blow by blow of some music and some wine and a swimming pool and ......... from a certain crazy Cuban.......

That was probably an unfortunate choice of words ...

### Stella — 2014-07-12T16:50:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-134098>

^^^^ if you came to Miami and went to SOBE you could probably find at least a couple dozen girls who looked better than the girl you ran into within a couple block radius. They've made the plastic surgeons loads of money. It's so funny that even my teenage sons know who's been fixed up and who is natural. So easy to tell esp when they are lying down on the sand!!!

And yes life is so much better since I've been on my journey to optimal. Perhaps even better because my DH has started to make changes and reading 50 shades. The wine tastes better, the music sounds better and improved redox does make a significant difference on the intimate dimension of relationships.

lol Audrey you are tough! It has been around 30 degree C here last day, uh! but nice! I just take cold short showers when I feel overheated, just, they are not so cold but they really cool me down

You are way more cold adapted than me now Audrey - maybe I ned to come down under for some CT  that would be so much fun.......

I think i have a problem  i have never missed someone less in my life... I just enjoy it so immensely to be alone  I wonder what is wrong! errr.... i feel like a 5 yo that is in love with the nature and the world - but why don't I miss a partner? Kind of weird, seriously! But I have to say I have never been happier in my life.... i might have to accuse Jack
maybe I am traveling back in time and now i am at 3 yo age...haha I like this

### prAna303 — 2014-07-20T22:38:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-135001>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=134988)
>
> i have never missed someone less in my life... I just enjoy it so immensely to be alone  I wonder what is wrong! errr.... i feel like a 5 yo that is in love with the nature and the world - but why don't I miss a partner? Kind of weird, seriously! But I have to say I have never been happier in my life.... i might have to accuse Jack  maybe I am traveling back in time and now i am at 3 yo age...haha I like this

I sort of feel the same.

Many of my friends can not stand to be alone, it gives them so much stress so they search for substitute. I think of it as a thermodynamic issue. When you reach a threshold, a certain amount of energyflow you become in sync not just in yourself but also the world around you. Now you start to see that we perhaps can go long times without a so called serious partnership...

Ok, how i feel... I see all my short times with girls as a bonus, the sublime and vivid extra bonus that makes life, outstanding. Do i miss a family? Some think it is sad when i say, no... Have i felt and feel love? Yes, so many times and so many short bursts of that energy lift, just like the sun gives away flares from time to time.

Enjoy those moments without any regret, see them for what they are... When you meet the one that lifts you, you also lift then consider going the extra mile...

We just have to hold on ... we are on the ride of our life! enjoy the heck out of it!

### caroline — 2014-07-22T05:11:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-135159>

I think, for me, all the beauty in life is meant to be shared with those you love .... to me love amplifies, energises, completes everything.

But who is to say what is right for each one of us...... I just know, for me, when I see something beautiful or hear some beautiful music or have an amazing glass of wine ...... it is so much better when it is shared with someone you love.

But .... I have really just come to understand and appreciate and be really conscious of this the last 18 months or so.

I suppose before that - I was just making the motions of living ..... now I want to get down and dirty

I want to be fully conscious and engaged ...... easy - but not simple!

some things I know for sure ..... when you really look at some one and engage them - even if only for a second - both of our worlds change slightly ... a smile is everything - and I don't want to ever stop!

When I am really conscious - I am not invisible .... people always, always respond - and I can't get enough!

### Stella — 2014-07-22T23:35:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-135222>

I think I might have a dress in that particular color of red! And it does have the desired effect as well

This is a great discussion about enjoying life. To Inger's point you almost have to revert to a child like version of one's self inorder to enjoy life. Think of it no worries and no hang ups to keep you from living life and savoring every moment. Too bad that most adults are so beaten down by life and poor health that they aren't capable of living and enjoying the moments of beauty and simple happiness. The important thing here is that the mind needs to be working properly so that you can make that conscious decision to live in the moment and fully enjoy it. Rather than enjoy brief moments of happiness, I've decided to savor and enjoy every moment!

we are back to square one - yet again ..... pay all this forward.

Thankyou Peter .....indeed - seasons come and go. I am fortunate to feel better and better ...it seems to be all about seeing the world with new eyes and an open heart and mind.

I got the best "living day" present here ...from all of you...and it is a present that keeps giving and giving....

there is no end in site .......just a fantastic journey of discovery. I have discovered Mother nature in all her glory.
I missed so much for so long..... too long - but now I know better!

I have said this to a few people today .... I have had a fantastic life - has it been perfect? of course not - but it has been amazing .... and looking forward to much, much more!

I am in awe of all I have received ...from everyone here and really everyone that crosses my path every single day.

and then there is the brain pain.... awesome! keep it coming!

### caroline — 2014-08-02T12:46:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-136493>

I have a funny story about a young woman at a bus stop today. She started asking me questions about how would she know if a man was interested in her - and if she should make the first move, and if I was her mother what would I suggest.

I kept thinking how bizarre - why would she be asking me? I have been deeply in love with only one man my whole life.

Anyway, I warmed to the subject - as you do.... I started to remember some things that Jack has told us about how men communicate etc. etc. - I thought I did a pretty good job!! especially in light of my total lack of experience.

As I have often said ...it is quite hilarious the stuff I learn and remember here!

But - I guess it is all about how to live a life.....

### prAna303 — 2014-08-02T14:10:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-136498>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=136493)
>
> I have a funny story about a young woman at a bus stop today. She started asking me questions about how would she know if a man was interested in her - and if she should make the first move, and if I was her mother what would I suggest.

A sure sign of today...

Most humans live their life connected but also disconnected. We have a mismatch here, what once was basics for us is higher science today. How many do not know what they feel? If you base your feelings upon a platform that is made of social media without any real life testing, can you know what things mean?

I have never really made contact with girls the chatting way, neither old school or new age. I use a single tool all the time, i make many feel discomfort, it is a good test to see their potential...

With my eyes i tell them what i want with them... It must be a game between us otherwise i drop it there, words are so simple in comparison to seeing and talking that way. Therefor it takes a while until we chat but that trip is special, vivid and sublime...

To make it simple, if i want that girl who is looking at me i simply tell them that with eye contact and thru my body language. If she is in a good state, she understands it otherwise she just think i am a lunatic.

### caroline — 2014-08-10T12:27:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-137471>

I am contemplating tonite - what I have learned here.....

I am smoking a cigar ...... who ever would have thought I would like cigars! I am not good at it tho ... need some help with my technique! I did find out tonite - if you bend it - you can't really smoke it! As with other things in life - things don't work if they are bent!

I also discover that I really like to inhale ....that can't be good....

I also now love red wine ..... as well as cognac and brandy - my palate needs some refining tho.......

Y'all have given me lots and lots of great music to listen to ....... my fave being - shine on you crazy diamond - but it makes me cry... and "wish you were here" that really does a number on me....

and then there is all that other really great and really important stuff ....... and y'all know what I mean by that!

I can't forget that I now love dresses ..... my skinny jeans are sitting in the cupboard unused and unloved.

still haven't found that really important f\*\*k me red dress .....

Is any of this what Dr. K. intended????

This all makes me smile .... like really, really a big ass smile ...and yes - I think that is what Dr. K. intended!

If I am wrong ..... to bad ...too late ......I am addicted to lots of things now ...and I have learned it all here!!!

BTW ...what I am addicted to and in love with - and I am sure you all have figured it out... is Life....

I hope this has happened to all of you too!

p.s. .... I even love the science ...like really love the science - even if it tends to go in one ear and out the other!
as Dr. K. has said " the lites are on but is anyone home?" I may never forgive him for that - but that is another story for another day....

### Stella — 2014-08-11T04:28:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-137525>

I think living and loving life is what we should be doing, but many people are so worried about the future that they neglect to live and enjoy the moment. I was just discussing that with my father and step mother. Most people are busy preparing for the future and don't fully enjoy the only time they have, which is the here and now.

I'm glad you have really found what lights your fire, what you aren't willing to live without, because it adds to the enjoyment of the here and now. And that you are experiencing and trying new things. This is what keeps us young.

As for that f..k me red dress, we've got to go shopping together soon. And share some good wine and nice cigars with you. But you've got to move closer!!

### Michael Phils — 2014-08-11T08:51:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-137534>

Inhaling cigars haha now that is one way to choke I guess.

Looking forward to a cohiba 66 tonight. Or the ryj short churchill. Those and a partagas serie d no 4 are my go to cigars. Been smoking 2 every day for the last 15 years now. Best cigar I ever had was a 1985 dunhill estupendo. Dont expect to find any however. I have one box left from the two I had.

### caroline — 2014-08-11T10:13:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-137536>

> [Michael Phils said:](/goto/post?id=137534)

Best cigar I ever had was a 1985 dunhill estupendo. Dont expect to find any however. I have one box left from the two I had.

you don't inhale? I am such a newbie at so many things!

I got such a nice high .... I really liked it! Are these Cuban cigars you speak of? I have so much to learn.....

I bought some Sambuca tonite .... another one of my favorites!

Maybe I am going in the wrong direction???

I am now eating exclusively oysters and shellfish ...hope that mitigates some of the damage!

### caroline — 2014-08-11T11:11:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-137538>

I met a gentleman in the grocery store thus afternoon. He was looking at all my cans of oysters on the belt. I told him it was brainfood. He said he was taking a memory course at the university of Melbourne - using his computer ...and he was doing really well at it! he was so proud!

He looked very, very healthy and told me he doesn't take any meds at all, and we had a very nice conversation about seafood - which he hates! He does like sardines - so I convinced him to have a can of sardines every morning for breakfast ...he said he would do that - it was a really good idea!!

He is 90 years old! ... just lovely...

he made my day!

I chatted with another woman who I have met before at the beach ...she is in her mid eighties and she is a real going concern!

Next year she is going to do the "city to surf" run ...she is in training now - walking every day ....I will join her!

This is a fun run - 14K ...from Sydney to Bondi ...and it attracts serious runners as well as crazies! Guess which category I fit in???
It attracts more than eighty thousand people!

### Jude — 2014-08-11T12:21:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-137542>

Didn't a 27yo die as he reached the finish the other day??? Please don't lead out!!!!!

Sardines were on special for the first time EVER here ...so I purchased 30tins of the olive oil kind. All was well until I arrived at the checkout lineup...with my ugly big blue blockers on over my specs.
A chap in front was patiently waiting as the check out was finishing the first customer, he kinda snuck a surprised look at me ... so I smiled and decided to do a Caroline...well I tried.......and started unloading my stuff onto the belt AND the sardines.

He obviously wasn't interested in the why of the glasses at night ....but was very pleasant.......we didn't keep an eye on the checkout lady .....who then reached over and started to include my sardines in his order.......

Oh, what a scream......the ugly glasses were weird enough but the look on his face when he saw all the sardines being packed with his order was priceless. I didn't even try......I was laughing too much.......the girl at the check out thought I'd made good on a special but wondered why anyone would eat them. Giggled all the way home

### Lahelada — 2014-08-11T12:39:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-137544>

Good morning to the Australia All Stars!
Caroline,what an amazing encounter with that 90 yr old gentleman.
@[Jude](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/904/) wow 30 tins is hardcore. Kudos. You must have got that guys full attention if he did not notice that . Respect!

### caroline — 2014-08-12T09:44:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-137633>

I went to a charity luncheon today.... all the Aussies may know the guest speaker - Kathy Lette
\- she has written a bunch of books and used to write for sitcoms in LA. One of her books - Puberty blues has been made into a TV show and another has just been sold to the BBC.

She was such a scream ...it was mostly women - about 300 of them! She has a very unique speaking style that leaves nothing to the imagination! The one thing that sticks in my mind is that she thinks women should go bush! I really wish I had taken notes....

anyway ...the important part .... George Clooney once asked her out on a date - and she turned him down! George Clooney's fiance works for her husband! and she will be invited to the wedding.

I stopped to tell her how much I had enjoyed her and she gave me a hug ...so I have touched someone who has touched George Clooney! whaddayathink?

I know, I know ...I gottagetalife!

I am trying...... I think I need help!

one other thing - I bought a tangerine colored sweater and wore it today ...it felt really awesome!

### caroline — 2014-08-15T10:16:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-137890>

Dr.K. wrote some things in another thread today ....that I want to keep track of -

These are things I struggle with ....sometimes a lot. Some days seem so much harder than others ..- so I want to remember these things for me ....

These are in order of how they apply to me ....

#1 live your imagination - not your history

#2 I am not the product of my circumstances - I am the product of my decisions

#3 I teach people how to treat me - by what I will allow ..... I actually think this should be my #1

#4 The main thing is to keep the main thing - the main thing

#5 Trust is the glue of life

#5 You have to decide what your highest priorities are ...and to have the courage to pleasantly, smilingly, unapologetically to say "no"

#6 love is a verb. Love - the feeling - is the fruit of love the verb ....or - our loving actions.

It is funny how - some days - maybe even most days ...it is really easy to remember all the above.....

but - why is it some days ....I can't remember one single one of them ..... and wonder why nothing feels right and my whole world is upside down.........

### caroline — 2014-08-15T11:27:38+0300

I am pretty sure I have said this somewhere before ..... I have spent a lifetime being the "nice"person - never wanting to rock the boat, always worried about what others were going thru. never wanting to hurt anyone or say the wrong thing ....

### Inger — 2014-08-15T12:02:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-137897>

well, that is me too. I am by far not healed yet but i start to painfully clear see my flaws
that is also why i am not going to look for anything new on the partner front because i am just not wise enough yet. I do have learned if there is anything desperate about a man... run! Because there are plenty of those! This is a painful stage to go through... but it is way less painful when i do not push certain triggers with bad decisions

> I am not the product of my circumstances - I am the product of my decisions

how true. For me i already know a few things i should NOT do - what many others on the forum can do without ill effects, but I cant. And when i do - i suffer poor decision making and there it goes again. It is so hard to have to go this way so alone... to have to totally find out your own N0=1 it is so easy to get distracted.
it is darn hard work. But it is so much worth it... and the end is so sunny and beautiful - when i only make the right decisions and not letting what others do impact me!

I am seriously extremely broken - in my brain! I cannot even have a glass of wine.... lets not talk about dairy. This what I have come to realize. Very humiliating to be honest.
These are things i can control. There are many things i cannot, but i have to control the things i can. It is enough.
The more silent it gets around.... the more you see the cracks in the ceilings and why....

### caroline — 2014-08-16T11:08:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-138003>

This afternoon I went to see a local production of Phantom of the Opera at our performing arts centre.

I wasn't terribly interested in going because I have seen it so many times - except I love the dear friend I was going with ...and I left it up to her what we would see.

It was such an amazing performance ..... the music of course is so beautiful ...and it just fills your heart and soul to brimming over..... and this was local talent!

How sad if I had missed this..... It gave us so much joy to share this with one another ... my heart was aching from this beautiful music .... one should never miss an opportunity to participate fully and completely - who knows what the universe has in store for us at any given moment!

we just never know what mother nature is going to shower us with .......She certainly smiled on me today - but then, she does every single day!

I so easily get stuck in a rut .... even tho I know so much better...

I think I need to continually fine tune .....every single thing!

I need to remember that I am a product of my imagination ..... and not get lazy....

### nicld — 2014-08-17T01:58:59+0300

Phantom of the Opera is my favorite musical ever. The music just moves me. Glad you went.

### caroline — 2014-08-17T14:31:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-138087>

It has been a very rainy and windy day today...... I read back thru my journal a bit - it is quite something to see all the personal renovation.

I often thought I was just talking about silly stuff - and I was .....but the reality is that I think I need to be silly for a change.
I think I was always way too uptight and too serious.

Why did it take me so long to figure that out? Everyone's life has lots and lots of challenges for a multitude of reasons.

I used to admire people that could get drunk and let everything go ...... I could never even do that!

I really admire people who can work really hard and be totally focused ...and then play really hard and be totally silly and in the moment.

I think Dr. K. does that really well ..... I suppose he has too because of his day job - he has to be able to let things go - to be able to continue. As he always tells me - not everyone makes it ...he has to be able to get that to move forward.

I think not everyone learns this lesson .....It has certainly taken me long enough. I think maybe I finally got to the edge - a tipping point ... a place where you realise you have no control over anything and you might just as well say WTF ....and enjoy the rest of the ride! I must say tho - I had a wonderful safety net ...and that made it possible.

there were no thought processes taking place .... I can only see this in retrospect .... I did not see the safety net - I didn't know it was there.

For me - it seemed to take lots of coconut rum and lots of excellent wine and a cigar .... and Stella and Josh and Jack and Mickey mouse and a trip to the other side of the world ..... why? who the heck knows......... why was that the tipping point?? I guess a journey of a million miles begins with one step......

So - why do we spend our life worried about every single thing? ..... or is this a woman thing?

Why did I not dance at every given opportunity? why did I not wear a dress? why didn't I go without underwear more often?

I don't know what the answers are .... but I know that I can and will do all those things and much, much more.....
now that I know better .... I am doing so much better!

It is so great that we can talk about all this stuff here ..... we can see in 3D and living colour what an optimal life should look like!

Thanks Dr. K for providing this platform and shining your flash lite ..... and being an example of a regular guy living an extraordinary life ...because that is what you choose to do. You are willing to do the work, you are wiling to remain curious and ask the questions..... and you know how to have a great time!

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-17T20:20:23+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-17T20:39:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-138096>

Jeremy and I went out to a fabulous dinner dressed like we lived in a refrigerator box. When we walked into the restaurant all the high society of NOLA just gasped. Then when the owners and maitre d came up to us and hugged me and shock our hands and Nick the bartender high fivin' us while they just watched this unfold. Even a badly dressed person can fill a room with energy. Then they wisked us off to the courtyard with no wait or ressies and already had a bottle of wine on the table because they knew what I was going to drink and eat. I did not have to order for me. I ordered for Jeremy..............They all had looks on their faces that were classic during this and Jeremy was unaware of what was unfolding. The old lady behind him kept lookign at his mohawk and dreadlocks and wife beater T shirt. LOL

My advice, is create waves and ruffle feathers of people. Disturb their thoughts with your energy. Watch the effect. We sat down in the courtyard with people dressed in 5000 dollar suits and all they could do was see the crazy food plates we kept ordering and the spectacular wine we drank. By the end of the night one of the men in an Armani suit dressed to the nine's stopped me in the john and asked me what I did and what Jeremy did for a living and the guy was just stunned as he urinated next to me. His face said it all. I told him to forget pretension bro and just live your life and your terms. I asked him did he create more energy in a room when he came or left that room? I told him you felt compelled to come and speak with me as I am pissing for some reason? I guess he felt that energy when we both came into this joint and wanted to know its source. Screw what people think. With that, 4 ladies came to our table, one was my wife with her friends and did round two with them. We finished and went to the 'alternative side' of the French Quarter where my new place will be and I took Jeremy into a cross dressing bar. Our new neighbors told us it was the best neighborhood tavern so we went to visit our neighbors...........and needless to say Jeremy was hit. It was not too crowded but Jeremy said he you'd never know that by how many times big huge ugly women with fake hair nails with deep voices had to touch him to slide past him..............LOL. Great night and we had breakfast this AM and just got some rays water and crab omelettes with cognac sauce.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-18T03:41:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-138127>

Jeremy is a welder of metals. I am a welder of science. He puts pieces together to make art and I put physics together with biology to make sense of life. When you look at another person, you decide to share with them things. These things are thoughts and all thoughts contain electrons. When you spend enough time with them you begin to bond or weld. You begin to see them as an extension of your thought or feelings. What the two of these people begin to think or feel then become magnetically connected in the present moment. This is truly how quantum entanglement of minds and thoughts happen in our species. Few people see it yet, but most sense it but find it hard to put into words.

I think it would have been more fun to see him figure it out for himself ...one day...

Will Jeremy do a sculpture for your new hang out? Could he use some of those coins for it?

That will give you even more positive energy/electrons from someone you have never met ....but he is an angel on your shoulder too.......and BTW - I do know that for sure!

### caroline — 2014-08-18T06:53:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-138138>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=138127)
>
> Jeremy is a welder of metals. I am a welder of science. He puts pieces together to make art and I put physics together with biology to make sense of life. When you look at another person, you decide to share with them things. These things are thoughts and all thoughts contain electrons. When you spend enough time with them you begin to bond or weld. You begin to see them as an extension of your thought or feelings. What the two of these people begin to think or feel then become magnetically connected in the present moment. This is truly how quantum entanglement of minds and thoughts happen in our species. Few people see it yet, but most sense it but find it hard to put into words.

I said earlier that I didn't know there was a safety net ..... but, of course, I definitely perceived it.

I can remember distinctly feeling the love and caring ....the safety net....

I can remember those feelings - acutely .....being magnetically connected.....being welded together ...the quantum entanglement. It is very, very real.

I can't remember much of events, or meals etc. ...... but all those feelings/thoughts will remain with me forever - it is like they just happened - One second ago.....a huge quantum treasure for life. They are permanently engraved....

Years ago - before jackkruse.com ..... I would have said I was in the right place at the right time - now I know so much better - so much more....

### caroline — 2014-08-18T07:45:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-138140>

We are having really horrible and wild weather......very heavy rain and crazy high winds......

so - I guess what I am saying ..... I have lots of time to think today .......

I have never, ever written anything in my life before ... I was never interested in keeping a diary/journal - not in the least...

I really don't know where all this is coming from.... I only know that I feel compelled for some reason.

It is like it has to come out .... like - really, really has to - as if I have no control. I have never experienced this before.
and - BTW - it is not that I feel compelled to share ....I am just doing it for me. I guess a lot like Josh - he has written heaps of stuff for us - but the reality is ...he is just working thru it for himself.

The last few nites - I had gone to bed - but I was so agitated because I hadn't said some things that were overpowering me .... and then just had to get up and write a few things down - I, literally, couldn't sleep until I did.

### prAna303 — 2014-08-18T08:44:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-138141>

“Growing old is unavoidable, but never growing up is possible. I believe you can retain certain things from your childhood if you protect them - certain traits, certain places where you don’t let the world go.”
\- Johnny Depp

Very much the same happens to me also... Being and living like a bum makes you stand out IF you are not acting it. When you simply have a strong presence in a room you make a shift, like dropping gasoline on open flames. Humans who accept nature as teacher, preacher, healer, destroyer and lover often have a strong flame...

Like i said before, it is not your look that matters, it is how you use energy and transfer it...

A small scene from the great movie - The Rum Diary

### caroline — 2014-08-18T10:01:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-138142>

Peter - you said something awhile ago that has really stuck with me ...and I need to remember....

I had said that I thought of you as mysterious - even tho you are always very open.

You said ....."When you are open for everything - it makes you quite unpredictable because others will not really know how you will react"

I have found this to be so true in my own life .......now that I am much more relaxed and easy - everyone finds me quite unpredictable ....they are always wondering what crazy thing I will do next! It is so much fun!

I just love being crazy and silly and in the moment and laughing for no reason .......whatever took me so long......

It really frustrates me tho ..... that it took me so long.....

the odd thing .... I am a pretty quiet person ..... but that makes it even better because something is obviously simmering below the surface!! that makes me mysterious! YEAH!

### prAna303 — 2014-08-18T10:29:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-138143>

> I have found this to be so true in my own life .......now that I am much more relaxed and easy - everyone finds me quite unpredictable ....they are always wondering what crazy thing I will do next! It is so much fun!

Perhaps this is also because you accept that life is not just a lot of "i must". When someone has loads of time compared to the one who measure you seems to be extravagant.

> I just love being crazy and silly and in the moment and laughing for no reason .......whatever took me so long......

I do not think it is being crazy and silly, that is in other eyes but just as a cat can play around with nothing, do you think it consider it self silly? Do not think so, it is us and others dogma that makes that label... I think it is very simple... You are alive.

> the odd thing .... I am a pretty quiet person ..... but that makes it even better because something is obviously simmering below the surface!! that makes me mysterious! YEAH!

In modern time, the one who acts living will always be mysteroius... When the herd is bowing to the new queen of technology a living creature walks by with grace.

### caroline — 2014-08-19T01:04:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-138227>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=138154)
>
> I hated the poem from line 3 onwards ..... really pushed my buttons  I've re-written it from the perspective of my own n=1, but its raw, personal, and probably not right to post it in Caroline's journal ..

please share Sue ....

I remember a similar poem years and years ago and I have never forgotten it...

the line I particularly remember ..... to go barefoot more often ....to go barefoot earlier in the spring and later in the fall.
to me - that said it all .... and how apropos now ....
It was about living a life .... not half living....
to throw caution to the wind ..... I have been trying to learn this lesson my whole life!
I realise I have been too serious my whole life ...... too much baggage that I should have shed long ago ....

Not nearly enough wine and dancing and music and loving....and going barefoot!
not nearly enough red dresses in my life......

### Clayton — 2014-08-19T01:17:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-138233>

### caroline — 2014-08-19T01:31:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-138241>

> [Clayton said:](/goto/post?id=138233)
>
> I really like your outlook Caroline..im working on adopting a similar philosophy

You are lovely and young ...don't waste time worrying about things you can't control ..... please take a page out of Jack's playbook!

I once asked Jack how is it that he is so wise since he is quite young.

He said when you grow up in New York City ..... life is compressed ...and you learn early to squeeze life's lemons - hard and often!

I am paraphrasing .... I am sure he said it much better!

Dance at the very edge of your life! Do every crazy thing you can think of ..... and then do more!

### caroline — 2014-08-20T08:00:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-138371>

I went to see the movie ...The hundred foot journey....

What a charming, beautiful, lovely movie..... It brought back so many memories - I had tears thru most of it.
This was our life for so many years ...... all the wonderful people and great food and over the top experiences and really, really hard work .... all the life long friends we made.

Dr.K. reminded me of this the other day when he talked about going out to eat with Jeremy and how it doesn't matter what you wear ....but how your presence and energy and spirit and good will can fill a room. Jack and Jeremy sure do have that in spades.

The restaurant they went to is a trendy and up market spot with folks in expensive, designer clothes and rolex watches etc. and probably lots of Jimmy Choos in the crowd! At first glance people would have thought they don't belong because Jack does like to wear bad shorts!

Little did any of them understand ... when the owners and the whole entire staff saw Jack and Jeremy walk in ....they absolutely made their day! It is all about hospitality and taking care of people and making them welcome and comfortable and, of course, great food!

As a former restaurant owner ...what always mattered the most - the people who put the biggest grin on our faces - the people who lit us up .... were the "real" people.
I would never have noticed what any one wore .... it was their energy and kindness and generosity of spirit that would make our day when they walked in.

You can never do enough for people like this .....they are fun and happiness and sunshine all rolled up into one.

Some customers - would bring such negative energy - things were never cheap enough or fast enuf etc. .... and we would always wonder why they hadn't gone somewhere else. Thankfully they were few and far between.... but how sad for them.

so - our place was the opposite of where Jack and Jeremy went ..... quite plain.... think Carnegie Deli in New York City.

The last time I was there - we were sitting beside a cabbie clutching his meter .... and two tables over - were some famous actors. You went for the food!

Sometimes a yuppie would come in - dressed to the nines .....and be certain he had landed somewhere beneath him. It would be very hilarious to see the body language.

Over the years we met so many kind and generous and lovely and fun people from all walks of life ....I have had an extraordinary life!

This movie made me remember a lot of wonderful years ..... and all the people with the great energy....

People who we loved to see come in ... and hated to see leave..... the people we couldn't do enough for - and oddly - these same people were a huge and constant and loving support for us....

### caroline — 2014-08-20T08:25:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-138374>

and just so you know Dr. K. ......I really think your shorts are just fine ....... I just like to tease you - because I can!

Before the conference - I had asked Josh if people dressed up at these things ...... Josh said yes - and Jack would be wearing a jacket and tie.....

### Sue-UK — 2014-08-20T11:53:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-138376>

When I read the story of jack and Jeremy going into the restaurant I thought he was spending his surplus electrons, no hoarding, not leaking ones he couldn't afford to lose ..... No sucking any from anyone else ......

New experiences, reflecting on old ones, its interesting to observe what happens to the electron

### Lahelada — 2014-08-20T12:32:52+0300

Caroline, I would have loved your restaurant. It also happened jn my previous career . I have recently had this thing about adding up things and one of the
things I added up were the.
customers I came in contact with
and counselled. The figure was a
staggering 100000. Outstanding figures were the Finance director of a financia institution who we started a banana tree growing contest with,the actor with the well modulated voice we fought over to talk to and who

### Lahelada — 2014-08-20T12:34:37+0300

### caroline — 2014-08-21T01:24:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-138410>

What a lovely idea Lah ...to keep a total......

I couldn't possibly \[I don't think\] .... it was 35 years of intense, extravagant, over the top hard work and breathtaking moments of amazing people who really fed us - as much as we fed them.

All the food talk - which it turns out .... was really life talk....

I was lucky enough to do all this with my best friend ...and lover....

### caroline — 2014-08-21T01:45:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-138413>

I am so emotional all the time...... I just don't seem to know where my life is headed these days.....

Dr.K. is responsible for so much of this ..... unintentionally - but he always strikes a chord with me.....

Jack was talking the other day about a journal he has kept about all his wonderful wine - all the people he has shared wine with.

That is such an outstanding idea ...... I so wish I had kept a journal years ago...

I can remember thinking a few times that I should be ..... so many wonderful, crazy stories - so many wonderful, crazy people .......

it seems to me that our life - when you look back .... is on this insane trajectory. I was going to say - out of control .....but in retrospect - it is cosmic energy in the driver's seat!

How else can I explain that I took over designing our kitchen - I knew I had to be in the middle of everything ... not stuck in the back somewhere!
I knew if I didn't talk to all these strangers .... they would pass thru my life and I wouldn't have any idea about their passions, their loves, their desires, their family........ and so I did.......

It is a good thing this is a journal ...and you can enter - or not .... at your own risk!

I thought I had gotten all this out of my system ......

All these magic moments ..... I now see clearly how everything is so connected ... it is all so amazing - it takes my breath away......

I still don't understand how Jack is so wise..... He just knows how to pack a lifetime into every single day....

looking back - maybe I did too ..... that is a new thought! ..something to think about......

### notsoperfick — 2014-08-22T19:39:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-138581>

Is good advice No?
**If I Had My Life Over - I'd Pick More Daisies**

**Nadine Stair**
If I had my life to live over, I'd dare to make more mistakes next time. I'd relax, I would limber up. I would be sillier than I have been this trip. I would take fewer things seriously. I would take more chances. I would climb more mountains and swim more rivers. I would eat more ice cream and less beans. I would perhaps have more actual troubles, but I'd have fewer imaginary ones.

You see, I'm one of those people who lived sensibly and sanely, hour after hour, day after day. Oh, I've had my moments, and if I had to do it over again, I'd have more of them. In fact, I'd try to have nothing else. Just moments, one after another, instead of living so many years ahead of each day. I've been one of those persons who never goes anywhere without a thermometer, a hot water bottle, a raincoat and a parachute. If I had to do it again, I would travel lighter than I have.

If I had my life to live over, I would start barefoot earlier in the spring and stay that way later in the fall. I would go to more dances. I would ride more merry-go-rounds. I would pick more daisies.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-22T19:55:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-138588>

Good or bad advice are relative. “Is," "is," "is"—the idiocy of the word haunts us without us realizing it. If the word were abolished, human thought might begin to make sense. I don't know what anything "is"; I only know how it seems to me at this moment.”

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-22T19:57:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-138589>

With no fact as a reference, what is normative to one is purely a matter of preference to others. This is why we must use E=mc2 as our point of reference.

### yarralea — 2014-08-23T05:06:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-138652>

If I had my life over: I'd pick more daisies.

As I read this I started thinking of a satirical rewrite. So over the top negative, one would see the opposite message (of picking daisies) Kind of like a book "How to make a heart attack", in which the author gives detailed instructions how to :form kindey disease and failure, a heart attack etc and all chronic lifestyle diseases.

If I had my life over, I'd work harder, and longer hours- preferably in a job I hate with people I cannot stand. I would not play team sport, so much more cost effective to just buy a treadmill and watch hours of TV in the warm comfort with the horrors of the world on CNN to keep me company. I'd hang on to a heap of negative people in my life, just so I can feel better about myself. I'd eat whatever crap I could find, and if it were handed to me in my car from a little window and it was upsized, all the better- you only live once and I would just fix whatever issues arised after they got really bad by taking like a bootful of medications. In fact I'd take more party drugs too- why settle for a year or two of fun when you could decline into a zombie state over a decade. And I wouldn't get married and have any kids- that just weighs you down and holds you back from achieving the aforementioned list. I would have changed my education, not doing something as poorly paid as teaching- I would have earnt a masters in corporate finance, and maybe a double major in marketing. And I've read too many books, I could have avoided that too- I could have done just as well by reading NW magazine, or any other publication featuring fat and thin stars without make up. And I definitely would not have used my own intuition. Why bother? Just let the world take you along with everyone else!

hahaha

And I definitely would not have used my own intuition. Why bother? Just let the world take you along with everyone else!
>
> hahaha

But... you forgot staying up late every night to watch good stuff on TV and the internet... ;-)

### caroline — 2014-08-27T11:49:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-139030>

I suppose y'all know this ...and I guess I did too...

A bottle of cherry vodka doesn't make things better .... well it kinda does for a little while... It is kind of nice to be oblivious for a little while - I think it was more like a coma....

but the next morning ....oh my.... at the very least you feel like a mac truck has hit you ...and you still have the same problems you had the nite before....surprise, surprise!

But the very worst thing .... the most destructive thing ....it totally wrecks your sleep.

Years ago - I would have worried about how I felt in the morning - \[not that I have done this very often\]
but that is so incidental ...I deserved it - it was self inflicted!

but to wreck my sleep is a whole different ball game .... my sleep is now sacred. If you are gonna love me - you will have to let me sleep for at least 8 hours and preferably nine.

Absolutely non negotiable.... autophagy and all that jazz!

see Dr. K. ..... I do retain the important stuff.......

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-27T14:41:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-139033>

Thousands of candles can be lit from one candle, and the life of the candle will not be shortened. Happiness never decreases by being shared. Your appreciation of others leads to their elevation. Their latitude increases because your attitude builds them up. This leads others to increase the longevity of gratitude in others. This is part of the tapestry being woven here.........below your perception...............Eventually it becomes self evident when you have collected enough electrons. I cannot tolerate anything more than wine at this point in my life.

### caroline — 2014-08-30T10:53:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-139357>

I just re read an an old thread ...and this really hit home for me and I want to remember it ...

"I have nothing left to give - so I am ready to receive" I have been dancing around this thought for ages now - and couldn't quite grasp what happened and certainly couldn't put it into words properly ...but now I know

I have received incredible things/Treasures of love and friendships in so many ways since that happened.....it blows my mind how my life has really just started.....

I guess I shouldn't complain that it took me this long ..... as long as I got here....

It is quite something to be that vulnerable .... all the pretence falls away ...and you are really left with love - and it comes at you from all directions and keeps coming .....I am absolutely in awe of where I have landed....

everyday things happen to me now that are priceless beyond measure ..... it just makes me ache ... a good ache ..lol

### Inger — 2014-08-30T11:02:08+0300

wow Audrey... I love it all

could it be that they all together with all that DHA works kind of like a drug...??? and yeah, sure there are pain and sad moments but it is like it does not matter and it just belongs to it... as the joy is so overwhelming it covers it all anyways so you just are not scared of the pain at all

and yeah... when the heart swell and expands... it aches a bit but it is such a good ache  i know what you mean
i think it is my favorite thing

### caroline — 2014-08-30T11:46:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-139365>

Hi Inger .... yes - that is how I feel too. Of course there are sad moments and pain but it does seem like it doesn't matter - it just belongs ... part of this magic we call our life

I have this good ache most of the time these days...... it feels mesmerising to me. I just kind of relaxe into it.

I spent the day with a very dear friend yesterday ...and we just laugh the whole time ...over everything and anything.

The best thing .... she told me how happy and relaxed I was!

No one has ever accused me of that before!

I always had a million things to do, a million places to be...... now - I just smile

### caroline — 2014-08-30T12:31:12+0300

I just saw this in an email just now ....and it is exactly how I am feeling....

"How to live an orgasmic life"..... check! This is the word I have been looking for to describe how I am feeling. One would have thought that word would have instantly come to my mind!!!

"to be no one but yourself" ..... check! I have come to be really okay with - me... That is a first! well - except I do want a bigger brain.

### notsoperfick — 2014-08-30T16:14:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-139394>

We all have to please others for most of our lives because that way life ticks along better. When you get to live life for yourself it's no surprise that you feel differently about yourself and what you can do. No more meals to plan, no thinking how to free up some time to do what **you** want, go where you want to and stay there because you don't have to rush home. It's a freedom that you never thought you'd own and it has brought it's own euphoria. No doubt you miss a significant other because who doesn't like to share good things. If you have been there and done that though, maybe the gap isn't so big than never having shared a life and love. The danger is having that freedom but holding back because you've not given yourself permission to embrace it. Just my thoughts you understand.

### caroline — 2014-08-31T00:01:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-139412>

But not having some one to touch is excruciating .....

### Inger — 2014-08-31T10:08:39+0300

I know Audrey... i know exactly what you mean ^^
I always have loved touch... it has been so healing for me - extremely healing. I and my ex husband touched all the time! We always hold hands too when we walk.. we still do when we meet!
Now.. when i am alone. I just touch myself, or I touch trees, or I touch my cat, i take time every day to cuddle with her, she loves it, and is always on my lap... I touch the grass. I let the sun touch me... nude tanning is so so wonderful for this... very sensual! And the wind. It really really helps!
I love to be with small children, they love to touch and hug.. it is very therapeutic! It instantly calms me down and makes me feel so very good....

Audrey you need to join a dance course! I am sure there are some where you live? There you get a dance partner too.. who knows what more can happen..
Maybe you need to watch the newspaper for some dates...  I am so freaking scared to go for that blind date but i do not care - no pain no gain  i would be so fun to have an oyster baby

### notsoperfick — 2014-08-31T19:56:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-139476>

"But not having some one to touch is excruciating ....."

What is holding you back? In today's world there has never been so many people looking for someone to love yet they all seem to exist in a vacuum, hoping, dreaming, longing and wishing? Your freedom seems to be both exhilarating and a ball and chain? Are you fearful of making a mistake or the wrong choice? Nothing needs to be forever, one cake can be chosen, tasted and either embraced or rejected. If it isn't to your liking, don't take more than one bit, leave it and move on to the next cake that attracts. If you stay on the pavement gazing longingly through the shop window but never venturing inside you will always be cakeless. Be brave, try a few samples  It may be that after a few tasting sessions you realise that it wasn't cake that was missing in your life. Just don't stand still or wait so long the shop closes or sells out of cake.

### caroline — 2014-09-05T01:12:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-139943>

Good morning Louise! Thanks for popping by...I am very well! I feel like someone slipped me some magic mushrooms! LOL

Of course some of the blogs are tough ...always remember that Dr. K. has to write for Doctors and scientists and all of us in between too ...and he has to make sure to dot all his i's and cross all his T's and provide sites for everything or he gets ripped apart. He is always getting attacked for something in the paleosphere.

He has always told me he is fine with this \[I hate it!\]....this is his mission - his reason for getting out of bed in the morning.

Dr. K. also said from the very beginning that he would never dumb it down - we have to pick up our game ...no matter how many times we have to read something!!!

That is why I always tell everyone to get his book ....it is the first two years of the blog .... in easy to understand terms. Also make sure to listen to all Jack's podcasts ....he has a great/earthy delivery style - that makes it much easier.

Also ....Jack always tells me that I don't have to speak Chinese - to eat Chinese food! Don't underestimate the importance of this. He says I am lucky because I have no preconceived scientific notions and have to work hard to try to get at least some of this!

Did you read the guest blog that Stella and I did about our adventure in Florida with Jack?

Dr. K. also did a great newbie thread did you see that?

Always ask questions ..... he always tells us there are no stupid questions - how else are we going to learn?

I did try some cake \[well had a look\]..... not my favorite....... but at least I know that my hormones are in serious overdrive......

I discovered I may be quite shallow .....

### caroline — 2014-09-06T01:05:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-140055>

well - I had once said I couldn't ever be with someone who was very overweight and unhealthy -

Of course I do get that if the love of your life is less than perfect \[as we all are\] ....that isn't the issue. But at this stage of my life I need someone healthy with tons of positive electrons ...and ready for anything! if you know what I mean

and Dr. K. has said you fall in love with some one and then help them to get healthy...... I have enough energy for that!

However - the clock is seriously ticking ......

I have been thinking about a gorgeous man I met the last time I was in central Asia. He was quite overweight - not as big as dr. K. used to be - but maybe almost. He was the loveliest man ever. He had the most beautiful nature and smile and dressed beautifully.
He simple adored his wife and she totally adored him ....so I guess I am not shallow.....

so what am I looking for? I might just as well make a shopping list!
intelligent, kind, loving, fun, lots and lots of positive energy and he has to think I am the best thing since sliced bread ... oh - and on my fantasy list .... around the clock oxy....

How's this for an impossible situation......

### caroline — 2014-09-06T01:06:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-140056>

and good hands..... just saying....

### caroline — 2014-09-06T09:48:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-140102>

This afternoon I decided to take the bus home - it was starting to rain. What a life affirming adventure that was!

There was a lovely young bus driver ....and we were chatting away about so many things. He doesn't have a partner right now but wants to be a foster Dad. He is in the process of moving quite a bit north of Sydney - so I suggested that he will perhaps find the partner of his dreams there.

I told him I had joined eHarmony and he said he was thinking about it and asked me more. I told him about the fat squishy cake!

I said maybe I am getting too old for the site - so He asked me how old I was ...he said a lot of really lovely things and that he thinks we should have coffee!

I told him about the squishy cake that wants oxy once a month ..... and I said I am expecting/wanting/needing oxy twice a day!

He asked me for coffee again!

Did I say .... he is 39 years old .... just awesome - and such a cutie pie

and not squishy in any way, shape or form!

My week just keeps getting better and better.....

### caroline — 2014-09-07T01:52:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-140160>

Who in the world am I? I don't think I know yet....I am still growing into my life. Everyday there are new and amazing insights and revelations.
I am in perpetual motion.... Mother nature is spinning me round and round so I get the 360 degree view...I don't intend to stop spinning and growing and learning and listening .....until I am "done"

I think I have stumbled across many answers ...And then the questions change ....so I have to look for more and different answers.....

### prAna303 — 2014-09-07T02:18:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-140163>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=140160)
>
> I think I have stumbled across many answers ...And then the questions change ....so I have to look for more and different answers.....

When you start to observe the details you lock that situation and it becomes static. It is physics...

Just came back from a local bar / nightclub, wanted to say hello to an old friend who is bartender... How much this, the things we discuss can be described in a bar today...

The normal hot girl walks in... Guys lock on like fighterpilots, out goes the magic and what they want is impossible, to take her home. Why? She senses the final destination and that is to early to decide, perhaps five drinks later...

Funny thing, she stood next to me for ten minutes, i did not feel a thing for her, guess what... She gets angry at me. She did nothing inside me so why bother because i must feel that lift to go into that realm.

We tend to observe to much with tight rules, we want the result not the funny inbetween quirky games.

### caroline — 2014-09-07T02:31:14+0300

Do you ever feel like you are twitching with life? Sounds like a disease ...lol

I feel like my insides are a bunch of excited electrons.... And I literally can't wipe the smile of my face....

> The normal hot girl walks in... Guys lock on like fighterpilots, out goes the magic and what they want is impossible, to take her home. Why? She senses the final destination and that is to early to decide, perhaps five drinks later...
>
> Funny thing, she stood next to me for ten minutes, i did not feel a thing for her, guess what... She gets angry at me. She did nothing inside me so why bother because i must feel that lift to go into that realm.
>
> We tend to observe to much with tight rules, we want the result not the funny inbetween quirky games.

Everyone is chasing the energy, it seems.

### caroline — 2014-09-08T10:18:45+0300

### caroline — 2014-09-10T10:53:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-140516>

It has been a gorgeous day ...74F - just lovely.

I am on this insane high all the time ...what is this? I feel like skipping!

I have had such a good day ...and then it got extraordinary....

I ran into my little angel boy Jay at the beach - with his beautiful Mom...... and I have a picture! and I got a big hug!!

I haven't seen them for several months .....Jay remembers my name and how many times we have met and where I was sitting and what I was wearing ...and asked me if my knee was better. The last time I saw him I had a huge scab on my knee.

Jay is six..... what a remarkable gift this little boy is. His Mom had three miscarriages before Jay came into their lives ....

Violet was telling me that with the three pregnancies and subsequent miscarriages ...she wasn't sick at all and didn't know she was even pregnant at first. With Jay she was very sick for awhile ....
I wonder why that is?

Other than that .... a lot of old men said "Hi" to me......

I am just thinking of Kovita now .....

I have talked about Jay before in my journal ..... our very first meeting was remarkable for so many reasons...... brings tears to my eyes .... but everything seems to these days...

### caroline — 2014-09-10T11:21:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-140519>

My experience with a dating site is just hilarious ..... it is unbelievable the things that have happened. I may need a minder.....
Stella is adamant that she will skype with and if there is another .... and grill their ass!

It is all too funny ....I couldn't even make this stuff up!

This just seems so wrong .... but great entertainment value!

### caroline — 2014-09-12T13:40:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-140726>

I saw this tonite ..... and pass it along to JK and everyone here ...

One cannot think well, love well, sleep well,
if one has not dined well...

too much wine ..... I feel great..

### Inger — 2014-09-13T11:11:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-140848>

too much wine again...

Better stop the wine after 5 PM or you will never have oxy dreams....lol
Had one amazing oxy dream tonight, so strong it woke me up - boom -    today, first time since I moved here that strong... looks like I finally start to relax and be over the hardest part!
Wow.. I LOVE those dreams

Who needs a boyfriend when you wake up to amazing orgasms...

My dating experiences cured me 100% for any desire to look for a man...haha... maybe you did experience the same, Audrey?
Seems I needed it to really see what a beautiful heaven I have as my life

I really wonder about your dating experiences Audrey....

### caroline — 2014-09-15T11:46:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-141049>

Everyday I feel like I am empowered by the beauty and whisperings of Mother Nature....there is so much magic in simple things.

Today I got big hugs and huge smiles from people I barely know.....

The genuine affection you attract when you have a lot of positive energy is huge....and then there is this smile I can't wipe off my face!

It is so empowering when people are genuinely happy to see you and are grinning from ear to ear ....there is a certain kindness and openness and love that flows like a river.....

What a journey of self discovery.....

### caroline — 2014-09-17T05:04:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-141235>

I bought a pair of bad shorts yesterday! all frayed and distressed ...just awesome! Now I am ready to go out to dinner!

I also got a T-shirt with a naked man on it ...kind of "David" like ....with a fig leaf!

there is a theme developing here..... or I guess - the same old one....

yesterday on the train I was reading a new book ..... Sexual awareness - by Barry McCarthy

bad move .... I couldn't sit still!

This is a very interesting book .....now I just need someone to practise on....

### caroline — 2014-10-22T23:04:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-144754>

Thanks notso ..... she was arrested and spent the night in jail ...and then sent to a facility ....she has a history in another area. The police advised me to charge her - to get her off the streets ...and also so I would have some recourse \[victim's compensation\] if problems arose later to my jaw, teeth, neck etc.

There is a lot of people wandering around with diminished capacity etc.. In Australia - what happened to me is called a 'King hit" A young man died the other day because of this. I never really appreciated this before - when you are hit - it is all about how and where you land. I am very lucky I didn't need a neurosurgeon.......

Thankfully I got a huge amount of TLC ...... If I had been on my own it would have been pretty tough.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-05T05:29:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-149261>

You need to try some of the 2010 Grange............WOW is all I can say.

The 2010 Grange arrived with much expectation and does not disappoint. This is a powerhouse, structurally superior to both the 2009 and 2008 vintages and breathtakingly dense, long and precise. The nose has cola, blackberry, vanillin, hard brown spices of all kinds, coal smoke, meaty charcuterie elements and a strong tarry, savory note that speaks of the 85% Barossa Valley componentry. The palate has super deep tannins that fan out through flavorsome black fruits. These are purposeful tannins; they bristle on the palate, tantalizing and assertive yet playful, strong not aggressive. The power here is the thing; this has mouth-coating density and terrific drive, so tightly coiled, it gives enough away to suggest a very, very long cellaring wine is here. This is a classic Grange that will please the serious collectors. A wine of genuine pedigree.

### caroline — 2014-12-16T07:12:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-150252>

There has been a terrorist hostage siege here in Sydney that started yesterday and ended in tradegy overnite.

After what happened to me a couple of months ago - I try to be so much more aware of my surroundings.

We are going to Sydney on Thursday for a couple of days - Opera House, Sydney Fish market, cirque de soleil, dinner etc. We were so looking forward to this .... and now .... it really gives me pause.

I still feel a bit of stress since I was attacked - especially since I pass the spot nearly every day or two. It took me quite awhile to understand that I could easily have been toast ...and how lucky I was.

All of this makes me appreciate my life and all the wonderful things/quantum entanglements that have happened in my life the last number of months.

My life is magic - every single day I am so grateful for so very many things and the wonderful people in my life.

Thankyou so much for the people I love and those who love me unconditionally and all the people I am lucky enough to call "friend"...... and thanks to one spectacular person - what a wonderful surprise at this stage of my life - I am the luckiest person alive.

### caroline — 2014-12-31T01:20:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-151214>

It is New Year's eve here and the sun has just come out. I love soaking up the early morning rays.

We usually have breakfast on the patio ...... it is such a peaceful time for me.

I almost feel like I have gotten too peaceful and too contented .....I guess it is okay for now - but it feels a little lazy too.

It seems to me that emotional highs - whether good or bad ...sure take a toll on us.

Now that I am able to be "all in" and give my all it feels like a rebirth...... recharged, reborn, seeing and doing so many new and different things, appreciating simple things .... and really living.

Someone put windex on my glass eye .... Thanks JK!

Thankyou to everyone for all the friendships and all the caring and all the help that goes on here ...I wouldn't trade any off this for anything

I do need to pull up my sox a little ..... I got a little too complacent and a lot too happy! Too much wine and lovely dinners ...and it seems I still love to cook - that is a real surprise! Well cooking is too strong a word I guess - I love raw stuff! But it is a lot of fun remembering all the foodie stuff and I seem to automatically pick out all the good stuff and enhance it. I thought I had forgotten most of it. Lots of wonderful memories are flooding back to me and enhancing the present and so many things are making me smile non stop.

We are such a wonderful composite of all the good, the bad and the ugly in our life ...as long as we keep learning and loving and moving forward - nothing can stop us!

Happy New Year everyone!

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-31T06:29:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-151218>

So tonight I am getting ready for a blow out event tomorrow.........had another VIP event for a new group tonight............and during the meeting I thought about the people who did not come to NOLA.

In 2015 we should never compromise yourself.......you are all you have in the end. A great leader of them self has the courage to fulfill their vision because it fulfills its promise from the passion they exude in life, and not the position they hold in life. There is no end or beginning in life....... There is only a passion of life. Great passions, can elevate the soul to great things. Purpose may point you in the right direction but it’s passion that propels you. When it rains upon you, take no quarter. Set fire to the rain Caroline.....When you set yourself on fire, people love to come and see you burn.......Ignite your inner bonfire in 2015. The world needs your heat.

### yewwei.tan — 2014-12-31T17:55:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-151246>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=151219)
>
> These VIP events sound so awesome ...... I had the definite feeling they are for braniacs tho - but I would have loved to be a fly on the wall and watch you interact with Yew!! a couple of awesome dudes getting to know each other and sharing oysters and ideas....

Hell no, these things aren't for "braniacs" only (whatever that means).

Case in point: Rubin talked about his "top 3 supplements", to which I mentioned my top 3 (Resveratrol, Curcumin, Quercetin). I then mentioned the top 3 again on the Q&A, and Dr Patel told me something that I didn't know -- that I should be combining black pepper and turmeric to get curcumin 8x more bioavailable.

There are no stupid questions, and I think this forum serves as a great platform for people to post their "stupid questions".

My complaint with the forum is that there isn't a place to consolidate this information. That's something that I'll be slowly working to fix .... as soon as I get over the craziness of Jack's NYE party

See, my problem with jackkruse.com is that I currently don't feel like it's the best community it can be. Jack can post information on his blog that elevates the thinking of the few who get it (whatever that means), but those people don't have full ability to elevate the thinking of other people. So 2015 will be the start of several community experiments, and I just hope to find a way that allows for everyone in the community to collectively pull up the entire boat.

sidenote: I'll still be prodding Jack to give us more info though, and hope that the blogs never slow down ​

When you see the energy of a well-run live event, I think it's hard to say that anyone is a fly-on-the-wall; the best insights that you get from a trip (like the Kruse Cruise) is probably not going to come from Jack.

I was asking Jack, "Do you think you're the best person to tell Rubin about what he needs to do?". He didn't know, I don't know either. Sometimes the best advice comes from a "clueless" caroline

\----

I know it's already the New Year back in AUS, so happy new year
