### caroline — 2015-01-01T02:53:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-151279>

Okay Yew ..... back to my tirade.....

You mention "those who get it" and you would like to see them be able to elevate the rest of us. That is a lofty ideal to be sure......but - let's use me as an example \[as one of the clueless ones\] .......I have no science background what so ever .... but I do get the overall picture that Jack is painting. JK has said I am lucky because I have no pre - conceived notions about all this science. I do my best - but it is getting overwhelming now .....I don't even know enuf to ask a stupid/dumb question - let alone a smart one!

Do I want to give up? ....hell no.....I just do my best and continue to do what I do. Do I want to be able to get all this like you do .... of course I do!

As far as building a better/stronger community - build away ...try different things and see how it goes. All of us need lots of help at times and there has been awesome threads where guys like you and Neil have stepped up.

This is a great community of support and respect ..... and since you are a mere male and maybe don't see it ..... there is a ton of stuff/support/caring that goes on behind the scenes. A lot of us support each other thru private messaging and personal emails and skype. We work thru a lot of personal stuff. Stuff that worries us, enrages us ,family issues, heartbreak stuff, things that Jack says that we just don't get and need clarification.

I personally think that JK's thread about the importance of newbies was awesome. We all have value and contribute in some fashion.

I can't wait to see what you have planned .......

A lot of us support each other thru private messaging and personal emails and skype. We work thru a lot of personal stuff. Stuff that worries us, enrages us ,family issues, heartbreak stuff, things that Jack says that we just don't get and need clarification.
>
> I personally think that JK's thread about the importance of newbies was awesome. We all have value and contribute in some fashion.
>
> I can't wait to see what you have planned .......

A forum is designed to be a stream of events, that's where the caring shows, and the forum is always going to be necessary. Personal issues can use the forum as a catalyst, but should be taken private to the appropriate channels.

However, in those conversations, there will come useful knowledge that is applicable to many others . That knowledge must be extracted from the stream and put into documents that can be constantly evolved and referred to . Those documents should be editable by members of the community, so that the collective knowledge pool grows together with the community.

One of the things that I told Jeremy during the NYE party was that knowing less than some other "smarter" people in the community doesn't make you less useful.

Neither I nor my Dad can tell my Mom to live optimally. But her sister has far more impact. All we need to do is to know enough regarding optimal health principles, and bear the context of the listener in mind when conveying that knowledge.

When it comes to making inconvenient changes in their lives, some people need to hear the right information, from the right person, at the right time. That is what a forum does well, and should be continued.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-02T16:42:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-151377>

> [ssj3 said:](/goto/post?id=151364)
>
> So what does the community want/need?? And what are the steps/achievements needed to get there??

Heheh, I've got a plan, but I'm still in holiday mode now . Promise a similar write-up that I gave to Jack within the next 1-2 weeks.

But in general:

-   Community edited wiki with appropriate community contribution guidelines
-   A way to deliver "virtual classroom" content
-   (I want this, but not 100% on how to do) Guidelines and platforms to organise local meetups between community members

As with any new endeavour, we'll start small, experiment cheaply and quickly, see what works and what doesn't, remain transparent about the entire process along the way, tweak the approach with new feedback, and hope for the best.

I go into this not knowing what the community wants and needs. If it's kind enough, it'll give me a chance to experiment, and I just hope that those experiments will give a way for other people in the community to contribute and voice what it wants and needs.

ie: Community wants and needs will always be changing, so the first step is to create an organisational system that allows the community to respond to those dynamic wants and needs.

### caroline — 2015-01-28T06:51:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-154059>

I seem to have had a lot to say today ....so I thought I would enter a thought here that has captivated me lately.

I have always thought that giving all of yourself was a very scary thing ...and it is. It is pretty easy to pour all of yourself into your work - especially if you love it.
....but to give all of yourself to someone else is another matter all together.

I now know it is a sign of strength - not weakness. It is very liberating to be that open - to be at ground zero and willing and eager and open to anything and everything.

this puts everything in a whole new dimension..... Quantum entanglement and quantum magic! When you know better - you do better!

Just something else I have learned here at jackkruse.com. \[even tho I am behind in the blogs ... but I am having the time of my life!\]

Probably for the first time - I am really living - no regrets - giving 150%

Is everything perfect? heck no....when is it ever? but all the rotten stuff no longer colors my world. My world is in quantum technicolor!

I hope yours is too.....

### NeilBB — 2015-03-07T06:09:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-157697>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=157691)

> Poem by Leonard Nimoy.....

> What work will I leave behind?

> that I existed?

> "I am here!
> today....

>
> I believe the deepest impression is made´
> in those moments
> when I can say
>
> I Care
> I love

In my opinion, art is more important than science. Why? Art can reach and inspire far more people. Immediately, on a visceral level. Science is complex, meticulous, error-prone, and highly specialized. It takes a ton of work to understand and appreciate. Sure, Feynman saw the "art" in science but he had a very special mind. Few do.

The "right brain" can vault us forward when the "left brain" has to wait to confirm and reconfirm every painful detail." I say this not as an artist of any kind, but just as a lover of art who happens to have degrees in engineering and medicine. But without my deep fondness for dramas like Star Trek (speaking of Nimoy), and many kinds of great music, I would be exactly like all my soulless and clueless robotic colleagues today. Of that, I am sure.

I say this not as an artist of any kind, but just as a lover of art who happens to have degrees in engineering and medicine. But without my deep fondness for dramas like Star Trek (speaking of Nimoy), and many kinds of great music, I would be exactly like all my soulless and clueless robotic colleagues today. Of that, I am sure.

Art for me is the golden rule.

I use it to watch my own functions, use it to see if something is wrong, how?

If i feel out of sync i will never see the world expand when watching arts. The greater i feel, the more sublime things i crave, small details that wakes big feelings inside and outside of me. I search for others who share these things, i watch how others do in their life, often based on how they look at art.

When someone reaches a new level of energy in the unit called you, your senses sharpen up and details show up. We call it other things in this forum, redox potential is just one... When this happen you see many more ways to go thru the world compared to when you are ill.

I think, like this... If you live a life, doped with modern lifestyle you get so numb that you must have big booms to even get you going. If you separate from the herd way of thinking and doing you open doors to another world, way greater and more sublime than the one you came from, this is where you start to love poems, love arts, love music, adore dance and pretty soon you start to act yourself...

### caroline — 2015-03-07T11:57:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-157723>

I love all this from Neil and Peter. I am living my life to the max fi_nally. I got a second chance ....and so all the loving and talking and caring and music and dancing and observing ......and oddly - being observed ....has penetrated every single pore of my being. Right now the science has to take a back seat to my life.

JK has said I don't get it because of poor DHA and bad water in Oz .... but I actually think I am getting it for maybe the first time in my life!
To go dancing is magic ...to go to a concert is magic ....to sit quietly with someone you love may be the most magic of all.

We are emitting something extraordinary .....people/strangers will always give us a second glance and give us this lovely smile ....and for a brief second we share something - we are all beaming!

I don't know what "this" is ...... something passing between people after a brief encounter - a moment shared.

I think I finally do get it......._

### Linz — 2015-03-07T12:48:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-157729>

> [NeilBB said:](/goto/post?id=157697)
>
> In my opinion, art is more important than science. Why? Art can reach and inspire far more people. Immediately, on a visceral level. Science is complex, meticulous, error-prone, and highly specialized. It takes a ton of work to understand and appreciate. Sure, Feynman saw the "art" in science but he had a very special mind. Few do.
>
> The "right brain" can vault us forward when the "left brain" has to wait to confirm and reconfirm every painful detail." I say this not as an artist of any kind, but just as a lover of art who happens to have degrees in engineering and medicine. But without my deep fondness for dramas like Star Trek (speaking of Nimoy), and many kinds of great music, I would be exactly like all my soulless and clueless robotic colleagues today. Of that, I am sure.

Neil, I watched selection by a very top university from a top school and they completely bypassed the candidates with a talent for music/art/sport on top of great academic achievement in favour of those with time for academics only. At the time many were offended but amazing to see how much more freedom they had to develop all their skills outside those narrow walls.

Caroline, so happy for you. If you are coming over here...

### caroline — 2015-03-20T08:15:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-159010>

I just got some proper hiking boots today - never had a pair before .... can't wait to go camping and hiking ...and sleeping under the stars!

These boots are leather and heavy ..... I swear they have a mind of their own right now!

We have soaked them in water - and I wore them grocery shopping - pretty funky look

I do want some pale blue hiking sox tho ..... these boots are pretty masculine looking.

### caroline — 2015-03-24T03:16:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-159328>

So I had my first hike yesterday in the National Park. It turned out to be an absolutely stunning day. This was an easy to moderate hike along the coast - with lots of sheer drops and beautiful rock formations and colours.

I developed a blister so we didn't do the whole 3 hour walk since it was another 3 hours back to the car.
But three hours was good for the first time and I loved every single second.

We then got to swim in a gorgeous bay and lay in the sand for awhile.

I am totally addicted to being outside as much as possible. We even walked to the ocean in a lite rain this morning.

I don't even like being inside in a restaurant or coffee shop anymore.

My hiking boots still have a mind of their own!

### caroline — 2015-04-05T00:20:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-160322>

It is Easter Sunday ...Happy Easter everyone!

We are going to see Rod Stewart today. I have wanted to see him for a very long time. He is performing at a vineyard north of Sydney ....the Hunter valley.

However - it has been raining non stop for 2 days ....should be interesting!

We have seats - so not on the grass ......but we aren't allowed umbrellas. It will be amazing whatever happens.

We are staying at an airB&B .... there was a story last nite about the young men who started it - in college ...and now they are worth billions!
such a simple/wonderful idea .....

P.s Happy Easter!

### caroline — 2015-04-07T12:38:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-160505>

Rod Stewart was excellent ....great concert with high energy - everything you would expect and then some!

Beautiful women, great musicians, and wonderful lighting etc.

It rained for 2 days and nights before the concert - so everyone was a little concerned. There was a couple of rain drops pre concert and everyone put on their ponchos - but that was it! The airb&b we were staying at was 15 minutes away and it poured cats and dogs there!

It is hard to get those ponchos on after a few wines - but everyone lends a hand!

It was so muddy tho - everyone's feet were coated in mud ...good thing we are wash and wear. I think everyone bonded because of the mud - great atmosphere. People beside us overhead us talking about getting something to eat and they insisted on sharing their food with us.....really nice ...and then we shared with girls on the other side of us ......quantum entanglement in action!

We got to see the sun set on one side of us .... and a blood moon rise on the other side - quite spectacular. Mother nature puts on quite a show......gotta love Mother Nature in all her glory!

### nicld — 2015-04-17T21:55:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-161563>

### caroline — 2015-04-18T01:27:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-161574>

Hi Nicole .... Trying to get outside as much as possible ...living life  Doin stuff
Luckily I have someone who loves to swim in the ocean and get out and walk and love and laugh....and simply sit and enjoy all the beauty .....and eat shrimp and oysters!!

A few of us are working on bringing JK to Australia next year .... There are an amazing amount of details to cover and take care of ....and decisions to make. Roy Palmer has been awesome mentoring us - but this is such a huge learning curve!

We have our FB page up and running - thanks to Roy and Rob and all their support and hard work.

Please stop by and leave comments and pictures and interesting stuff. We need all kinds of ideas/ thoughts ....anything JK to get people interested in coming to our event/conference down under and having the time of their life and interacting with JK and others and learning heaps and growing and enjoying an optimal ride to health and wellness

The three legged stool to the max!

FB ......GOHWell foundation.
Global optimal health and wellness

### caroline — 2015-05-08T04:06:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-163566>

It seems that there has been an engagement on this side of the world......I never dreamt that lightening could strike twice! I am so incredibly lucky.....

All of this has been a remarkable journey for me.... I can look back and see all the necessary baby steps - to get where I am now.

I can't wait to see what the future holds for all of us here and where we will be in 10+ years.

It has been a massive 4 years of magic and quantum entanglement and brain pain ....and I thank each and every one of you for all your support and kindness and caring.

Somehow .... I turned into a fully functioning adult ....taking responsibility for my actions and not being afraid to jump without a parachute

The biggest difference I notice ..... every day is so special - all the wonderful simple little things are pure magic.

Every single day is so exciting ...... I can't wipe the stupid grin off my face

The sun rise and the sunset ....and everything in between ...we have all learned so much here! I, for one, have learned to really live - not just do the motions of a life ...to be really "Alive"

Thank you everyone .....

Thanks JK.....

How appropriate ..... I feel so hungry for life ... everything and anything - I am not even selective! I want to go everywhere and do everything - even the smallest things bring such huge pleasure. >
> The waves are really huge today and the sound is magnificent. I am listening to Bethoven.........
>
> I am having the most amazing summer........funny how things turn out. I could never imagine that at this stage of my life
> I am sitting on a beach in bikini bottoms (I will leave you to figure out what else if anything!)
> The only thing that would be better is someone to share it with....but I needed to get to this point first. I have crossed paths with so many amazing people the last few years. Talk about quantum biology at work! I am really grateful.....
>
> I was looking for a blog yesterday......and once again.....I swear there are tons I have not seen! I really do think I am keeping up and JK is sneaking more in on us!
>
> When I read one again - I am stunned at how all the answers are right there! Could I have been that fogged in??
> The answer is yes, yes, yes!
> We must drive JK crazy with our questions when it is all here - we just have to dig for it....
>
> when Dr. k. goes "walk about" For six months or however long ......I am going to re read all the blogs once again and all the comments. Sometimes the comments are just as or more important
>
> I also need to re read some threads. There is so much gold......
>
> Now - back to the ocean and the waves and trying to keep my clothes on.........

Hi Caroline, this post is a blast from your past, but I wanted to get to know you through you writing...I swear that I am not stalking you...ok, maybe a little. But I wanted to know your story, and I figured the best way would be to read your journal, so this is where I am up to so far. You have an amazing take on life and things, and I love how you become quantumly entangled with those around you who match your resonance frequency. So many gems in this journal. I am very grateful for the journals being available to us newbies that really want to experience someone else's walk and how it turned out with the various ups, downs, challenges and wins. Thank you for sharing this and all that you do!

### caroline — 2015-06-20T09:28:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-169018>

Thankyou Daina so very much for dragging this up! It is amazing to go back and read earlier stuff in our journal and see how far we have come!

Just to update today ...... I was lucky enough to spend a magical/entangled morning at the ocean with my very special guy....... pure bliss...

When your hands and heart are open ..... you do receive gifts! It is a little scary at the magnitude of the gifts that are coming my way - I feel very humbled.....

I certainly have a lot to pay forward......

However, I tend to forget that I still need to take care of myself to get well - even though I feels light years better than 2 years ago I still have a long way to go.

"Dancing" all night... Nice...

### caroline — 2015-06-24T08:27:54+0300

### caroline — 2015-06-24T14:31:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-169464>

okay, okay, okay ...... is my life's work going to be getting rid of spam??????

I had been wondering about JK's pre cruise party ...and you had to go in drag ... well what /where you wanted to be in 10 years time. I figured I couldn't go because I am in a really, really happy place now after struggling for a lot of years. Plus , I have always loved what I did and was very happy doing it ...and I was really good at it.

But ...... I could have gone as a giant SPAM!!! waddayathink??????

In ten years time I want to be still very healthy and happy and loving every second and lighting up like a christmas tree when I see my guy!  and loving cheesecake and port ! \[on special occasions of course!\]

I suppose I could have gone as a Christmas tree?????

What would you guys have gone as ? where do you want to be in ten years????

I keep thinking ...isn't it okay to have lived a really full life with all it's twists and turns and ups and downs. I have had an amazing life ....now I just want to be. I want to suck up all the simple things that I missed before and didn't understand the importance of. I am finally really okay with just being ....and I am attracting so much more magic with just being peaceful and quiet.

Every single day is better than the one before .......I don't want to be anywhere else ....I don't want to be something/someone else ..... surely this must be more than okay? Isn't that what this journey is all about?

Well ....I could use a lot more money ....then I could have gone on the cruise - but that wasn't to be.

I do get to walk to the ocean with a very special man and see a spectacular sunrise ...... I am really okay with that

We get to sit at the ocean and have a coffee and be part of so much positive energy and interact with so many people doing the exact same thing ....just taking it all in....

I just realised that Ten years ago - this would have been my dream I just came full circle!

As usual .... I don't know where all this came from ....

### caroline — 2015-06-30T04:55:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-170001>

What a lovely story Lah ........ It wasn't me!

We have been on a rescue mission. Tony's son and grand daughter were on holidays and Chris fell and badly hurt his leg/knee ...looking like a torn meniscus ...where have we heard that before????? - so we had to go up and bring them home and we took a friend to drive his Ute home.

Life sure turns on a dime! Chris has his own business - operates a lot of heavy machinery, earth moving stuff and digging stuff. All of this stuff has a clutch and it is his clutch foot that is the problem. He is wrapping his head around all this really well ...and trying to figure out what to do without an income for 4 months.

You really find out who your friends are. One of his friends told him what ever you need, whatever I have is yours - doesn't get much better than that.

Chris is a really fine man ....takes after his Dad ..... and I am overwhelmed at being included in their family.

### fitness@home — 2015-06-30T22:56:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-170118>

> Life sure turns on a dime!

It certainly does. That's when family and friends can _really_ make a difference.

>
> Hello beautiful....sounds like life is full and happy for you these days!

Hi Krista - yes very full and very happy ... but also a huge learning curve after being on my own for so long.

The art of give and take ..... I probably couldn't even have done this before - or it would have been a real struggle. When you learn to stop and smell the roses and just simply enjoy and appreciate every single moment...... you get so much more back and you can give so much more.

I read somewhere yesterday that as we get older we lose our hearing ..... we forget the clock is ticking.......

### caroline — 2015-07-14T07:13:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-171896>

The most magic fun thing just happened.....

I have been thinking that we need to get out for an evening. There is a gorgeous theatre not far from us. The man that used to own it dumped millions$$$$ into it before going bankrupt. It is such a beautiful venue.

We have being trying not to spend money because we are in the middle of a reno ....and our meagre money will only stretch so far.

But I thought ....blow it .... and I went to the theatre website to order tickets to the Etta James story. She is one of Tony's favorites so I thought I would surprise him with tickets. Just as I was going to order them the guy on the radio started talking about it and that he had two tickets to give away if we would just call into the station and leave our name and he would have a draw in 45 minutes.....

well .... guess who won two tickets?????? I just about fell off my chair when he announced my name!

It was the most surreal thing ..... my life is surreal .......

### notsoperfick — 2015-08-12T17:09:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-174496>

Has Tony converted to your way of living Caroline or does he just accept some bits but not others?

### caroline — 2015-08-13T01:53:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-174523>

Hi notso ..... Tony has always been very keen to keep himself healthy. He works outside because he has his own core drilling and cement cutting business.....so he gets tons of sunshine and grounding everyday. Of course the heavy machinery and road saws etc. aren't so good - but all the set up and planning is at least half of the job and he is usually kneeling on the ground. the trades here wear shorts and t-shirts all year so they get a decent amount of sun on their body and you generally don't see an overweight one. They are very lean and tanned and very strong.

I suspect it is like Jeremy - they have great feeling in their hands and feet when working with earth and rocks etc. He climbs around like a monkey and is very agile! ...which has many benefits

He has always drank a lot of water and seems to know what to eat intuitively - for him. He started eating a lot more protein when we met and it was not so good for him. He needs lots of veg and some fruit and small amounts of protein ...and does eat seafood everyday now. He has also always taken a cold shower and now loves to go in the ocean all year. He always loved the ocean - but never really thought about the healing benefits of it all combined.

Now it is more likely him dragging me out of bed at 50 am to go for a walk to the ocean! Also - the harder he works - the more he walks and stretches early in the morning so he is also getting the great early morning lite.

Tony has really made me understand more fully .... everyone's n=1 can be pretty different. His body processes food so much better than mine and he is very tuned into that. It seems to me that is the important part.

Another observation - generally trades here take their own lunch and snacks - they all have assorted eskies /coolers and and take regular water breaks and sit on the grass.

So much seems intuitive ....everytime I have been on a job site there is a great sense of mateship and pulling together to get a job done. Add in all the outdoor time - seems to be a win/win.....

### Jenny S — 2015-08-25T05:22:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-175611>

are you rained out up there?

### caroline — 2015-08-25T07:09:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-175622>

Hi Jenny! pretty much - and very high winds.

How are you doing? tell me......

### Jenny S — 2015-08-25T09:55:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-175625>

ok thanks, just finished packing boxes for removalist to pick up tomorrow. Leaving for Hervey Bay on about 10th Sept.
Husband very stressed & depressed but not moving. He is driving up with me & staying about 2 weeks then flying home.
Can't wait to get back to beach, can feel myself slipping backward having been home from Cairns for 2 months now.
Been the usual winter here, not much sun, now windy but below average rain.

### caroline — 2015-08-25T12:12:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-175631>

I am moving on the 9th .......what a nitemare! I am so sorry your DH won't be staying with you for very long ..... but who knows what the future holds.

How close will you be to the beach? There are times you can't go in the ocean?

### notsoperfick — 2015-08-25T17:03:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-175653>

You're moving Caroline? I know you said you were definitely going to America to live a while back but I suspect it won't be that far this time? More new beginnings

### caroline — 2015-08-26T03:09:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-175744>

Just moving 10 minutes away ......so still very close to the ocean but we will drive and park and then walk in the surf etc and meet friends and have coffee and seafood and just suck it all in! Massive electron supply with lots of rocks and conifers and surf and sun.

That was my one condition on moving - that we still go to the ocean as often as possible and I now have a great backyard with year round sun..... and a deep bathtub for two!

we hope to put in some veggie beds and something for C/T ......and an outdoor sauna is on our list.

We also want to travel in the outback and camp ...and Ayer's rock - and to see much more of this beautiful country. We need to track down some water falls for optimal effect.

This past year has been full of incredible new adventures and wonderful, positive people.....certainly does prove you are never too old to grab life and hang on for dear life

All this intensity/love/oxy really does get your heart rate pumping!

All of this because I was open to new possibilities and took a chance..... and I got the most amazing gift of a life - all entangled with a very special little pommy guy. We lite each other up - how extra ordinary this all is.....

### caroline — 2015-09-20T13:38:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-177426>

Dr. K. said ......... "You can find anyone that will tell you what you want to hear, but the only one worth valuing is the one that tells you what you need to learn. Nothing carries meaning. People carry meaning in our life. We are the porters of importance and entanglement. Night. four more consults in the AM".

I love this and want to remember it.

I have been missing my old apartment terribly for some strange reason ...and yet it is only a "thing" a place that I turned into and became a fully functioning adult so it holds a lot of importance/significance in my journey to wellness.

I have had the most incredible year of entanglements .....absolutely nothing is more important.
Amazing people have come into my life and I am learning to let people go who drag me down and steal my energy.

I was told yesterday that I am precious - how extraordinary..... why is it so hard to take stuff like that on board?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-09-20T16:34:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-177437>

Why is hard? Because you are not yet gravitational lensing well just yet. You are stealing light instead of collecting and redirecting.............that must change.

### caroline — 2015-09-21T03:09:13+0300

Thanks Dr. K. - I can see I have to get caught up on the blogs!

I did sit outside this morning in the backyard with amazing full sunshine on almost all of me. Big step for me - didn't care one bit about the neighbours!

....and I was eating salmon - pretty much a win/win

and I forgot one other thing ...... great oxy to start the morning  - and then a cold shower ...and now off to the ocean and a swim in the rockpool and a lay on the rocks.

### caroline — 2015-11-14T02:09:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-181940>

We Are just on our way to wine country and Hope Estates to see fleetwoodmac. So excited - they are playing their music on the radio. It is pouring down cats and dogs ..... So I suppose we will be in mud up to our eyeballs!

Lots of wine and lots of fun - even if it is raining. We will be meeting new friends tomorrow that we met a couple of weeks ago at a concert there. Everyone really bonds and has so much fun even if the conditions are less than ideal - probably because of it.

Anyone seen Fleetwood Mac in concert? Apparently this is the first time they have all been together in 15 years.

### fitness@home — 2015-11-14T03:52:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-181945>

Never saw them but loved their music. Have fun!

### nicld — 2015-11-14T18:03:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-181975>

DD saw them and said they were really good. Hope you stayed dry.

### caroline — 2015-11-15T01:24:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-181991>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=181975)
>
> DD saw them and said they were really good. Hope you stayed dry.

It was absolutely pure magic .... Possibly better than the Eagles!

We got soaked and mud up to our knees ..... So much fun! I had to take my fit flops into the shower with me.
We ripped up a poncho for the floor of the car.

I think they felt a little sorry for us and were on stage for almost three hours - no breaks - an amazing concert and the wine flows very freely!

### caroline — 2015-11-15T01:36:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-181992>

It is amazing to me that the three concerts we have seen recently ....The Eagles, Rod Stewart and now Fleetwood Mac are all about the same vintage and they all have given extraordinary concerts. The energy levels are so high and the music and the stage stuff - lighting etc - blows me away.

Maybe it is because of all the black/ purple/red lites?

They definitely aren't getting older - just better!

I suppose when you love what you do and survive all the BS on your journey and entangle along the way - greatness, in one way or another ensues!

Tony paid an absolute fortune for those tickets last nite .... But he has been in love with Stevie Nicks his whole adult life!

He had a really expensive camera with him last nite ..... And forgot to check the memory card - got pics of the first 30 minutes! C'est la vie....

### caroline — 2015-12-06T02:30:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-183452>

Tony just sent me this......

If you ever fall in love......
Fall in love with someone who wants to know your favourite Color and just how you like your coffee.
Fall in love with someone who loves the way you laugh and would do absolutely anything to hear it.
Fall in love with someone who puts their head on your chest just to hear your heart beat.
Fall in love with someone who kisses you in public and is proud to show you off to anyone they know.
Fall in love with someone who makes you question why you were afraid to fall in love in the first place.
Fall in love with someone who would never ever want to hurt you.
Fall in love with someone who falls in love with your flaws and thinks you are perfect just the way you are.

The ONE they would love to wake up to every day.

This is exactly how we feel about each other ..... We are so very lucky.....

I wish this for everyone.....

### caroline — 2016-02-15T12:43:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-189079>

Happy Valentine's Day everyone!

### caroline — 2016-02-23T11:25:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-189624>

I want to remember a special week we just had on the north coast of N.S.W. So I am plopping it here.

We stayed in a little fishing village - on one side was the ocean and the other side was a river. We had a deck overlooking the river and another deck where we could see and hear the ocean.

There was no Wifi and very poor cell service. We spent all day outside wether it was on the deck for meals or at the ocean.
We found a great beach for swimming and tanning nude and just taking it all in.

We bought a scrabble board. We also finally found a cribbage board. That was really hard - no one seemed to know what that was!
We had to also buy a dictionary - because Tony speaks Pommey and I speak Canadian!

We never once turned the tv on.

We had the most remarkable sleeps and my dreaming was off the charts!

The next week we didn't fare as well for reasons I don't understand. We stayed at an air b&b that was lovely with a gorgeous courtyard full of native plants and trees and flowers and a fountain and lily pond and a huge sculpture the host lady had made. At the back was a large deck with a day bed etc and backed onto a reserve.

We slept very poorly and I felt quite down .....something I never am. So what was the problem at this lovely architect designed home? We spent a bit of time mulling this over and concluded that even tho the host lady was quite pleasant and helpful she was only doing this for money.

Were we getting negative vibes even tho she had a smile plastered on her face? She made sure we knew that her
Art/paintings were for sale as well as her partners photographs.
I watched her as Tony talked to her about something and her hands/body language spoke volumes .....even tho she had a smile on.

It is amazing to me that We would pick up such negative energy and it would affect us the way it did. That seems quite weird - doesn't it?

Btw .....no wifi in this home! And we spent huge amounts of time at the beach in the early morning and walking and talking to lots of interesting people ....And we bought an ukeleLe!

And - tada (drum roll) .. A first for me/us ...... This was a clothing optional beach! And we did!

### nicld — 2016-02-23T15:56:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-189644>

Sounds like it was a great time!!!!

### caroline — 2016-03-04T05:02:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-190163>

Don't be less than your best just to make others around you comfortable.

Let's get uncomfortable and spread this state of feeling awesome far and wide!

### caroline — 2016-03-05T00:44:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-190203>

> [Linz said:](/goto/post?id=190175)
>
> Thank you Caroline! Smiling too
>
> Very nearly lost it for a short second when the girl in the chemist's expression changed to 'Wish this nutter would hurry up and go,' just because I asked why they didn't stock fluoride-free toothpaste.
> DH is mastering the skill.

i needed to go to a Doc last week so decided to go to Tonys practice here where I live now. It is just a 10 minute walk. I love my regular Doctor but he is a train ride away. He is very interested in all this and we learn together. He is very open and works with me on BHRT.

Back to the new Doc .....she is a gorgeous, bright, positive young woman with the best smile. I asked to have blood work done because I haven't for quite awhile and we were wondering if my iron was low, or thyroid issues etc.
There has been a huge emotional, all be it very positive and wonderful upheaval in my life and I was pretty sure it was taking somewhat of a toll and once I settle in better I would bounce back.

She asked me what I was eating - and I said almost exclusively seafood. She was very sceptical and said I should be eating more meat! She is a very self assured woman so I thought I would let that pass for now.

I got my blood work back yesterday and called the office because I wasn't sure about their protocol. The receptionist said the Doc would call me back. We were out shopping for seafood .....what else! But I was slightly worried if something was the matter. She called me back in 10 minutes and I was sure something must be the matter to have called me back so fast!

It turns out my blood work is perfect! Imagine that?!

So here is what she is going on ....the thyroid numbers are perfect - lucky me! But ....she really hasn't any idea if my T4 is actually converting to T3 - or a lot of it to rt3 like it has been doing a couple of years ago. Now maybe it isn't - maybe my thyroid is okay - but she really has no way of knowing that for sure without a saliva test.

She was a little worried that my cholesterol was too high and asked me if I exercised at all etc.

She was very positive that high cholesterol was a problem - especially when I told her it had been high for some time.

Where do I go with this? She is very positive about what she knows to be true/ what she has been taught. I thought Docs where more clued in about this lately - but obviously not the case.

I am not going to argue with her about this just yet - she really doesn't know me at all ....fair enough. I do not want to undermine her .....after all she is the Doc.

So I will go to her when I need something because she is so close ..... and honestly she is a very lovely and caring young woman - and I love that about her.

I am so lucky to have another great Doc who is very dialled in to Jack Kruse and all this stuff we are learning.

How many people aren't that lucky tho?

### nonchalant — 2016-03-05T02:00:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-190205>

Audrey, my young doc was all gung-ho about cholesterol and vitamin D pills and such years ago. But as the years pass, I think he is seeing that there is a large void in the middle of all his training. It no longer bothers him that I've stopped taking all my prescriptions and most of my supplements. He is starting to expand the places he is searching for knowledge, even alternative medicine.

I feel a bit sorry for doctors. They get their degrees and know absolutely everything, then slowly come to the realization that there is little their knowledge can do for so many of their patients. Over here, they don't even get to spend much time with each patient. It must be very frustrating.

I talk about sunlight, but it doesn't penetrate. I did give him Pollack's book. Perhaps we will get time to discuss it someday...

### caroline — 2016-04-08T03:31:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-192315>

We just spent a lovely couple of days in Sydney. The weather was stunning - the hottest April day on record.

We had tickets for the Opera in the Harbour one evening. We walked to the Domaine a few hours early and along the ocean.

They build a stage in the Harbour with the Harbour bridge and the Opera house and Sydney in the background. It just takes your breathe away!

After the Opera we walked back to our hotel .....up 125 steps cut in the rocks. I am really pleased with my fitness level after all the walking and climbing.

We want to do a three day hike in Tasmania in October/ November. We have to carry everything with us \[food etc\] so I am a little concerned about carrying a big back pack ....I will have to start practising!

We have been doing the whole30 and it really is pretty easy to eat in restaurants when you are very specific. Of course there is always sashimi!

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-14T21:02:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-192712>

Don't survive, thrive. Do life soaring. Our cells read and react to our environment; it shapes us and we are shaped by it.
The six best healers in the world that I know:
1\. Sunlight
2\. Un-fluoridated water
3\. magnetism/grounding to Earth
4\. seafood
5\. Self Confidence
6\. Friends
That is all...........Carry On.
[http://www.nature.com/articles/srep12029](http://www.nature.com/articles/srep12029)

Here is the key that most of the critics miss..........the order of the recipe is critical to the success. Your perception will be thrown off if you do it other ways. I can think of 3 recent members who went of the rails this way. The last 4 series pointed this point this out but few understand the implications. [Neil Billeaud](https://www.facebook.com/neil.billeaud) and I have been offline talking about it. Your BUN/creat ratio and ketosis must be started before DHA loading. The blogs have been clear that the exclusion zone inside of cells manifests due to proteins within becoming hydrophilic first. People understanding of the recipe construction have not been correct. How do you do you make things hydrophilic? You slowly add in electrons. The way to add them is step 4 above. The closer you are to the equator means you have more push from your current (DC) and then you may not need as much........unless there is something peculiar about your environment, job, eyes, skin.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-05-07T04:02:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-194309>

Light doesn't simply create probabilities, It guides them. Nature will naturally select the most probable, but we can let the most improbable become actual by our 'light choices'. The stronger the light radiation, the less probabilities we should expect and the outcome more determined. With lower powered visible light we can look inside cells at mitochondria of plants and animals. Photosynthesis, the basis of the entire food web uses this one octave of light. With X-rays, which penetrate much more than visible light, you can see inside your body at the bones, with neutrinos, which penetrate much more even than X-rays, you can look inside of our Sun where light emerges.

### caroline — 2016-05-07T05:24:26+0300

We spent a few hours at the ocean getting a ton of lite between dips in the ocean and talking about how important this all is to us.

Tony gets all this instinctively ..... He has gotten rid of his transition lenses, goes without his glasses when out Walking first thing in the morning, does his exercises first thing on the wet grass, wears his blue blockers and eats a ton of seafood while barefoot outside.

I am very lucky.......

### caroline — 2016-05-29T04:25:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-195564>

I heard two wonderful things on the radio this morning ...... I can only remember one for now.

A bunch of park rangers were taking down a shark net at the ocean to clean it. There was a lot of sea vegetables etc. stuck in it - and amongst all that was tiny seahorses.

The park rangers tenderly and with love took the sea horses out of the net and placed them on the sand and told them "wiggle if you want life"

I think that works for us too!

Have consideration and care for others - what greater purpose......

### caroline — 2016-06-23T13:20:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-197125>

I have accidentally discovered a new weight loss tip it seems ......

inadvertently I weighed myself before and after oxy ...... and I was down 1K \[2.2lb\]

how good is that????!!!

so I figured .....oxy twice a day for a week or two.....

waddayathink??

We have been out looking for a new car and Tony has been explaining to the salesmen that we don't want bluetooth and heated seats and cruise control. etc. .... and telling them about EMF and JK and some of them are really listening.

I am so proud of him.....

At the end of a very long day looking ....we found this 10 year old car with a sunroof!

anyone know anything about a Nissan Xtrail?

### caroline — 2016-06-23T13:36:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-197127>

Something so hilarious happened today ....

We got home really late after a long frustrating day looking at cars.

I got out of the car and I kept saying to Tony ..."what is that???" He was still on the other side of the car and was getting very frustrated with me because I kept saying "what is that???" over and over...
He kept saying "Wtf are you talking about???!!!"

You can never imagine what it was/is - it is still there ...... a car engine was delivered to me!

It seems that a shipping label on something sent to me about three months ago .... was found and stuck on this engine.

So now we have an engine blocking our foot path - at the front door.

I can't remember laughing this hard in a long time at how ridiculous the whole thing is.....

I've made big changes and more to come. We are moving within the next couple of months. Can't wait woohoo

### caroline — 2016-07-29T08:22:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-199090>

The craziest quantum thing on my FB page.....

The last two days, when I go to my FB .......a pic comes up of JK at dinner with us in Orlando - talking about this optimal journey we are all on etc.

Right beside the pic of Jack is a pic of the new camper trailer we just ordered.......

I just keep looking at both pictures Side by side .....and going WOW!

The universe speaks .....we need to listen....

### caroline — 2016-08-27T06:13:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-200308>

Tony and I had a lovely meet up yesterday with JennyS and her new partner.

Even tho Tony and Tim are new to all of this - they intuitively get all of it.

It is great to have a conversation with like minded people!

Jenny is doing remarkably well ....she had to make some really, really tough descisions - and she hasn't looked back!

Both Tony and Tim have worked outside most of their life - and it has paid huge dividends. They are both very healthy and their brains and intuition are in overdrive.

She is looking happy and healthy and we had such a nice time chatting. We can't wait to meet up with them again when we go to Queensland to Pick up our camper.

Thanks Jenny and Tim for taking the time to stop and visit with us on your way back up the coast.

Quantum entanglement for the win!

### caroline — 2016-09-19T03:45:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-201587>

Tony and I have just celebrated our second anniversary ....we went back to the scene of the crime - the ocean!

We had seafood of course - while enjoying a gorgeous sunny day and watching the surfers and the waves.

Perfect....

### caroline — 2016-10-30T08:13:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-203445>

Our camper trailer is finally ready for us to take possession. It has been a huge and steep learning curve. We are total newbies.

Other campers are so friendly and helpful. They come over and introduce themselves and are a wealth of information.

Patriot campers did a three hour handover with us ...but ..... things are sure different when you have to do it yourself!!!!

I have some great observations from our first few days ....

People actually greet each other and stop to have a chat and offer moral support and advise. Everyone assures us that things will get easier!

People are sitting and reading books! Lots have bikes on board and everyone walks!

we see the sun rise from our bedroom loft and sit outside as the sun goes down.

We are sleeping so much better ...one nite we didn't wake up at all. Other nites - just one bathroom break.

Lots of folks have big rigs and travel all over - virtually living in heir caravans.

Everyone can't wipe the smile off their faces!

We have learned not to wear unnecessary clothing - the less washing the better - so no underwear!

Mostly been off all technology ..... that is making a really, really huge difference.

we forgot to bring glasses with us - but wine tastes just fine direct from the bottle!

Now if we can just remember where we put the keys!

### caroline — 2016-11-29T04:42:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-204471>

After a month + of living outside ..... some observations.......

Being on a computer a lot is an addiction! There is life without your iPhone, iPad, computer, TV, newspapers.

We didn't even know who had won the election until a day later!

We slept so well - except for the night there was an earthquake in New Zealand. Our sleep was very disturbed that nite.

We have had wonderful experiences, met so many kind, generous, like minded people ......

But - now what????
I hate sleeping inside. I can't tolerate being in the same room when a TV is on. I feel like I am being electrocuted or supercharged- but not in a good way.

We have had so much enjoyment from the simple pleasures in life ......

Now we have to re adjust and re think ..........now that we absolutely know so much better.

Thanks Dr. K.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-29T16:50:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-204483>

You slept bad the night of earthquake because your mitochondria were sensing the pulsations of the earth. You live on a planet that has several space around it that vibrate. The sun's light makes it vibrate. The sun's light excited water deep in the Earth to give Earth a pulse rate we call the Schumann resonance. How much do you know about resonance? The volume of a mass is a function of the information contained on its surface… The surface of your brain is special and quite unique. Did you know light resonance controls CNS processing speeds in your brain? Your skull is really a bony cavity and within the brain in your skull is another cavity and your brain has 4 cavities inside it. Cavities are the perfect antenna for standing waves or scalar waves. For those of you who don't know what a scalar wave is by its word description let me give you a visual example. A guitar works this way. You strike a string and wave enters the wooden guitar cavity and vibrates in place. This is a standing wave that makes noise. The wave form generated from the string resonating with the air inside the cavity. So what are the guitar strings in us? The inner mitochondrial membrane is the guitar string. What is the guitar cavity? It is your mitochondrial matrix. What strikes the strings in the mitochondria? The sun and magentic field waves. What noise should a mitochondria make as its scalar wave? It is a 100 Hz frequency wave that is critical in mitochondria.

What is the wave forms that strike our inner mitochondrial membrane to cause this mitochondrial resonance? It is the environment of this planet. What is Earth? The Earth is a sphere of rock within a sphere in gas with a water skin. A sphere within a sphere creates electrical tension. Electrical tension creates standing waves. Schumann taught us this in 1951, and yet we still don't realize he was teaching us about how energy can or can't flow from outside in or inside out. Energy also has a directional flow because of the second law of thermodynamics. The Earth is a sphere surrounded by the ionosphere. That tension becomes a pulse wave for thing that live within both spheres. Those waves affect things alive on the surface of the planet. When the tension changes in the environment the pattern of signaling changes between things in a cell. Artificial man made blue light and non native EMF's change the tension in that sphere to increase ubiquitination rates on all things that live upon its tectonic plates.

One of the things I emphasize in my teachings is that the body is very wise.

It has billions of years of quantum wisdom built into it. It works even when you don’t understand how it works. It is a survival machine. It thrives ONLY if it given good inputs from nature and does its best to survive if given bad inputs. You are what you settle for with your choices that alter the strings on your mitochondria.

The solar wind strikes the water in earth to cause magnetic and techtonic effects in our planet. We think these effects are due to molten iron in our core but we might be dead wrong about this belief. The effect of light on water may explain a lot more since it is now clear water is found deep into the mantle of the Earth as this cite below shows.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-29T17:56:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-204488>

Volcano's occur because of plate tectonics. Earth is the only planet we know to have plate tectonics. Water mixes with ocean crust and gets subducted at convergent plate boundaries. Introducing water into the mantle promotes melting and weakens rock, helping out the motions of plates like grease. Water inside our cells faciltates energy transfer using plate like tectonics of the inner mitochondrial membrane as it oscillates like an earthquake. We know redox potentials control crystal growth in living cells and in geology in our universe. Might volcano's really be surface markers where water is turning rock into liquid crystalline structures to transfer energy? Might it be why electric plasma is always seen before,during , and after eruptions? Might local environmental EMF's from the sun and magnetic field be the trigger for eruptions? Might we understand how the water at the base of a volcano might be the key redox driver of the process in geology? I think so. I believe this process is equivalent in our cells and tissues. In living things I believe this is controlled by the Faraday effect on the side chains of hydrated proteins and by the Pockel's effect by lipids on cell membranes. Members heard about these effects in the November 2016 webinar. Redox is a function of the light we allow to hit surfaces in your tissues and the amount of that light you assimilate in your liquid crystalline proteins. It turns out the redox potential of a volcano might be related to the amount of solar energy absorbed by water underneath a volcano to set the stage for eruptions or dormancy. ### caroline — 2016-12-03T10:45:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-204700>

Thanks Dr. K. ...... I love that - "you are what you settle for with your choices that alter the strings on your mitochondria"

In one camp ground and area in Queensland - there was a lot of volcanic red earth. We chatted with a woman who was planting her garden. She told us everything grows wonderfully well there. The plants and bushes and flowers were gorgeous up there. Upon reflection - Everyone seemed quite happy and content and productive in this area. There was so much positive energy. Also - this area is at a higher elevation.

This area has a lot of people who enjoy a somewhat alternate life style. No fluoride in the water! and all kinds of organic fruits and veg.

I can only imagine what wonderful things that must do for you - working in volcanic soil.

When we first came to Australia - this is the place we wanted to live. Things didn't work out then - but I still really love it there.

Wow - the forum is so quiet ....as opposed to the fire works on QFB!

### caroline — 2016-12-26T04:32:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-205785>

I hope everyone is having a lovely Christmas surrounded by those you love.

Our Christmas lunch was a moveable feast. We started out with nibbles and drinks under a tree for some shade. We eventually had to change our location to another area of the yard for dinner.

We were then able to move everything to the deck for dessert and coffee in the early evening.

It was a very hot day and pretty high winds - so it was a challenge! We did manage to be outside all day and into the late evening - so overall a win/win.

It was pretty hilarious to keep moving around the yard - but lots of willings hands to keep picking up everything and moving.
Our yard is completely on a slope so Tony kept having to get bits of timber to put under the chairs and table - it was all good for a laugh and some pretty funny pics.

The most important part is sharing the day with people you love and having lots of laughs and hugs - great oxy!!!

### caroline — 2017-01-16T09:09:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-206897>

I am really finding that coffee is not my friend. I maybe wouldn't even have noticed if we hadn't changed brands. The new coffee must have more caffein and sent me over the edge. My sleep was terrible - very shallow and waking every few hours.

I can look back over the last two years and see how my coffee consumption had increased and water had decreased. I now can see subtle changes that I wasn't picking up - like my sleep was less than stellar and an increase in appetite, less energy, and foggy thinking

We need to be so aware about everything we do and, of course, our surroundings and the foot print we leave.

If we want the best for ourself and our loved ones we have to be very focused - no excuses.

We aren't going to get a second chance to live the life we want unless we commit 150%. I have made the mistake of making excuses for not doing my best.

There are no excuses good/important enough for not fulfilling my quantum potential. The buck does stop here!

I am lucky to have a very supportive partner ...... but if I didn't - I would have to move forward regardless of the curves and obstacles in the road.

My journey to optimal is mine alone - no matter what life throws at me. I need to own that unconditionally.

There are folks here who have done incredible things for themselves in spite of very, very difficult circumstances - just by moving ahead slowly and surely - baby steps.

This is a little apology to Dr. K. - for making excuses for myself and others........

### caroline — 2017-02-15T10:38:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-208636>

I am putting this here to remember it .....it reminds me of JK and Sandy in lots of ways...
**HERO. KNOW WHAT THE WORD MEANS IN GREEK?**
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

“And what Plutarch taught them is this: Heroes care. True heroism, as the ancients understood, isn’t about strength, or boldness, or even courage. It’s about compassion.

When the Greeks created the heroic ideal, they didn’t choose a word that means ‘Dies Trying’ or ‘Massacres Bad Guys.’ They went with hērōs—‘protector.’ Heroes aren’t perfect; with a god as one parent and a mortal as the other, they’re perpetually teetering between two destinies. What tips them toward greatness is a sidekick, a human connection who helps turn the spigot on the power of compassion. Empathy, the Greeks believed, was a source of strength, not softness; the more you recognized yourself in others and connected with their distress, the more endurance, wisdom, cunning, and determination you could tap into.”

– **Christopher McDougall**
from [**_Natural Born Heroes_**](http://brianjohnson.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a8b0e968d05b8c09166451657&id=5fbe6efd69&e=598e4a6098)

Did you know the word hero comes from the Greek word for “protector”?

I didn’t until I read this book and I think it’s one of the coolest things ever.

Heroes CARE.

—> _“True heroism, as the ancients understood, isn’t about strength, or boldness, or even courage. It’s about compassion.”_

That compassion is the ultimate source of their superpowers.

[**Deepak Chopra**](http://brianjohnson.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a8b0e968d05b8c09166451657&id=7466c56a11&e=598e4a6098) echoes this wisdom in a fun little book called [_**The Seven Spiritual Laws of Superheroes**_](http://brianjohnson.us9.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=a8b0e968d05b8c09166451657&id=67e01aff3b&e=598e4a6098) he wrote with his son Gotham. He tells us: _“For superheroes, love is not a mere sentiment or emotion. It is the ultimate truth at the heart of creation.”_

This is also awesome: _“Buddha, or ‘the enlightened one,’ as he was known at this stage of his life to his disciples, proposed that there was a step more evolved than even enlightenment, or personal release from suffering. It was to share with others the wisdom gained and the experience of higher guidance, and in doing so elevate them to the same stage. Compassion in action. Love as the ultimate superpower encoded in total self-knowing and self-awareness.

Buddha called those who had evolved to this stage of sharing the ultimate truth bodhisattvas. It should be no surprise that the word bodhisattva translates as ‘heroic-minded one,’ or in common parlance ‘superhero.’”_

A big theme of the book is the fact that WHY we are building our strength matters. If we want to be a protector and hero to those around us, we need to focus on BEING USEFUL.

P.S. Check out the [**Micro Class**](http://brianjohnson.us9.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=a8b0e968d05b8c09166451657&id=a279017baa&e=598e4a6098) on this!

### caroline — 2017-02-28T04:24:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-209415>

we are away camping in the bush ...... no power, no wifi, no artificial lights, need to be careful with water, cold showers in an outdoor structure that is open to the sky, long drop toilets, possums, lots of magpies singing a chorus ......heaven!

except we had to come into a town to get glasses fixed,......

always travel with a spare pair of glasses!

We saw a magnificent milky way last night....... what's not to love???!!!!

### caroline — 2017-02-28T04:29:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-209416>

I forgot to mention the best part - we are surrounded by the ocean and lots of lakes and such interesting people who have come way down the coast to live a better, simpler, uncluttered life.

### caroline — 2017-05-24T11:26:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-214562>

something I heard yesterday and want to remember ........ Ubuntu

Ubuntu is an African philosophy ....
connectedness that exists - or should exist between people
behaving well toward others

I am because we are.......

### caroline — 2017-05-24T11:30:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-214563>

something completely opposite ....and completely horrible

The bombing yesterday in Manchester .......Tony grew up in this city.

### caroline — 2017-05-25T05:10:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-214622>

We went to the ocean today for the first time in quite awhile ......heaven!

We got talking to a very fit young man .....it turns out not all that young actually -58yo.

He is extremely fit - he is a long time surfer. He grew up within walking distance of the ocean and all the great beaches.

He is married now with 2 kids and he is making sure they grow up with the same lifestyle.

He has a job much like Jacks in many ways .....he is a pilot in the port.

He said he could swim to work if he wanted to!

Somehow he has always gotten all this - it is instinctive for him. He gets how all the radar when they are bringing a ship into port is zapping them. He has been instrumental in making huge changes.

He does work some shift work .....but - his day job is a surfer! He doesn't own a car - he has a bike.

Many years ago his Dad bought a new house in a sub division - very close to the ocean. His Mom got cancer and before long all the neighbours had cancer of some kind.

He figured out is was the electric meter on the bedroom wall where his mother's head would lay .....same place for the meters in all the houses.

He knew instinctively to move her bed and to change her environment. This was probably 30 years ago.

He just built a new house and they wanted to put the smart meter on his daughter's bedroom wall. He said no way - it is not going there.

I was telling him how a smart meter from another house could be affecting him. He said no ....but - his meter would be affecting the house next door. It is opposite the neighbours bedroom. He was going home to look up how to shield a smart meter and protect his neighbour.

I am so impressed with this young man - he does so much right instinctively. He eats very well, outside as much as possible - he surfs and is a life guard.

We had so much fun talking to him - instead of people thinking we are pretty weird - he was just soaking up all the information.

so it was a great day at the ocean for so many reasons!

### caroline — 2017-07-06T04:18:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-218045>

We have been taking about quantum entanglement and dopamine and light in amazing Emma's journal.

I have had yet another light bulb moment. I have been very weepy and sad the last couple of weeks. June is not my favourite month. My DH passed in June, it is his birthday and also our anniversary. I haven't really understood the sadness completely even tho I miss him terribly and have horrible memories of his illness etc.

It is his magic and light I am missing ....his touch and kisses and his kindness and love for me - and everyone who crossed his path. He was a quiet and confident man ....and everyone was drawn to him. When he passed I think he transferred his magic and light to me. Doug always gave me 150% in life .....and now I am the very, very lucky recipient of everything he was - and still is to me.

All the kids and dogs would make a beeline for him......

I haven't been able to understand all this completely before.

I am incredibly lucky to have a wonderful partner now - even tho I never ever thought that would happen. Up until I met Tony I had two men who served as a bench mark on what a real and good and kind and loyal and loving man should be ......Doug and Jack.

How could anyone live up to such high standards?
Sean mentioned in Emma's journal how he and his wife are so entangled and connected. Well - I am lucky enuf to have that again and so much more!

All of these men I talk about aren't perfect ....but yet they are - in so many ways that really matter.

Everyday my heart is so full it is about to burst - so happy tears.

Thank you Tony for caring enough to find out what quantum entanglement was and is all about...... That was the first question you asked me when we met for the first time. I love you to the stars and back......

> I am incredibly lucky to have a wonderful partner now - even tho I never ever thought that would happen. Up until I met Tony I had two men who served as a bench mark on what a real and good and kind and loyal and loving man should be ......Doug and Jack.
>
> How could anyone live up to such high standards?
> Sean mentioned in Emma's journal how he and his wife are so entangled and connected. Well - I am lucky enuf to have that again and so much more!
>
> All of these men I talk about aren't perfect ....but yet they are - in so many ways that really matter.
>
> Everyday my heart is so full it is about to burst - so happy tears.
>
> Thank you Tony for caring enough to find out what quantum entanglement was and is all about...... That was the first question you asked me when we met for the first time. I love you to the stars and back......

### caroline — 2017-07-17T06:35:36+0300

Thank you Rocky. How are you doing?

### endless — 2017-08-01T09:12:55+0300

Lovely post Audrey.....so happy you've been able to honour your husband's memory and still find love in your present.  Tony seems like a really special person and worthy of you, another very special person!

### caroline — 2017-08-01T10:50:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-220599>

Hi Krista - so glad you have popped in! How are you doing???

Thank you so much for your kind words - I so appreciate it.

### caroline — 2017-09-13T04:15:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-223511>

I decided to start the leptin reset last week ....I have been skirting around this for way to long! It is time to get down to business!

I am loving my BAB - altho that is the part I was least looking forward too.
It really is the holy grail of this RX.

I have been re listening to lots of webinars - so much I have lost focus on - I need to shape up .....literally and figuratively.

Dr. K. has remained steadfast in all these protocols - of course he has brought lots of new information and expanded on stuff when we \[women\] struggled.

Looking back over the last year - I lost sight of the important components ......

BAB - within 30 minutes of rising
leaving 4 hours between meals and at least that before bedtime
ABSOLUTELY NO SNACKING - no calories what so ever between meals
lots of good water

and of course - lots and lots of sunlight and grounding.

yesterday I had a BAB at 7:30am - outside sitting on the grass. I ate a small lunch of shrimp 1:30pm ......and then I didn't eat again until 7:30 this morning. I was not remotely hungry - only thirsty.

My biggest mistakes this past year .....not eating in the morning and then sipping on a BPC all during the day and snacking on fruit and cheese. Nothing too terrible - but I was consuming some calories all day long. Of course some times I would crash and burn.

The thing is - I was hungry all the time ....like really hungry - so always struggling. Tony would continuously comment on how could I possibly gain weight when I ate very little ????

I did so well on the reset the first time but I have lost focus this past year or so. I have thought it was because I am doing so much more cooking and just getting sloppy. Tony can eat pretty much anything. He spends a lot of time working outside, never eats between meals and his body is working properly. Mine - not so much.

Now that I am having a BAB - I feel in complete control ...and no hunger all the time!

My inflammation is subsiding and no cravings for fruit all day long.

Also too many vegetables were doing me in and too much fruit - I have never digested broccoli etc, very well.

I am going with a lettuce leaf now and then - for now anyway. Dr. K. says we don't really need carbs .........

It is spring here now and we have a fantastic crop of blueberries this year - first time since I have been here. Now I just have a few if I feel like it with my BAB.

I have no more bloating all the time ....and being in control is the absolute icing on the cake!

One question ....... is a BPC a good idea or not? I am having a weak brewed decaf with coconut oil and kerry gold butter while I make my BAB.

I did a BPC the first reset and used it as a means to get good fat. In one of the webinars- probably the leptin RX - Dr. K. mentioned it wasn't such a good idea - but I don't know if he qualified it later - I was multi tasking and maybe missed it. I need to re listen.

I am assuming it is the caffeine that is not a good idea? especially is you have adrenal issues etc.

I have also forgotten the huge connection between the leptin reset and C/T. I always take cold showers and then sit outside in the sun to dry off.

Last reset I did C/T but then switched to the ocean - so much more enjoyable for so many reasons.

### Inger — 2017-09-13T18:59:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-223559>

I so see what you mean Caroline! It is so easy to get distracted away from ones personal truth because of others! Especially partners..... when they can eat and do stuff we cant....

For me dairy is not good at all on the long haul... And I do best on very simple foods, like green salad with plenty of raw oysters on the side! Or lambs heart gently fried medium rare.. or any raw seafood or meat.. or raw lamb lung smoothie! I know not fancy and does not taste good but man it makes me FEEL good

I think all the pasteurized butter in BPC is not good, and also not too much coffee anyways! That would be me, at least...
I just reread Jacks Epi Paleo book and he writes about certain haplotypes and genetic mutations (often nordic people) that should avoid all dairy! That must be me...lol

The problem is, I do not get big issues, just a little stuffed nose maybe or other minor things.. but those are signs...

We need to never forget that we are women, more sensible and might need different things than men

### Inger — 2017-09-13T19:01:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-223560>

if I do BPC it is so delicious it is easy to have way too much! So I do not do it. And easy to use it for food replacement... Not good! When I drink my coffee black, I drink way less and just sip on it a little here and there, I cant drink too much of it. Or too fast

### Stella — 2017-09-13T19:25:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-223566>

So glad that you are doing a leptin reset. It's so good to have a forum to discuss our experiences because we learn from each other's successes. Fortunately we don't have to go thru trial and error because we have all the scientific data to back up this way of life.

Leptin resistance goes hand in hand with hyperinsulinemia. If you don't deal with leptin you will never get the other part fixed, thus weight gain and low energy levels. This is what the medical profession does not address. They all go by those stupid algorithms put out by the AACE. I've always wanted to ask physicians how prescribing metformin deals with the underlying problem of hyperinsulinemia. No unfortunately I could not ask those questions or many others. That's why I could confidently tell physicians that diabetes is a progressive disease and their patients would end up on my expensive medications. This is why the pharma industry is harvesting all of us as unwise consumers.

So you are discovering once again the basis to reversing weight gain, diabetes and so many other diseases. Fix your leptin resistance and you will fix so many other things. Jack has set out a path for all of us. It didn't take long for me the first time I did the leptin resistance protocol. Hopefully you will get the same results.

### caroline — 2017-09-14T11:27:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-223602>

You are right Inger - I was using a BPC as a meal replacement. Now I just have a weak one with mostly coconut oil and a little butter. I am enjoying it so much with my BAB and no ill effects so far.
I too love to eat very simply - raw steak and shrimp and salmon etc. - but I need to do better with oysters!

It is amazing how in control I feel .....totally not interested in anything that isn't optimal.
