It didn't take you long the first time! You were and are a starfish!
It didn't take me long the first time either - I am already doing so much better ,,,,,and - ta da .....sleep thru the whole night last night! I am drinking way more water and peeing way less - especially during the night.

### caroline — 2017-09-14T11:55:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-223605>

It is amazing to not be hungry all the time! A couple of days I had a BAB and a very light lunch around 2:30 and then didn't eat until the next morning. No hunger at all and just not interested ....but holy mackerel - am I thirsty all the time!

So many positive things......... except our grocery bill is huge now!

Now lets see if the extra Kilos melt away ..... I think I will have to fine tune it - but for now I will continue to stuff my face first thing in the morning! It feels so good!

We are going away with the camper for a bit of a break - I have the fridges stuffed with all kinds of seafood and protein.

This will be our first free camping with no water or power, and it will be quite cold at night.

Water is the most important part of the equation.....you have to really think hard about everything you need to use water for.

I have never used a porta-potty before and we haven't worked out the shower stuff yet.......

### caroline — 2017-09-18T04:56:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-223879>

It seems to me that there isn't anything better than camping! It was quite cool but sunny - so perfect. One night was -1c .....so that was very cool.

We had a campfire going most of the time. It is the most remarkable thing to sit around a campfire with people you love - just talking and laughing and having dinner.

The leptin reset went just fine....altho I did choose to have a couple of drinks. We were all sitting around the campfire having such a good time and Tony's son brought me \[unsolicited\] a Baileys, so I chose to have it and enjoy. I really didn't want to be a dick head about it, and Chris was just being really lovely and welcoming to me.

I do love that I now have the power to make choices. The next nite the Baileys came out and I opted out. I told him I was pretty sure that Baileys isn't on my elimination protocol!

Our Optimal Life is for living every single second - That was a one off - I made the decision - I do have the power.

Have any of you guys been extreme off road 4 wheel driving????? Yikes!

I am down to two meals a day - I could not fit anymore in. I have a BAB and then a lite, late lunch/early dinner about 4-4;30
Hopefully my clothes will start getting looser!.

### drezy — 2017-09-19T21:01:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-223978>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=223878)
>
> This shower bag is a great idea Dan - but they won't deliver to Oz ......

If I were a big jerk I'd go into the whole low dopamine accusation thing\* that occasionally happens around here. But I'm a medium jerk...

They are all made in China anyway so you can order directly from the Chinese here if it ends up seeming useful to you:
[https://www.aliexpress.com/wholesal...9091953&SearchText=shower+plastic+bag+camping](https://www.aliexpress.com/wholesale?catId=0&initiative_id=SB_20170919091953&SearchText=shower+plastic+bag+camping)

Set your country and currency to AUS/AUD in the upper right.

\* Note - Ever notice that it's only a matter of time between one forum poster becoming an instant expert on another poster's dopamine level? My above faux dopamine jab is obviously a joke, but I find this phenomena so interesting. The dopamine jab (being instant hormonal expert of another human) is like the mother of all insults here that displays some vaguely intellectual understanding of the science we all should be learning. Though couched in some science it's still basically the "Your momma's so fat..." invective.

Part of me still wishes this psychic ability were true though. I'd even pay for some remote psychic dopamine sensing services maybe linked to my phone number.

Call#1: "Stop looking at that donut. Your dopamine is low."
Call#2:"Stop looking at your son's friend's mom. Your dopamine is low."
Call#3:"Starting work on that garage door right now is a bad idea. Your dopamine is low."
Call #4:"Stop chatting up the 20 yo woman behind the counter at the coffee shop. She doesn't like you. It's just that you're a good tipper and guess what........ Your dopamine is low"
Call #5: "We know that you put a bag of chips in your shopping cart. Just put the bag down and back away."

### caroline — 2017-09-20T02:40:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-223982>

Dan - I can be your remote psychic dopamine sensor if you like???

That would be fun to tell you when you are being a dickhead......

### caroline — 2017-09-20T02:44:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-223983>

I, inadvertently, had a huge BAB this morning ....approx 70 gr. protein plus a BPC. I feel like a beached whale! I am gonna see if I can get thru to breakfast tomorrow morning. I find I get a little hungry later in the day and then put off eating because I am doing something - and the hunger goes completely away.

I am loving this - even tho I have been putting it off for so long...... idiot! ....low dopamine! low dopamine! low dopamine!

### drezy — 2017-09-20T15:38:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-224009>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=223983)
>
> I find I get a little hungry later in the day and then put off eating because I am doing something - and the hunger goes completely away.

Nice. If you can purposely structure your day around the hunger so you stay busy thats a good trick for noticing it less while you adapt to the new habits. Structuring your time and environment is way easier than bothering with willpower.

### caroline — 2017-09-21T02:59:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-224058>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=224009)
>
> Nice. If you can purposely structure your day around the hunger so you stay busy thats a good trick for noticing it less while you adapt to the new habits. Structuring your time and environment is way easier than bothering with willpower.

Actually Dan - no will power involved now. The hunger is so different - or lack of hunger I could say......and it is easier to just be satisfied with water.

Yesterday - I had that huge BAB .....and then wound up having lunch and dinner - go figure. I initially, in the morning, felt like I may never want to eat again! It may be a stress response - we have a lot on our plate right now, but I did eat a little because I was hungry. I had complete control.

But, contrary to other times in my life - a lot of stress made me stuff my face - low dopamine! - with all the wrong things \[carbs\] and I had no control at all.

Lately we have had a couple of very stressful days and I didn't want to eat at all - only drink water. \[as opposed to Baileys!\]

I can't believe how we are flying by the seat of our pants right now - and at this time in our life! It is all good. We see the prize and we are moving ahead - full throttle and let the pieces fall where they may.

Actually - right now the prize is freedom to choose our life - wherever and whatever that may be! You would think we would be a little scared - but the only issue is that there are so many details to take care of. Tony's sons are a little worried we might crash and burn but they are being cautiously supportive...even tho they really don't understand.

When we were camping in the bush - Chris had a bright light on and Tony asked him to turn it off. Chris laughed at Tony about being silly etc.

Then Chris came to me and asked - why no lights? I kind of dismissed the question because I thought he was just going to make more fun of us.

But Chris said " no - I really want to know - so I explained about blue light and melatonin and cortisol etc....and how blue lights from computers. phones, lights after sunset etc. really affects you. He really listened intently and said he has been hearing a little about cellphones etc.

We are selling our camper and Chris insists we will never get what we want for it ......I love proving him wrong! At first he couldn't understand why we paid so much for it - after seeing it - now he understands!

It is really lovely to have supportive family that do have our best interests at heart - even tho they may not exactly understand what we are doing.

They do get that we have this one life to live - and we are going to do it as optimally as possible - we are kind of a bench mark for being healthy and not letting our age deter us.

### drezy — 2017-09-21T03:32:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-224064>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=224058)
>
> I can't believe how we are flying by the seat of our pants right now
> Tony's sons are a little worried we might crash and burn but they are being cautiously supportive...even tho they really don't understand.

Everyone is always flying by the seat of their pants. If they don't recognize it then that's their shortsightedness.

He has good boys and they worry about you.

On the light at night front I always feel like being a bit evil and developing a strong blue LED apparatus that mounts to a face. If you want to dispute lights at night with me then I'll design it and you wear it 4 nights in a row. If you're feeling great after that then I'll concede that it doesn't apply to you...

### Verena1028 — 2017-10-07T10:51:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-225181>

very intresting to read, I really think about trying BAB for a while again...

### caroline — 2017-11-28T10:58:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-228993>

After 8 months of looking for a new location ......it seems we have found our new home. We don't know if we got it right - but we put a lot of time and effort and anguish into it. Fingers crossed that we made the right decision.

It has been hours and hours and days and months of travelling and looking at different locations and different properties and there has been a few disappointments. There has been so much discussion and a few arguments and lots of tension some days as we try to get this even reasonably right.

With this place everything seemed to fall into place from the moment we stepped into it. I sure hope our intuition is in prime working order!

Our primary focus is being on the ocean and more north for lots of great UV. This is a little fishing village so lots of fresh seafood
and ........TA DA - it is called oyster cove!

We are surrounded on three sides by a salt water channel and we have a great back verandah where we can watch the sun set.

There is so much to consider for a big move like this. We will be eight hours away from family - but we are mobile. We all have our own lives to live ....and we do the best we can for ourselves and those we love.

One funny thing .... we often contemplated settling for something less because we thought we would never get everything we wanted and we would agonise over it - should we settle? should we lower our expectations? But it turned out - thru a chance meeting with a complete stranger - who told us about this place...... quantum entanglement to be sure.

I seem to think we have so many amazing conversations and interactions with complete strangers who just happen onto our path. These random and wonderful people enrich our life so very much on our optimal journey.

Now I have to get back to reading the blogs instead of real estate listing! I am so far behind.......

### Lahelada — 2017-11-28T11:53:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-228995>

Congratulations ! Sounds divine.

>
> Our primary focus is being on the ocean and more north for lots of great UV. This is a little fishing village so lots of fresh seafood
> and ........TA DA - it is called oyster cove!
>
> We are surrounded on three sides by a salt water channel and we have a great back verandah where we can watch the sun set.
>
> There is so much to consider for a big move like this. We will be eight hours away from family - but we are mobile. We all have our own lives to live ....and we do the best we can for ourselves and those we love.
>
> One funny thing .... we often contemplated settling for something less because we thought we would never get everything we wanted and we would agonise over it - should we settle? should we lower our expectations? But it turned out - thru a chance meeting with a complete stranger - who told us about this place...... quantum entanglement to be sure.
>
> I seem to think we have so many amazing conversations and interactions with complete strangers who just happen onto our path. These random and wonderful people enrich our life so very much on our optimal journey.
>
> Now I have to get back to reading the blogs instead of real estate listing! I am so far behind.......

Congratulations! Sounds wonderful.

>

>

>

### caroline — 2018-01-16T11:10:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-232649>

We are almost packed up and ready to go ...one more day and then the movers arrive. It has been a long and arduous haul ....and we haven't self destructed.

All the stuff we accumulate over our life time is really disturbing. We have gotten rid of so much and we still have too much ....I can't believe I wanted to live in a tiny house!

I can't wait to get rid of more stuff once we get to our new home and see what we actually need.

I wonder if young people are more mobile than people of our generation?

### caroline — 2018-01-18T10:41:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-232813>

survived the move from hell.......

### Lahelada — 2018-01-18T11:41:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-232815>

And now you are on the move ! You are an example to us all. Now enjoy.

### caroline — 2018-01-26T11:14:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-233520>

moving really is the pits! I am positive boxes multiply!

change really is hard ........but there is so many positives.

### Lahelada — 2018-01-26T13:47:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-233527>

Caroline,you are a star !

### caroline — 2018-01-27T04:50:26+0200

I wish ....thanks tho

### caroline — 2018-02-04T06:11:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-234085>

we are two weeks into our new home and environment ........no TV and very limited computer time. My sleep is so much better.

We are inside too much trying to make this place resemble a home and shopping for things we need......but soon it will be a little more comfortable and most of the boxes will have disappeared.

We hope to be on the road with our new camper by the end of the month......and just enjoying mother nature.

It has been a very stressful few months .........but at the end of the day our life is so positive and we can't wait for what lies ahead........

### caroline — 2018-05-11T07:04:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-241009>

a little bit of an update.........

We have fully come to realise that our new home chose us .....rather than the other way around!

When folks ask us how we came to be here - we really don't have an answer. We had passed thru a few times and liked it. We knew that we had to go further north - following the sun ....... At a single moment in time we fell in love with this place and this home. All of it took place in the space of a few minutes.

It has't been easy - bit of a rocky road at times and so much work to make it happen.

Jack's Vermont talk is about having skin in the game ..... I have almost always taken that to mean in a monetary as well as emotional way.

It seems we are very invested in all of this - so to do the best we could ......have skin in the game ......our instincts completely took over.

We seem to get gifts every single day........we are fully invested in stretching ourselves to the max.

We are getting kayaks \[we are surrounded by water\] ......at my age?????? yikes! ....can't wait!

Tony has a ukulele and I am getting one next week ...and we have joined a music group.

We play lawn bowls twice a week ..... outside in the sun - what's not to love?!

We sit at the ocean and watch the sun rise and then swim in the rock pool and walk in the surf and collect shells. Maxed out on meditation here!

We are so lucky for so many reasons .........I sometimes wonder how we got so lucky when so many people are struggling.

We definitely have skin in the game ...literally and figuratively.

Every morning we walk in shorts and a tank top .......skin in the game. Everyone always asks "aren't you cold????"

........and they tell us that it is too cold to go in the ocean now \[it is 19c\] we will get chilled to the core and get sick. We sometimes mention C/T - and some are very interested. A Woman we have come to know and love tells her swimming/exercise classes about C/T now!

We are absolutely thriving in this place that chose us...........we have skin in the game! Our instincts totally took over and so many disparate things fell into place.

We never once thought that we are/were dreaming too big or stretching way too far. When I look back - I don't know where all that came from - how we had that much nerve or hutzpah or whatever you might call it.

So many things had to fall into place to make all this happen ......and here I am writing to you - sitting on our verandah ....overlooking oyster channel!

### Brent Patrick — 2018-05-11T08:56:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-241013>

Nice work Caroline and update

### caroline — 2018-05-11T12:11:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-241017>

Thanks Brent ....how are you doing?

### Brent Patrick — 2018-05-12T05:56:38+0300

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=241017)

Been good Caroline will PM you next few days

### caroline — 2018-07-10T07:23:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-245496>

I was just reading back a bit in my journal ......page 104 ....lots and lots of wisdom from Dr. K.

It is great to look back at your journal and see progress ..... and pain too.

What a fantastic learning curve this has been. I started this journey alone - way back when .......and now I have a wonderfully supportive partner to continue this journey.....

We are now living in a new home and new area pretty much surrounded by water and bush .....much farther north so way more sun.

We don't watch TV .....well, except for the world cup! .... and we are off all electronics as much as possible.

We are still definitely a work in progress ......life is good.

Keep moving forward and reap all of Mother Nature's benefits.

We have discovered how important friendships are ......I don't know why things are different here - but they surely are.

We are out in the sunshine and the ocean as much as possible. Everyone we meet says "you live here????" you have seen the light!

I can never remember exactly the things that Jack says are so important to a mitochrondriac.....

sun
water
grounding
friendships
love

Everyday it becomes clearer and clearer how important these simple things are....

### Brent Patrick — 2018-07-10T09:21:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-245503>

So Happy for you guys ...excellent decision you both made regarding location.

### caroline — 2018-07-25T02:50:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-246642>

I just want to save this here.....

_“There is no passion to be found in playing small—in settling for a life that is less than the one you are capable of living.”_

_—Nelson Mandela_

capable is the operable word .....how do we really know how capable we are if we don't give it a go?

No matter how scared we are to try something that we think is totally out of our realm of possibility.

No matter how insignificant we feel, we must reach ....and then reach some more.

It is so completely awesome to accomplish something that you never dreamt possible .....and then reach even further!

### caroline — 2018-08-07T06:38:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-247763>

I was listening to Keith Urban on the radio the other day - he will be touring in Australia 2019.

He talked about something a friend said to him .......DON'T LET ANYONE BLOCK YOUR SHINE!!!

I have been loving this so much...... imagine all the truth in those few simple words?

We all shine ...unless people in our life block us. It really is necessary to detach from people who limit or block our shine.

How sad \[and completely stupid\] for us when we allow people to degrade our one and only life..

### caroline — 2018-12-11T03:53:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-257767>

yesterday we were sitting on a park bench at the ocean having our picnic lunch. this is a beautiful area in this little town by the sea. A lady drove by on her scooter and stopped to chat. We thought she may be in her 80's but it turns out she is 93. She is bright as a button and she had a santa hat on! She had just been to a fundraiser lunch to raise money for needy people.

She was so lovely and happy and just brimming with life and love. It was such a blessing for us to have a little chat with her and receive all that positive energy and joy from her.

### drezy — 2018-12-11T17:55:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-257843>

I think you just met Mrs. Claus!

I'm glad you looked up from your mobile twitter feed.

### caroline — 2018-12-12T03:48:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-257930>

I don't have a mobile twitter feed .........should I? I can't even cope with FB!

This morning at our sunrise beach ritual - I stopped to talk to a lady I always see there. She walks the beach one way and then runs back. She also runs up and down the stairs a bunch of times........and then ........

A bunch of them swim to the next beach and then back. They do this every morning at 9:00am - all year. round.

She has been doing this for years .....she would be in her 60's.

I need to get her to take her sun glasses off .......

### caroline — 2019-02-02T07:41:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-262109>

a little update .....

we are well into an Aussie summer here and I am shocked everyday to see little kids who are absolutely white. I am thinking that parents must spatula on their sun block.

Beautiful little children who should be at the ocean au natural .......and having the time of their life.

A lot of pure white adults too .......do they ever go outside??????????

on the other hand we see young people and seniors at the ocean at sunrise! there is quite a few of us now who walk in the surf and play in the waves.

Tony always greets them with "hard day at the office Mate! someone has to do it!"

### _J_ — 2019-02-06T05:17:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-262375>

Sorry, this feels I'm walking into a roomful of talking friends and interrupting everyone. I've gone through a few of the forum threads and read many of your comments. I'm not used to online social networks so this is a bit new to me, and feels odd striking up conversation with people I've never met before.  I'm in Aus too, although a brit import, so please dont hold that against me!

### caroline — 2019-02-06T08:01:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-262385>

A friendly "hello" to you as well! What can we call you please?

Where are you located in Aus? We are in Northern New South Wales - on the east coast and not far from the Queensland border.

I seem to have a fondness for Brit Imports! My partner is from the UK ....Rochdale to be exact.

So .....the most important question...... What football team do you support??????

When you join a conversation you are not interrupting at all ........please add your two cents worth whenever you are interested or have a question.

Have you read Jack's thread about the importance of Newbies? ........Please do read it - newbies are very important here.

Have you started an optimal journal yet?

### _J_ — 2019-02-06T12:02:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-262394>

J is fine, and simply short for Jason, or Jay or Jase. I don't mind any. I am sat just off Manly/Sydney in the Northern Beaches. Rochdale? Rings a bell, and I've been up that way to Manchester a few times. For me, I am welsh and therefore had rugby drummed into me at the disregard of all other sport since I was about 7 years old. CT? Try playing out on the wing (very lonely and not as warm as the scrum!) in the middle of a british winter on a Saturday morning. I had no idea I was in training for 40 years later...  Thanks, will read Jack's newbie thread

### caroline — 2019-02-07T00:57:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-262444>

### _J_ — 2019-02-07T02:44:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-262446>

currently #1 in the league! he must be a happy camper. since I have no other, I will cheer on for them, for you, too

### caroline — 2019-02-13T08:23:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-263066>

> [\_J\_ said:](/goto/post?id=262446)

Can you believe it - We go to a Doctor who barracks for Manchester United .......extreme arch enemy. Appointments are very interesting - to say the least!

How are you doing???

### _J_ — 2019-02-14T02:38:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-263140>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=263066)
>
> Tony is gloating a little too much right now I fear!!!
>
> Can you believe it - We go to a Doctor who barracks for Manchester United .......extreme arch enemy. Appointments are very interesting - to say the least!
>
> How are you doing???

Im great, thanks for asking, how are you? Currently in mid 2014 webinars, and getting about 2 hours of research a day. I recently changed my commute to enjoy a bike ride in Manly (and swim), before taking the ferry, so I can do this daily. My aim is to continue through the winter, brrr. I've done cold showers through winter so far, but not sure what it will be like stripping off outside Queenscliff surf club in the middle of winter! I may have to stop, just to spare the locals the unfortunate views. Kidding.

Been thinking a lot about Sydney, and the coming 5g revolution. Where I live and work has quite low RF at the moment, so going to keep an eye on it.

My thoughts keep wandering towards the 2020/2021 JK members event in mexico, followed by a 12 month sabbatical around-oz journey in a campervan (complete with home-schooling kids!). I think my qubits and superpositions in my quantum computer/brain are trying to "realise" this event and so "destroy" all other possibilities

I can see you did a bit of this before you settled in Oyster Cove. How was it for you ?

Btw, I think MANU vs MANC fans secretly love it (the arch enemy bit that is!) - perhaps you should mention this to Tony

### caroline — 2019-02-14T07:07:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-263150>

Hi J. ...... I don't know if I told you this story before......

When we were travelling last winter up to Queensland - we stayed at a golf course that allowed free camping - as long as you patronised the bar. We hd such a great time there. One evening about 5pm the guy's \[manager/owner\] wife and three kids showed up. The wife picks them up after school and brings them to the golf club and they make the best pizza ever! We had a ball that night out on the deck ...drinking beer and eating pizza.

The reason I tell you this ....this family had been living on the road and travelling all over Oz for 8 years. Their oldest daughter, now 13 and her sister 11 were home schooled and they are the most lovely and entertaining and engaging and bright kids we have seen in a long time. They were happy to carry on a conversation with one and all and charmed all of us.

If things had happened a little differently for us ....I think we would have bought a 5th wheeler instead of the slide on camper that we did buy .....and been permanently on the road.

Hard to say I guess ....we love where we are for so many reasons .....this place really did choose us!

We are under no illusions tho .......things could change.

We bought our slide on from Summerlife in New Castle. They have a couple of used 5th wheelers for sale. One is almost brand new .....The man had a heart attack. This is just a small family company and they have been building 5th wheelers for 20 years. They are gorgeous people and have become good friends - they were just here for the weekend.

We have met so many people permanently on the road and they couldn't be happier.

It is great to be going thru the old webinars - but don't fall too far behind with the current webinars and live Q&A'a - they are worth their weight in gold.

re - C/T in the winter ....that is our favourite time to go in the ocean. There are loads of people who swim every day, all year around.

### _J_ — 2019-02-14T08:09:39+0200

### caroline — 2019-03-06T03:02:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-264548>

Wedding announcement.......

Tony and I will marry tomorrow morning, at sunrise, on the beach we go to almost every morning.

I was going to write this tomorrow or the day after .....but it seems appropriate today because of all the stuff on Jack's FB and the new webinar.

I thank Jack so much for empowering me and believing in me and kicking me in the butt a number of times!

All of it was necessary on my quantum journey.

I am amazed at our capacity for love. I didn't think my heart had room for anyone else - but it turns out that our heart has enormous capacity.

So many revelations the last five years \[especially the sex webinar!\], so many amazing adventures, so much to learn, so much life to live!

I learned to completely follow my instincts .....I was always afraid to before. I suppose I was afraid to really live.

So - here I am ....writing and living another great chapter of my life!

I owe so much to a lot of people .......especially Stella - my dear sister from another Mother. I can't imagine my life without you in it. My intuition drove me to Orlando ....and to you and Jack. I haven't looked back since!

Thank you to everyone here for all their support. We have always challenged each other to do better, learn more, live more, live without borders.

I think we all rock!

### caroline — 2019-03-06T03:10:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-264549>

I forgot to tell you \[if you are interested\]

The bride \[moi\] will wear a kiniki swimsuit under a pale blue linen dress. I will be barefooted because I will be walking in the surf. I do have a new pair of wedding oofas \[flip flops\] to wear to breakfast.

Tony will have to hose me off afterwards - because I will be full of sand and he just cleaned the truck!

We are going for a wedding breakfast after champagne on the beach - with all the people we see every morning.

I am so thankful every single day .....I am already starting to cry......

Alrighty, don't come crying to me then....

### Stella — 2019-03-08T09:22:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-264774>

So very happy for you my dear sister. We both know how important our meeting up with each other and Jack in Orlando. That was a turning point for both of us.
Look how different our lives are today compared to then. We’re different people. Fortunately we knew growth, no matter how painful, was the only way. Our lives are so much more streamlined, with only those people and things that matter. All those who don’t belong are gone. And Tony and I are here for the long haul!
All I can say is, this is so fucking awesome!!!
Love you to the moon and back xoxoxo

### caroline — 2019-03-08T23:49:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-264850>

> [Stella said:](/goto/post?id=264774)
>
> So very happy for you my dear sister. We both know how important our meeting up with each other and Jack in Orlando. That was a turning point for both of us.
> Look how different our lives are today compared to then. We’re different people. Fortunately we knew growth, no matter how painful, was the only way. Our lives are so much more streamlined, with only those people and things that matter. All those who don’t belong are gone. And Tony and I are here for the long haul!
> All I can say is, this is so fucking awesome!!!
> Love you to the moon and back xoxoxo

You, my dear Stella, made me cry. I have cried a lot the last few days ....tears of joy and happiness of course.

I am just bursting with joy - for so many reasons.

Everything you say above Stella, is so true.....there has been pain attached to this quantum journey but we always knew we were, and are on the right track.

That doesn't mean that things won't change ......they probably will. We need to keep fine tuning our journey to keep us all healthy and happy and well.

We are driving right now, on our way to Tony's son's wedding. We put tennis balls behind our back, on trigger points, and it is really painful ....but good pain!

I suppose necessary pain is a really good thing and it shows us that we are doing the right thing?

We have been open to all these huge changes in our lives ....it is an amazing journey of love and a life well lived. We fought for all this.

We got the transmission Jack!

Sometimes you just needed to whisper in our ear .....sometimes you needed to shout for us to get the message!

But, Stella and I have known the truth since we met each other and you.

So, so much to be grateful for ....and so much to pay forward.

### caroline — 2019-03-08T23:58:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-264851>

Just another thought ....

Newbies that come to jackkruse.com want answers NOW. They want to be told what to do, where to live, what to eat, what to drink.

None of this is a quick fix ....it needs time and patience and endurance and, most of all, an open mind and a keen ear to get Jack's transmission.

We have to be willing to change everything if need be. Nothing about this is a quick fix - we need to be diligent and focussed on what really matters ......baby steps!

It has taken Stella and I four years of working on our own masterpiece .....and we aren't done yet - not by a long shot!

We did, however, recognise the truth when we saw/heard it.

Jack's touch ....overtly or covertly has changed us for ever.

### caroline — 2019-04-11T04:23:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-267829>

I keep thinking about the things Jack has been talking about the last couple of weeks when he has been sharing his own struggles and pain.

So many people pass thru our lives daily .....how many can we actually influence? More and more I think - very few.

We see people at the beach every morning dressed in lycra or whatever from head to toe. They have hats and sun glasses and are on their phones almost continually. Lots of Moms and Dads bring their kids to the beach and leave them playing in the sand while they are on their phone.

It seems like no one is getting the message......they don't even know their is a message.

We do say something if a situation presents itself but it goes totally unheeded. They seem to think we are from Mars and what we say is very stupid.

Very close friends, who we love ....just seem to think what we do is quite weird. The very funny thing is ...we are really healthy and well and they all have all kinds of problems - some quite serious.

So we have decided to do our own thing .......and leave it at that. We can only keep getting Jack's transmissions and working on us.

I don't understand how it is that Tony and I have always been very open to new ideas and different ways of doing things.

What is different about us?????

When I ran across Jack on MDA - I instinctively knew he spoke the truth ....and when Tony heard all of this - he knew as well.

We do our best to soak all this up like a sponge.....that is our job and our responsibility .....to us. Everyone else is going to have to do this on their own in their own time.

I am very lucky to have my very own JT ....and I get to go to bed with him every nite!

Is that allowed?????

### _J_ — 2019-04-11T04:41:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-267830>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=267829)
>
> I keep thinking about the things Jack has been talking about the last couple of weeks when he has been sharing his own struggles and pain.
>
> So many people pass thru our lives daily .....how many can we actually influence? More and more I think - very few.
>
> We see people at the beach every morning dressed in lycra or whatever from head to toe. They have hats and sun glasses and are on their phones almost continually. Lots of Moms and Dads bring their kids to the beach and leave them playing in the sand while they are on their phone.
>
> It seems like no one is getting the message......they don't even know their is a message.
>
> We do say something if a situation presents itself but it goes totally unheeded. They seem to think we are from Mars and what we say is very stupid.
>
> Very close friends, who we love ....just seem to think what we do is quite weird. The very funny thing is ...we are really healthy and well and they all have all kinds of problems - some quite serious.
>
> So we have decided to do our own thing .......and leave it at that. We can only keep getting Jack's transmissions and working on us.
>
> I don't understand how it is that Tony and I have always been very open to new ideas and different ways of doing things.
>
> What is different about us?????
>
> When I ran across Jack on MDA - I instinctively knew he spoke the truth ....and when Tony heard all of this - he knew as well.
>
> We do our best to soak all this up like a sponge.....that is our job and our responsibility .....to us. Everyone else is going to have to do this on their own in their own time.
>
> I am very lucky to have my very own JT ....and I get to go to bed with him every nite!
>
> Is that allowed?????

What you say is so true. I feel this also.

A little under 12 months ago, I would have been in the "parents on phone" category. I think there is an opportunity to change for everyone, but it takes curiosity. I have always been curious about things, but was more "connected" with instant information from the phone to realise it had a cost. What changed for me was the realisation that the dogma I had listened to for 40 years was entirely wrong. The amount of truth bombs I've found out over the last several months has pretty much re-wired my understanding and was pretty exhilarating at the same time! I still feel like a kid with a weird bug in the schoolyard, and want to show everyone what I've got. My wife usually tries to signal me from somewhere when other people's eyes glaze over

It is sad, and in some ways strange, that you can't rouse other's curiosity when you share some of these truth bombs with others...

### caroline — 2019-04-11T05:24:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-267831>

I suppose that always remaining very curious is the big key......

### caroline — 2019-08-01T01:59:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-275287>

We are going to Europe next month and will be staying in Barcelona for a week.

We are staying at an airB&B and this is a message we got from the host gentleman... We have been exchanging emails and he is such a special man.

I love it so much and I need to remember it......

quote ....."I want to say thanks for the compliment, but it is what we have experienced that gives us form and soul.
Nobody escapes from what life is ... and being special is being mortal and divine at the same time.
We are all special but there are many people distracted!
enjoy the party!!!" end quote

This man - he is called Chorch - is a musician and loves horses. He sent us a picture of his front door as part of our instructions to find his place....it is some fantastic artwork that I haven't figured out yet!

Should I be scared?????

Nah ....life isn't for sissies!

### caroline — 2019-08-07T08:18:34+0300

### caroline — 2019-08-07T08:29:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-275560>

We have been seeing something at the ocean the last couple of weeks that is so special.

A man and woman have 2 boys. The older boy - about 15yo is my guess. He seems to be paralysed from the waist down. They wheel him down to the beach on the sand and to the water's edge in a stroller type thing that has balloon wheels.

they get him out of the stroller and in the water and he gets himself on a surf board using his elbows/arms. His parents take him out into the surf and the waves and turn him around. Sometimes they just paddle him back to shore ....but other times - the Dad stands on the board and gets his son in a standing position in front of him and surfs him back in. They all take turns paddling and splashing around and have the best time.

It is so heartwarming to watch.....it brings tears to our eyes to see people making the very best of a heartbreaking situation.

This family has so much joy and love and positive energy ..... and we feel so grateful to watch all this in action.

### Anne V — 2019-08-07T18:29:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-275595>

maybe the 11th depending where you are would you fancy meeting.?
it seems everybody loves Barcelona .
Carole and Nick are around Manchester area.

### caroline — 2019-09-11T11:07:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-277625>

I want to keep track of some things from our travels.....

Today we are staying in a different airb&b.....this is what is on the wall permanently in their kitchen ....

“LIFE isn’t about waiting for the storm to pass...

It’s about learning to DANCE in the rain”

### caroline — 2019-09-11T11:52:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-277626>

More thoughts....
We were in Ireland last week. The weather is cool, rainy, not much sunshine....
But..
There is a gorgeous promenade along Galway Bay .....and people are swimming in the ocean. It is about 13c!

For days I have been wondering what all this travel is doing to our redox. Before we left home we really doubled down on tons of ocean time and as much sun as we could get.

We were sitting on a bench overlooking the bay when a young woman stopped and asked me if I was cold. I had shorts and a T-shirt and sandals on.
I said that I prefer to be cold etc.
It turns out that she swims every morning in the bay. I was so impressed ....but really because of her attitude.

This is where she lives. She loves it. She is very passionate about her life and just embraces it ....all of it!

How wonderful to just embrace our circumstances ....all of them, and make changes where necessary.

When this young woman needs sun ....she takes a 2 hour flight and soaks it up.

I love their passion for their life ...they are very involved in the well being of their country and politics. We learned really early to stay away from discussions about politics!

We are at a loss tho because we know so little about this beautiful country....we need to change that.

There is a certain intelligence, I think, to totally embrace where you are in your life.

This young woman said to me “I love it here and so why not make the best of all the opportunities that my environment affords me”

I observed that most people here are quite lightly dressed even tho it is cold and damp. Last Sunday there was hundreds of people swimming in Galway Bay.

Back in Oz ....our friends think we are nuts to wear shorts and T-shirt and swim in 18c ocean. Everybody is huddled under layers of sweaters and hoodies and hats and track pants.

The joke in our neighbourhood is that I don’t own a sweater or long pants!

Is it all the adversity that the people of Ireland have endured that makes them stronger ...both mentally and physically?

It seems to me that they don’t settle ....they just keep moving forward.

A sign in an Irish pub ....God gave us beer ....that proves he loves us and wants us to have fun!

Love it.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-09-11T16:26:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-277644>

Doing things others don't understand and expect is how we redefine how we will think in our futures.......

Cultivate the habit of defining, refining, redefining and redeeming your opportunities in any small way today. A failure is a temporal event, not a permanent trademark on a person. Wounds create wisdom.

### caroline — 2019-09-11T20:03:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-277660>

I guess that is what I really wanted to say .....keep refining and redefining and redeeming our opportunities.

Opportunities are all around us ....we need to be open to it all.

Take the blinkers off ....have a look around!

The young woman I spoke about above ....her Mom is 75 and swims everyday, all year in Galway Bay. How’s that for some serious C/t!

### caroline — 2019-09-11T20:06:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-277663>

I don’t really get why some folks here are bothered by what others think....

We love all the possibilities of being different, being unique ....doing what is best for us....exploring it all.

### caroline — 2019-09-12T12:34:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-277699>

This is an adventure to be sure .....but we could do without the horribly long plane flight from Oz. I thought we would be able to get some sleep but that didn’t happen.
Oddly, we are sleeping very well even tho we are in a different place every couple of days.
two nites in Dublin we slept great ...in spite of serious nnEMF
2 nites in Manchester ....slept great.
We are finding this hard to explain.....but are grateful.

Crap things keep happening .....Tony’s new phone died just before we left home. He had loaded all the banking and trip apps etc. his phone has most of out contacts at home.

Landing in Dublin Tony discovered his return Visa has expired ....even tho he is a U.K. resident and also an Australian resident. It took a couple of hours on line and $699.00 to straighten that out.

A few days after arriving here a friend messaged us to enquire if we are all right. Apparently our town was under immediate fire threat. The national park surrounding us was burning out of control. Apparently fire fighters from all over did an amazing job and no homes were lost.

I guess that’s life......

In spite of all of this we are sleeping better than ever! Go figure....

We have the important things .....each other and our sense of humour.

We are heading for the west coast of Scotland today .....and we are going to be outside as much as possible. We are going to put a lot of miles on our new hiking boots!

And eat seafood for breakfast, lunch and dinner!

### caroline — 2019-09-12T20:46:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-277729>

Wouldn’t you know ....our hire car which is almost brand new has broken down so we are sitting at a service centre waiting for assistance....could be a couple of hours.

This is the weirdest trip I have ever been on!

One thing I keep thinking about, which is very, very sad, the thing that has caused the biggest headache for so many reasons is the fact that Tony’s phone died before we left home.

Even the fact that our home could have burnt to the ground while we are away ....took 2nd Place.

That is a very sad commentary on our life these days.

### caroline — 2019-09-13T10:33:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-277770>

We finally arrived at 10pm at our new Airb&b in Scotland. It is simply amazing.

It was the Robertson jam people’s family home. It has been split into two gorgeous apartments with 12foot ceilings and floor to ceiling windows. Our bathroom has a floor to ceiling stained glass window.

It is so lovely. We have a 4 poster bed and beautiful bed linens and the host is about to prepare us a Scottish cooked breakfast.

Every cloud does have a silver lining .....indeed

Life is only what we make of it.....and the sun is shining brightly in Scotland - Yay

### Phosphene — 2019-09-18T16:12:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-278152>

Hey, did you think about contacting amazing [@ScottishEmma](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20059/) ? I wonder if she’s there for the summer?

### caroline — 2019-09-18T20:59:24+0300

### caroline — 2019-09-18T21:06:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-278198>

Another wrinkle today ......we are wondering why we left home....

We were told when Tony became an Australian citizen he could still use his EU passport to travel since it was pretty new.

Now it turns out that the airline might not let him board our return flight to Oz .

It cost us $699.00 for that info!

### Inger — 2019-09-19T13:02:04+0300

You can always come to my place if you want and you are around the corner of north germany   Food and living for free  as long as you want or need! Oysters as much as you want too

((hugs))

### Phosphene — 2019-09-19T14:42:17+0300

Is at all this due to Brexit nonsense? I forget if Tony is British. You’ll weather the bumps.

I think I would purposefully toss my passport in the nearest deep body of water for a chance to live with Inger!

### caroline — 2019-09-19T15:35:03+0300

Live and learn and keep smiling! All this travel sure takes a toll on our bodies ....and mentally as well.

I suppose it is keeping us sharp ....LOL

### caroline — 2019-09-19T21:45:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-278293>

I have succumbed to FB ......in between drinking beer and baileys. Don’t tell anyone.....ssshhhhh.

We have neglected whiskey tho .....have to work on that. When in Rome......

Altho - we were sitting beside a lovely gentleman in a pub and he was drinking Guinness with a whiskey chaser.

Not bad at all.... I am very adaptable!

I still need to try haggis .....and I love blood pudding. Me and blood pudding go way back - when it was really made with blood.

### caroline — 2019-10-10T11:45:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-279957>

I couldn’t even find my journal ....but when I did a search I landed on page 81.

I really liked what I read! Our journals are a repository of our thoughts and growth and connections

I am proud of my growth, I am proud to be connected here from day #1.

I love this place!

### caroline — 2019-10-10T11:54:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-279958>

We have been in Barcelona since last Saturday. I am loving these people ....in the Gothic quarter especially. These are happy people. They love music and wine and laughing. I see very few yuppies here, just real people living real lives.

We are going today to see Gaudi’s masterpiece ...Sagrada Familia. I am not much of a sightseer, usually my thing is walking and people but this city and Gaudí are mesmerising.

Gaudí valued nature and form and function above all else....I am in love with this man’s message...

### 5G Canary — 2019-10-10T15:51:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-279979>

I love that her cup is always overflowing onto us....

Sounds like a beautiful adventure...enjoy your day. Safe travels!

### caroline — 2019-10-11T17:38:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-280104>

This has been a beautiful adventure, but some days are so f’ing hard! We are pretty tired and then we get invigorated and speechless by what we see.
Some days we think we will never do this again ....too hard. Other days we are so energised by what we see and the people we meet.

Sagrada Família has been a real eye opener. We weren’t even going to go - too expensive and too touristy yadda, yadda, yadda. We finally thought ...we are here let’s just go. It cost us about $150.00 Aus. Which is a lot but this has been a monumental experience of our life time. It is simply unbelievable what Gaudí accomplished and all the architects that follow after his death. They expect to have all the towers complete by 2026 ....the 100 year anniversary of Gaudi’s death.

some days we think ...why did we do this to ourselves - we could be in our comfortable home and at the ocean every day and surrounded by wonderful friends.

instead, every few days we are in a different place, staying at different Airb&bs, meeting new hosts, learning new rules of existence and trying to learn how to make the best use of our time while here.

we have made lots and lots of wrong turns ....but complete strangers have been wonderful. Sometimes offering advice even before we asked because they notice our perplexed expressions.

would we do this again .....hell yes ....after we rest up for a year!

of course, we aren’t home yet ....lots more opportunities to make more wrong turns and meet more lovely people.

our host in Barcelona is the most engaging man with so much joy for life and a kindness that just envelopes us.

this is really disjointed isn’t it....

I am pretty positive that Dr. K. Is 100% tuned into Gaudí ...

### Phosphene — 2019-10-11T18:27:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-280111>

Barcelona and Gaudi’s masterpieces are on my bucket list. Made it close (Madrid and Seville) but not quite there. Making mosaics used to be a hobby of mine and I still have all the tools and supplies in my dark basement work area. I should get myself a nice sunny “She-Shed” for the yard and take it all up again. Something so soothing and invigorating about taking broken bits and puzzling them back together into something new and dazzling.

Just like we’re doing with our lives, with the help of Doc and this community.

This mosaic paradise is indeed magical as well—my happy place in Philadelphia. I wonder if all those ceramic-encrusted walls will protect from 5G?

[https://www.phillymagicgardens.org/about-us/virtual-tour/](https://www.phillymagicgardens.org/about-us/virtual-tour/)

### caroline — 2019-10-12T08:46:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-280185>

We wound up in an Irish pub last nite drinking with a bunch of midwives from Ireland. You couldn’t even make this stuff up! They were a raucous bunch ....drinking shooters and singing at the top of their lungs!

they all had such a Generous spirit and they gave Tony and I hugs and wanted pictures.

I love this place ....anything seems possible here.

maybe Gaudi’s influence???

something to remember....

### caroline — 2020-01-07T06:52:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-284322>

Tony and I spent yesterday afternoon figuring out how to incline our bed. It took some books from our library, some pieces of wood from the garage and some thought on how to keep the bed base from falling apart when we tried to lift it onto the blocks. It took a drill and another long piece of wood to nail it together temporarily.

We have only inclined about 3.5 inches to start with and we will see in a couple of weeks how we go.

We both slept well and didn't need to get up in the middle of the nite to pee. Oddly, our bowels were a little different today.....strange

When I was standing on the headland this morning, watching the sunrise - I thought about the last five years with Tony and all the stuff he has adopted/taken on board since we met ....

#1 laying naked on the wet grass at sunrise
#2 taking cold showers
#3 no lights on after sunset
#4 no TV - unless it is the world cup - then all bets are off
#5 going to bed early because we get up for sunrise
#6 lots of seafood
#6 lots of bacon
#7 lots of butter
#8 Listening to Jack's podcasts
#9 swimming in the ocean everyday - all year \[we both really love this in the winter time especially\]
#10 no sunglasses
#11 being outside as much as possible with minimal clothing
#12 Tony now has 3 Kiniki swimsuits
#13 Getting his junk in the sun
#14 walking, exercising outside everyday
#15 Tony understands how important this forum is to me but often wonder what the heck I am doing!
#16 I often hear him talking to people about the importance of being outside, seeing the sunrise and not wearing sunglasses.
#17 He seems to always have to explain to friends and family why I eat mostly carnivore and why it is my n=1.
#18 Tony seems to be able to eat "shit on shingle" in a reasonably healthy way. He doesn't have cravings or addictions and does most things in moderation.
#19 He has always valued a loving relationship and kindness and integrity, and has always been able to move forward when necessary and leave things behind that weren't working.
#20 the value of oxytocin according to JK. Tony is the best hugger I have ever met .....and he is really great at other things too........

The end .....for now. Lets see what this year brings!

### Christine_L — 2020-01-14T22:00:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-284581>

I love this list!!! Excellent reminders of what we should all be doing. I needed to read this as much as you needed to write it.

### caroline — 2020-04-01T04:10:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-288211>

When we decided last February to take our camper and go to Tasmania for a couple of months we sure had no idea, like everyone else, what was in store for all of us.

all the sightseeing stuff on this beautiful island seemed to be closing down as we arrived.

finally we have managed to change our booking on the ferry to go home this Friday.

we have been hunkered down in an RV park on the ocean with 16 others as we all wait for our departure date.

every day some one leaves and we all wave and wish them good luck and safe travels.

We all have lots of questions but no one has answers.

many people across the world are in dire circumstances. We are so lucky to be in this beautiful spot and quite comfortable. We don’t have power or hot water but are making do and remaining positive.

it amazes me the resilient of people and the infinite ways they are coping and, in fact thriving.

folks are rediscovering music and books and learning new ways to do their everyday stuff.

of course there are people that refuse to adapt and just moan and groan about the imposition of this world wide health crisis on their daily life.

this crisis has brought out the best and worse in everyone it seems.

we hear stories of people slashing caravan tires with out of state plates. Lots of caravans getting the finger etc. etc. Towns putting up signs that they don’t want us here.

we have all heard stories of amazing compassion and thoughtfulness and love.

we owe our healthcare professionals and essential workers so much gratitude.

thanks will never be enough.

I hope everyone here is well and staying busy and finding new things to do and new ways to do our everyday stuff.

For Tony and I ....we are going home to clean out the Garage and our walk in closet. This is stuff we have been putting off for two years.

Our home needs a deep clean and the garden needs TLC.

BUT .....our first priority is to get as much sun as possible.....so we may just lay outside with as little clothes on as possible and listen to music and read books and have a glass of wine- or two.

Come to think about it ....that is what we normally do - that is why the garage needs a good clean out!

Apparently we can’t go in the ocean ....there are lots of secluded beaches but we will self isolate.

lots of love and heartfelt best wishes to everyone here and especially to Dr. K.

safe travels everyone ....sending you all a big Koala bear hug XO

### caroline — 2021-10-06T07:30:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-304604>

I haven't been here for so long......

So much has changed .......and definitely not for the better.

I am writing this from the Peoples Republic of Australia.

The state of politics and healthcare in this country is heartbreaking and devastating and outrageous.

I have lived in Australia for 17 years and have loved living here.

We live in a beautiful little fishing village on the ocean. We are surrounded by a huge river system and national parks and the climate is fantastic.

We spend everyday at the ocean and when it gets cooler we head to tropical north Queensland for more sun.

This should have been the perfect place for us to live in nature and thrive.

We have kept thinking ...surely things will get better. People will get what is happening and WAKE UP.

No one seems to get that being double vaxed will lead to being triple vaxed and then what? ....a jab every 6 months?

They will need to comply to every request or forfeit freedoms unless their freedom passport is updated.

I admit that I have taken my freedom for granted. I have always thought that freedom was my God given right - that it was automatically bestowed on me the day I was born.

This has all been a rude awakening.

Today is kind of the last straw ....I feel devastated and humiliated and definitely like a 2nd class citizen.

We live in a small cluster of homes with a swimming pool and tennis courts and a club house.

As of today - we can not enter any of these facilities or meet up with friends unless we are double jabbed.

As of October 12th - we lose all our freedom.

We have both loved living in Australia - we have thrived here and built a nice comfortable life.

We have worked hard all our life and been decent and loving and kind people ....and We don't intend to be 2nd class.

This is totally outrageous what is happening here.

When Jack first started to talk about getting out of Australia .....I thought we are too old to make such a major change in our life - once again.

But ....now I think we are older and wiser and we are healthy and happy and we can do anything we set our mind and heart to.

I can't imagine just yet what the future holds for us - but we definitely are a work in progress!

Jack has the flashlight and We are paying attention!

We will move forward and who knows where we will wind up.......

This will take a lot of thought and hard work and soul searching.

Are we up for the challenge?

Of course we are ...there is a reason I have been hanging around here for so many years

### caroline — 2021-10-11T06:02:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-304745>

Today is October 11th, 2021 - dateline Yamba, Australia.

This is freedom day for Tony and I. We are not single jabbed, we are not double jabbed and will not be.

Today we have lost our freedom in a country that is supposed to be the land of the free.

People around us think that their life has returned to normal today ....they don't seem to know what we know - on any level.

These are all the same people who distrust every politician and yet are following, hook line and sinker, the narrative that the politicians are constantly telling them.

We aren't particularly brilliant people Tony and I but we were paying attention when all this started.
The world was told that the governments \[under an emergency situation\] would authorise the vaccines even tho they hadn't completed their trials.

We were also told that natural immunity was a great thing.

Fauci told us that the PCR test wasn't ideal - too many false positives \[69%\]
and yet the whole world, at various times, has been in lock down because of them.

They are now vaccinating 5 year old children.

On the radio today - some government official said "if you get the jab you will be 30 years younger"

I feel so proud that we have stood up and spoken up for what we believe.

This is all a no brainer......

### Jacks — 2021-10-19T03:29:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-305033>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=304745)
>
> Today is October 11th, 2021 - dateline Yamba, Australia.
>
> This is freedom day for Tony and I. We are not single jabbed, we are not double jabbed and will not be.
>
> Today we have lost our freedom in a country that is supposed to be the land of the free.
>
> People around us think that their life has returned to normal today ....they don't seem to know what we know - on any level.
>
> These are all the same people who distrust every politician and yet are following, hook line and sinker, the narrative that the politicians are constantly telling them.
>
> We aren't particularly brilliant people Tony and I but we were paying attention when all this started.
> The world was told that the governments \[under an emergency situation\] would authorise the vaccines even tho they hadn't completed their trials.
>
> We were also told that natural immunity was a great thing.
>
> Fauci told us that the PCR test wasn't ideal - too many false positives \[69%\]
> and yet the whole world, at various times, has been in lock down because of them.
>
> They are now vaccinating 5 year old children.
>
> On the radio today - some government official said "if you get the jab you will be 30 years younger"
>
> I feel so proud that we have stood up and spoken up for what we believe.
>
> This is all a no brainer......

Hi Caroline

So sorry to hear of what is happening there in your small community with you and your partner. So miserable, but proud that you are standing up too. It all seemed to happen so fast in Australia and we have all been watching on in horror from over here at how it is all playing out - and realizing that we are next!
I remember emailing with friends from college who live in Perth at the start of the pandemic and telling them of all the dumb things happening here and they were disbelieving of all my concerns. They were also smug about the lack of viral spread over there. I have not reached out to them since that time as I felt they were not open to discussion about what was happening as to them it seemed all so distant and unreal. I wonder how they feel now.

I too feel good for standing up and not accepting all this, particularly after writing to schools/colleges/work about jab policies and pointing out the illegalities and also the fallacies in their policies and thinking. We too have been having similar problems in our town. Luckily we can still buy food, but we cannot eat at restaurants, cannot go to indoor events, gyms etc. without the double jab. I actually don't care anymore as I don't want to do any of those things. Eating out is overrated these days - you don't know the ingredients or whether the food is organic or not so better to cook at home. And most of the community here is double jabbed, which shocks me to the core. How could all these people I know, who were distrustful of politicians and big business (like you mentioned), be duped - they even had their own kids jabbed without question as soon as they could, which I find shocking - no thought, or questioning, or concern about the lack of safety - and now efficacy. It feels so dystopian and unreal. I think it is all because people have been brainwashed to be so afraid of the virus, they will do anything to stop the anxiety that their fear creates.

But we can still go hiking in nature - that is free, and does not require a mask/jab and we can still buy food (and I grow some) so I don't need to comply with their silly rules. I have been going through the same thought process of what to do, like you. I liked JK's recent Blog Post as I realized it is along the lines of what I have been thinking too, particularly the video at the top regarding how to deal with such a sick society. I don't particularly like living where I am, but we have many family reasons for staying here at least for now (unless it becomes completely untenable and then we will leave), but I do feel energized by the idea of developing a parallel society to eventually replace the current society - like the folks in France setting up their picnics in the street, when not allowed in restaurants. I have thought that there must be enough of us out there to develop our own workarounds/businesses and this is a chance to build something new. The challenge now is finding others who think similarly with the same enthusiasm.

Anyway, I wish you the best with your tough decisions ahead. Big hugs! I hope you find some allies.

Jackie

### caroline — 2021-10-19T04:30:59+0300

Very well said Jacks .....we need to stand together and be strong and find alternatives.

We spoke to a young man at the beach today. He is mid to late thirties. He personally knows of 8 young men who have been devastated by the jab. One very healthy friend had a really serious heart attack after the 2nd jab and had been totally healthy before.

It is heartbreaking to see people we know scrambling to get their kids vaxed.

We look around in total disbelief at what is happening.

One little cafe in our town is open all nite and not in the daytime and will serve anyone!

We need to be smarter and stronger and more resilient and think outside the square!

Hold your family close xo

Read in the news today that 40% of CA State workers are holding out. Hurray! I'm not in a small minority after all. Just most people in my division are jabbed.

### caroline — 2021-12-09T07:13:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-307283>

Dateline ....The peoples republic of Australia - December 9th, 2021

Things seem to be getting worse and worse here.
We, of course, were living in a fool's paradise and thinking things would surely turn around and go back to normal.

We thought, surely everyone would see what is happening, surely they would open their eyes and see that we are now living in a fascist country.

Nope, nada - the jabbed see us as the enemy of the state.

We are going to pack up and get out of Dodge.

This is such a hard decision - but necessary. It is not for the faint hearted.

There are so many details to pull together and so much to make happen.

At this stage of our life it is a huge challenge.

Do we have the intestinal fortitude?

Do we have the strength?

Do we have the courage?

We will see ...we are a work in progress

Jack has given us the tools and we have been paying attention.

to be continued.............

### caroline — 2021-12-11T03:02:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-307365>

Today I decided to stop feeling sorry for myself ....we have to do this - so just get on with it!

Tony is learning to speak Spanish .....He said to me this morning "Good morning wife"!

It is better to just keep busy ....baby steps.....

so many hurdles - but we are tough and resilient

### caroline — 2021-12-22T08:41:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-307821>

We are moving ahead slowly - going thru a lifetime of stuff.

We don't really know where to start so we just do something, anything - everyday.

We have been able to sell a few things - so more bitcoin!

I keep thinking of people that escaped from nazi Germany with just the clothes on their back.......

All I know for sure ....we have way too much stuff and it feels debilitating and depressing ....it has all been an anchor.

Time to let go and be free.....

We have way too many balls in the air and nothing is falling into place yet. I haven't been able to cross one single thing off my list yet.

We are so grateful to be working on all of this with Laudy and Vince - great for moral support when we feel snowed under and drowning in unsolved problems.

Haven't figured out how to get out of here yet....it isn't going to be easy or cheap.

Onward and upward .....with a glass of wine......

There will be lots of tears ....so more wine!

### Freebird — 2021-12-23T16:53:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-307850>

I gave away and threw out a bunch of stuff about a year and a half ago. Including most of the furniture. I don't miss ANY of it AT ALL. And of the stuff I have left, which fits in a mini van and my car (although I don't have a mini van - I hired someone cheap to move it), I only unpacked about a third of it. And of the 1/3 I unpacked, I only use about 1/3 of that.

It's getting simpler and simpler.

### caroline — 2021-12-24T00:34:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-307856>

As life gets simpler you think better - without all the clutter.

### caroline — 2022-01-01T06:56:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-308180>

quote ....."I want to say thanks for the compliment, but it is what we have experienced that gives us form and soul.
Nobody escapes from what life is ... and being special is being mortal and divine at the same time.
We are all special but there are many people distracted!
enjoy the party!!!" end quote

this is a quote from earlier in my journal.
this man's name is Chorch ....we stayed with him in Barcelona 2 years ago.

He is a very special man and it was a privilege to stay with him.
What Chorch says is so relevant today.

### caroline — 2022-01-01T07:10:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-308181>

_“There is no passion to be found in playing small—in settling for a life that is less than the one you are capable of living.”_

_—Nelson Mandela_

capable is the operable word .....how do we really know how capable we are if we don't give it a go?

No matter how scared we are to try something that we think is totally out of our realm of possibility.

No matter how insignificant we feel, we must reach ....and then reach some more.

It is so completely awesome to accomplish something that you never dreamt possible .....and then reach even further!

..... this was also from way back in my journal - funny how it applies now - in spades

> ..... this was also from way back in my journal - funny how it applies now - in spades

capable is the operable word .....how do we really know how capable we are if we don't give it a go?
\====================
To give it a go, some people need to have their back to the wall.
You are reminding me of my time in late 1960 and then early 1970

Then again 1972, broke my left hand, so not able to do physical work.
At the time looks like a bad thing, but it was blessing. (hint, hint, Jack's knee)
Had to recall that I had master's in engineering.
Nuclear Power Plants here we come.
And so on.

Happy New 2022 year!!

### Jacks — 2022-01-03T19:11:04+0200

### caroline — 2022-01-06T11:17:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-308419>

Getting closer to escaping this place we used to love so much.

Our house sold the day after listing it. We never even got a chance to clean much and the garage was a total disaster because we have been pulling stuff out of everywhere and going thru it to discard or give away.

I am amazed at how detached we have become from our "stuff"

Nothing matters except us and making a new future, new opportunities, and lots of new experiences.

It isn't going to be a piece of cake .........

None of us knows what the future holds ....no crystal ball .........but we love our life together so we will be okay ...more than okay - we will be great!

### caroline — 2022-01-07T00:31:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-308451>

I gotta say ....this is really, really hard.

We were walking on the seawall this morning at sunrise and I had tears running down my cheeks.

Why do we have to leave the home we love and the town we love and friends and family.....

Simple answer ....the average aussie is, apparently, too self absorbed and hypnotised to see what is really happening, and they won't stand up for freedom. I am pretty sure they don't get that they have lost their freedom.

I think some do see - but they don't want to give up their comfortable life ....so they will roll up their sleeve when asked - and no matter how often apparently....and take their five year olds as well.

End of pity party

### John Schumacher — 2022-01-07T02:00:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-308453>

> [Rocky said:](/goto/post?id=308335)
>
> I really don't want to John but CAlifornia where I live sort of sucks and it's getting worse with vaccine pass requirements to go to a restaurant.
>
> I see you live in Elk Grove, how are you doing up there?

My life has always been a simple one.
I've worked 50 years at various locations, typically rode a bike to work, putting in on average 12 hour days, raised three daughters.
I've never been able to afford going out to a restaurant; my wife and I may have lunch out once or twice a year.
Travel is not part of the budget.
Now, I'm retired, living on less.
My grandparents past in their thirties and forties, so I don't have longevity in my mRNA genes.
So the simple life hasn't changed much.
I'm preparing myself for my next career - I'm studying chemistry, biology, plasma physics and electrical engineering.
I typically do all my studying outside.
I've enjoyed every sunrise for the past 50 years, exercise daily, etc. -> [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/granpa-johns-optimal-journal.23952/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/granpa-johns-optimal-journal.23952/)
I'm grateful to be alive.
I would prefer to be in El Salvador, but don't have the fiat(cash) to fly down and check it out.
I'm not stuck in Elk Grove emotionally.
I just need to be real about what I can do no matter how much I would prefer something else.
