### Marko Pollo — 2022-01-07T04:25:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-308462>

> [John Schumacher said:](/goto/post?id=308453)
>
> My life has always been a simple one.
> I've worked 50 years at various locations, typically rode a bike to work, putting in on average 12 hour days, raised three daughters.
> I've never been able to afford going out to a restaurant; my wife and I may have lunch out once or twice a year.
> Travel is not part of the budget.
> Now, I'm retired, living on less.
> My grandparents past in their thirties and forties, so I don't have longevity in my mRNA genes.
> So the simple life hasn't changed much.
> I'm preparing myself for my next career - I'm studying chemistry, biology, plasma physics and electrical engineering.
> I typically do all my studying outside.
> I've enjoyed every sunrise for the past 50 years, exercise daily, etc. -> [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/granpa-johns-optimal-journal.23952/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/granpa-johns-optimal-journal.23952/)
> I'm grateful to be a live.
> I would prefer to be in El Salvador, but don't have the fiat(cash) to fly down and check it out.
> I'm not stuck in Elk Grove emotionally.
> I just need to be real about what I can do no matter how much I would prefer something else.

I agree with you John, whether you think it is possible or not, you are absolutely correct. We have become very comfortable with our modern lives and it can be hard to imagine another reality. I think about my young grandmother coming to America from post ww1 Germany. She didn't have 2 nickels to rub together and spoke no English. Ended up owning a family farm and her kin go on forever. Think about all the people in the past and present who are going through much worse than us. All the migrant workers who used to take a long dangerous journey for a chance to work and send money home. There are Venezuelan refugees walking all over SA looking for a chance. Think of all the people in Afghanistan and the middle east, living in hellish wartime conditions. Maybe our lives are just too comfortable to feel like we've hit rock bottom and need to make tough changes or perish. I hope that we can all stand tall and nut up. We are strong and resilient enough to thrive through the chaos. Good luck and God bless, wherever you are!

It all part of a way of life.

### Jacks — 2022-01-07T04:39:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-308464>

I have come to appreciate the simple things in life in the past two years. Just started reading "The Most Beautiful World our Hearts Know is Possible" by Charles Eisenstein - really reopening up my mind to craziness of the world we live in today.

We also have a huge problem with homelessness in our city - I realized that we don't really know why there are so many people on the streets. I then decided that perhaps we should ask them why and what they want rather than forcing things onto them.

So I talked to one fellow at people's park a few days ago - he loved being there and had heard about people's park before he moved here. He was fixing bicycles from broken bikes people had left lying around and loved his work. Was interested in teaching the young ones at the park. Said he loved being outside and seeing the sun every day. He seemed so free and happy. Our trouble is we want to fix them as they are not convenient and upset our nice neat tidy lives due to the ugliness of the shanty towns that are erupting everywhere. But hey - I sort of see why so many people want to drop out of the rat race - plus it is so damn expensive to live here. I have a riend who knows several professionals who live in their cars in San Francisco as they can't afford an apartment!

> [Marko Pollo said:](/goto/post?id=308462)
>
> I agree with you John, whether you think it is possible or not, you are absolutely correct. We have become very comfortable with our modern lives and it can be hard to imagine another reality. I think about my young grandmother coming to America from post ww1 Germany. She didn't have 2 nickels to rub together and spoke no English. Ended up owning a family farm and her kin go on forever. Think about all the people in the past and present who are going through much worse than us. All the migrant workers who used to take a long dangerous journey for a chance to work and send money home. There are Venezuelan refugees walking all over SA looking for a chance. Think of all the people in Afghanistan and the middle east, living in hellish wartime conditions. Maybe our lives are just too comfortable to feel like we've hit rock bottom and need to make tough changes or perish. I hope that we can all stand tall and nut up. We are strong and resilient enough to thrive through the chaos. Good luck and God bless, wherever you are!

In the last 2 years a number of sayings have become part of my everyday conversions with folk, ' hard times make for hard men while good times make for soft men', and, 'we can choose between dangerous freedom and comfortable slavery'. People I talk to on my day to day whether young or older like myself is we are going to have to pool together our skills and learn resilience if we are going to make it through this, we have no other option as there is little chance of escaping these islands.
I do have to add that all you folk on this forum have helped me so much with your knowledge, thank you, in turn my friends and family have benefited.

### caroline — 2022-01-07T07:29:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-308478>

Thanks for all your input Richard ....much appreciated.

Yes - it is getting harder to escape ...we haven't worked that out yet.

**To make something free and** **publicly** **available he could patent it and declare it free.**
He could also say that it is meant for the average person who is miles away from the need to be placed in ICU.

What I have in mind is **getting and surviving with the jab (and numerous follow-up boosters)**.

[https://kvlab.com/chlorine-dioxide-products/chlorine-dioxide-kit-w-hcl-activator-NKP-H4](https://kvlab.com/chlorine-dioxide-products/chlorine-dioxide-kit-w-hcl-activator-NKP-H4)

### Inger — 2022-01-07T23:09:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-308500>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=308451)
>
> I gotta say ....this is really, really hard.
>
> We were walking on the seawall this morning at sunrise and I had tears running down my cheeks.
>
> Why do we have to leave the home we love and the town we love and friends and family.....
>
> Simple answer ....the average aussie is, apparently, too self absorbed and hypnotised to see what is really happening, and they won't stand up for freedom. I am pretty sure they don't get that they have lost their freedom.
>
> I think some do see - but they don't want to give up their comfortable life ....so they will roll up their sleeve when asked - and no matter how often apparently....and take their five year olds as well.
>
> End of pity party

Caroline ...

it IS hard. I know. I did it too, I left (Germany) everything that I loved, my oyster beach Nirvana!! Nude beaches... wonderful people... my loved home.. people that loved me and i loved them. It hurt like hell. Trust me.

But my belly just said... leave. So I did. Now I am 2 months here already. It hurts less and less. Often I feel like being in Nirvana, really. There is joy and beauty and love everywhere, remember that  People are everywhere. And i can tan nude and swim here in way more places that in Germany, because here are so little population and beaches everywhere.. wild beaches. Way more beautiful beaches.
The air is cleaner.
The snow is magic! And so is the ice cold. I have a own ice dipping whole on this property!
My family is close, my dear siblings and their kids.. they love their aunt Inger sooo much. That melts my heart, really.
I can go to shops and cafes, and mask is not needed. It is just a recommendation. I can go to the library! I can go eat out too in a restaurant. I feel like living in a dream, kind of. That I can do all this. No mask, no test, no vaccine. It is pretty magic

If you cant get out though, always remember that true freedom always is from the inside, and no one can ever take that away from us. You will be happy everywhere. It does not matter. Life is one big adventure, and let us enjoy every day of it. Pain and joy, anything. It is just like different spices. Yum
HUGS

### John Schumacher — 2022-01-08T00:16:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-308504>

> [Jacks said:](/goto/post?id=308464)
>
> I have come to appreciate the simple things in life in the past two years. Just started reading "The Most Beautiful World our Hearts Know is Possible" by Charles Eisenstein - really reopening up my mind to craziness of the world we live in today.
>
> We also have a huge problem with homelessness in our city - I realized that we don't really know why there are so many people on the streets. I then decided that perhaps we should ask them why and what they want rather than forcing things onto them.
>
> So I talked to one fellow at people's park a few days ago - he loved being there and had heard about people's park before he moved here. He was fixing bicycles from broken bikes people had left lying around and loved his work. Was interested in teaching the young ones at the park. Said he loved being outside and seeing the sun every day. He seemed so free and happy. Our trouble is we want to fix them as they are not convenient and upset our nice neat tidy lives due to the ugliness of the shanty towns that are erupting everywhere. But hey - I sort of see why so many people want to drop out of the rat race - plus it is so damn expensive to live here. I have a riend who knows several professionals who live in their cars in San Francisco as they can't afford an apartment!

[https://www.lifehack.org/articles/lifestyle/top-8-benefits-living-minimalist-lifestyle.html](https://www.lifehack.org/articles/lifestyle/top-8-benefits-living-minimalist-lifestyle.html)

> [Richard Watson said:](/goto/post?id=308477)
>
> In the last 2 years a number of sayings have become part of my everyday conversions with folk, ' hard times make for hard men while good times make for soft men', and, 'we can choose between dangerous freedom and comfortable slavery'. People I talk to on my day to day whether young or older like myself is we are going to have to pool together our skills and learn resilience if we are going to make it through this, we have no other option as there is little chance of escaping these islands.
> I do have to add that all you folk on this forum have helped me so much with your knowledge, thank you, in turn my friends and family have benefited.

Over the waters - we are together

### caroline — 2022-01-08T03:17:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-308510>

> [EWO said:](/goto/post?id=308506)
>
> I am proud of you--and I believe in the truth of these words (below). Especially that then "Providence moves too/ All sorts of things occur to help one/ that would never otherwise have occurred." You will not know the who and the how until you look back on them and realize that they were the right people or resources at the right time, and that they could not have been anticipated ahead of time. Trust in the mysterious unfoldings that follow from deep felt commitment and faith that you will complete your journey.
>
> Until one is committed,
> there is hesitancy,
> the chance to draw back,
> always ineffectiveness.
>
> Concerning all acts of initiative
> (and creation)
> there is one elementary truth,
> the ignorance of which
> kills countless ideas
> and splendid plans –
> that the moment
> one definitely commits oneself,
> then Providence moves too.
>
> All sorts of things occur to help one
> that would never otherwise have occurred.
>
> Whatever you can do
> Or dream you can, begin it
>
> Boldness has genius,
> power and magic in it.

thankyou .......We totally see providence at work. Things falling into place that seemed like bizarre coincidences
Yes - definitely trusting in mysterious unfoldings!
Once we fully committed - it is pretty amazing how things are falling into place.

### Jacks — 2022-01-08T04:01:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-308515>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=308510)
>
> thankyou .......We totally see providence at work. Things falling into place that seemed like bizarre coincidences
> Yes - definitely trusting in mysterious unfoldings!
> Once we fully committed - it is pretty amazing how things are falling into place.

Interestingly this happened around us getting a puppy! I was not fully committed initially, but when I finally started looking at options to adopt and committed to an appointment at one adoption place, my daughters art teacher's announced her dog was going to have puppies and she promised us one! So the commitment thing seems to work ...

### caroline — 2022-01-08T04:31:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-308517>

absolutely. It is quite strange and fascinating how things are falling into place.

We are usually not good at selling things on market place - we suck at selling things actually - but today has been totally bizarre .

I advertised a shabby chic bench for $150.00 - which is quite a lot for this little old shabby bench. It was sold minutes after it went up and I have since had 10 enquiries if it is still for sale. A lady just offered me $180.00.

I have also sold today our lounge room sofa and chairs and breakfast bar stools.

### caroline — 2022-01-13T02:41:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-308648>

Tony and I have gotten our get out of jail free pass!

The government of the peoples republic of Australia is allowing us to leave.

We have been given permission  to exit Aus.

We have exit visas!

Our house is sold, our camper and truck have sold and now we have to get rid of all house stuff - somehow.....

It is astounding how everything has fallen into place.......hope nothing comes unstuck.

Family members, who won't let us visit, are being very vocal about how we could be making such a stupid move ...and we are complete and utter fools and morons.

We are pretty proud of ourselves

It is unbelievable how energised we are....and looking forward to the future.

Whatever the future holds ....looking forward is a great feeling.

We will be travelling very lite and not encumbered by our "stuff"

The anchor is being lifted......

### caroline — 2022-01-14T05:14:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-308687>

I think it is all great sign ....All the big stuff has fallen into place.

the rest is just minutia ....driving us crazy but we can and will cope.

The bank put a lock on our account yesterday - they accidentally cancelled our credit cards and debit cards.

The lady asked if we wanted to put in a complaint - Tony said "no" so the lady filed a complaint against herself.

Life is weird and wonderful ....

### caroline — 2022-01-14T05:18:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-308688>

The lady who listed our house for sale has covid. We sat across the desk from her and she said she wasn't feeling well but not covid. She is double jabbed and still was very sick.

We seem to be fine. She tried to get us to tell us but my phone doesn't seem to be working so we didn't know.

fingers crossed

Like I said in my post above,
I do hardly understand you're (quoted posts).
But guessing what they mean,
reminds me story of my Mother.
My Mom and Dad (the two of them alone), lived on their farm in (Communist) Poland.
My Dad dies.
I am asking my Mom to sell all she got and come to live with us in USA.
She did just that. Came to the USA in October 1980. Eventually, she stayed with us for 25 years till she died.

The best part of this story, relative to you Audrey/ [@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/).
My mom was **not allowed to bring with her ANY money**. Old clothes ok.
\-------
Conveniently for us, we were financially helping both of our parents.
All the money my Mom got from selling farm, cars, and so on, went to my DW's father.
I do not recall now, but I think I had to pay for my mom's airplane ticket (because she was not allowed to spend her money on that.

..........

we are completely energised and looking forward to this adventure. It will be whatever we make of it!

common sense and perseverance

Hi Sue, on another matter, making payments on gas, elec, water , Ct ...and mortgages entirely voluntary can be stopped , and recovery for deathpledges = mortgages . there is no lender , no borrower.... the bank is just an agent no currencies exchanged , a trust is created. we are the settlors and become the beneficiaries of the trust. ### caroline — 2022-01-21T00:10:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-309016>

Independence Day for us will be Feb 24th!

Stella was just reminding me of what Jack always tells us …..”be very careful who packs your parachute”

I am becoming so aware of our circle of six ….it doesn’t seem like it is family but dear friends who turn into family and supporting us 150%

this is the most amazing adventure. It is really freeing to get rid of all your possessions that we thought we couldn’t live without.

this is all completely energising us

> this is the most amazing adventure. It is really freeing to get rid of all your possessions that we thought we couldn’t live without.
>
> this is all completely energising us

I am happy for you.

Wonder if you want to explain how, you will be paying your bills during travels?
Cash, credit cards?
I recall going from the USA to Canada and my (otherwise valid) credit cards didn't want to pay for my coffee in Canada.
.
If you are immigrating from A to B. Closed your business in A, you must have closed your credit card accounts, and the new accounts in B are not opened yet.

### caroline — 2022-01-21T05:04:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-309023>

We are looking into getting a wise card. People that are already in Mexico - that is what they are using. I am more worried about our phones and sim cards.

Thanks Jan - We so appreciate your support xo

### Marko Pollo — 2022-01-21T18:36:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-309037>

I feel like having to make many small ATM withdrawals is a high security risk, expensive, and a clumsy way live. People can steal your number or follow you. I have never had problems carrying stacks of hundred-dollar bills in my pocket. I have always preferred the new style bill, as the money changers preferred them also. Now some changers prefer different bills like 20S. No cab, small restaurant, or businesses can or will accept large bills. If you try to change them in when exchange rate is favorable, most changers won't do it. I would carry just under $10k USD. Relax, no one knows it's there. When you arrive at new destination, relax, open a bank account, and put in most, but keep enough to get established. Your money should last quite a while. Learn to solve problems without using your wallet or acting like a rich gringo. Its an invitation for more problems, but know you have some cash to buy your way out of an uncomfortable position if need be. Banking in Latin America may be unlike anything you have seen. The client is treated pretty poorly. Usually take a number and wait for an hour or more. Lots of people paying bills. Relax, you have no control, and no one cares about the service you are used to. Relax. You only need to cash for beer and oysters. I emphasize relax because dealing with inefficient stuff is hard for me, but I'm not in control. Shouldn't be a problem getting a phone wherever you are. They are generally a lot cheaper. I wouldn't want an iPhone or fancy one as it makes you stand out. You may find that even clothes you used to wear are nicer than what you see. Sure, there are rich locals who love to show off, but most don't have the disposable income to needed to stay fashionable. I would also think of passing jewelry on to family members and replacing it with trinkets. Even a shitty watch can be a beacon to a thief. Good luck! I wish I was joining you two. Oh, to be young again!

we are far from young cronologically ........ but we make up for it in spirit and enthusiasm!

### caroline — 2022-01-28T03:46:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-309277>

It is a very weird experience - getting rid of all our possessions. It gets easier as time goes by. our stuff doesn't seem to matter much anymore.

We continually wonder how we accumulated so much unnecessary stuff.

The other funny thing ....I keep bonding with the people who are buying our things

We have a lot of Malbec left - we need to start drinking more

We have been meeting the loveliest people.

I don't understand what is happening.......

We now have a ton pf people that we will keep in touch with.......they are already researching our destination.

### Jacks — 2022-01-28T05:23:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-309279>

"if you build it, they will come". Seems like you are attracting the best people! Sounds like a great experience.
I am finally finding people and groups who I can relate to in my town.
Though I think we could be doomed for high school here with the potential Newsom Bill for schools.

### caroline — 2022-01-28T05:57:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-309280>

We are also finding that we don't bond with cheapskates!

Lots of lovely people like what they see and just hand you the money. For some people it is never cheap enough. Not impressed with those people. There is a different mentality that doesn't float my boat.

Third world have all kind of habits that are not always obvious.
Criminals are protesting that no punishment for $950 damage is not good enough, they want to raise that limit.
Ah wait,
they are not criminals
they want to steal, but only what laws allows
and that is in USA.

.....

### caroline — 2022-01-31T03:49:08+0200

Yes I saw that this morning. thanks for thinking of us.

It is a very weird organization - even tho we have a paid membership - they never let us know about stuff. We are booked on a commercial flight.

### caroline — 2022-02-02T01:26:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-309440>

Well it seems the fear factor is raising its ugly head.

We have been so committed for so many reasons - of course, freedom is the main reason.

But, all of a sudden, leaving our comfort zone is causing me angst.

One can become way too comfortable. Inertia sets in.

Too many people getting into my head saying all kinds of negative things.

I have to keep reminding myself of the holocaust and how many people left with just the clothes on their back and they were in grave danger.

We get to leave in comfort and with, at least, some of our possessions.

We are so lucky ....

I need to deal with my monkey brain!

Maybe right now ....more wine is the answer!

### caroline — 2022-02-06T07:50:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-309595>

16 days to go ....well actually 15 days. It is 5pm and it has been a very long day.

Will we be ready ....we have to be. It might take a couple of all nighters tho!

A couple of glitches but we are working thru them.....

What doesn't kill you makes you stronger!

It is very,very easy and appealing to stay in your comfort zone.........

Good to be alive and thriving......and wish BTC had stayed down a little longer!

The Aussie $$$ sucks against the American greenback. That really, really hurts!

### caroline — 2022-02-07T03:28:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-309640>

funny thing ....We are a little sleep deprived but we are both so energised and highly motivated.

I recommend this crazy adventure to everyone!

Get out of your comfort zone and really thrive!

Jack had a great post on FB this morning....make sure to read it.

This guy Jack is quite an amazing, unusual, and real human being.....warts and all.

really thankful and grateful for so many things..

### caroline — 2022-02-07T03:34:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-309641>

### Jack Kruse — 2022-02-07T20:39:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-309665>

Protecting your freedom is the only thing that matters right now before you are controlled by the gov't grids in OZ. Even worse in Canada because few people in Ontario they are being enslaved.

### caroline — 2022-02-08T00:06:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-309707>

I can't believe that I hold Canadian and Australian passports.

### caroline — 2022-02-08T04:33:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-309725>

To prove a point - Here in Oz

I have a cheque \[$1500.00} denominated in Canadian$$ - I get it once a year from a very small investment.
My bank - Westpac is a very large bank in Australia.

I tried to deposit my cheque in our joint account which I have had for 18 years.

The said - NO - we don't do out of country cheques anymore.

I tried to order US$$ .....and they said -NO ....we don't do foreign currency anymore.

Another bank we deal with - The Commonwealth wasn't open today. Tony called and they said they would call him back AFTER business hours.

Tony gave them a piece of his mind and asked what it is that they actually do these days.

Sad commentary on life in Oz these days

### caroline — 2022-02-13T08:50:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-309994>

We had a little get together with friends in a park this morning. We are getting so much positive feed back ...it is very heartwarming and encouraging and life affirming.

Everyone says we are so brave......

We are seeking freedom ........who knows where this journey will take us.

We are travelling very light and we are ready for whatever comes our way.

We will live every day optimally...

We will give it 150%

We were given a calendar of sunrises and sunsets ...how appropriate.

### caroline — 2022-02-19T08:06:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310288>

We surely are a work in progress ........

Everyone has so many questions for us...

Why are you going to ES?

Do you have family there? No

Do you have friends there? No

You have been before???? No

What about all the drug cartels?????

It is a third world country!!!!! Why would you go there??????

One woman this morning actually had a snear on her face....but then she thought the country was called San Salvador...so her geography isn't up to speed.

I am quite sure that everyone thinks we have lost our marbles

We are loving it!

no second thoughts ...absolutely no regrets

### caroline — 2022-02-22T23:10:41+0200

Tony and I are so glad you are helping to pack our parachute!

Now we will be waiting for you two beautiful people to join us ....sooner rather than later.

Make it happen Laudy - if anyone can do it it is you!

You Laudy are a remarkable woman and a force to be reckoned with

see you on the other side ....

### Laudy Cincotta — 2022-02-23T00:19:28+0200

Oh dear dear [@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) we didn't do that much but so glad we could be there to help, even though it was from a distance.

You underestimate also what a force you yourself are and what you have just achieved. it would have frazzled most people half your age, you have made a massive move that many would not, be proud of all you and Tony have achieved, I am so very proud of you, you are an inspiration my dear friend!

I cannot believe though with this insanity we are unable to see you before you leave, pre covid I would have flown up and spent a few days with you but then again pre covid you wouldn't have been leaving, but it will be wonderful on the other side to finally catch up, it will be even sweeter!

Now to find a time that works for us both to catch up each day, cant break with tradition!

Make sure you find a beautiful place for us to enjoy some traditional foods, thank you for all of your love and kindness, cant wait to see you soon enough, xxxx

### 5G Canary — 2022-02-23T00:43:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310456>

Congratulations on your new adventure!

I read this and thought of you....

“It’s risky to put yourself out there. It’s risky to be vulnerable, but those people who do are the creators of magic elixir.

They bring hard-earned secrets back to the tribe as a gift of tough Love. They choose to become medicine, which could only be discovered while in the deep thrones of allowing the journey to be the thing. Then they bring that powerful medicine back to the tribe in the form of HOPE.

Those carrying magic elixir have access to the crossroads between Nature and the human soul. They bridge the gap between the sacred and the banal. They take what they learned between worlds and develop new forms of courage and endurance which they inject into their elixir.

They have learned how to compress Infinity into bounded form. They fit it into a frame, a book, a painting, a method, or even a red pill. They bring it back to the tribe, or they leave it on the trail for others to discover. It becomes legendary, mythological, something that ordinary people can get drunk on and gain access to extraordinary experience.”

### Jack Kruse — 2022-02-26T16:28:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310535>

Dissatisfaction and discouragement are not caused by the absence of things but the absence of vision.

What do you expect in life? If your "expector" has expired you feel defeated and your sense of that life owes you something increases. You become a victim in life, and you don't think you can learn any longer. Your sense of wonder and curiosity has vanished. An attitude of positive expectation is the mark of a superior personality.

The law of attraction has proven time and again that what we expect is what we get. High achievement always takes place in the framework of high expectation. Success is an inside job. People rise to the level of expectation they create and we can also fall to the level of expectation if we are not careful. Today set your tone for winning. Success is a self-fulfilling prophecy. The more you believe you will succeed, the more you will succeed. Success is easier in place that promotes freedom. That is why Black Swans are pushed to move to better environments.

The more you believe you will succeed, the more you will succeed. Success is easier in place that promotes freedom. That is why Black Swans are pushed to move to better environments.

Concise and profound.
Thank you.

### caroline — 2022-02-27T12:26:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310564>

We are in Dubai on this first leg of this marathon escape to freedom.

we opted to treat ourselves and fly business class but I don’t think it was the best idea.

yes - it is very comfortable
Yes- the food is delicious
Yes - the seat reclines to a bed and we are given a sleep pad and duvet and nice pillow etc
Even a lovely leather bag packed full of high end toiletries

But we are totally surrounded by mega EMF. It is like a cocoon of every possible device.

it literally is a pod and we are encased in some very serious EMF.

we will need to de activate ourselves once we arrive.

Luckily we know how to do that - thanks Jack

### caroline — 2022-02-27T12:37:44+0200

The more you believe you will succeed, the more you will succeed. Success is easier in place that promotes freedom. That is why Black Swans are pushed to move to better environments.

You have always taught us that our life is a self fulfilling prophesy and success is an inside job
Tony and I have never been victims or quitters….I guess we have always looked on the “sunny side of life” how appropriate!

Our life will be what we make of it ….

some friends have messaged us to see how we are going. They fear for us. They worry that we may have done the wrong thing even tho they believe in freedom too.

lots of people think things are easing in Aus.
They aren’t seeing the big picture.

### caroline — 2022-02-27T12:46:06+0200

Will bitcoin stay down for 5 more days….

Although I have sat in the front of the plane plenty in the past and will gladly take an upgrade, now I fly coach and console myself that I am making over $300 per hour just sitting in a chair. I bring pocket steaks so I'm not hangry or forced to eat shit airport food. Enjoy the trip and don't worry about the emf. I'm excited for your journey and new life! We were just going through my mother's stuff and found a home made heart shaped pillow with the name uss President Harding, the ship her mother came to the states on in the 1920's. It reminds me of you two boldly starting over to seek a better life. Best of luck to you!!!

### EWO — 2022-02-27T21:07:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310587>

I came across a post of yours from Sept '21 the other day, when it had just dawned on you that a move could offer new opportunities to you both--but you still didn't really seem to think it was possible for you. From the outside, it wasn't clear that you were going to make the leap. That you have is incredible and I'm so excited to see what unfolds for you. Don't worry about the price of btc...it won't matter in a few years. Different schools of thought about DCA and lump sums. You'll have to decide whether you are more comfortable putting it all in at once (even if it goes down) or whether you feel better DCAing in even if it goes up after you begin. Remember where you are in the halving cycle.

### caroline — 2022-02-27T21:30:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310591>

Thankyou for the encouragement and support.

getting ready for the final freedom lap.

I am feeling a little lost right now. A little homeless.

I will be just fine. We need to buckle up and enjoy the ride.

Tony is busy learning Spanish!

### caroline — 2022-03-01T11:40:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310631>

We arrived in Mexico City last nite. Pretty darn tired and wondering why we left our comfortable home…

of course we know why - just feeling a little sorry for ourselves right now

We didn’t pick the best hotel.

Aussies aren’t used to tipping so we are giving people way too much.

huge learning curve….

### EWO — 2022-03-01T16:37:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310634>

[@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/), remember moving is one of the single most stressful life events. You are not just moving but changing countries and continents. It's normal to feel a little lost and even homeless. It's going to take time, and this is the in between time that is likely to feel pretty uncomfortable. But it is also the opening to a new adventure for you and Tony. You did it! You both did. You made it safely across the oceans, across the continents. I am so proud of you. You are not entirely alone. There are people from this group who are a lot closer to you geographically now. And we are not that far either

### EWO — 2022-03-01T19:05:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310656>

Enjoy this reminder...you are on your way to a new paradise.

### caroline — 2022-03-02T04:22:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310665>

> [EWO said:](/goto/post?id=310634)
>
> [@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/), remember moving is one of the single most stressful life events. You are not just moving but changing countries and continents. It's normal to feel a little lost and even homeless. It's going to take time, and this is the in between time that is likely to feel pretty uncomfortable. But it is also the opening to a new adventure for you and Tony. You did it! You both did. You made it safely across the oceans, across the continents. I am so proud of you. You are not entirely alone. There are people from this group who are a lot closer to you geographically now. And we are not that far either

Thanks for all that and all the reassurance. We have never had jet lag this bad before. Everything is much better tonite. We had churros and coffee!
We are trying to get back on schedule. It is 8:15pm. We will go to bed in an hour and hopefully be much better tomorrow.
Thank goodness we don’t need our ivermectin or hydroxy since we don’t know where they are!
We have decided to forgo any sightseeing and just keep walking and getting fresh air and sunshine.
Mexicans are so lovely. They have treated us with kindness and patience and are putting up with Tonys pathetic attempts at Spanish. Even tho he is exhausted from dealing with all the luggage at every turn ….he spent a couple of hours with Duolingo …while I had a nap.

yes ….moving is so stressful but changing continents is a whole different ball game!

at the Airport in Mexico customs know we are only here for 4 days so they wanted to know why 4 big suitcases and backpacks etc. Tony explained that our whole life is in those four bags!

that is a really daunting fact ….everything we own is in our luggage.

I see that bitcoin is on the rise. Our house closes in 4 days….

### caroline — 2022-03-02T04:28:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310666>

> [EWO said:](/goto/post?id=310656)
>
> Enjoy this reminder...you are on your way to a new paradise.
>

Love this thanx

### caroline — 2022-03-02T04:58:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310667>

Yay ….we just stumbled across the ivermectin!

we talked to a young man at the airport. He is double jabbed plus a booster …and he has had co 19 twice. Very sick and in hospital.

he is in the energy field …we told him that ES needs him…

### caroline — 2022-03-03T03:39:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310686>

Didn’t sleep last nite …jet lag still kicking our butt but tonite will be better.

we haven’t seen any tv and not online so we don’t have a clue what is happening in the world.

we really have no plans in place. We will ask for help and advice when we need it.

we will be staying at an airb&b in SanSalvador for two weeks while we settle in and find our way around. We have a really lovely host family who are very welcoming and helpful when needed. Also there is a lawyer in the family who will help us deal with legal stuff

since it really wasn’t possible to do any forward planning because we had too much to take care of before leaving ….the airb&b is turning out to be a great idea.

It feels really comforting and very cost effective while we adapt to a new life and not thinking in aussie$$

we are just flying by the seat of our pants and trusting our instincts.

the small village in Aus that we lived in was cut off because of flooding just days after we left. We were very lucky to get out when we did.

We are feeling confident …..

right now our most immediate problem is figuring out where to get a grilled steak!

We notice that Mecicans are quite overweight. The young girls generally have a big mid section. Their diet is pretty unhealthy….way too carb heavy.

### caroline — 2022-03-04T04:47:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310702>

Leaving Mexico City late tomorrow afternoon for San Salvador.

we are feeling pretty overwhelmed ….

but ….Tony’s attempts at Spanish are keeping us in stitches.

### caroline — 2022-03-04T23:30:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310716>

Waiting for our flight to ES. We are so excited to see what changes are in store for us.
We have met so many lovely people on this journey.
So many little things in life make a huge difference.
Tony asked a woman at the front desk this morning where he could buy some prunes. She has been so beautiful and kind and helpful during our stay. She first had someone check the kitchen and then sent someone to her home to bring back some.
Above and beyond ….

### Richard Watson — 2022-03-05T08:09:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310724>

Pleased ya made there.

### Anne V — 2022-03-05T12:18:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310725>

Welcome Home Audrey and Tony

### caroline — 2022-03-05T16:29:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310726>

Where are you?

### caroline — 2022-03-06T03:05:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310741>

We went grocery shopping this morning. Everything is so much cheaper.

I have no idea what to eat at this latitude. Vegetables are very appealing all of a sudden. Should we be eating less meat and more veg? There was gorgeous looking seafood but we don’t have the capability to cook very much right now.

any thoughts on what to eat this close to the equator?

there was nothing for breakfast in our little tiny apartment so we had to eat out. I am so embarrassed to say that we had breakfast at a donut shop. How American!

in this area all the American chains are well represented. I thought we would find lots of little local cafes.

Salvadorans are quite overweight so we will definitely stay away from local everyday foods that are a staple of their diet.

no one speaks English ….

### Marko Pollo — 2022-03-06T19:26:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310760>

fish + lime/lemon juice = ceviche.
I think not eating and enjoying the local food is a mistake. Just don't consume sugar and soda pop and limit the carbs. most locals eat/can afford mostly carbs and like most westerners are limiting fat consumption to lose weight. Ive not been to ES but my in experience with small latin cafes, there is usually no menu, just a pre fix plate of rice and beans with a piece of chicken or a pork chop and a small salad or bowl of soup. Trying to order something else is futile. Let go and enjoy the journey. Smile and say gracias. You are a refugee now but will regain control of what is important to you over time. Chicharon, singular, or Chicharones, plural, are fried pork nuggets that you may want to try! I am fasting today and am now dreaming of cheesy pupusas with squash blossoms! I'm so jelly of you two!

### caroline — 2022-03-06T21:47:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310770>

The supermarket here is a total wonder. We are so enjoying browsing and trying to decide what we would like to try.
Thankyou Marco ….I didn’t even think of getting some of the fresh fillets and some limes!!
I should have since sashimi is my favourite food.

we will head to El Zonte in a few days and see what Jack is smiling about.

I realise I know more Spanish than I thought. I have been reading cookbooks my whole life.

food is a universal language.

the locals seem to drink a lot of Soda. It is dirt cheap.

thankyou Marco for all your tips. Very much appreciated.

### Jack Kruse — 2022-03-06T22:11:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310771>

Our chef cooked us Chicharones last night. We went to San Salvador and El Zonte today. I posted the pics on Twitter and IG.

### Jacks — 2022-03-07T02:16:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310785>

So, Caroline, all in all - how long did it take to get out of OZ after you made the decision? Would be good to know for others contemplating the same move.
You could write a guide to help others.

### caroline — 2022-03-07T15:21:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310791>

> [ND Hauf said:](/goto/post?id=310780)
>
> Hey Caroline you probably know this butI’ve used a couple translator apps on my phone when in a bind. Luxury I don’t remember having thirty years ago on my last trip south.

Yes ….everyone seems to have a very up to date iphone ….and use google translater.

in the USA did you not learn Spanish in school?

just like in Canada we were required to take French.

### caroline — 2022-03-07T16:58:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310796>

> [Jacks said:](/goto/post?id=310785)
>
> So, Caroline, all in all - how long did it take to get out of OZ after you made the decision? Would be good to know for others contemplating the same move.
> You could write a guide to help others.

I have to think about that Jacks ….we had known for a long time that we had to get out.
The thing for us ….we both immigrated to Aus and we loved living there. We loved the climate and the aussies.
We totally loved where we lived for the last four years. A little fishing village on the ocean …population about 5,ooo. We were almost at the Queensland border so pretty tropical.
We spent time every morning at the ocean walking and swimming and meeting up with like minded people. There is a huge surfing culture in this town.

we were pretty isolated from all the Covid stuff in a way - life just went on but of course there were all the lock downs.
Since we aren’t jabbed we couldn’t cross borders and for a long time we couldn’t go into pubs, cafes or get a haircut etc.

I think the day we had to have identification on us once we left the house was the biggest insult. It really felt like nazi Germany.

I am of European heritage …Germany and Ukraine …so I always read a lot about the Holocaust etc as a kid. My Dad left Austria when he was 18 for a better life in Canada. I had an uncle who was in a concentration camp for a number of years.

looking back the last few months …there was one insult after another and our freedom was being eroded.
A lot of people we knew said they wouldn’t get jabbed …but they did for one stupid reason or another …like they wanted to go to the pub.

Tony has a new granddaughter …born last October - and he was not allowed to see her. We were supposed to join them for Christmas but then weren’t allowed. This is a son who Tony was very close to.

this all became very personal and untenable for so many reasons. This was even more crystallised because our daughter in law is a doctor. The whole situation was insulting.
People who seemed to love freedom didn’t really care at all!

I think by the end of December we knew we had to and wanted to go and joined a group trying to arrange private charters out of Australia. There was supposed to be a flight out around Feb 18th and we were in touch with others hoping to be on this flight. By the middle of January nothing seemed to really be happening and we became aware that we could leave Aus if we flew Emirates to Dubai and then Barcelona and onward to Mexico City. Total time from Sydney to Mexico City was almost 60 hours. We are still tired!

there are so many moving pieces to this puzzle tho. So many things had to fall into place to make this journey to freedom a reality.

the biggest thing is the people who stepped up to help in their own way.
LAUDY discovered a travel agent in Melbourne=who knew how to get us out.

One weekend friends were in town unexpectedly. They had built our camper and we bought our truck thru them. Jim offered to sell it all for us …and he did - in three days he got us a great price - pretty much what we paid for it

our house sold in less than twelve hours for a great price.

that was all the big stuff taken care of.

the rest of it was a huge emotional journey. The above mentioned son really lost it in a big way.
We decided not to send our stuff in a container - too expensive and we didn’t want to be tied down.
It was a big job to sell all our bits and pieces….to get a decent price wasn’t easy but we met so many fantastic people and we bonded with these folks taking home our much loved possessions.
We met so people who had to get jabbed because they have a mortgage to pay and needed to keep their jobs but they know what is really going on.

It was hard culling our stuff down to what would fit into 4 suitcases. Things I thought I would never part with eventually went to new homes. It became easier and easier when we realised it was just stuff and we held memories in our heart …and it was time to make new memories anyway.

I have saved the most important for last …. Dr. Jack Kruse

I stumbled across Jack on MDA - probably 10 years ago. My husband had just died and I found this new focus of an optimal health journey.

this has all been an amazing journey of health and wealth and love.

Tony and I are totally amazed how this has all fallen into place.

Jack has taught us to trust our instincts and the best doctor is the one in our head.

what a journey we are all on here…

remarkable, inspiring, gut wrenching at times but amazing and beautiful and life affirming.

we are healthy and well and always inspired to do better and be better and keep learning and growing.

for some reason I thought learning Spanish would be easy…

they speak at the speed of lite!

we have met so many lovely, kind people on this journey.

thankyou to so many people who cross our path every single day.

we feel like the luckiest people alive.

so grateful to be here!

### caroline — 2022-03-09T02:17:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310832>

Random thoughts .......

we are feeling homesick ........we haven't been in the ocean for at least a week.

We have lost most of our suntan for some reason

The lawyer we are going to hire just called to chat ....and he met Jack yesterday.

small world especially here in El Salvador

We were getting worried that property prices have really gone up but it seems like we may still be able to afford a roof over our head.

It seems like a lot of movers and shakers are arriving in ES.

definitely not our crowd.

We had a long conversation with Nancy and Tom Watson this afternoon - very helpful to have boots on the ground to advise us on all kinds of things.

### Jack Kruse — 2022-03-11T00:07:37+0200

What happens when CPI is skyrocketing, the FED Fund Rate is at ZERO and the Yield Curve is inverting ALL AT ONCE? Now try to find a time where that also happened in the past...

We no longer have a 3 legged stool for life. It is now 4 legged. Light, water, magnetism, Bitcoin.

If BTC has to displace one of the original 3, water is the one. That should make everyone in Mexico realize why staying there vs ES now makes very little sense.

### caroline — 2022-03-11T14:58:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-310946>

I am really homesick today........missing our routines, missing cooking great food, missing our beach and the surf etc etc. missing all the people we chat with every morning, missing our nice comfortable bed......

I suppose that saying I am homesick is the wrong way to say it. I am not missing being in a fascist country. We are just out of our comfort zone so we have to work harder to improve our situation.

We are really happy to be here - really happy we came -

There are so many solid reasons to be here.....

uncomfortable is good - makes us stronger

wealth=health

light, water, magnetism and bitcoin!

### Jack Kruse — 2022-03-12T18:07:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311039>

You do not need a new house right away. Airbnb it for some weeks to see where you want to go. Stop rushing it.

### caroline — 2022-03-12T19:43:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311056>

We are in an airB&B now but have to leave here this week so we have to decide what area we want to be in. Jeremy will help us with that next week.

Around the coast airb&b's are really big and expensive. People here, like everyone are now going down the airb&b route instead of renting because it is far more profitable.

This was a huge problem in Aus. In Byron bay in particular. Istead of renting out flats everyone was turning them into an airb&b and people could not find apartments to rent.
The apartments that were available were ridiculously expensive and unaffordable for the average guy.

Where we are now is okay and inexpensive but no kitchen - except for a microwave and no garden at all so we are inside way too much on our computers trying to track down stuff.

We are trying not to erode our money too fast ....and our money is in Aus$$$

Of course ...Renting for awhile would be ideal.......

Thanks for the suggestions and help........we are a work in progress.

Any thoughts/ideas are very much appreciated.

### John Schumacher — 2022-03-12T20:59:47+0200

[@Anne V](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22299/) - I have done the "HeartMath recommendation for **breath work:" in for about 5 to 6 seconds and for about out 5 to 6 seconds**, making sure the breathing is smooth, unforced and comfortable. I have tried longer slower inhale times and longer slower exhale times.
However, my HeartMath score has never improved in four years of "training." However, if my HeartMath score does "improve", I feel a bright-white pain from the center of my body which eventually fills the room with its very-very painful bright-white light.
I have spoken with HeartMath coaches. They have never heard of such an experience and have not been able to offer any help.

### caroline — 2022-03-12T23:59:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311069>

> [Anne V said:](/goto/post?id=311057)
>
> Can you think you are on a long holiday ? 1 year away to discover ... if the mindset change , it will be better . if you wish , i can teach you HeartMath breathing , it will help you a lot xxxHugs

Thanks Anne ....I am just sort of recording my feelings on any given day.

We are really fine and very happy with our decision to come to ES. We just have this immediate problem of finding another place to stay. We are trying to weigh all our options and figure out what is best for right now. We don't have a car and not sure yet if we should be taking local buses. We thought it would be okay but Jeremy says we should be very careful.

However - I have been to Mexico and took local buses there and Peurto Rico and we always took local buses in Nassau. They were great fun. We never knew for sure where we would end up. It is a great way to meet the locals and see stuff.

The place we felt most unsafe was the U.S. Virgin islands. I was given a spiked drink at the beach bar on the first day and was out of it for a few days. Some one tried to get into our room that night. Other people in the hotel were robbed.
The locks on the doors were really flimsy. My DH just pulled the nite latch off without a problem and had security fix it. We just didn't feel safe anywhere there and hated it. It was beautiful.

In Puerto Rico we were standing waiting to get on a bus ...I was kind of off to the side with my little girls and I saw a guy slip his hand into my husbands pocket. I punched him!

On the other hand - we were in Disney world/Florida and I left my handbag on a chair in the dining room. It had in it every bit of money and credit cards we had on us. We had just put everything in my purse for reasons I can't remember.

The staff had my handbag when we realized two hours later what we had done.

Very thankful.

### caroline — 2022-03-13T00:00:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311070>

It is just hard to be spending all this money when we could be buying Bitcoin!!!

### caroline — 2022-03-13T00:07:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311071>

If we were younger - I would like to buy land and do an eco village of small houses.

I love the idea of tiny houses ...with huge outdoor living areas and a pool ...and a view!

We don't want a big house with multiple bathrooms to keep clean. We want to be outside.

the four legged stool!!

### caroline — 2022-03-15T16:45:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311129>

We are spending the day with Jeremy Franco today. What a great guy. I don’t know how we would have managed to get our TIN number without him. No one speaks English in government offices.

we are heading for the coast and to check out different areas and looking forward to a seafood lunch.

not sure how this day is going to go. …..we both have Bali belly

we have been really careful with water….but something got us

Montezuma revenge.

Wonder what advice to suggest about water.

1\. Listen to [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) , he advises (in the last PowWow) that water should be a first thing that you buy when in this types of countries.

2\. Note what locals drink, you do the same. After a while you will get used to local water.
For that you should be stabilized and close to the bathroom at first.

3\. Get chlorine dioxide water.
(Assume that you are in the tropical jungle where malaria and other goodies run supreme).
If you use it or not, you should have 10 - 20 kits with you, and understand how to use it.
And what it does.
It not only kills bad stuff in the water
it also eats silverware, (you do not want to drink your silverware).
[https://dokumen.pub/forbidden-health-9788461742721.html](https://dokumen.pub/forbidden-health-9788461742721.html)

### caroline — 2022-03-17T01:16:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311183>

We had a long day yesterday ......Jeremy took us to all the usual places along the coast.
We are very surprised at how dry it is here.
There was mountains of fresh seafood
We had a wonderful seafood lunch at a spectacular location.
I am positive that Jack will second that.

We went to Hope house and bought t-shirts - I love the story of Hope House and how all this started.

A reporter and camera guy filmed Jeremy buying btc at the chivo ATM.

They interviewed Tony about why he came all the way from Australia.
I was so proud of him ....he talked about the reasons we are here .......health and the equator and bitcoin. Tony was telling them about all the health reasons to be here he almost forgot to mention bitcoin!
The four legged stool!

Jeremy is a great student of Jack's - and he is paying it forward,

We saw people come into Jeremy's cafe and ask him about bitcoin.

This isn't easy - mostly because of the language difficulties - but we are moving forward little by little and we are good.

except for bali belly......

### caroline — 2022-03-18T15:37:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311229>

We met with our lawyer yesterday ......
I thought I had brought everything we needed but I had dropped the ball in a couple of important areas and now it is going to cost us big$$$$ to sort this out.

Jeremy took us to a friend who has a little shop and fixes cell phones and computers.
Our phones are pretty screwed up ...another BIG lesson learned.

I had read multiple times to have an extra phone with a sim card from your home country. I didn't really understand why at the time - but I sure do now!

I do have my old sim ....but the carrier isn't supported here in El Salvador so, even with international roaming, I have no service.

Do you have any idea how many times I get a code sent to my old number??????
and, of course, can't access it.

This is causing both of us so much grief and frustration - and wasting so much time.

This phone guy - Douglas is his name - Is the most polite and lovely young man.
He spent 2.5 hours on my phone ....and then wouldn't take any money.
I gave him $20.00 but he just didn't want to take it.
Since we are going back next week to sort out Tony's computer issues he agreed to keep the $20.00 and put it towards his work next week..

We are moving today to another airb&b in San Salvador and a new area to explore.

### Lahelada — 2022-03-18T19:14:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311239>

From what I can see Australia shares only the B5 frequency range with El Salvador, which limits you to roaming via TIGO (3g and 4g) and Movistar (3g). You may have to actively select those carriers for your roaming. The phone will select the strongest signal but it may not be the SIM compatible one.
However, depending on your Australian phone (age,price) you may not have the B5 range available. You could then try your SIM in a Salvadoran phone (celular libre) that is not bound to a cellphone company.

### JanSz — 2022-03-18T19:57:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311241>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=311239)
>
> From what I can see Australia shares only the B5 frequency range with El Salvador, which limits you to roaming via TIGO (3g and 4g) and Movistar (3g). You may have to actively select those carriers for your roaming. The phone will select the strongest signal but it may not be the SIM compatible one.
> However, depending on your Australian phone (age,price) you may not have the B5 range available. You could then try your SIM in a Salvadoran phone (celular libre) that is not bound to a cellphone company.

[@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/)
you got [@Lahelada](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6531/)
attention. Likely also few other people who know this stuff (and hopefully are also willing to condense complicated multi-page information into one or two short test lines.

Find out on your phone a page like this, take its screen shot and post it.
May narrow down your search.

Or most likely just buy local phone and be over with.

### caroline — 2022-03-20T03:39:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311306>

We have moved to another airb&b in another district in San Salvador.

This is a historical area with lovely old homes.

the traffic is even crazier here. we love to walk but crossing streets is an absolute nightmare.

our tiny little space is fine ....lovely but tiny.

the main house is very traditional and Grand - with a capital G. I have never been in home like this before. It is so very simple yet very elegant.

Our Host - the lady of this beautiful home is delightful and warm and welcoming.

The main living areas have very high ceilings. There is a second lounge area and dining combination with floor to ceiling glass that overlooks an atrium - open to the sky. The thing is when you leave this area and walk towards the back of the house - this atrium is open on two sides and is very much a part of the house - and the kitchen is open to this atrium as well.

I wish I could explain this better but all I could think of was the three legged stool,
She has sun and rain and whatever is happening flowing into the middle of the house. There are stone floors and plants everywhere because her and her family were in the flower business.

Maritza took us shopping today in her 35 year old car, She also has an orange truck.

We love all the stuff happening on the streets. Vendors selling fresh fruit and bread and anything you can think of really.

Everyone greets us and each other.

We have decided to forgo trying to solve our various problems and enjoy the weekend.

Tony now has bali belly with a vengeance . We ate at a local cafe that was recommended and we shared one plate of assorted grilled meats and salad and chips so we ate the exact same things. I am fine.

### Lahelada — 2022-03-20T19:39:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311313>

Sounds like a lovely new place!
Maybe it was the salad ? Agriculture in places with water shortages uses "treated" water which may or may not be 100%. Local pipes/tanks can be contaminated, even if the water quality in the area is generally fine. (Brushing teeth with tap water?) I had steak in Mexico once which unbeknownst to me came with salad on the same plate. I did not eat the salad, but the cross contamination was there and I still got violently sick.

### caroline — 2022-03-20T23:13:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311320>

we were told the salad was organic but what the heck does that really mean ...and water is water here.
Tony probably ate more salad - while I ate the chips!

### caroline — 2022-03-21T23:40:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311356>

what I know for sure.......

Salvadorans are lovely, generous, hospitable people

Everything would be so much easier if we spoke Spanish

We had to go back to the computer shop with our phones etc. Once again Douglas wouldn't take any money.
Once again we forced $20.00 on him and made him promise to buy bitcoin.

On a daily basis so many people have gone out of their way to help us....Today a Salvadoran man walked us all over a huge shopping mall to help us find a claro shop. He wants to go to Prince Edward Island in Canada because he loves Anne of green Gables.

Buying realestate may be tricky because they think that white people with blue eyes have a lot of money

I learned how to drink water out of a plastic bag!

### JanSz — 2022-03-22T00:07:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311357>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=311356)
>
> Buying realestate may be tricky because they think that white people with blue eyes have a lot of money

Possibly you do not want to buy real estate now.

After about two years in USA, I am visiting Poland. Must have been around 1973 or 4.
Not really intending to buy anything, I have found nice place offered for sale.
Price is X
I said ok I am buying
Guy said no, I really meant 2X
I said ok I am buying
Guy admits that he really does not know the worth of his place, not many sales.

Many first generation immigrant Poles in USA want to live in two places at the same time.
USA and Poland.
That is heavy psychological load.
My grandfather was one of those.
He spend most of his working life in USA, then went back to Poland when old.
Grandma was in Poland all the time raising children.
Now it is Ukraine there, not a Poland, that's another story.
I am guessing that he had sex with my grandma not much more than the three times.
They had 3 children.

I solved this problem by
not visiting Poland
buying grave in USA

[@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) consider buying grave first. That should give you a mental anchor.
Real estate can wait. Rent a place where you want to live.
...........................

### Lahelada — 2022-03-22T04:50:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311365>

You probably didn't expect me to chime in but.. I agree with Jan. Don't buy too quickly, especially if your budget is under 120k. There are things to consider you will probably not think of yet. Eg important to know when the house was built. It will give you an idea whether the house was built to seismic code, whether it has already resisted an earthquake. Don't rush things. You will get a feel for where you need to look.

### JanSz — 2022-03-22T14:38:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311370>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=311365)
>
> You probably did not expect me to chime in but.. I agree with Jan. Don't buy too quickly, especially if your budget is under 120k. There are things to consider you will probably not think of yet. Eg important to know when the house was built. It will give you an idea whether the house was built to seismic code, whether it has already resisted an earthquake etc. If you are buying in a city you also want to take a look at the neighbour's house. If they have built a three storey house very close to your perimeter theirs should be to code,too.. If you buy from a developer try and find out their reputation. Once the stucco is up, it is not so easy to see how much rebar went into the construction. Don't rush things. You will get a feel for where you need to look.

whether the house was built to seismic code
\=======================================
I was involved in designing Nuclear Power Plants.
Lots of that work is protection from seismic events.
Once spend couple weeks in San Jose CA on my job.
Nothing to do afternoons, so I am calling real estate agent and he drives me around showing his nice houses.
One of the houses stays in my mind.
The agent was salivating over how it was build to resist seismic events.
Basically a box resting on flat slab. Slab is larger than the box.
Gas, water, electric is coming up from the slab and before is attached to the house goes thru the loop that allows flexibility.
During seismic, the house can slide over slab at least one foot in any direction and none of the utilities breaks.

Thanks [@Lahelada](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6531/) I didn't know that [@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) prefers dancing to a seismic music.

There are variety of seismic events.
Some just ground shakes.
Some ground shakes then opens up.
Some all of the above plus sitting on a volcano.
etc
.........

### Lahelada — 2022-03-22T18:31:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311377>

The whole region has made great improvements in construction in the last ten years! The construction is usually brick and mortar or cinder block, so the fortification is in concrete columns and reinforced concrete beams halfway up the wall and on the top of the wall. The whole house is usually set on a concrete slab.

### JanSz — 2022-03-22T18:52:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311378>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=311377)
>
> The whole region has made great improvements in construction in the last ten years! The construction is usually brick and mortar or cinder block, so the fortification is in concrete columns and reinforced concrete beams halfway up the wall and on the top of the wall. The whole house is usually set on a concrete slab.

During an earthquake, is the whole house able to slide over that concrete slab (without damaging anything)?
How many stories houses could be built that way?

Skyscrapers could also be built in that kind of area, but the construction is different.
My only skyscraper experience was working on 36 or 37 floor
1 Penn Plz, New York, NY 10119
.
Especially on windy Saturdays when only a few peoples were on the floor. and all was quiet.
The building was moving significantly sideways, making significant noise.
Feeling that movement I was thinking about the steel to glass connections, hoping for sufficient clearances.

.

Yes, indeed! However, a lot of progress has been made and these days people are more prepared. Actually most quakes under 5 are not even felt very much.
Let's not scare people though. We are not used to it in other parts of the world. There people take the small ones in their stride. One needs to take precautions and look at things with that perspective in mind, prepare well and then take things in one's stride.

### caroline — 2022-03-26T02:09:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311558>

We are so very lucky ....we have a wonderful family in our new airb&b.
They and their friends have adopted us.

They are such a wealth of information and are helping us in so many ways.

I really totally screwed up what we needed for residency before we left Aus.
Paying the price now.

Just a simple thing like the name on my passport has to be the same on all supporting documents ...and it isn't.

Luckily our new family has come to the rescue and their family lawyer is coming to the house to give us a second opinion.

We are still plagued with phone issues.....

We have found a beautiful park that isn't too far from us so we can walk and sit in the sun and exercise.
It is a very old park but President Bukele has recently had it renovated.

There is a big sign with the various trees and plants and shows photosynthesis.

We are so incredibly lucky........and so happy to be here.

I think I need to write a book on how not to screw up when changing continents!

### caroline — 2022-03-26T02:12:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311559>

Premium ground beef is $3.79 a pound ....and it is making fantastic hamburgers! we love hamburgers!

This park is immaculate. There are always lots of people sweeping and blowing leaves etc.

i wii ask

### Marko Pollo — 2022-03-27T02:36:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311601>

Pick your battles. The Latinos culture is very conservative in general, especially away from beach towns. many guards just enforce rules they were told to enforce. Not taught to think outside of box. Law and order is desired by many people because the lawlessness they know is way worse than the mostly peaceful protests we experienced.

### caroline — 2022-03-27T18:08:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311607>

yes we figured that. people are very conservatively dressed. Even tho it is quite hot most people wear jeans and hoodies.

We should have known better ........This is the city - not the beach.

All the years I spent in the Bahamas it was the same ....you wear whatever at the beach but you would never go into Nassau unless you were fully clothed.

Where we used to live in Aus ....it was a beach town - but sometimes you would see women dressed in really skimpy bikinis walking down the street - not really appropriate.

In the Bahamas - when you went to church - you dressed up in your Sunday best. That meant a suit for a man and usually a dress for a woman ....no matter how hot it was.

### Jack Kruse — 2022-03-28T21:18:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311640>

Not everyone will make it to your future. Some people are just passing through to teach you lessons in life. When you struggle with your partner or a situation you are struggling with yourself. Every fault you see in them touches a denied weakness in yourself. Do not recoil from this reality. Embrace the suck. Embrace its discomfort and go ALL In. You'll get your wings when you jump into uncertainty. It will be the hardest thing for you to ever do...........but you must do it when you are walking through the valley of uncertainty with someone.

Remember, to be efficient with your time when you are in times of uncertainty. You have no time to waste when you are walking the unknown path. Value the cost of efficiency. The key idea is when visiting a place like this the key is to never stop moving toward improvement. Once you slow down or stop on this road, the result will be predictable.

A book I hated in college written by John Milton, 'Paradise Lost', said it best. This one quote has been on my mind for 4 hours now. “Long is the way and hard, that out of Hell leads up to the light.”

Sometimes friends tell you what you need to hear and not what you want to hear.

### caroline — 2022-04-11T01:39:10+0300

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=311640)

> Not everyone will make it to your future. Some people are just passing through to teach you lessons in life. When you struggle with your partner or a situation you are struggling with yourself. Every fault you see in them touches a denied weakness in yourself. Do not recoil from this reality. Embrace the suck. Embrace its discomfort and go ALL In. You'll get your wings when you jump into uncertainty. It will be the hardest thing for you to ever do...........but you must do it when you are walking through the valley of uncertainty with someone.
>
> Remember, to be efficient with your time when you are in times of uncertainty. You have no time to waste when you are walking the unknown path. Value the cost of efficiency. The key idea is when visiting a place like this the key is to never stop moving toward improvement. Once you slow down or stop on this road, the result will be predictable.
>
>
> A book I hated in college written by John Milton, 'Paradise Lost', said it best. This one quote has been on my mind for 4 hours now. “Long is the way and hard, that out of Hell leads up to the light.”
>
> Sometimes friends tell you what you need to hear and not what you want to hear.

Yes - we all need friends who love and support us -and tell us what we need to hear.

But you need to be careful and mindful to not destroy someone's self worth and integrity and confidence.

A good friend in our life is so powerful.

A good friend is someone who listens - really listens and doesn't make assumptions

A good friend is supportive .......not destructive

No one can walk in our shoes ......but a wonderful, supportive friend will walk beside us and pick up the pieces when needed.
Offer advice when asked
give unconditional love always
and yes ......tell us what we need to hear sometimes

and - make us a mojito and cheesecake!

### caroline — 2022-04-11T04:11:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311949>

things would go way better if we spoke Spanish.......

Salvadorians are gorgeous people.....

We finally got ourselves some pupusas yesterday

About 2 blocks from where we are living ....there is a tortilla assembly line.

They have a huge cauldron of corn boiling over a wood fire - in the street

There are about 6 ladies who form the tortillas and then they cook them on a grill over another wood fire.

the smell is divine.........

### caroline — 2022-04-11T04:17:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.5458/post-311951>

**The Real Mojito**

Rating: 5 stars

-   717 Ratings
-   [487 Reviews](https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/147363/the-real-mojito/#reviewSection)
-   [127 Photos](https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/147363/the-real-mojito/#reviewSection)

This is an authentic recipe for mojito. I sized the recipe for one serving, but you can adjust it accordingly and make a pitcher full. It's a very refreshing drink for hot summer days. Be careful when drinking it, however. If you make a pitcher you might be tempted to drink the whole thing yourself, and you just might find yourself talking Spanish in no time!

I just found this ....read the last line of this recipe!!

I am in!

### JanSz — 2022-04-11T15:09:00+0300

### caroline — 2022-04-17T05:29:07+0300
