### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-02-10T14:57:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-75204>

Aussie N. Wow did the people not swim b/c they felt it was 'cold'? I swam up until Nov. when I got too wussy to go back in lol. Air temp -3'C and water hovering a few degrees above 0'C.

Ooooo well. Can't wait until it's 24'C here and water 20'C.

### ashryn — 2013-02-10T16:30:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-75213>

I did the beach twice this weekend.. It was most awesome.. The waves were perfect for body surfing even if the kiddies and the over50s body board brigade got in the way all the time. I think it's just because you were at the dog beach AN, there was plenty of perv factor at trigg.

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-11T04:06:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-75345>

I think you are right ashryn. The dog beach is the only place I go as I'm too busy to go just for myself. I have to fit it in with an additional task. And the dog would be very upset if I went to the beach without her.

Right today.
Last night I had the most incredible dreams. Is dreaming part of this process of healing? I had what I felt were really important dreams - chunky, full colour, full story line and they felt as if they had substance. I was in a place where I was at home or at feeling at home at work and I realised that some people had come in with machine guns. They were soldiers from a different country. I had trouble believing this to start with but I sidled out of the room and went into another room where I sank to the floor in disbelief, then got up and told people they should be really careful because there was more than one person with an automatic machine gun out there. They didn't want to believe me, or believed me but didn't think they would cause trouble. When they heard shots they got afraid, but some still didn't want to believe they should change what they were doing. I thought I'm getting out of here. But we were rounded up and told we would be OK just so long as we followed orders. We now had to do what we were told. But the controls were not great and I thought again I'm getting out of here. Most people decided they had no choice but to go along with the new authority. I didn't and decided to leave, leaving behind all these people who thought they had no choice.
When I slipped out of the place there was a multi-billionaire who said, yes you can come on my yacht. He wasn't sure if we would make it out before we would be captured but given the lack of numbers of controls we might make it. I was one of a few dozen people on the yacht and the billionaire wasn't part of the story after that. The yacht was a super yacht but it had to go down this narrow canal to make it out to sea and freedom. We were mildly anxious about the trip, and I remember being slightly concerned about the beating we would get if we couldn't escape, but it was worth the try. I woke just before I reached where I could see the sea.

Then another big dream featuring my mother getting fined for wandering along and checking out the train tracks. I had decided these train tracks weren't where I was going, but you had to go by their rules and non railway people weren't allowed on the tracks.

I'm quite happy with my dream interpretation - I've just put it here for later reference.

Deep dreams plus deep sleep. I wonder how I would have felt if the air conditioning hadn't woken me with cold at 10pm (to turn it off) then with the heat at 2.30 am to turn it on again for half an hour. Sleeping in over 80 degrees F of heat usually leaves me drained but despite the wakefulness I felt I had a better than usual sleep.

I'm going to have to change my air con settings for the next two nights as we are in the middle of a heat wave where its over 100 F or thereabouts for 5 days with nights mostly about 80 - 85 inside without the air con. Its supposed to drop down to 70-75 but that is only at the night's coolest and the house doesn't drop that low. I'm off to New Zealand on Wednesday to see my sister who's just out of hospital after heart surgery following a heart attack.

### ashryn — 2013-02-11T04:19:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-75351>

Ooh good dreams! Especially the one that validates your choice to go against the flow.. I wish I could remember mine too.

Its good weather for putting in a pool tomorrow
good luck with your sis in NZ.. Hopefully you will get the chance to share some of the kruze magic beans with her..

### caroline — 2013-02-11T06:06:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-75365>

Good luck seeing your sister. Don't forget to take your own snacks on the plane ........

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-11T08:00:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-75371>

Snacks, Caroline, I thought Jack said no snacks. Just kidding . I thought I would have BAB and pick up some plain chicken at the airport half way through my journey. i'm only eating twice a day but perhaps I should just take some lamb from home together with some salad instead. Yes, better idea. I'm cooking a lamb roast tomorrow night so I can take some of that with me.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-02-11T17:14:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-75391>

OMG AN please don't eat airport chicken... You might get MSG and weird headaches. Your energy will be zapped that's for sure. Glad to see you're making the lamb. It's not hard to have it in a baggie and eat it like a jerky. Throw some CO oil on it...and voila.

Ugh. I tested stupid experiments and I deserve what I got....I just though that I was somehow 'immune' now...NOPE. Sigh.

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-12T00:21:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-75444>

I'm not sure what I'll be doing for breakfast the morning after tomorrow - I'll still be in transit. I'm not sure. In the past I would have had eggs, but they are off the menu because of AI. Or nuts, ditto. I'm not able to take any food from home because I can't get food through customs into New Zealand. Now is it better to have eggs which might be problematic, or chicken, ditto, or nothing. I won't feel like eating as it will be like eating at 2.30am home time, but it will be morning and Jack insists we eat within half an hour of dawn/waking. Any ideas anyone?

I know what you mean about testing experiments. I had a little mango yesterday and I shouldn't have. We eat mango here when its hot and we are in the middle of a heat wave. Its supposed to have the sugar to salt proportions needed for the heat but my body said it was too sugary for me at the moment.

It is currently 6.20 am and about 85F (31.5 C outside my front door in the shade) outside. It hasn't dropped the temperature below that all night. Ughhh... I went around yesterday with ice stuck down my pants. Its interesting that I can now fit two blocks of ice inside my once tight pants. I've lost a few hundred grams, but I'm losing centimeters from around my pot belly.

### caroline — 2013-02-12T00:30:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-75448>

> [Aussie Nana said:](/goto/post?id=75371)
>
> Snacks, Caroline, I thought Jack said no snacks. Just kidding . I thought I would have BAB and pick up some plain chicken at the airport half way through my journey. i'm only eating twice a day but perhaps I should just take some lamb from home together with some salad instead. Yes, better idea. I'm cooking a lamb roast tomorrow night so I can take some of that with me.

AN - are you finding 90 or 99% chocolate here? I can get a 85% choc at aldis and woolies but I think they have too much sugar....

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-12T07:21:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-75497>

I've had 90% Cadbury's chocolate but its hard to find. 90% Lindt is easier to find but more expensive. I'm off chocolate at the moment for autoimmune reasons - it sets of a flare, or did last time I had it, though I'm hopeful I will be able to add chocolate and eggs and tomatoes back into my diet at some stage or other.

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-12T07:26:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-75499>

It is hot here today. At 7.00 am it was 32 C (90F). By lunch time it was 40 C (104 F). I do NOT like it this hot. And I especially don't like having to eat a fatty BAB at 6 in the morning in the heat. I get indigestion. I really don't like stuffing myself so full. At all. Grizzle over.

Getting ready to leave tomorrow morning for NZ. I am likely to be offline for a week from tomorrow as I may not be able to access things when there.

### ashryn — 2013-02-12T07:48:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-75504>

Hey, just a thought on the travelling BAB... If you haven't been to sleep, do you change the timing to coincide with your big sleep in the dark? Can you take beef jerky to eat for BAB? Can you call it intermittent fasting and not eat till after you have slept? Perhaps you could do a protein shake if you will be on the plane.. You can find low carb ones in some of the bodybuilder friendly health food stores. Or do they use egg white for the protein? Mark sisson makes one i think that might be ok in a travelling situation, but I haven't looked too much into them, so this might not be useful. (Nor do I know if anyone sells them in perth or if you have to buy online)

RE the weather... I'm in aircon so not suffering much, however there are two poor bastards digging up my backyard in 40degree heat. I have given them an esky full of ice and water and soft drinks in the hope they won't die of heat stroke. All the sand dust covering them should be protecting their skin a bit at least.. And they are both wearing thongs so I am a bit happy that they are getting electrons and vitamin D... (But I'm not going to say that to them, I doubt they'll thank me for pollyanna-ing them in this weather)

### caroline — 2013-02-12T09:18:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-75514>

Can you take cans of sardines?

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-12T11:09:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-75518>

I hadn't thought of a can of sardines. I could probably take them. Mind you I've not eaten sardines for... decades. They were never my favourite food. We used to have them on toast on sunday nights in my childhood. But it could hold me through in the morning.

I just got my labs back. Some 13 pages of tests. On the basis of them I self diagnose a continuing leaky gut. As I still haven't heard from Jack about the tests I should order I'll get my husband to scan these tonight or tomorrow morning so Jack can look through them and consider them alongside my history for ordering more tests.

Basically my fasting glucose is continuing to drop from 5.9 in 2009 to 4.7 now (ref range 3.5-5.4)
Fe studies all in the middle range.
LDL is 4.4 (.5) and total cholesterol 7.3 (<5.5) but my HDL is now up to 2.5 (1.1-3.5) and a substantial improvement up from 1.4 a few years back. Coronary risk ratio is down from 4.4 in 2009 to 2.9 now (.5). So its just the LDL that is a bit high.
Renal function OK except eGFR is slightly higher than it should be. Liver function fine.
HsCRP is 0.47 (<5.00mg/L) which they characterise as low risk and Jack wants below 0.5. Yay!

TSH is 1.87 (.5-5.5), an improvement on the 2.83 in 2011 but higher than it should be from what I've read in Jack's stuff.
Free T4 14 (11.0-21.0)
Free T3 3.7 (3.1-6.0) I think these are different tests to what Jack does
This suggests my thyroid is still struggling, though its a vast improvement on the past, especially as I've been able to go off the meds.

RBC folate 1286 (629-1453)
Vit B12 is 1465 (139-651) - highly associated with supplementation, liver damage or haematological malignancy. Hope and expect its the supplementation - theres Vit B in with the betaine hydrochloride I take with breakfast.

Vitamin D 25OH Vit D 101 nmol/L (51-150) Improvement from 5 years ago when I was at about 49. No supplementation over summer.
Haematology in the normal ranges
CCP Abs <1 (<5) anticyclic citrullinated peptides

HLA B27 (flow cytometry) Equivocal - same as last time. I think this is the ankylosing spondylitis AI marker. I used to be positive. Daughter has this condition and its genetic.
Antinuclear antibodies (ANA) Positive speckled pattern (titre 1:80)
Parietal cell antibodies negative (<1:20)
Smooth muscle antibodies (ASM) weak positive (titre <1:80)
mitochondrial antibodies (AMA) negative (<1:20)
So there is likely to be something to watch in here.

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-20T01:02:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-76577>

I've just got back from a trip to New Zealand to see my sister who is just getting over having stents put in post heart attack. She was not well by my standards. I'm still processing it all.

Food-wise I was good and my weight shows that - not quite at the top of my OK range I allow myself. Sleep went to the dogs while I was there as I faced her and my mortality and the general unfairness of life.

What was exciting was that on the first morning after flying over I was five hours earlier in time. I needed an alarm to wake up but as soon as I drew the curtains and the light streamed in I could feel my hormones change - in the click of fingers. It was amazing. My body said, "oh, its morning. I'll wake up." And I stayed awake till the evening drew in. Then it was an early night. I did need melatonin to go to actually sleep for two nights, but after that I was fine. I've never adapted five hours so quickly in decades.

### endless — 2013-02-20T01:32:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-76580>

Did you do any grounding for jet lag? I'm curious about that!

I can imagine it would be hard to eat the BAB in such heat....I'm sitting in snow here so it's a lot easier.

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-20T05:40:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-76623>

Yes, endless, I took off to the beach at the first opportunity. Couldn't stay anywhere near as long as I wanted as my sister wasn't well enough. Other than that it was bare feet all the time and choosing to spend time just walking on the grass.

### caroline — 2013-02-20T08:22:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-76633>

My thoughts and prayers are with you ....

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-21T03:08:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-76805>

Thanks, Caroline.

I'm interested though in my emotional responses to the difficulties I'm going through. While they are similar - grief is grief, annoyance is annoyance - there is something different. The amount of pain I'm going through is different. I seem to sink into a gentle weeping grief when facing the inevitable, not a gut tearing grief that affects every muscle of my body. When I made a silly mistake while away that cost me a couple of hundred extra dollars I was annoyed with myself but I only thought about it a few times, I didn't beat myself up continuously causing huge pain all that day and the next and the next. Its different and much gentler. I put this down to an improved hormonal cascade.

So its back to normal. My weight was down a little, which is nice. I find it richly affirming to keep seeing that I'm no longer the 85 kg that was so awful five years back. Now down below 65kg and oh so slowly dropping. It would be nice to drop faster but at least it is directionally positive.

My sleep was bad while I was away and my first night back wasn't good. Last night I went to bed as soon as it was dark - about 8pm and went to sleep immediately, waking at 1.20am, stayed awake for a couple of hours then back to sleep from 4am till 6am. The difference was that both parts of my sleeping night were very deep and restful, not light and wakeful, with about half an hour between 3.30 and 4.00 being light. (Blasted dog came and barked outside my door every half minute till I let her in - blast, I've let her train me!)

### caroline — 2013-02-21T09:24:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-76851>

I hope your sleep gets back on track quickly - That is the one thing I won't let slide if I can help it. The last trip I was on - we had 4 overnite flights. It was just a disaster for me. I will never do that again.

Glad to hear that your weight is directionally positive! I am finding that I am where I always wanted to be and thought I should be - 57 K - but I seem kind of pudgy now - like I really need to lean down some more. I am wondering if it is all the progesterone - I just seem rounder. That wouldn't be a bad thing on a different frame - like an hourglass...

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-22T07:19:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-76996>

Caroline, the weight thing is interesting. I rather wonder how I will end up. I definitely have another 10kg to go - well that much fat anyway. If I can I would like to improve my muscle mass but don't know how much of that is possible at my age of 62, so if that happens I might not drop that amount in weight, but I definitely want to change shape and get rid of the rest of my spare tires around my middle. So are you thinking you should be a different shape and not quite so round? I know what you mean about the hourglass figure - I'm straight up and down when I don't have the spare tires.

When my husband was hugging me this morning he ran his hand down my back and sides and said he could no longer grab handfuls of fat, and how nice it was I was slim. He never used to complain about me being fat, but I don't think he liked it any more than I did. I'm not overly worried as I think my body shape will sort itself out if I focus on the health thing. I'm eager to continue to make improvements. I want my brain and thinking ability back too.

Sleep is difficult when its too hot. It was air con weather yesterday - 39 C and muggy (over 100F). High humidity is awful in the heat. I don't like sleeping with air con so I turn it off as soon as I can, but I don't sleep as well in the heat.

I thought I had weathered the air trip very well but realised yesterday that I didn't have the usual energy levels in my muscles so I took myself off to the beach. Unfortunately the sea was too rough to swim in safely and it was fully of lots of seaweed so I was restricted to walking along the beach occasionally becoming splashed as the waves came in higher than I was expecting. The water was silky smooth against my legs - something I hadn't noticed before.

### caroline — 2013-02-22T09:40:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-77008>

AN - I am straight up and down too - so when I gain weight it all goes to my middle and I hate it and feel so uncomfortable. I don't know what to think .... before I went down two sizes but my weight didn't change - now it seems to be opposite. My bum is rounder and my skinny legs are rounder - not bad things - just curious. I was feeling quite lean before and now don't. oh well - feeling great otherwise!

Hope you get a good nite's sleep tonite.

Are you having a warmer summer than normal? It is cool here and rainy.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-07T14:57:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-from-frustration-to-success.5481/post-79461>

Sometimes we allow what we hate, to accomplish what we love........

# Thread: The 9th most important thing... What you don't know won't hurt you
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 57
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Barry — 2013-01-16T10:12:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/post-71025>

The more we learn, the less we know. I know Jack will challenge this, but this is how I see it. And like Iâ€™ve said many times before, I am expressing my thoughts and opinions here based on my context. And everyoneâ€™s context is different. So with that in mind and with the launch of Jacks first blog post of his new seriesâ€¦ hereâ€™s whatâ€™s come to my attention:

The knowledge, information and depth of intellectual property here and on many other sites as well as some books, articles etc is mind blowing to say the least. Personally, I love every nugget of new information and theory I learn about nutrition, health, performance and how the human body works. From ketotic pathways to CT to Iodine to Epigenetics to SCN to Schumann Frequencies to SIRT-1 to Cyctochromes to Redox Signalling and Transcription factors. From VitD3 to Inflammation to Cell Signalling to Movnat to Dopamine to Zeitegars to CRP. From mTOR to GH to AMPK to NF-kB to TLRs. From MHS to Fermented Veg to Offal to Raw Milk to Real Food to Real Life to the Real World to Happiness to me simply being a person.

I hope I havenâ€™t lost you. There is a point to this. The point I am trying to make is that there are a lot of things we can do to improve our health, living and existence on this earth. And really, Iâ€™m probably only scratching the surface with what I have just listed, Jack Iâ€™m sure has a longer list. Yet Iâ€™m not all that sure that anyone in this world is really capable of putting everything possible into practice. We know that cold and dark are good for you, great. Try getting your partner or other family members to lie in a cold room at nightime with the lights off. We know what Epi-Paleo foods are good for us, great; try eating that way when you are travelling with work, at a friendâ€™s for dinner and not eating from home. You know that Movnat and sunlight and oceans are good for you, try doing that if you live in the middle of a big land locked city. You know liver is good for you, brazil nuts are good for you, oysters are good for you, grass fed pasture raised beef is good for you, coconut oil is good for you, green tea is good for you, sea veg are good for you and drinking Malbec from Argentina can be good for you too.

But we live in the real world. The real world isnâ€™t always cold and dark. The real world sometimes presents you with chicken instead of oysters, grain fed instead of grass feed beef, broccoli instead of sea veg, olive oil instead of coconut oil and Shiraz instead of Malbec. The real world might have the TV on late in the evening with your partner, a light in the kitchen that you are sharing with your housemate and an EMF device that you have to use for your job. The more you learn, the more you know, the more things you notice about your daily life that are not â€œoptimalâ€￾.

But hereâ€™s the thing. There are millions of people on this earth that live long healthy happy lives without doing CT and eating MHS. There are in fact millions of people that eat grains, donâ€™t eat fish, donâ€™t CT or Movnat and yet are still healthy and happy. When you look at tribal communities with the longest life expectancies, like the Andorrans, they know nothing about Na/K pumps, CPT-1 and Leptin Receptors yet these are the humans on this earth that live longer than those supposedly living â€œoptimalâ€￾.

So what I am really getting at here. Iâ€™ll make one thing very clear. Science and learning is an absolute must. Of relevance, what Jack is doing here in terms of connecting the dots is very innovative and almost revolutionary. Having said that, I donâ€™t think Jack will ever find all the answers. I think if we are truly honest with ourselves and truly scientific, we have to accept that the science is infinite. The learning will therefore and should never cease. We are the most complex dynamic adaptable and ever evolving machines on this planet. In addition, we are not the same, we are individuals, N=1 life long RCTâ€™s. Thus, the findings should never come to a halt. Hence, we should never come to an end point; we should never find perfection or achieve â€œoptimalâ€￾. Unless you accept that, you are at risk of creating the biggest inhibitor of health and well being that exists â€“ STRESS !

Iâ€™ll put all this into real laymanâ€™s terms. At the end of the day, itâ€™s about being real. I donâ€™t have all the answers, Iâ€™ll be the first to admit that and hold up my hand. Iâ€™m not even claiming that what I have written here is right, itâ€™s just my observation. What Iâ€™m saying in practical terms is this: do what you can and what feels right for you, put what you think is the best framework in place and find contentment. Embrace the imperfection, there is no generic perfection, we are all individual, so it is YOU who defines your own perfection, not Jack or any other guru out there.

The right framework does seem to be at least something which is becoming apparent to most. One of the things that is an absolute common functionality amongst all those that live long and healthily is reduced STRESS. Every time you read about centenarians or cultures that live disease free, it is this one common trait that they all have. Another is the outdoors and movement/exercise. Another is they eat from the earth. There are no nutritional commonalities other than this. Some eat a lot of plants, some eat meats, some eat dairy and some eat from the sea.

So the framework consists of low stress, outdoors, exercise and movement, and eating natural foods. Honestly, you can put whatever else you want around the frames to try and make your life better but I think they are trivialities in comparison. To use an analogy, if I was building a house, Iâ€™d get a solid foundation laid and strong walls and frames put in place. After that, you can have any other piece of material you want, any design, any shape, any colours, any furniture, any electrics, any sound system, any kitchen appliances or any security system you want. The build quality of the house is not determined by these things though. Just remember that and donâ€™t get too caught up in them. If a strong storm hits, it will be the walls and foundation of the house that will prevail the most, not the surround sound system or the fancy furniture. Cover the main bases, chill out, get some air, move around and then relax. Oh, and eat something natural. Sometimes, what we donâ€™t know wonâ€™t hurt usâ€¦.

1 more and I'm out... I promise { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

â€œBeyond the walls of intelligence, life is definedâ€￾

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-16T10:37:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/post-71027>

Beautifully written Barry.

you can change the world if you are willing to change yourself. knowledge is power, and pursuit of knowledge is an excellent endeavor. but you are right. we can get lost in the forest b/c we're too focused on the trees - and not experience the wonder that the forest is. so many people go through the motions of life and miss out on what is the magic of life. we are designed to live and suck the marrow from life. this is a great reminder to not just strive for optimal, but to live life in your pursuit....

### freesia — 2013-01-16T15:17:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/post-71067>

Love your perspective Barry, and hope you will stick around well after #10!

Just paying close attention to some foundational things -- which in essence, for me, adds up to a good night's sleep, and the ability to and wake refreshed, has given me a much wider balance beam. Stress levels way down for starters right there!

You mentioned the Tuscany Valley in another thread. That's got to be good!

Anne.

### KiwiLauren — 2013-01-16T15:30:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/post-71072>

"The most beautiful thing we can experience is the mysterious. It is the source of all true art and all science. He to whom this emotion is a stranger, who can no longer pause to wonder and stand rapt in awe, is as good as dead: his eyes are closed." (Einstein)

Thanks, Barry!

Barry in EMF 2 you get the special call out by name. I think you will rather enjoy it.

### freesia — 2013-01-16T16:32:53+0200

"A reader of my previous Antarctic posts once told me that this trip wasnâ€™t an adventure â€“ it was an undertaking only to be regarded in the most serious scientific manner. How uninspiring. ### caroline — 2013-01-16T16:40:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/post-71096>

I love how you write and your perspective and how you put things in context .. but ...you forgot one very important thing ......Love ....... being loved and loving is everything all rolled up into one.

### Inger — 2013-01-17T03:11:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/post-71157>

I am thinking totally opposite then you, or what you write in the post above Barry.

I am just honest here. I do apologise if I got you all wrong Barry.. because that might be possible for sure with this not-yet-healed-brain..lol

How I think.. to me it is an amazing puzzle.. I intuitively have sensed all the stuff Jack shines his flashlight on.. and when he explains it scientifically I just feel like WOW!!!!!! It makes me happy, it makes me almost blow up from pure joy because then I know WHY! And I have it so much easier to help people in my life, as I can explain it WHY.
I can only say what I see.. and I see so many suffering human around me. All the time. They all wonder how do I have this amazing energy.. and I then tell WHY. They see me eat my raw elk meat at work.. drink my sewaweed water.. they even want to taste it! They tasted my raw reindeer too..lol and said it was not bad! People suffer. They want a better life, more energy, more power.. but they do not know what to do and when they know they have such a hard time to change because they are so lonely as our modern world is full of stuff that destroys. The need someone to show, to live as an example! They might one day take the step.

I do not accept TV in my home. The one that sleeps in my bed will have to have it as cold and as dark as I have. I do not compromise in my home at all (I do respect others choises that differs from mine fully, for sure, but in my home are my rules and only me decides what to put in my mouth). I am rock hard. Because I love me. And weirdly enough.. not one has complained yet! They actually start to like how it feels.. because sure to sleep in cold and dark makes you feel great! To eat well makes you feel great too!
To me, my lifes gets stripped off more and more.. and it makes me want to fly.. To travel is no issue at all to me foodwise. I take my food with me. Or I am always able to find something healthy. If I know I am going some place where I will not, I take something with me. This has never been a issue. When I visit, I take my own food too. Or my friend or family knows me and offers something they know I'll eat. I am not concentrating on the food at all, the community and to be together is important to me.
I do not get it into my head why you see it as a struggle Barry, I just do not get it. I am honest. I am feeling the total opposite... so weird!

And if there are times where I have to work a bit more because of all I know, the benefits overgoes all the struggle. There is a deep confidence.. a peace.. that I do not want to miss out.

You always have to think how can I do it better where I live. It makes zero sense to me to compare about the Andorran or other countries people that live long and happy lives. It is so far taken (or it might be they do just the right stuff! Walk barefoot outside, use little clothes.. eat seasonally and local foods.. have much less electronic equipments, ipads etc.). What I see around me here is something totally different. And how I felt before... was not good at all.

I more and more realize, how amazingly many choises we have. We just do not see them!!!! It is totally MAGIC. There are solutions to every issue, I am 100% confident. I have seen enough amazing things last years to know that. Let us work in getting our eyes open istead! Look, it feels just amazing to see all these doors open up.

I feel kind of sad Barry.. because I feel you make beautiful things turn into struggles... you might not do a favor to some...
There is two ways to look at a half full waterglass we use to say here. Someone sees it half-full and someone half-empty. It is all about from what angle you see it.

If you have a thirst of learning and development, you will not feel like you write above, but instead like a child hungry for knowledge...
I wonder if that is ment about the thing said in the bible, if we do not become like children we will never reach heaven?

### caroline — 2013-01-17T04:33:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/post-71165>

My Dear Inger ..you always and completely blow me away with your insights .... I fee like a child learning at your knee...you are a fountain of wisdom for some one so young...

### Barry — 2013-01-17T05:47:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/post-71177>

"There is a pleasure in the pathless woods,
There is a rapture on the lonely shore,
There is society, where none intrudes,
By the deep sea, and music in its roar:
I love not man the less, but Nature more"

### Charlotte — 2013-01-17T07:15:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/post-71185>

I can see no contradiction in both views, they both hold value. Isn't it all a question of where we are at at any given moment, where we come from and where we are going, where our focus is and how we feel in this moment? Mind can be a dangerous thing, when we use it to compare. Truth is beyond words and opinions and all thought is past projected into future, it is never NOW.

### Dali Dula — 2013-01-17T09:40:45+0200

Barry,Inger you guys are both comets blazing down the ancient pathway. Blaze on!

Stress is the final frontier.

It is so far taken (or it might be they do just the right stuff! Walk barefoot outside, use little clothes.. eat seasonally and local foods.. have much less electronic equipments, ipads etc.). There is two ways to look at a half full waterglass we use to say here. Someone sees it half-full and someone half-empty. It is all about from what angle you see it.
If you have a thirst of learning and development, you will not feel like you write above, but instead like a child hungry for knowledge...
I wonder if that is ment about the thing said in the bible, if we do not become like children we will never reach heaven?

Inger and Barry see the world differently yet they do not realize they are on the same path to the same destination. We perspectives in life is what causes this myopia. I know I used to suffer from it. But now I see things from the myopic point and from the worldly view because I forced my perspective to change because mind is always open to new ways to perceive a problem. Dali Dula hit it out of the park in my view. There are many roads in life and biology that filter one final common pathway. Barry attacks things from looking at the "problem"......hence why he sees the struggle and challenge in life. Inger see the "beauty" in the journey. Where Barry see challenge, Inger sees the silver lining. The difference between the two is how your realities are constructed from your experience up to now. Instead of focusing on the problem (Barry), Focus on the solutions to the problem (Inger). In reality, both ways will bring you to optimal. The challenge for me is to take this complexity of relativity and make you understand it easily.

People cannot do what they do not understand. When we are faced with tasks that are complex, overwhelming, and challlenging we must break it down to smaller pieces for others to digest and weave a web of understanding for them. Today is the time to take your me's to their we's. Make your perspective simple for others by making the package of knowledge smaller to digest. Don't waste time with the huge problem; spend it finding small solutions to the problem! When you break things down into smaller parts you are adding facets to their knowledge base to create wisdom. In life, diamonds are multi-faceted as well, and it shines all the more brightly because of that. When you tackle life this way, you too will shine more brightly.

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-17T22:31:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/post-71327>

Barry and Inger....I love what both of you write.

Barry...we ALL live in the real world...those in the high-rises and those in the wild. Everything is equally real. Stressing about not living in the Amazon serves no function, but learning to shape and change who we are is also waiting for anybody who choose that path. Comparisons can lead to resentment and stress...inspiration doesn't do that...I find inspiration in the knowledge I'm gaining here...

THIS...!

### Inger — 2013-01-18T15:11:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/post-71448>

I am not alone Anne. There is another magic.
My ex hubs wants to sleep by me often even if I would rather be alone. He sleeps close close to me in a freezing bedroom and I warm him. My skin is always so warm. Sometimes my feet are a bit cold and I stick them between his legs to warm them up.
Men I have met never complained about my habits ever. I am very open with them always.
I have no issues with socialisation. I prefer to talk with people instead of sitting in front of a TV. I wish you could see how I live because I think you misunderstand. I am far from perfect too. But I live. I enjoy life and I suck it in and I learn new things all the time that makes me so thankful. The things I learn I test and share with the people in my life. The benefit from it too. My mom and dad benefits. Others too.. there are magic things happening all the time Anne!

Oh.. I do not travel with high meat. I leave it home for a week (I am no longer time away anyways) I just take care of certain things so it will be ok.

So the further north the more you must live after the seasons... that is what I feel.

### diane — 2013-01-18T16:31:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/post-71465>

Barry, I find your posts thought provoking, but in large part agree with Inger. And this is just my experience - others I'm sure are different.

I think a lot of it comes down to what "optimal" means to you. To me, it doesn't mean the quest for perfection. But living the happiest life for me, where I can feel my best and avoid disease as much as possible.

Where I'm coming from (and I think many here are) is a place where it was so stressful living the way I was. The feeling that I can't think properly and perform at work the way I used to. The dread of getting out of bed (and mostly dragging myself out) because I knew it meant a day of no energy and wanting to crawl under my desk for a nap in the afternoon then barely be able to drive home from being so tired. The frustration from people telling me to "eat more whole grains and veggies and less fat" and "work out more" to lose weight. And trying one thing after another than failed and then feel bad about myself because I thought I failed.

When I look at what Jack has laid out here, much of it has been fairly easy for me to incorporate. Why? Because I've seen results. And when you see that something works, it's easy to turn the lights off at night, wear blue blockers. I love being outside so this isn't hard for me. I love seafood so this isn't hard for me. When I hear of people weighing themselves day after day and stressing because the scale doesn't move - that's the kind of stress I would like to avoid.

Funny thing is that once you start feeling better, you want to feel even better. So I try new things to see what works. I watch and listen to other people and pick up some of these things to try myself. Other things I don't try. The one message that I have picked up here is that I have to find out what works best for me, and that's not going to be the same as for anyone else. I thirst to learn, and this is the first time I've really been able to start putting science together. But the more I learn, the more I want to learn.

And Inger, although I so respect and admire her, I don't think she's perfect (even though she's amazing!), but I also don't feel bad that I don't live like Inger. The message that I've gotten from here is to aspire to find Inger's intuition within myself - not that I have to copy her. Does she have interesting ideas? Absolutely! Does she help many people here? You bet. But I am not Inger so I can't live the way she does. But I do hope that I am working to develop her intuition and her way of looking at the world, because it would really help balance me out.

This attitude alone is a huge step forward for me. I spent my life measuring myself against everyone else and then being jealous because I didn't feel that I measured up. That caused me a lot of stress!

To understand and accept that I am different, and I am doing the best I can every day has been so freeing to me. To understand that I have to find what works for me and not everyone else has been empowering. I think that people who come looking for the "answer" will find it stressful trying to follow everything and live up to what they define as perfection, but I do hope that many are seeing the underlying message here as well.

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-18T16:39:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/post-71466>

Go Diane!! excellent post. ....it is the underlying message that is the point, not the surface copying or comparisons.

### caroline — 2013-01-18T17:21:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/post-71473>

Excellent Diane - I suspect that you have got Dr. K.'s message in spades. I find too that the more I learn here - I can start with small things and it starts to snowball. I think when we start this journey we don't know where we will end up. 1 year ago - I didn't know that I would prefer raw meat and fish - but I do. I use to watch TV off and on all day and into the early morning now - I am almost completely moving away from it - this past week I haven't probably even watched 30 min all week and the Australian Open is on and I love to see Federer and Nadal etc play. I just can't/don't want to sit in front of the tv anymore. I use to go to bed at 2 or 3 in the morning - last nite I was in bed at 8:30. I didn't plan on any of these changes exactly - it is all evolving and I am feeling calmer and certainly have more joy and more patience and more caring. I have always loved being at the ocean and - guess what- I find out that it is a great place to be for earthing etc. and for my soul .. what a win win for me. I feel like things are falling into place - like how they are meant to be - for me. I think that is Dr. K's brilliance and his intention - please correct me if I am wrong ..... that is the path he is showing us and we can go or not - our choice

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-18T17:26:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/post-71474>

Thanks Diane, spot on.

Honestly I don't think I've seen anybody here who swallows everything whole without even asking or wondering why. Everyone I've seen is doing the best they can with what they've got.

All I see is Jack laying out a roadmap with explanations as to where he's going and why. I figure it's up to me to test each step of the path and see if its working or workable for me. And I have had to find workarounds for things that didn't (all that protein for breakfast, for example. I just cannot do it although I am now thinking perhaps I should switch to FISH because I'm pretty sure I could meet that protein requirement if I do that instead of bacon and beef).

It is as with all things in life ... your mileage may vary. Everyone is different, everyone has different issues and different needs. And NO ONE else can fix you. But it's nice to have someone pointing out roadblocks, traffic jams, and wide open freeways.

> This attitude alone is a huge step forward for me. I spent my life measuring myself against everyone else and then being jealous because I didn't feel that I measured up. That caused me a lot of stress!
>
> To understand and accept that I am different, and I am doing the best I can every day has been so freeing to me. To understand that I have to find what works for me and not everyone else has been empowering. I think that people who come looking for the "answer" will find it stressful trying to follow everything and live up to what they define as perfection, but I do hope that many are seeing the underlying message here as well.

BINGO many here need to read this post.....excellent.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-20T12:28:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/post-71835>

Welcome Barry, did you read any of it though?
His story is as unusual as his disclaimer. And what he said, blew my mind, literally. It ended a lot of things. And if I hadn't met him, I would not have believed it.

In the light of what Dr. Kruse has told us recently about mitochondria, electrons etc. something started to make sense that I could not get a grip on before. Why did this guy actually give off light? Now I tend to think that he produced so many electrons that they were visible and why did his thymus gland reach its original size at the age of 49? And why could he see the light enter his body through the third eye even when his eyes were completely closed?

I am neither religious, nor philosophical nor any follower of any group or guru. But I KNOW that what he is saying is true. Not because my mind wants to believe it but because I have experienced it. Only glimses, but this man was in his natural state (a natural man as he described himself.)

What happened? I do not know, I can only guess, maybe a complete rewiring of the brain, all glands and every cell of his body? He did not know either nor did he care to know, I guess, but he described his state and one thing is sure, he used his brain only, when needed for a specific task or when asked a question. Other than that there was no thought, no division between ME and YOU, not even an US. There was no identification with anything, no conditioning. And he said it is the only peaceful state there is and I believe that because without identification there is no conflict, no right, no wrong, no stress. Duality with all its frictions comes to an end.

I wonder now, if through the methods, Dr. Kruse is giving us here, we come a little closer to this state which is what I had aspired for years to reach and finally gave up on as there just was no roadmap to follow. (but hope dies last, even though that also has to die) So I continued to live my life and still do, but one thing I have understood and that is that my body is my vehicle with which I get around and I want this vehicle to be optimal, so it serves me well. How optimal one wants his vehicle to be is everybodies own desicion, I agree with you there completely as it is ourselves that have to bear or enjoy the consequences or maybe we have good genes and get away with a lot more than others do. And in the end we are all unique in the way life expresses itself through us. However, I do follow Dr. Kruses advice in many things, in some not, as I acknowledge that he knows a lot more about epigenetics, diet, CT as I do and I do not feel inclined to start studying medicine or nutrition. So I give it a shot. But with my own common sense and understanding included.

That is our freedom and neither you (as I understood your post) nor I have that taken away from us.

### Inger — 2013-01-21T02:48:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/post-71970>

Barry..
it is just that "What you don't know won't hurt you" is a lie.

Tell this to Lioness7 on our forum, her baby got leukemia and it was the only baby sleeping with a cordless "phone" on its side...
She didn't know.

Tell it to me, that ate a horrible diet in my childhood.. and suffered so many allergies and other issues... and I had no clue it was a bad diet I had but gladly feasted on pastries and stuff..! All the issues disappeared when I changed my WOE.
I didn't know.

Tell it to all the people that suffer big time, eating crap, living crappy.. but have no clue it is wrong what they do and so they feel confused and depressed because they see no way out!
They do not know.

Tell it to all the people dumping toxic wastes in our oceans.. destroying our earth..
Many do not know either.

I always tell my mom (that is pretty ignorant at times and thinks it has no consequenses..), mom we need to be more aware. Because all our actions carries consequenses. Every single of them. It is up to us, what the consequenses are going to be.
But we have this lazyness in us that "do not care".. until we got to suffer big time... it might already be too late.. and then we wish.. if we only knew!!!!

Our family friend died of borreliose 2 years ago, he was only 40 yo with big family and many kids.. a very lovely family. WHY? HE DID NOT KNOW. Hubbys brother dies of Melanoma recently... he was a top athlete in his youth and always very healthy and sporty.. but he did not know.. and when he got to know it was too late. He suffered from bitterness in the end it was terrible.
Barry!!!!! My heart cries when I read your stuff because I have seen all this.. all the pain that comes from not knowing.. and how if you know, can save and what a blessing it can be.

Barry... I think you are not aware what you are talking about here. I think you are not aware what powers you are feeding because if you knew, you would not write what you do.

The path to freedom and happiness is not a broad and easy one. It is stony.. and narrow.. but so cosy and exiting and so totally worth it! With its pain and all.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-21T09:14:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/post-72003>

Inger, you said: " But we have this lazyness in us that "do not care".. until we got to suffer big timeâ€¦ it might already be too late.. and then we wish.. if we only knew!!!!

That seems to be a contradiction. My experience is that many actually do not care and they believe what they WANT to believe. People are lazy, I agree totally and especially lazy to think and find out for themselves. And then later to say... if only we knew!!! seems to be a big lie also. I am sure you have stood below a high-voltage transmission line and heard the humming. It sounds terrible and it made me think and read about electricity and stay away from it as best as I can. We also have intuition, especially you, and you do a lot of things right without "knowing". And sometimes who knows better does not necessarily do better. Thats true for people who have taken on responsibility for themselves and others like the people you find on this forum. And not wanting to know what is uncomfortable is unfortunately one of the most-spread traits people have.

I have an electrician here at work whose wife has cancer, since over 3 years. She had surgery, numerous repeated cycles of chemotherapy and radiation treatment. I have spoken to both of them, bought them books, told them to cut carbs, bought them even supplements and offered them the help of my doctor at my expense. Thanks to colostrum she had an easy time with chemotherapy and radiation treatment, no more metastases but now she suffers from other diseases. No, they choose to stay with the doctor who treats her to death I am sure and now they will do surgery on her ovaries which I do not think is necessary. (She does not even have cancer in her ovaries, just a swelling) If I was a doctor, maybe I had a better standing, but as it is!!!

So my question is: does it really matter what I or anyone else is saying? Everyone believes what they want to believe and what suits their lazyness and protects their interest and feeds their self-deception.

We are all learning here and we all have our concepts and do not necessarily agree with all Dr. Kruse is saying. Maybe out of lack of knowing better, not believing it all, not wanting to believe or even believing but still not listening because it does not suit our own purposes. Whatever. And all this is ok, because we are not sheep, well maybe some of us, (ok Barry?) who jump on and off any bandwagon that comes along) but question Dr. Kruse also. This is a process called active learning and quite necessary.

The way I understood Barry is that he is following the protocol as good as he sees fit, but that he is not going to stress himself over it, as stress is also causing disease. And he prefers to compromise sometimes so he can still do and enjoy the things he likes. To me, that is his freedom.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-22T14:58:34+0200

Haha and Anna K about the three magnesiums (lol) I decided that there is no two ways about it, magnesium is a GOOD idea for me. But here's how I decided what will work for budget, sanity etc. (lol).

I like magnesium oil I have epsom salts. Out of the three magnesiums I think malate is the best choice since it's the one before bed time and to promote better sleep and skeletal muscle relaxation. I bet this is what most people on here need most anyway. That's what I'm doing for me anyhow.

I need my Vitex (for now at least) and vit D3 is a good idea. Co q 10 I am just going to rely on beef hearts (they taste so damn good anyway). The d-ribose thing I will just have to take people's word for it on it's energy boosting...but it's not in my budget right now.

Mg, D, and Vitex for me....also can't wait to add back in coffee....been 3 weeks w/o it. I only gave it up so that I can replete my mag faster. I realistically should not start drinking it until ~ June. I will probably cheat and at least treat myself like 1x a week. Gotta have some luxaries sometimes lol.

Also I test with sleep. If something makes your sleep suck, can it. If your sleep is fine even if it's something Jack would not approve....keep it. That's the story and I'm sticking with it. lol

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2013-01-23T11:57:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/post-72513>

Some of the responses to Barry are simply reactionary/bandwagony. It's hard to see much actual discourse on here with this.

1\. Barry has not disagreed with any of the significant concepts of Jack's quilt, yet tone on the anti-barry bunch is that HE'S WRONG, he's saying the same things Jack is!

2\. What I see is extremely simple, Barry is just added a practical layer of the concept of overall health being a function of many factors - one being how any individual handles all the information.

Clear this up, how is incorporating all this information in one's life - and evaluating/judging the reaction you and your body have to it a negative thing. How is that something you CAN disagree with. You simply must put it in context for the individual right? Don't we hear the CONTEX, CONTEX drum all the time? Just because Inger isn't overloaded and unstressed doesn't mean someone else won't be. CONTEXT. No two hormone panels are the same, so why on earth would everyone, at any point in time, be in the same place?

3\. Barry used a turn of phrase to make a point "what you don't know won't hurt you". How ironic that so many that are fond of platitudes and generalities when it supposedly supports what they are doing, goes detail oriented at that moment some phrase is contrary to your thought framework. Pointing out that there are exceptions to that is obtuse and intellectually lame. Do you think that Barry thought there was nothing out there that would harm you if you didn't know about it? The flip flop on logic here again, takes away from actual discourse and learning on the topic at hand.

The topic again seems to come to overall health and wellbeing. Do you deny that some people are unable, and or do not benefit from the same steps/methods you do? So what if it is a factor of faulty neural pathways and broken thought - it CAN have a negative health impact when you weigh the net benefit any given behavior or action have.

It's not discourse in this thread, it's hubris and shows a glaring lack in how what is being said is giving no actual thought or consideration for others when said in a way not to share helpful things, but to make yourself feel better about the framework you've built. Here is a platitude for yalll. . . "... doth protest too much, me thinks". and my favorite "The Truth is Self Evident".

What do those phrases mean? It does not matter because they are simply a turn of phrase or platitude and has no bearing on actual exchange of ideas. Just as popping into a discussion and announcing that if you know better you do better has any meaning or bearing on the learning/conversation at hand.

Along the "doth protest too much" pathway I'm lighting up with a flashlight, Being myopic and unable to conceptualize alternate realities does not serve everyone the same. It's a protective thought process that allows you to keep going along and feeling secure in your behaviors. I see nothing wrong with it, and in fact see the benefits to it all over the place. Being able to generate your own framework is part and parcel of humanity. It's why we have religion.

If you are not secure enough in your ideas here to accept that others have their versions and it's not the same as yours, perhaps you have found a new religion instead of learning to do better.

### Barry — 2013-01-23T16:14:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/post-72556>

Let me just make a few things clear here,

For those of you who think there is some sort of "throllish" behaviour going on here, think again. I not only stand by every word I have written but I have shared a huge amount of intellectual property, of my own free will. Just check out FST post.

Secondly, I'm not looking for back up here. This is my court so I'll play me own game. I don't "thank" anyone who agrees with me for that reason. I will say Anna K and Cavemam are bang on the money. Still, I'll happily fight my own battle.

The truth is self evident, collective fear stimulates herd instinct, and tends to produce ferocity towards those who are not regarded as part of the herd.

To be continued...

### endless — 2013-01-24T02:19:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-9th-most-important-thing-what-you-dont-know-wont-hurt-you.5484/post-72629>

I don't think anyone is painting you as a troll, Barry, I may not agree with everything you say but I don't feel the need to attack it. You've given me a lot to think about. I think that should be encouraged, don't you? Perhaps draw out the conversation instead of trying to shut it down?

I love hearing all of these ideas, and the passion behind them....but arrogance is such a turn off for me.

This should be interesting. I had some coffee after a few weeks (ya I can't even go a month without it lol) after trying to test if I need to back off of it due to some troubles with magnesium levels in my cells. Anyway, I had some this morning. I felt alright but later on I started to feel a little bit dull in the head and tired in the eyes. In the afternoon I went outside in barefeet and no coat and did a few pushups in the snow---my dull head and tired eyes went away instantly. It is 6:15pm and several hours later and I still feel normal. We shall see how I sleep tonight. (the big test). If this is a way I can still enjoy my beautiful single origin coffees then I will gladly do this. (As I maintain my magnesium supplementation).

After finishing this comment I do realize it's not exactly on topic, if only for the relation to any 'outdoor activity' that you do but in much much more rigorous/complex way. hehe

With all this New Year's energy floating around, we want to support you in mobilizing for an Optimal 2013! Please plan to join us tonight for a free Q&A with Dr. Jack Kruse. No questions are off limits ... ask about the recent Women's Fat Loss webinar, the Mitochondrial Prescription, CT, any of the protocols, or any of your own burning questions about disease or optimizing your health.

When: TONIGHT, Wednesday, January 16, 2013
Time: 7 pm CT
Where: http://www.spreecast.com/events/free-community-qa

To join us for this tonight's call:
Go to Spreecast.
Create an account via Facebook, Twitter, or your Email
Login to FREE Community Q&A: http://www.spreecast.com/events/free-community-qa

\*\*Special Announcement\*\* - We will be sharing the details on the 90-day Optimal Reset that begins with the JackKruse.com community on Monday, January 21, 2013. All members may participate--participation is FREE! The registration link will be given out during the live Q&A on Wednesday.

This call will not be recorded, so plan to join us LIVE!

See you tonight,
Misty & Dr. Kruse

### Rick — 2013-01-17T11:38:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-are-invited-free-new-years-qa-with-dr-jack-kruse-tonight.5485/post-71220>

I concur, thank you Jack and Misty! Logged on at 10 min to kickoff but then had connectivity problems and missed the first half-hour, so I wanted to ask a couple questions of the attendees:

1\. I totally get the concept that athletes should be burning fat for fuel primarily rather than re-feeding/fueling on fruit and sweet potatoes (ala Robb W. and crew), but Jack answered this question and I totally missed what he was saying, something about getting the mitochondria to burn lipid fuels more efficiently with D-ribose? Can anyone elaborate please on Jack's answer? I do understand that lipolysis generates far more ATP than gylcolysis; I was most interested in what Jack said about how long this takes and why many athletes fail on ketogenic Paleo templates (aside from not doing epiPaleo) and how D-ribose improves mitochondrial fat burning to let athletes become the most efficient fat burners?

2\. The second reply to my question on chromium picolinate I did hear, but just wanted others' opinions on Jack's answer: so he said that Cr3 picolinate can release -OH ions which in the literature has been shown to damage DNA (while other literature, RA Anderson was one researcher I believe, says Cr3(pic) is perfectly safe even in mod to high doses so Jack seemed to be saying the conflicting results have more to do with the test subjects' context. High level athletes that have great serum Zeta potentials and excellent mitochondria will be able to clear the hydroxyl ions and avoid oxidative damage. But, since Cr3(pic) is helpful in activating the low molecular weight chromium binding molecule (LMWCr) which amplifies peripheral tissue insulin sensitivity, if you use the picolinate to help diabetics control serum,glucose, they could be at risk for DNA damage at mod to high doses because they already have poor mitochondrial function and Zeta potentials?

So I'm wondering if using Cr3(pic) for diabetics should keep dosing to <= 200 mcg/day and also would it be wise to add L-arabinose with the chromium for its synergistic effect on carb metabolism?

Anyone have any thoughts on either of these points please chime in and I thank you in advance for your input!

Rick

# Thread: Progesterone help
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progesterone-help.5486/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Eve — 2013-01-16T12:12:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progesterone-help.5486/post-71038>

Around July, I had my hormones tests (blood serum). Progesterone was bottom of range and Estradiol was low-normal. T and DHEA were low-normal as well. I started for a couple of months with 10mg transdermal P, days 14-28. Then I think for a month I did 20mg P, days 14-28. I thought maybe I wasn't on enough, so I bumped it up myself to 40mg. After a few days of doing this, it started my period (I don't have my agenda with me, but I think it was about 10-14 days early). I think the same thing happened the next month. So I did without the P (or only used it a few days) for a couple months as my periods kept coming early. Now I am back to 20mg days 14-28 (just started last night - day 15 (forgot to apply it on the 14th)).

Is this to be expected when trying to increase P? I had my hormones retested around October and my P only went up a tiny bit (a couple points) and was still in the bottom quartile. I was thinking of asking my Doc to switch me to Prometrium and the lowest dose available is 100mg.....Any thoughts?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-16T15:57:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progesterone-help.5486/post-71084>

100 mg oral or vaginally?

### Eve — 2013-01-16T20:14:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progesterone-help.5486/post-71140>

If I get Prometrium, I was going to apply it which ever way is best...Any recommendations? Thank you

### Claudia — 2013-01-16T21:09:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progesterone-help.5486/post-71143>

I felt nothing with 100mg oral prometrium. I didn't know it was available vaginally. Wouldn't that be preferable?

I had a compounded Prog. cream, and my doc gave me 200mg. My prog. is just on the low side. I had horrible side effects from this 200mg cream, brain fog, depression, unable to function, and 5 lb gained in a few days (that I'm still trying to lose, it doesn't budge), wanted to crawl into a hole, felt so sick, just HORRIBLE.

I'm not sure what to do now. I still have the 100mg oral Rx. I will not go near that cream. I was thinking about another cream, 20mg dose and maybe vaginally.

# Thread: Multiple sclerosis and vitamin D
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/multiple-sclerosis-and-vitamin-d.5488/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Optimalbound — 2013-01-16T14:22:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/multiple-sclerosis-and-vitamin-d.5488/post-71055>

A study on multiple sclerosis was recently published in the Multiple Sclerosis Journal. The study looked at 6649 MS patients in Norway, born between 1930-1979, as well as their mothers and siblings. Looks like an observational study to me.

Some findings:
\- Children born in April had a 11% elevated risk of getting MS.
\- Children born in November had a 5% lower risk of getting MS.
\- Children born in February had a 12% lower risk of getting MS.
\- The risk of MS was higher the further north one was born.
\- The negative effect of being born during spring was higher for those born furthest north,
with children born during April og May in Northern Norway having a 28% elevated risk.

Comments from the scientists performing the study: "For those living in Northern Norway, Vitamin-D synthesis may be absent for up to 5 months during winter. The results from this study strenghtens the hypothesis that lack of sunlight and Vitamin-D during the pregnancy or early in life, is an important factor in developing MS. There may also be other factors, such as infections during pregnancy influencing the development of the nervous system and the immune system, and thus having an effect on the risk of developing MS later in life".

(http://msj.sagepub.com/content/early/2012/12/20/1352458512471094.full)

# Thread: Nutrition for Myelin in Infants  Adults
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutrition-for-myelin-in-infants-adults.5492/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet

### byboozian@gmail.com — 2013-01-16T16:27:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutrition-for-myelin-in-infants-adults.5492/post-71094>

The past few weeks I have been turned on to the significance of myelin to brain function and development. I have a 4 month old baby girl, so on top of trying to get to optimal myself, I have been trying to learn as much as possible about the development of babies and especially how their brain develops as they grow older. While my current understanding of all of the topics involved with these areas is limited, I feel I have a decent start thanks in large part to the articles on this site and books like "The Other Brain" which I have been devouring.

While there is a lot of information saying that the brain stops myelinating around 20 years old, the evidence provided by Dr. Wahls and others that have reversed MS and other brain degenerating disease suggests that this isn't exactly correct. I was wondering what people here think about this subject and if anyone can point me to any information they found valuable in learning about brain development in children as well as repair / maintenance in adults? What specific properties of food aid in this process? What types of exercise and learning methods work the best for children in regards to this? I know that brain development is a very broad and intense subject, so any other topics that you think I might find interesting are welcome also.

While my current understanding of all of the topics involved with these areas is limited, I feel I have a decent start thanks in large part to the articles on this site and books like "The Other Brain" which I have been devouring.
> While there is a lot of information saying that the brain stops myelinating around 20 years old, the evidence provided by Dr. Wahls and others that have reversed MS and other brain degenerating disease suggests that this isn't exactly correct. I was wondering what people here think about this subject and if anyone can point me to any information they found valuable in learning about brain development in children as well as repair / maintenance in adults? What specific properties of food aid in this process? What types of exercise and learning methods work the best for children in regards to this? I know that brain development is a very broad and intense subject, so any other topics that you think I might find interesting are welcome also.

For social-emotional development as it relates to mylenation, HPA axis and optimal brain development, my fave is Dr Allan Schore.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-18T08:22:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutrition-for-myelin-in-infants-adults.5492/post-71369>

hi byboozian, for now I think that your milk is the best. When I imagine paleo-people, I am sure that after stopping to nurse, they have started to chew real food for their babies (probably even while nursing to adapt them to real food).
And then the children ate the same as their parents, so if you show the good example and you keep the baby away from all that neolitic food, its brain will develop best. I think that vaccination might pose a problem too, so avoid it if you feel confident enough. I have heard terrible stories here about children who developed severe problems after vaccination.
You might want to look into Montessori Kindergarten and School. I prefer this to the Steiner Schools, as children can decide themselves what they want to learn and at which pace and how deep they want to get into a subject. (my youngest son went there)
Then there is a book I read when my children were small which is from a couple in Russia who made an interesting experiment with their 7 children. It is called "the Nikitin children". They were both teachers, tried to have the government let them do an experiment with school children which they did not get permission for. From the first day of birth, they implemented what they considered best and one of the things they did, was keep the babies from day No. 1 in a very cold room, only with sparce clothing. The babies did not mind, but became very active kicking to keep warm (shows, how intelligent the body is). They were not rich, and one of the parents always stayed with the children as grandmothers, neighbours and others thought they were crazy and tried to sneak around to "save" the babies from their parents. I think they grew most of their food themselves (its been a long time I read it).
Okay, if you are interested, just get the book. I only give you the quintessence of it. These children were extremele sociable, very intelligent (the house looked like a school with chemistry-physics and other tools), very early independent. One son skipped several classes and went to university at 15 yrs. Two daughters were among the world class gymnasts, and so on. Depending on their particular innate capabilities, they developed extraordinary skills. Its a book worth reading and at the end, they give details about games they developed and played with the children to enhance their skills. Important to say was, that the kids were never forced to do anything, but for example when the dad saw, that one of the children showed interest, he immediately went and asked if he could participate.

# Thread: The Trivium Method of Education
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-trivium-method-of-education.5495/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### byboozian@gmail.com — 2013-01-16T17:14:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-trivium-method-of-education.5495/post-71110>

I haven't seen this topic mentioned in the forum, although judging from most of the people posting here I would guess that the majority of you already practice it whether you know it or not. In a nutshell, the Trivium is a method of learning that consists of grammar (who, what, when, where) logic (why) and rhetoric (how). The order of those is very important and when practiced it allows one to become auto didactic or, a self learner. It teaches people how to spot logical fallacies and dissect arguments systematically so you don't get caught up with emotional or unrelated subjects.

This method of teaching and education was used in America up until around 1890's and is a major reason why one teacher was able to teach 30 different students of all different ages while getting better results than the modern school system does. The teacher would show the student how to learn a subject, which allowed that student to translate the same skills of learning to any subject he or she choose in the future so they didn't require a teacher to learn a new skill or topic.

Putting the things I've learned while studying the trivium into practice is the main reason I have ended up on this site and it has helped me more than anything else to cut through the BS you find on the subject of nutrition. Hopefully some of the other people on here will find this a useful tool in the future. The website below is where first learned about it and although some of the interviews are a bit eccentric, I found it extremely useful.

http://www.triviumeducation.com/

# Thread: My labs-schnitzeljagd
