### AdrianaDr. Radler-Pohl — 2013-01-17T09:53:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-labs-schnitzeljagd.5504/post-71207>

Probably you guys here are wondering what the german term "schnitzeljagd" means. Well - as kids we had a fantastic game which erveryone had lots of fun. We would choose some who would be the "treasure" we would hunt and then they would go around leaving little traces everywhere eg. on the ground, or a piece of writing with a puzzel .... leading to the next trace .... and so on - of couse they did it without the others watching often the day before ..... then the hunt would start .... and the little pieces would eventually lead to the hidingplace. It was always a lot of fun and huray. ..... But this is the way I'm feeling on my way to optimal. Bits and pieces here and there .... a puzzle here and there leading to the ..... TADA!!! ... next puzzle. OK - done complaining ... was mad this morning missing the Q&A!

I got my recent labs and would appreciate some input ... THX in advance everybody ... 53 yr. old female, postmenopausal, breastcancer 6 years ago

ASI:
6am 8220pg/ml (1800-14500). ...Jack says to be in the top quartile ... def. not!
8am. 3166pg (1300-10300)
12am 2010pg (700-5700)
14pm 2296pg (600-4700)
6pm. 1356pg (300-3300)

Now the steroids (saliva) morning sample
DHEA 542pg/ml (90-460) ??? too much?
E2. 1,5pg (,2)
Estriol 5,0 pg (<21,0pg morning, 1:200) sucks! right?
Testosteron 35,6pg/ml

... its not that but ... exept the ratio ... Or is the cortisol too low?

Thyroid panel

TSH. 2,81mIU/L ( 0.27 - 4.2 mIU)
fT3. 4,4 pmol/L. (3.1 - 6.8)
fT4. 19.3 pmol/L (12 - 22)

Antibodys Thyreoperoxidase 10.6 kIU ( < 34 kIU) .... some say Hashi when you have some ... even when low ... hmm.

VitD 25 (OH) 268nmol/L ... (62,5 - 170) ... guess I over did it ... backing off right now
Vit 1,25 (OH) 102 pg/ml. (17-53)

Ferritin 307mcg/L (15 - 150) !!! WHTF???! ... if you excuse my German language ...
CRPs. 1,14. mg/L

In a later test The Doc here suggested to test Serotonin ....
Serotonine 81.82 mcg/g Kreatinine (148 -230) !!!! ... another WHTF ....

Well - that has been a lot of typing ... and Im really bad and slow ... so thanks for your input ... it is very much appreciated!!!

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### KiwiLauren — 2013-01-17T16:03:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-labs-schnitzeljagd.5504/post-71257>

I am pretty bad at labs, so take this with a grain of 'salz'....

You didn't appear to test Progesterone. I think you need to. You want your ratio with E2. And you want to know how you're doing with P, especially with a history of breast cancer. If you're not supplementing estrogen, I would imagine that estriol would be low postmenopausally, so I wouldn't worry too much.

Re: vitamin D, I'd try to convert those to the US units. When my Vitamin D tested 'high' and I converted it to US units (from European ones like you have) it turned out I was still below where Dr Kruse recommends! I think the reference ranges listed only reflect what that lab/country recommend.

You seem to have good FT4 but aren't converting it well into FT3. That could mean high reverse T3. Or something completely different - perhaps Patty has ideas here?

The good news with your ASI is that you've got the right curve/pattern occuring, which is super. I don't see melatonin, though. Did you test that? That would be helpful info.

I don't know about that high DHEA. Are you supplementing? If not, perhaps I'd be concerned... any PCOS symptoms?

And re: high iron... I think (if I've understood properly) that you could be one of those iron accumulators (he talks about this in one of the BG posts) and also that this can be a sign of poor blood viscosity... looks like the Mitochondrial Rx for you.

Viel Gluck!

### AdrianaDr. Radler-Pohl — 2013-01-17T17:00:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-labs-schnitzeljagd.5504/post-71265>

Progesteron 70pg/ml ... postmenopausal range 10-50pg/ml .... so my ratio is 0,021 and should be > 1/200! ... but in my Consult with Jack he was betting that it was higher than this .... he was right spot on ... sigh!

.... could someone give me a hint about in whitch blog of the BG series this ironcollector was mentioned .... Im only familiar with Estrogen collectors ... Im learning new things every day ...

and ..... my VitD 25(OH) is equivalent to 107 ng/ml for american standard ... divided by 2.5 is the conversion to ng ...

### MamaGrok — 2013-01-17T11:10:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/free-qa-summaries.5505/post-71218>

This forum needs a new post!

I took notes last night, and will share what I have, and hope others will add to it. I was grading Latin papers for half of the time, so certainly didn't get it all down.

I am truly amazed at Dr. K's command of so many seemingly disparate aspects of human health, and the wee tiny nitty-gritty details of them.
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
Floroquinolone antibiotics rob tissues of electrons. They block GABA receptors in the brain.
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
Lyme or similar illness - treat first with antibiotics to get rid of the bacteria. Then really focus on grounding in ocean if available and mitochondra. When losing electrons, you're trying to lose by CP system (non-oxygen-based, very fast, very inefficient at making ATP) or Kreb cycle...
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
Adult-onset shellfish allergy - ground in the ocean! Get a magnet 10x the strength of the earth's magnetic field and put it under your mattress.
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
The three Mg's of the mitochondrial Rx:
\*?am for Mg threonate (how much?)
\*Midday Mg Orotate improves mitochondrial function (heart is most stressed at this time)
\*Nighttime 800mg Mg malate for skeletal muscles

All orally. Can dissolve if desired. Use up whatever Mg you have first, unless it's Mg oxide.
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
High cortisol = Leptin Resistance + bad ligth at night.
High DHEA = high androgens & likely PCOS.
PCOS without diabetes means artificial light at night is #1 problem. EMFs may also be having a tangible effect. Too many carbs out of season, too much at night, too.
PCOS is the train stop before diabetes.
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
Pg:E2 ratio should be 250:1 to 500:1
(Mamagrok note: HOW CAN THIS BE? I used the calculator shijin gave at http://new.instacalc.com/421 and even putting in optimal numbers, I get no more than 91! Does anyone have anything remotely close to this? What are your raw numbers? Or did I get the ratio numbers wrong?)
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
Short of liver or kidney failure, any amount of protein (as long as it's high enough) is fine
Optimal BUN/Creatinine is 1:10 to 1:15.
Too low = dehydrated = mitochondrial inefficiency.
CO2 level T3 conversion
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
If ONLY HCG works for weight loss, that's the best sign that mitochondria are shot.
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
Autism means methylation problems in the brain. Defect is often in utero & post-natal, especially has effects for the first 6 years.
Find what the meth. defect is and treat with the right supps and electron-dense diet.
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
"If child is born to mother in her 40s (low progesterone egg), would maca help him?"
Yes, maca helps raise progesterone.
Maca is not great if you have thyroid issues. ( { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. For raw, try oysters, ceviche, tartare, sous vide, MHS. Make the rest cooked
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
Mitochondrial Rx supp's:
The three Mg's listed above
Acetyl l-carnitine 600mg 3x/day
R lipoic acid 200mg 2x/day
D-ribose 10gm/day (tend to put in my coffee)
CoQ10 ubiquinol 400-800gm/day
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
Good point about balancing supplements. (Very isolated nutrients can lead to imbalances that we don't even know are possible. Food is the best source of nutrients.) No one person has the same labs as anyone else. Youâ€™re hovering between 50-70%. When you have a stressor, it drops you below 50%. Sleep, energy, donâ€™t wanna be near hubby, all goes down.â€œ
(What was he referring to with the %'s?)
Use magnetic pad under bed, block EMF, food, ocean.
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
Trying to fix PgE2 ratio? Progesterone vaginally.
Not really a fan of transdermal bc of different pharmokinetics in different women. Ditto transdermal T for men.
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
On plane, take shoes off and put them on metal posts on the plane.
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
PQQ isn't for the mito Rx, but plays a role for older folks, perimenopausal women, and crossfitters waking up to the damage they've done.
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
Scoliosis can be reversed. Big time deficiency in electrons in musculo-skeletal system. Upcoming blog on it!

Osteoporosis by def'n = severe LR
"Can you have good body comp and still have LR?"
Russian cosmonauts had severe bone loss after 18mo at space station, despite being completely fit. People thougth it was lack of gravity. Detailed testing showed they were all completely LR! Completely screwed up ATP recycling. They fixed it for next time by putting magnetic field in the capsule they went up in.
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

Low D means high body fat (b/c of low dopamine??)
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
Removing gall bladder makes you an estrogen collector. Pg:E2 in gut WILL be upside down. GB concentrates estrogen to get rid of it and put it in gut for excretion. GB removal screws up phase 1(2?) liver detox, slows excretion, and body reabsorbs the estrogen. Makes gut flora pro-growth, and you want short fermented carbs.
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
Cinnamon = tons of chromium. So does nutmeg. I have no problems with using chromium picolinate.

These piezoelectric ligaments bring a DC electric current to every disc in our spine allowing us to retain the water in our discs to avoid degeneration, loss of signal on MRI, and degenerative disc disease. ### tellmisty — 2013-01-31T20:34:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spreecast-will-the-reset-broadcasts-and-webinars-be-on.5508/post-73506>

Hey all,

The challenge with video broadcasting of any type is that it is at the mercy of three main feeds: mine, Jack's, and yours. The connections between all of these must be able to stream all the information that's being sent across the optics. I know that Jack's and my computer both have adequate memory to handle video conferencing. We also both have high-speed broadband to handle the streaming. But any number of variables could affect the connect when we're live ... something could be happening with our service providers, your computer or your broadband connection could have challenges handling all the data, etc.

My advice would be to be patient with the technology. If it's REALLY choppy for you, it's likely due to either your computer or your internet connection, as we had plenty of people who were able to view with minimal distress. We don't have the control to make this any more perfect! I will see, though, if we can find an option to allow for call-in to a conference line at the same time as the calls; that may help some. This won't be mobilized for this call, but we might be able to have it set for the next one.

### caroline — 2013-01-31T22:23:19+0200

### nonchalant — 2013-01-17T12:32:52+0200

Does anyone know what 3 labs will be needed for the 90 day reset? I'll be out of town this weekend and I want to get them ordered.
I have a feeling it will be Vitamin D, DHEA, and hsCRP.

### KiwiLauren — 2013-01-17T14:35:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/90-day-optimal-reset-labs.5510/post-71246>

> **Nonchalant said**
>
> Does anyone know what 3 labs will be needed for the 90 day reset? I'll be out of town this weekend and I want to get them ordered.
> I have a feeling it will be Vitamin D, DHEA, and hsCRP.

I don't know, Nonchalant, but as those 3 have always been Dr Kruse's 'down and dirty' way to get a feel for the big picture, I'd say you're correct. Have fun wherever you are going!

### Kiera2 — 2013-01-18T21:32:40+0200

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-04T07:45:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/90-day-optimal-reset-labs.5510/post-74038>

My doctor today wouldn't order a DHEA test for me. He suggested he refer me to an endocrinologist if I wanted hormone panels done as he didn't know much about them. I don't really want to go down that road as seeing specialists is incredibly expensive. But I am getting the hs CRP and vitamin D done.

He only wanted me to have the ordinary CRP as the "hsCRP is so sensitive its meaningless. Everyone has inflammation so it doesn't tell us anything." But he did write it up. I offered to pay for the reverse T3 twice and I'm not sure if its in the shorthand version of the request for tests - he's ordered TFTs - will cover that. He was very resistant to anything that wasn't in his usual work up panel. You should have seen the shock and disgust on his face when I dared mention leptin resistance in passing in the conversation about rT3. He wouldn't order an HbA1c as it wasn't necessary unless the insulin was very high.

He had no idea what a VO2 Max was or where I might get it, nor where I could get a neuro-cognitive tests as outlined in Jack's optimising labs blog, other than, after a discussion about what it was and why I considered it important, suggesting I consult a physio for the former and a registered psychologist for the latter.

I was not impressed but he was at least polite to my face so it could have been much worse. And all these tests are being paid for by the government - so once I get the results I might be able to get the other things I need from another doctor who is likely to work only outside the government system.

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-04T07:47:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/90-day-optimal-reset-labs.5510/post-74039>

Tomorrow though I can't have my BAB within 30 minutes of waking as the blood test place doesn't open up until 2 hours after I would normally eat. But I guess that's permissible on one day.

### caroline — 2013-02-04T09:40:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/90-day-optimal-reset-labs.5510/post-74044>

I ordered a saliva cortisol panel from ZRT labs - no script

### ashryn — 2013-02-04T11:28:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/90-day-optimal-reset-labs.5510/post-74052>

AN, I am going to see my doctor tomorrow to ask for the same things.. But not vo2 max.. That's a fitness test. You can do one at a gym or I know Murdoch uni is set up to do them.. Dexa scans too.
You can do the cognitive tests online.. Just google them. There's some free sites.
Based on your experience I am going to say to my GP that I don't need her to interpret for me because I have the resources at home, but I can't monitor my progress and spot any changes without having the correct tests to check against in the first place. Wish me luck with that approach!
i'm not sure she will order the hbA1c because it is specifically recommended for diabetics, and the heart disease indications it used to be used for have been largely disproven now so if she's up to date in her reading she will know this, then again, if she's not, I'll be able to use the "I'm eating a load of saturated fats now and want to monitor the signs closely" line and see how that works out.. I'm also taking my DD in for the same tests because she's doing the reset as well, and I'm hoping it will add some weight because she's so young..

All this is under the assumption that the Dr will freak at the high fat mod protein no carbs concept, and want me to get all the tests that could possibly prove me wrong!

This is the doctor that gives me duramine without an appointment these days despite it being an addictive amphetamine (or analogue) with some serious side effects (stroke for one) that broke my metabolism beyond belief before I wised up and stopped taking it. Every time I go in to see her she is looking fatter and more tired.. Probably from following the CW diet advice. Maybe I'll be able to convert her to paleo when she sees my results!

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-05T07:49:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/90-day-optimal-reset-labs.5510/post-74253>

Ashryn, how did you get on with your doctor? Its my experience that they listen to you then take notice of your requests and they do as little as they can unless they absolutely agree with you. What annoyed me is that he ignored what I was saying and didn't have the courtesy to say he wouldn't be prepared to let me have the rT3.

Still waiting for Jack to get back to me with his assessments of what labs I need. Not sure how long that takes him but no doubt he will get to my history when time permits.

### ashryn — 2013-02-05T12:09:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/90-day-optimal-reset-labs.5510/post-74263>

Great news! My doc ordered every test I asked for, except salivary cortisol because she said the labs don't like them because they don't think they are accurate enough (suppose it depends on what you're testing for, but whatevs..) I'll bet I can get my medical scientist friend to order it for me.

she did say she isn't sure if the RT3 is covered by Medicare, but ordered it anyway.. She took a look at my list and listened to the reasons I wanted them all, and just started writing them all down. DD and I are going in on Monday morning ( she has busy work this week and can't take any time off) to get them all done.

Dr didnt even balk at the hba1c which I thought she would.
She asked about whether it is that french diet the high protein high fat one.. i said no, it is a variation on the paleo diet with additional things like observing circadian cycles to make sure you produce melatonin at the right time, and eating a large breakfast to help reset the leptin receptors that allow you to not store every single piece of carbohydrate as fat.. she said 'that all sounds pretty reasonable', and asked how I found the 'new diet' I said sheepishly, "the Internet", expecting her to do her narna, but she asked more and wrote down the name! She seemed interested and I think she might do more reading about it. How cool is that?

I can imagine it must be a refreshing change for a GP to see a patient that actually wants to take responsibility for their health.

The only thing I have ever gone to see her about in the last decade is being fat.. And to get referral to a physio after a car accident... She has watched me try everything and knows nothing has worked (at least nothing that's sustainable) so when she heard about how well this worked for me in the beginning, and how well I am feeling in general, maybe she was willing to entertain a new idea, or at least trust me that I have done some thorough research. She was probably happy that I wanted to test to make sure everything is good rather than just play around with myself and possibly cause some problems..

Whatever the reason, I am very happy with her response, now it just remains to be seen how many of them the lab will do on Medicare.

Nana, I have had that experience with every single male Dr I have been to, and one female who tried to convince me to get an abortion when I was 18. Sometimes I think all those years of studying just convince them that they are Einstein and the rest of us are monkeys.. Then again, it is possible that when it comes to medical care, most of us are monkeys, I guess it would be just as easy to get jaded in that profession as any other. Maybe more..

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-06T08:50:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/90-day-optimal-reset-labs.5510/post-74399>

That's great, Ashryn. i'll wait and see how my tests have gone and may then go and see your doc to see if I can pick up the others.

# Thread: Looking for suggestions for ways to track changes in cognition and memory
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-suggestions-for-ways-to-track-changes-in-cognition-and-memory.5515/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Optimalbound — 2013-01-17T16:10:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-suggestions-for-ways-to-track-changes-in-cognition-and-memory.5515/post-71258>

I have at long last managed to get my 86 year old mother's doctor to take her off statins (Simvastatin). Yay!

My mother has experienced a rapid and noticable decline in memory and other cognitive abilities over the last year or so, and it has been a very disturbing and distressing process to witness. I haven't established exactly for how long my mother has been on Simvastatin (she no longer remembers...) but i reckon it has been maybe three or four years.

I am very anxious to see if any of these symptoms will be reversed too some degree now that she is off Simvastatin, and I am looking for advice on how to objectively detect any such changes. I have googled a bit, but haven't found anything useful yet. Are there good and simple tests to measure memory and cognitive skills in a manner that is precise enough to be useful for detecting progress?

Any advice is very much appreciated.

# Thread: Cost of oysters
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cost-of-oysters.5517/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-17T19:29:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cost-of-oysters.5517/post-71297>

How much does it cost for oysters and other shell fish where you live?

Here in Western Australia the cheapest oysters I've been able to find has been $12 a dozen, some of which are very small. Usual price is $14.85 and more. Scallops are about $30 a kilo ($13.60 a pound). Mussels are as cheap as $12.80 a kilo ($5.80/lb) but usually more like $18 a kilo ($8.20/lb). This is not a complaint, but I do wonder how people manage to afford to eat oysters as a dozen oysters is only half the quantity weightwise as mussels and I eat a dozen of those at breakfast.

### KiwiLauren — 2013-01-17T20:03:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cost-of-oysters.5517/post-71302>

> **Aussie nana said**
>
> How much does it cost for oysters and other shell fish where you live?
> Here in Western Australia the cheapest oysters I've been able to find has been $12 a dozen, some of which are very small. Usual price is $14.85 and more. Scallops are about $30 a kilo ($13.60 a pound). Mussels are as cheap as $12.80 a kilo ($5.80/lb) but usually more like $18 a kilo ($8.20/lb). This is not a complaint, but I do wonder how people manage to afford to eat oysters as a dozen oysters is only half the quantity weightwise as mussels and I eat a dozen of those at breakfast.

$12 a dozen for oysters is cheap for here (New Zealand). There are some who farm them and sell them online (next day shipping) but otherwise they are usually closer to $20/dozen for me. I buy the tinned smoked ones and rinse/drain them repeatedly -that's how I afford them. Scallops are a rare treat. Prawns/shrimp are a bit less expensive, so I'll buy a frozen bag and have a few at a time. I eat more fish than I'd like (I prefer shellfish by far) but it's way better than not eating seafood... I go to the local fish market (I assume Perth has one?) and buy whatever is fresh and on special! (Also, you can often find whole squid for pretty cheap - today at my fish market it was $8/kg - and I've learned to clean one and eat it. Easy. Good luck!

### diane — 2013-01-17T20:08:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cost-of-oysters.5517/post-71305>

I can find oysters for about $13/dozen or so (in Canada). I don't get them often, and LIke KiwiLauren, I get smoked oysters and mussels as well. Sardines are cheap as well. Mussels are cheap here - I can get a dozen for about $3. I buy frozen scallops and shrimp - and a package is $7 or $8 (which will be 2-3 meals). I also eat fish, but don't get to the fish market as much as I would like!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-17T23:12:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cost-of-oysters.5517/post-71331>

$1.75 a piece ... yet another excuse ... I mean REASON ... not to slurp the slimy things.

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-18T10:32:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cost-of-oysters.5517/post-71387>

5 for $2 at the international market... DH asked for oyster shucking gloves and knife for his b-day....he got them... b/c well I. WANT. MORE. OYSTERS

### BJK77 — 2013-01-18T11:29:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cost-of-oysters.5517/post-71393>

I usually pay $9-12 per dozen at Whole Foods and have them shuck them for me, but my local grocery store has them for $3 a dozen starting today!! I just ordered a shucking contraption thing on amazon earlier in the week, so fingers crossed it arrives today so I can have an oyster feast for the next week { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### Inger — 2013-01-18T13:23:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cost-of-oysters.5517/post-71414>

I pay 2 â‚¬ (2,60 dollar?) for one oyster. Some shops they are more expensive, 2,50 â‚¬ for one.
I order 12 pieces every Friday, oysters from Bretagne, France. Yes it is expensive but it is my medicin.

And I love the taste too for sure... fresh shucked by me with a few drops of fresh lime.. yum!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-18T14:10:01+0200

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-18T14:24:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cost-of-oysters.5517/post-71429>

You all are making me feel like a big baby. bother.

OK OK OK I guess I'll go buy a few ... DH will swallow them if I can't work up the nerve.

### Inger — 2013-01-18T14:34:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cost-of-oysters.5517/post-71435>

I never use gloves either, just my hands and the shucker knife.. sometime a cotton towel. Las week I cut me though. But I do not care too much as my wounds heal so fast.

Darleen.. you will love them soon enough!!! I promise! Just try them a couple of times even if they are not your favorit. That is how it went for me at least! Drop some fresh lime juice on the top! And eat them ice cold.

Don't give them to your hubby for gods sake! He eats crap and do not deserve this gold... you do!

### BJK77 — 2013-01-18T14:36:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cost-of-oysters.5517/post-71437>

> **DarleenMB said**
>
> You all are making me feel like a big baby. bother.
> OK OK OK I guess I'll go buy a few ... DH will swallow them if I can't work up the nerve.

Darleen,

I was terrified to try them! (I'm still wimping out over the MHS too) My first step was to try them cooked and honestly - I HATED them! Swore I'd never eat another ever. I ended up at Whole Foods one day and they were doing a shucking demonstration. I listened to all these people talk about how great they were, yada, yada, yada . . . and finally broke down and said I'd give them one more try. I figured maybe the ones I had before were just bad.

They shucked a dozen for me and I brought them home for dinner figuring DH would eat 11 and I'd force 1 down (DH loves oysters anyway he can get them - he desperately misses my mom's oyster stuffing at holidays!). I think in the end DH got 3 or 4 of them

Now I find myself craving them, but ONLY on the half shell. I'll never do cooked again.

Give them a try . . . what do you have to lose?!

### Inger — 2013-01-18T14:41:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cost-of-oysters.5517/post-71440>

I would not EVER cook a oyster. A very bad sin in my world.

I never buy cooked either. To me their beautiful life force disappears when cooked. Too sad.
I bet they do not taste that good cooked either. In any restaurant I have worked as a waitress in Europe oysters were never cooked, ever!

### kathylu — 2013-01-18T17:35:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cost-of-oysters.5517/post-71476>

oh yes we do...unfortunately I don't get home from work unitl 5....dh at 6 and we have to pick up Chaos and Havoc some where in that time frame

### prAna303 — 2013-01-18T02:48:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-we-think-these-would-work-for-emf.5518/post-71353>

I used to work with electricity back in the days. I would say that all kind of "magic stuff" do not work properly. As Anna K. wrote there can be some problem with grounding if you donÂ´t know what you are doing. ItÂ´s not that easy to filter EMF or ESD or any other stuff because you must know what you are looking for. I have seen accidents where people got hurt because connecting mats the wrong way and so on. If you like to try out stuff, consider some starting kits from a componentsupplier (see link). ### freesia — 2013-01-17T23:19:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sea-crop-for-your-bath.5519/post-71332>

Sea Crop is distilled from the Pacific Ocean and is generally used as a nutrient for the garden.
https://www.facebook.com/pages/Sea-Crop/206610026133103

Back, more than a year ago when I was learning how to grow photosynthetic bacteria and some others with paramagnetic qualities, { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I stumbled into this slightly strange, nonetheless charming, community of people experimenting with all kinds of applications.

I didn't use Sea-Crop myself, but I know it was very highly rated. I don't know for sure, maybe someone else does, but perhaps it was a good electron donor? Or the balance of minerals enhanced electron uptake? Anyone have an opinion on this?
I can't get here, sadly. But if I could...I would absolutely put some in my earthed ice bath!
As it is I use my homegrown bacteria, seaweed, and mg chloride, but it's still a LONG way from the Pacific Ocean.

If anyone does use it, check in here with a report!

I feel a bit bad after the Q and A last night. The beach is right at the end of my street. Half a block away...and I don't use it nearly enough. It's not cold enough most of the year for CT the way I like it, but I really need to take better advantage anyway and get my feet in the wet sand more.
..I don't generally make New Year's resolutions but this year I just did, and that's it!

This winter will be different and I'm really looking forward to it. Meanwhile my dog is really going to love some extra walks along the water's edge.

**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-think-i-found-why-i-cannot-digest-sea-food-that-well.5522/>

**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### x_danny_x — 2013-01-18T01:44:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-think-i-found-why-i-cannot-digest-sea-food-that-well.5522/post-71349>

I was reading on why the Japanese have no problems digesting large amounts of sea food. They have develop certain bacteria/viruses that help digest sea food since so many years of doing so.

I compare t to me and I certainly have not eaten sea food until I found Jack Kruse. So certainly my body was not too used to it. This is a theory though of mine.

However, I found out that I wasn't eating right either, still trying to do so with so much in my hands right now. I believe I was also missing PROBIOTICS which will help ur digestive system. A product that seems to have is called Chia-Kombucha, which is generally made of sea weed, kelp, and some other ingredients.

If you get stomach problems, also u should have Bentonite Powder on hand. Anybody tried these two? I think it will help me increase my digestion of sea food.

How It Works:

Bentonite Clay is a very unique substance, formed primarily of Montmorillonite, which is an extremely flat crystal flake, that carries a relatively strong negative ionic charge. The negative charge is compensated for by adsorbing a cation (either Sodium or Calcium) to the interior of the molecule, this is what makes it either Sodium Bentonite or Calcium Bentonite Clay. To put it another way think of the Montmorillonite crystal flake resembling two pieces of bread, with a strong vacuum pulling the lunch meat in between them, whatever lands there (Sodium or Calcium) determines what kind of sandwich it will be. Now when you activate this clay sandwich it will magnetically grab the junk in your body (heavy metals, toxins, acid, etc.â€¦) and trade them for theâ€￾ lunch meatâ€￾ taking the toxins out in your waste. The two different clays although very similar in their inert state (dry) have very different applications when activated (mixed with water).
its amazing stuff, all natural too

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-18T09:52:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-think-i-found-why-i-cannot-digest-sea-food-that-well.5522/post-71383>

# Thread: Exercise and mitochondia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-mitochondia.5523/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-18T05:29:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-mitochondia.5523/post-71363>

Now I know that I'm not supposed to do exercise while I'm working on sorting out my mitochondria, but can I have some clarification about what this means in practice? I've spent years adding in incidental exercise to my lifestyle as I don't go to the gym - so now I have to look at that.

So can I still walk the dog for 45 minutes at a slow to moderate pace, or is that out? This plus the other things I do leave me physically tired.
Can I do my volunteer work which means lifting and carrying lots and lots of books - individually and cartons of them? I have the strength, but cumulatively the repetitiveness of this adds up to energy use that leaves me physically tired.
Can I do my gardening? this isn't really relevant at the moment as its mid summer but as we move into autumn it will be. Being on hands and knees stretching to deal with plants in the garden is as much a work out as the gym ever was.
Do I still go down on my hands and knees to scrub my floors? Old fashioned I know, but can I? Or does this have to go? The same for cleaning windows, washing out cupboards, etc.

Jack has suggested I stop my karate lessons for the time being, and I'll stop the cardio part definitely. But if the Sensei agrees, physically would it be OK if I turn it into a mindfulness exercise until the mitochondria come right - a bit like Tai Chi? Or is Tai Chi out as well?

So how much is too much? What criteria do I use to judge? Comments and suggestions appreciated.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-18T06:39:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-mitochondia.5523/post-71365>

Aussie nana, I think you can do anything that does not make you tired. When you come home from walking with the dog und you still feel ok, then I believe that is ok.
You must judge that yourself. When you feel tired or even exhausted you have used up all your energy which you need for repair. So I would think to avoid exhausting yourself is your first priority.
I may be wrong, but that is what I understood from the mitochondrial webinar. So listen to your body real close.

### kathylu — 2013-01-18T09:12:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-mitochondia.5523/post-71372>

I think the very slow movements of Tai Chi would be great! Do a yoga dvd every morning. Shorten your walks a bit, so you come back refreshed, not tired. Do lots of CT...that feels like a workout at the gym to me, plus you get all the other benefits!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-18T09:57:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-mitochondia.5523/post-71384>

Kathylu's idea is good........do it on Earth too.

### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-18T18:21:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-mitochondia.5523/post-71492>

The trouble is I do come back from the walks feeling fine. And an hour's full on karate has me feeling absolutely wonderful for hours afterwards and the next day. So obviously feelings are not enough. I think its about total activity in a week. If I can do 10 hours of what is closer to HIIT than cardio and another 10 hours of walking, and not too vigorous work in a week perhaps I should cut that in half so I never get to the point of being exhausted.

I'm already doing Hanna somatics for an hour a day (a sort of gentle stretching). I'm doing the CT as cold as I can without setting off those darned urticaria welts - so face dunking + wearing ice around the house + a cool bath in the evenings + wallowing in the light surf at the beach when I can get there 1-3 times a week. I walk in bare feet when I can (5minutes across the park during the dog walk - only 5 of the 40 minutes is bare-foot-able) and bare feet all the time around home and in the yard.

### KiwiLauren — 2013-01-18T21:07:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-mitochondia.5523/post-71517>

This is just a guess, AN, but I wonder if your 'feeling good' after a workout relates to cortisol. Did you get an ASI done? If you're in that phase of altered cortisol where you are running higher than optimal levels of cortisol, it could mean that you're getting further spikes from exercise like karate, which will give you a temporary high/increase in energy while further running down your mitochondria (and hormones, etc). I think many people get addicted to exercise for that very reason. I do not at all mean to imply that you are addicted or even that you have an altered cortisol pattern... just thinking out loud about how to think about physiological reactions that are immediate versus more sustained longer term healing ones.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-21T22:11:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-mitochondia.5523/post-72224>

I know for me starting at the 5 month mark is when I felt 'addicted' to karate. It was more like 3-4 x a week for the longest while and feeling good. Then I just felt surged and wanted to go like 5-6 x/week. I didn't understand about adrenals etc. It also led to a really bad crash (plus personal and family stress) plus a program I was never meant to do in Sept-Nov/Dec. of 2011).

It was basically the worst time in my life ever. It took me a year to recover. I'm not lying either. At least 2013 I am starting off with a direction of improvement rather than dragging myself out of a whole (same time but last year). It's much better.

I can say that at least I benefited greatly from this site if at times I felt frustration or had set backs to my recovery/progress due to some un-knowing errors which didn't help.

So yes I am out of that hell hole and hope to never even reach the surface of that again.

Onwards and Upwards. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-22T19:28:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-mitochondia.5523/post-72385>

I climbed a tree today in -20'C. I felt really good when doing this. I then did some extra light hiking on the lake.

Glenn PDQ: Please explain what you mean by 'birds are way ahead of us on this one'. Thanks.

### Glen PDQ — 2013-01-22T23:43:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-mitochondia.5523/post-72451>

> **Zorica Vuletic said**
>
> I climbed a tree today in -20'C. I felt really good when doing this. I then did some extra light hiking on the lake.
> Glenn PDQ: Please explain what you mean by 'birds are way ahead of us on this one'. Thanks.
>
>
>
> http://www.the-scientist.com/?articles.view/articleNo/23786/title/Plugging-the-Mitochondrial-Leak/
> According to Gustavo Barja at the Complutense University in Madrid, pigeons leak barely a tenth the ROS of rats, and live nearly ten times longer, yet their resting metabolic rates are similar. "ROS leakage is so low in pigeons that they can afford to have much lower antioxidant levels than rats, and still live longer," says Barja. "The question is, why are pigeon mitochondria so leak-proof?"

### duceswild557@hotmail.com — 2013-01-24T12:23:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-mitochondia.5523/post-72662>

@jack, you said "Not if your LRâ€¦â€¦â€¦..it means you need more electronsâ€¦â€¦before you can lose them again" and I think that's great advice for anyone who is considering endurance exercises, but that's simply not the case for anaerobic HIIT, specifically something like the Sprint 8 protocol. 30 second bouts of all out physical exertion 3 times a week is only 12 minutes of workout per 7 days. I think it's great that you lost weight and got into shape using cold therapy and without exercise and I by no means encourage anybody to do endurance exercises of any sort. But there is no reason to put off doing a HIIT protocol like sprint 8 until one becomes leptin sensitive because HIIT will increase leptin sensitivity:
1)http://jap.physiology.org/content/111/3/715.abstract
2)http://suppversity.blogspot.com/2012/08/the-iranian-hiit-solution-three-200m.html?m=1

It also increases mitoChondrial capacity in t2 diabetics.

3)link 3, I can only post 2 per post so I'll post below with links 3 and 4.

I really like your blog but I feel like your biased against real exercise like HIIT because you didn't use it for your transformation. Phil Campbell used it though in a clinical setting n=11, with phenomenal body Recomposition results and I think you would strengthen your own position in health and longevity if you looked more into this. 4)link 4

### duceswild557@hotmail.com — 2013-01-24T12:25:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-mitochondia.5523/post-72663>

3) http://jap.physiology.org/content/111/6/1554.full.pdfÂ And 4) \[video=youtube\_share;geVJqKCFk-c\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=geVJqKCFk-c&feature=youtube\_gdata\_player\[/video\] from above.

Also, I only post this here because I feel like if you incorporated this into your program you'd probably be the best blog on the net for health and longevity tbh.

### joupnorth@hotmail.com — 2013-01-26T00:40:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-mitochondia.5523/post-72675>

HIIT increases my cortisol, which in turn pumps my blood sugar. If HIIT increases mitochondrial capacity in T2D, i'd be all over it, but probably not at the cost blood sugar stability. Unless someone can show me otherwise....? Due respect for Mercola for the good he does do, but I don't think he's the last word on these matters.

Everything I've seen points to HIIT improving blood glucose levels, insulin sensitivity and leptin sensitivity. I'd question your sources if they're telling you that HIIT will compromise your blood sugar stability.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes.5530/post-71617>

### Destiny — 2013-01-20T10:31:13+0200

How does Juil help me make this connection?

Traditional footwear, made with rubber or plastic outsoles may serve a comfort need for us, but it certainly doesn't help us connect with the earth. On the contrary, it blocks the necessary flow of electrons into our body and prevents us from heeding the earth's natural resource. By placing Energy Flow Technologyâ„¢ (compound copper conductors) directly through the footbed and outsole of the sandals, Juil offers a direct conduit between our bodies and the earth's surface. You are able to go about your day, in comfort, while heeding the earth's reviving electrons necessary to sustain healthy, stable lives.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-20T11:14:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes.5530/post-71817>

Maybe we don't need shoes.
A few weeks ago I came into my office and there was a man standing there looking around. I asked if I can help and he said that he was just looking around as he was attracted by the company logo on the outside wall of the factory. It says: Natures Intelligence.
We were talking a few sentences and he seemed like an ordinary person. Then I looked down at his feet and... he wore neither shoes nor socks and it was freezing cold outside. He had his car in the shop and while waiting he walked around the neighbourhood and so came to our office.
His feet were quite red and I said: you are not wearing any shoes!!! (thinking by myself that he was some kind of a beggar or something along those lines, too astonished to really think anything much in this moment)
He told me that he had stopped wearing shoes a year ago and was going barefoot summer and winter and he explained that since he did that even his ganglions were receeding. He explained that since he did that he had the most amazing changes happen in his body. Especially also to his gut. As we talked it turned out that he was a proctologist and phlebologist from Augsburg. Thats when I took him seriously (here are my prejudices)
He had to leave so my curiosity was not stilled. So last week I called him to invite him to dinner, as I want to find out more about this. He said he would call me back. If he doesn't by end of this coming week, I call again.

### Lyndra — 2013-01-20T12:36:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes.5530/post-71837>

> **caroline said**
>
> These all seem like good ideas ... if anyone gets some can you report back on sizing? There used to be a German brand of clogs that were felt - Does anyone remember those? I used to see them alot in Canada. They were meant as slippers as I recall but everyone wore them outside too. They looked great with jeans.

I have some Stegmann boiled wool clogs I got many years ago. Unfortunately they have rubber soles so aren't going to be good for earthing. However, I also have a pair of Halflinger boiled wool slippers which are all wool, including the sole. They don't have enough durability for serious walking but I use them when I go stand outside in the morning to get some sunshine.

### laawindy1 — 2014-02-15T04:32:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes.5530/post-110358>

I have found shoes from earthrunners are extremely good. They have long experience in earthing shoes. They are promoting a more grounded way of living that is possible when connected to the Earth’s electric potential of zero. If you like you can try it.
[www.earthrunners.com](http://www.earthrunners.com/)

### Gagnrad — 2014-02-15T12:11:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes.5530/post-110386>

> [Lyndra said:](/goto/post?id=71837)
>
> I have some Stegmann boiled wool clogs I got many years ago. Unfortunately they have rubber soles so aren't going to be good for earthing. However, I also have a pair of Halflinger boiled wool slippers which are all wool, including the sole. They don't have enough durability for serious walking but I use them when I go stand outside in the morning to get some sunshine.

You reminded me of pattens. Not that pattens are clogs, but there's a kind of similarity.

People used to wear these in medieval times, strapping them on over their shoes, when they went outdoors, since even in town -- or possibly particularly in town -- it could be very muddy outside in streets and yards. There's a pair in the bottom corner of the van Eyck "Arnolfini Marriage" painting:

[http://www.nationalgallery.org.uk/paintings/jan-van-eyck-the-arnolfini-portrait](http://www.nationalgallery.org.uk/paintings/jan-van-eyck-the-arnolfini-portrait)

Doubtless Giovanni di Nicolao Arnolfini (the guy on the left who looks like Vladimir Putin) wouldn't want to get whatever costly shoes he wears indoors exposed to the mire!

I suppose reflection on clogs and pattens is interesting as showing that not everyone was grounded through their feet/footwear at all times, in all seasons of the year, and in all places up to the 1960s when rubber- or plastic-soled shoes became very common. Still, I expect bare feet or thin leather soled-shoes were closer to the norm.

# Thread: Pregnenolone / DHEA Side Effects
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnenolone-dhea-side-effects.5531/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-01-18T14:04:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnenolone-dhea-side-effects.5531/post-71421>

It's so good to be back here! I was avoiding the forum for a while due to being sick, traveling, the holidays, and then not being able to log in for weeks . . . not to mention the forum was super slooow when I could finally get on, but I'm so glad to hear the forum is going back to vBulletin { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Anyway, question for all you seasoned hormone experts! I had a consult last week with Jack and it was pretty obvious that I needed to increase my DHEA and Pregnenolone supplementation - my labs were very low. I had been doing 40 mg DHEA and only increased to 50 mg last Sunday. I added Preg in at 25 mg, so fairly low doses, BUT I'm an absolute wreck lately and am wondering if it could be from those slight increases.

I just binged on an entire head of cooked cabbage and now feel like I'm gonna vomit! I haven't had cravings (and still don't really) or binged on anything for years. I have no idea where this came from. I've been eating a bit more cabbage lately than I probably should, but not an entire head at a sitting! My emotions are all over the place too, but mostly just feeling super depressed and worried about everything. There was a stabbing at a school not far from me this morning and I nearly drove to both my kids' schools to pick them up. I've spent all morning (when I should be working) searching for houses at the beach and bawling my eyes out because I'm not sure DH will move . . . but I've got this irrational, urgent desire to uproot my entire family and get us near water, yet away from society. That thought alone then makes me hysterical because I don't want to leave the rest of my family! Poor DH . . . coming home to me today is not going to be fun . . .

So do you all think this is just a temporary meltdown as my body adjusts to the slight increase in DHEA/Preg? Is this a common side effect or what?!?!

### vkiernan — 2013-01-18T14:18:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnenolone-dhea-side-effects.5531/post-71427>

Hugs to you. I know the feeling all too well and it is no fun. DH will deal with it, just be sure to let him know as soon as he comes home if not sooner. This should at least ward off an argument or otherwise cuz he will know it is the damn hormones speaking and not you. I can't really help you but I wouldn't think that small increase would make you react in such a way but that is only based on my own n=1. Where are you cycle wise? Maybe early peri-menapause or just a bump in the road? Are you taking any hormones?

### BJK77 — 2013-01-18T14:31:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnenolone-dhea-side-effects.5531/post-71434>

Thanks Vicki! I just finished a cycle; otherwise I would have assumed it was PMS rearing it's ugly head. I do take Progesterone too - 100 mg for now until my Prometrium comes. I think my labs do show early peri-menopause . . .

I'm sure DH will be understanding. He usually is, which of course tends to make me even more emotional because I feel so guilty for acting like such a crybaby! Being a woman is tough

### vkiernan — 2013-01-18T14:44:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnenolone-dhea-side-effects.5531/post-71441>

> **BJK77 said**
>
> Thanks Vicki! I just finished a cycle; otherwise I would have assumed it was PMS rearing it's ugly head. I do take Progesterone too - 100 mg for now until my Prometrium comes. I think my labs do show early peri-menopause . . .
> I'm sure DH will be understanding. He usually is, which of course tends to make me even more emotional because I feel so guilty for acting like such a crybaby! Being a woman is tough

I can tell you that sometimes when adding progesterone, my pms is at the end of my cycle instead or prior. Don't know why this is but it started when the p was added to my routine. Ride it out a few more days and see if it gets better.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-would-like-to-announce.5538/post-71510>

Just got a call from my endocrinologist who diagnosed me with Hashi's last year. She reluctantly tested my TPO antibodies at my request....but said once it is positive, it's always positive. Guess what....it is negative! Thank God and thank Dr. Kruse for the information......I eat 1-2 brazil nuts per day and a ton of fish and seaweed.

Wow! That is the dream! Congratulations! Keep up the good work

Motivation to keep on the right path - once your body knows how to make antibodies it never fogets. You will need to remain viligiant in regards to your health. I will keep working to be like you. What a great example to have out there!

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-01-29T23:53:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-would-like-to-announce.5538/post-73238>

> [ColdBren said:](/goto/post?id=71510)

Guess what....it is negative! Thank God and thank Dr. Kruse for the information......I eat 1-2 brazil nuts per day and a ton of fish and seaweed.

ANother reason why endos suck though -she actually fights you on testing for antibodies?! Geesh. You should always know what those suckers are up to.

### caroline — 2013-01-29T23:57:13+0200

> [nuttmegs17 said:](/goto/post?id=73238)

> ANother reason why endos suck though -she actually fights you on testing for antibodies?! Geesh. You should always know what those suckers are up to.

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=73239)

> great analogy - I love that! We truly do have to know what those suckers are up to! wonder why she doesn't really care to know??? What would be her thinking?

I will tell you a quick story that also illustrates why they suck. My endo at a prominent US hospital WOULD NOT test me for hashis even though my TSH was steadily rising. Finally got it. Then she told me I was fine (actually my Dr told me that my endo said in my chart I was fine). I asked to tsee the labs and it clearly showed antibodies. Even my Dr was confused. The endo then explained to us, that yes, I had antibodies but wasn't sick enough to treat. FAIL! i NEVER went back.

I assume your Dr is just stuck in convential wisdom like the rest. Be sure to fight to get them tested when you can. If they start to appear again, it means you need tweaking.

That is just amazing though. Were you on thyroid meds?

ColdBren that is very exciting news! Way to go!

### ColdBren — 2013-02-03T04:49:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-would-like-to-announce.5538/post-73891>

Thanks again guys, love the support! I had another test done about 2 weeks later, still negative, I the numbers were 6 in one and 9 in the other. I never took thyroid meds, other than armour in a tiny amount for about a week. My doc, not only didn't want to retest me after diagnosing me a year earlier, but she sounded so unhappy about the result....lol! Oh well, I have tried to be epi paleo, but fail a lot, and I also eat a lot of rice and some potatoes. I really think the brazil nuts, fish and seaweed were crucial and I did ct a lot last year, lots of sun too.

Oh- mama grok, I started the brazil nut a day based on one of dr.k's blogs that explained how selenium has been shown to lower hashi antibodies.

# Thread: Pegnenolone help

**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-01-19T01:20:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pegnenolone-help.5542/post-71548>

my preg was tested on Oct 2 and was high 21 nmo1/L range 0.3 - 6.3
I had been supplementing for 2 months daily with 50mg on the advice of Dr.K.
However I forgot to stop taking it before the test. Dr. K. and my Doc both thought it was supplement driven.

I had it retested 2 months later and it is still 21 - and I did stop taking it the day before this test

So do you think I should have stopped taking for longer before the test? or it is just high and I should stop taking it?
My progesterone was very low on my first saliva cortisol test at the end of August - it was 7 \[range 12-100\] and my DHEA was low 2 \[2 -23\]
That is why Dr. Kruse suggested DHEA and Pregnenolone and Progesterone

My last cortisol/saliva test - the progesterone has come up to 43 and DHEA to 2.5

Any thoughts?
Thanks in advance

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-19T11:45:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pegnenolone-help.5542/post-71594>

When I test Dzugan advises me to take all supplements. You might try backing down to 25mg and see where you are at
You might want to consider supplementing w/DHEA as well.

### caroline — 2013-01-19T16:06:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pegnenolone-help.5542/post-71640>

Thanks Gretch - I do use Dhea. I am wondering what is happening to all that preg? is it turning into pg? or cortisol? or where is it going? or is it not going anywhere?

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-20T09:41:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pegnenolone-help.5542/post-71784>

Hows your lipid panel.... you might have some pregnenolone steal going on...

### caroline — 2013-01-20T20:18:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pegnenolone-help.5542/post-71923>

Thanks Gretchen - I finally "asked Jack" he says it is good so I will just go with it - but I really don't understand - my number is so far north of the range....

# Thread: Lamb heart?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lamb-heart.5543/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-01-19T03:05:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lamb-heart.5543/post-71564>

How are you preparing Lamb heart? I made some heart Tartare style tonite and I am really struggling with it. I think this is going to be a problem for me since I don't really like lamb ...I know that sounds un Australian .... I really dislike the smell - altho I do like the little tiny loin chops that cost a fortune. I just remembered that a lot of the smell is in the fat so I guess I should have trimmed all the fat off - but we want the fat - right?

### KiwiLauren — 2013-01-19T03:38:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lamb-heart.5543/post-71567>

Caroline, I don't really like cooked lamb much either (I buy the mince and add lots of herbs & spices). But lamb heart I find easy to eat. I just buy it in the supermarket (which I'm assuming you can do in Aus, too), slice it into thin & small pieces, put it in a dish with a chopped raw onion, garlic, whatever herbs I have on hand, sea salt, pepper & either apple cider vinegar or lemon juice. Then I let it sit for a while (or often I do it at night and put it in the fridge and eat it for breakfast). It has very little taste - certainly not strong - and is really easy to eat. And, yes, keep the fat on it - it has a soft texture and is easy to eat. Lauren

### caroline — 2013-01-19T16:35:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lamb-heart.5543/post-71645>

thanks Lauren - that is pretty much what I did with it - I will see how I feel about it today.

### endless — 2013-01-19T17:06:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lamb-heart.5543/post-71652>

The lamb heart I had was whole, so I'm not sure if it is prepared in any way when you buy it, but I cut off the large, hard chunks of fat near the top of the heart before I used it. Softer fat I left on...but frankly I think that if the fat is a deal breaker you should trim it off and eat the rest. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I always toss with lemon juice and onion at the least...I like Thai flavors so I often add a bit of fish sauce and red chile paste too.

### caroline — 2013-01-19T18:02:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lamb-heart.5543/post-71666>

This is the first time I have bought Lamb heart - it seemed pretty well trimmed I think - there was no hard chunks of fat - Do you let it sit for a day? I just seem to be feeling really squeamish about everything the last couple of days. Maybe I have done too much earthing and my electrons are rebelling or I am over charged???

### diane — 2013-01-19T19:06:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lamb-heart.5543/post-71677>

I was at this amazing raw food pet store today - they had every kind of meat, organs, bones for dogs. I saw lamb heart on the "menu" - and asked to get some. And they were out! Darn it! I do want to find a source, but doesn't seem to be the easiest thing here. Beef or pork heart, no problem. But lamb I have to keep searching!

### Inger — 2013-01-20T13:18:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lamb-heart.5543/post-71847>

Caroline, I would say cut off the fat if it is what makes it yucky for you. At least most of it. You could fry it up a bit to get it moist and eat it or use it for cooking or eat plain and raw. I would cut lambs heart thin an make a carpaccio.. it is the most delicious thing IMO! Do some raw onion and dried crispy mushrooms on the top too! And of course a good natural balsamic vinegar and EVOO and fresh ground pepper and salt.

The other pic are lambs hearts made into rolls.. I filled them with minced elk meat that I made nicely spicy, and some raw fat inside too..

Caroline if you cannot get it dow raw, try to slice it in thin pieces and fry it very gentle into medium ++.. just let it warm up and fry a tiny bit and add salt and pepper and eat. I have done that some times too and it was good too!

### caroline — 2013-01-20T17:43:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lamb-heart.5543/post-71894>

Thanks Inger - that is what I did with it .... but I wasn't feeling the best and I forced myself to eat it and then the next day I think I had the flu so will give myself some time before trying again

# Thread: DIY'ish grounding straps.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diyish-grounding-straps.5544/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### freesia — 2013-01-19T03:28:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diyish-grounding-straps.5544/post-71566>

A simple, low budget option, for those in the US, if to go visit www.lessemf.com and purchase a ready wired earth plug which can be attached to a wrist strap.
Total cost $9.95

On this page http://www.lessemf.com/ground.html 1/3 the way down is a PLUG TO GATOR
Alligator clip on one end, 3-prong molded plug on other end. Easiest way to ground fabrics, drapes, plastics. Six feet long. Only the round lug is conneted to the wire. The 2 flat prongs are not connect to anything. Grounding Cord, 3-prong/gator 6ft (Cat. #A295-3) ...... $6.00

...You attach the gator on that to another gator....such as this one 2/3 down the same page
VELCRO ANTI-STATIC WRIST STRAP
comfortable 2 cm wide, black nylon Velcro strap
interior surface is coated with conductive carbon
completely adjustable
coil cord with 1 Mohm resistor, connects to strap with snap, stretches to 60 inch
cord terminates with banana plug or alligator clip (included)
Velcro A-S Wrist Strap (Cat. #A469) â€¦â€¦ $3.95

This is essentially what we've made at home, except we have a banana socket & plug connection instead of the two gators.
They look very fetching plugged in and hanging up on the headboard...
They work.

We checked our wall sockets first with a "power point and earth leakage meter" (approx $20 here) to make sure the earth point is actually earthed, and that the power point is safe and wired correctly. They should be (!), but Dr Mercola mentioned on the Earthing video with Stephen Sinatra, that he takes a meter with him and a number of wall sockets are not actually earthed....maybe in older buildings??
There are detailed instructions for testing printed on the meter.

I had trouble uploading pics tonight...will try tomorrow. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. **Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diyish-grounding-straps.5544/post-71568>

Scuse various typos. It won't let me edit!!

That's a pic of one our earthing straps at the bottom of the post.

### Martin — 2013-01-19T08:14:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diyish-grounding-straps.5544/post-71573>

Very cool. Thanks for the inspiration.
I dug out my wrist thingy and mattress thingy.

# Thread: Anyone know about ingrowing hairs?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-know-about-ingrowing-hairs.5546/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ashryn — 2013-01-19T08:34:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-know-about-ingrowing-hairs.5546/post-71576>

I have a history of ingrown hairs... On my lower legs they are not so bad, but on my upper inside thighs they seem to grow very deep, and cause hard painful red lumps (big ones.. The size of a cherry) that either last for a few months if i don't touch them, or leave big purple scars if I try to squeeze them. (I tend not to do this unless I can see something in there trying to get out.)

I have never seen a doctor about them, (though a friend had one the same and did and the doc said to just jeave it alone and it would go away) and so I have no idea if there is a name for this problem. I don' get them all the time, and mostly don't think about it since upper thighs will always be hidden from view... Except, maybe now they won't be so I am wondering what causes it and how to heal it.

Has anyone else had experience with this kind of thing? I am interested to know if it too might be healed by epipaleo and CT.

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-19T10:14:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-know-about-ingrowing-hairs.5546/post-71589>

not much help here I used to get them in my armpit when I shaved. when I went back to waxing every 3wks -they stopped... same for the bikini zone/upper thighs...

just my N=1.... you might consider seeing a dermatologist if its painful or gets infected regularly... I think there are certain types of cysts that require lancing to avoid infection.

### caroline — 2013-01-19T16:18:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-know-about-ingrowing-hairs.5546/post-71641>

not much help here either - I used to get them on my lower legs but haven't for a long time.....but not as you describe either....

### kzimm — 2013-01-19T16:50:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-know-about-ingrowing-hairs.5546/post-71646>

If they seem almost like boils, painful and taking a long time to heal maybe look into this condition. Hidradentis Suppurativa. I believe it's on the spectrum of AI. This blog has some informative links.
http://www.primalgirl.com/ If this isn't the case, so sorry for subjecting you.

### ashryn — 2013-01-19T18:24:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-know-about-ingrowing-hairs.5546/post-71670>

Hey, thanks colorado.. I have read about that, and since I had eczema, it isn't inconceivable that this is the right name for my condition, (though mine is only mild) I am leaning towards the idea that everything will turn into some kind of autoimmune disease if the cause is not removed.

Since I have changed the things I do, I expect to see this occur less often (or never again!) Has anyone noticed their leg hairs grow straight with paleo and CT and so don't grow inwards any more?

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-19T21:04:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-know-about-ingrowing-hairs.5546/post-71715>

I get ingrown hairs on my upper, inside thighs sometimes and have a few small purple scars to prove it. My esthetician tells me it's from waxing and the hair is starting to grow in weaker, or something like that. Not sure how true that is but that was her opinion. I have never had them the size of cherries but they do sometimes get red and a bit painful. If I know it's an ingrown hair I use a lancet ( this is what was recommended to me) to free the hair and then apply melaleuca oil and then I don't get a scar.

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-20T08:43:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-know-about-ingrowing-hairs.5546/post-71778>

Lancets are the little plastic things that attach to the device used to prick your finger when testing your blood sugar. That's what was suggested to me because they're sharp, disposable, etc.

# Thread: Leptin Rx vs Epi Paleo
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-vs-epi-paleo.5548/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Reset 2013
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TheKid — 2013-01-19T10:37:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-vs-epi-paleo.5548/post-71590>

Jack (or anyone else),

Are we using the the Leptin Rx version of eating for this challenge or are we using the Epi Paleo version.

I am asking because I re-read the Letpin Rx blog and in it, Omega 6 fats were discouraged. For this reason, olive oil use was discouraged in favor of the saturated fats. But the Epi Paleo template makes heavy use of olive oil as the preferred oil to combine with your seafood.

So which is it?

Also, I don't know about other folks, but my 23andMe results showed that my consuming monounsaturated fats (olive oil is mono) would potentially help burn abdominal fat. So that further confounds the issue, at least for me and other folks with that genetic trait.

Good luck everyone.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-19T14:33:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-vs-epi-paleo.5548/post-71623>

You should always eat electron dense (Epi-paleo) using the Leptin Rx rule to control your circadian timing to food to account for artificial light energy stealing. All to be revealed........in EMF 2.

# Thread: Will folks start using Viagra with CT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-folks-start-using-viagra-with-ct.5549/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Eric — 2013-01-19T11:00:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-folks-start-using-viagra-with-ct.5549/post-71592>

# Thread: Mitochondrial Rx substrate info thread for DMSO
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-rx-substrate-info-thread-for-dmso.5553/>
**Subforum:** Factor X
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-19T14:09:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-rx-substrate-info-thread-for-dmso.5553/post-71616>

In the â€œJournal of Biological Rhythmsâ€￾ 1994 winter edition, Nelson R.J. and Blom J.M.,
published (in part) the following abstract:
â€œAdult female deer mice (Peromyscus maniculatus) were housed in either long (LD 16:8) or short (LD 8:16) days for 8 weeks, then injected with the chemical carcinogen 9,10- dimethyl-1, 2-benzanthracene (DMBA) dissolved in dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) or with the DMSO vehicle alone. Animals were evaluated weekly for 8 weeks after injection. None of the animals treated with DMSO developed tumors in any of the experiments. Nearly 90% of the long-day deer mice injected with DMBA developed squamous cell carcinoma. None of the short-day deer mice injected with DMBA developed tumors. Small lesions developed at the site of injection; short-day females had less severe lesions and healed faster than longday females.â€￾

None of them got cancer. After EMF 2 you'll know why.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-20T10:01:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-rx-substrate-info-thread-for-dmso.5553/post-71795>

So the short day mice didin't develop cancer even the ones not treated with DMSO? The long day ones all got cancer but the ones with DMSO did not develop cancer and the ones without DMSO got cancer?

So the short days alone or the DMSO in long days was protective of cancer?

Simple language for the simple folks helps better.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-20T10:29:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-rx-substrate-info-thread-for-dmso.5553/post-71805>

No Zorica........leaving bread crumbs forces you to open the door to your third eye. You do not realize the closure of this eye is precisely what got you into to trouble. When a meme is placed in your hardware it can run amok.

Time to begin to think using your endogenous curiosity. When you fight the method it tells me more about your dopamine level than you want to know.

### Glen PDQ — 2013-01-20T16:49:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-rx-substrate-info-thread-for-dmso.5553/post-71882>

> **Zorica Vuletic said**
>
> So the short day mice didin't develop cancer even the ones not treated with DMSO? The long day ones all got cancer but the ones with DMSO did not develop cancer and the ones without DMSO got cancer?
> So the short days alone or the DMSO in long days was protective of cancer?
> Simple language for the simple folks helps better.

All the mice received DMSO either by itself or as a cocktail with the DMBA.
DMSO is an, arguably, harmless solvent used as a carrier in this mouse study. It is sometimes touted as having positive health benefits.
DMBA is the carcinogen the study used..

.................................... SD mice ---> NO cancer
.................................. /
Inject only DMSO -------->
..................................
..................................... LD mice ---> NO cancer

.....................................................SD mice ---> NO cancer, less severe injection site lesions healed faster
.................................................../
Inject DMBA dissolved in DMSO ----->
..................................................
.................................................... LD mice ---> almost 90% got cancer, more severe injection site lesions healed slower

There should have been a 3rd group using the carcinogen without DMSO in my opinion. Otherwise, how do we know DMSO didn't exacerbate effects from DMBA in Long Day mice? There are those who believe that DMSO makes most things more bioavailable whether they be good or bad.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-20T17:00:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-rx-substrate-info-thread-for-dmso.5553/post-71883>

We dont.........but guess what......there is a deep lesson here to learn. When you restrict how you look at a problem to food, macronutrient break downs, the components in a Rx, you lose perspective.

What is the key of all life?

Protons, Neutrons, electrons.

Nothing is more foundational correct?

If you had a very difficult problem to solve would you dive into the the myopia of looking at diet or components or would you consider foundational physics, where it meets chemistry and biology.

Just think about it. To simplify something that has stump really smart folks how would you tackle it as a rookie?

EMF 2 takes the protons, electrons, and neutron approach. The rest is for rookies. I said I would never write a diet book and swear to you here ......... I never will and yet I know after EMF 2 they will pay $$$ to do it.. IT is not about what you eat. It never has been..........and anyone who says otherwise has not thought long enough about life. EMF 2 will prove to you that every thing I said here is 100% correct.

### Glen PDQ — 2013-01-20T17:11:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-rx-substrate-info-thread-for-dmso.5553/post-71887>

I do get that the short day played a significant role.

Long nights mean a body potentially has more time to be in contact with the ground. I guess that slight negative charge is important.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-20T17:15:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-rx-substrate-info-thread-for-dmso.5553/post-71889>

Glen read what I just posted in EMF 1 comments.

I have the biggest truth bomb you can imagine............ready to go.

You need to vote.
