# Thread: Vitamin D question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-question.5621/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Marco — 2013-01-26T07:27:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-question.5621/post-72704>

Maybe an obvious Vitamin D query for the folks here, but this one's been buggin me for a while.

Why supplement vit D in the winter (e.g., now, in the Northern Hemisphere)?

Isn't it more in tune with our evolution to be Vitamin D-poor in the winter, then Vitamin D-replete in the summer?

I can accomplish the latter with plenty of sunbathing in season. (God willing, only a couple more months and the sun is high enough again).

Am supplementing now, but it strikes me as wrong from an evolutionary perspective.

### cantweight — 2013-01-26T23:01:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-question.5621/post-72756>

I agree with your line of thinking...I stopped supplementing D for myself and family last April-Oct as we were outside constantly and swimming with very little sunblock use. I thought our D levels would have been soaring. Major health declines noticed in Sept....labs run...whole family had a massive drop in D levels. Distance from equator, bodies ability to assimilate....whatever the reason we did not make it and suffered for it. Seems necessary to supplement all year round just to keep heads above water so to speak. Levels not attainable naturally....for my clan anyway.

### NittDan — 2013-01-27T00:13:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-question.5621/post-72775>

Truely cold-adapted individuals would not have the same D requirements as those of us who are not there yet.

### Ted_Hutchinson — 2013-01-27T01:20:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-question.5621/post-72785>

We know dark skinned peoples [Traditionally living populations in East Africa have a mean serum 25-hydroxyvitamin D concentration of 115 nmol/l.](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22264449) 46ng/ml and these levels rise during pregnancy and lactation and with age.
Paler skins living further away from the equator where protection from hot sunlight is not required make far more vitamin D3 providing 7 dehydrocholesterol levels are high (7 dehydrocholesterol) is the precursor molecule to cholesterol and providing atmospheric pollution (urban living) is low.
I doubt most people will make as much vitamin d as traditionally living peoples because of the atmospheric pollution and the cultural requirement to wear clothing most of the time when outside.
Most of our modern meat and poultry also spends a lot of it's life indoors and so has a lower vitamin d status than wild caught meat and poultry would have had.
While you may be lucky to obtain raw milk from grassfed cows which have had the sun on their backs the chances are that most here will be using dairy products that won't have the natural amount of vitamin d nor the natural amount of lactoferrin.
Human DNA is set to produce Vitamin D3 from dawn to dusk and melatonin from dusk to dawn.
These natural hormones regulate our immune function.
However well intentioned we may be most of us will not get our 25(OH)D levels up to the levels required to remain vitamin d replete through the winter and consequently will have lower than optimal immune status through the winter if we don't use effective strength supplements.
It may indeed be natural to get colds and flu in the winter but is it sensible when there is a cheap and effective alternative?
It may be natural to get hay fever in spring but why make yourself vulnerable to allergy when you can remain vitamin d replete?

Sure having a low vitamin d status in winter would weed out the weakest members of the community.

Perhaps getting rid of the weakest most feebleminded members of society isn't such a bad idea?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-27T03:28:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-question.5621/post-72817>

If you are trying for a disease reversal and not CTing Vitamin D3 is great for you to use......if you CT like mad you can avoid it. CTing in a lake, river or ocean is the optimal form of CTing as well. I like TED response

### Marco — 2013-01-27T18:32:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-question.5621/post-72879>

Thanks for the responses! Any thoughts on the seasonality angle (human body expects to be lower in Vit D when light is low)? I'm thinking along different lines than cantweight, namely supplement in the summer, even if I'm getting lots of sun (replicate the hours of sun exposure I should have instead of the at most one hour per day I actually do have). Then back off in the winter... little sun, little supplementation... just go Vit D-poor for a few months. Seems like what the body evolved with...

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-01-27T18:42:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-question.5621/post-72883>

According to Dr Mercola's site, the sun has to be 50 degrees or higher to get any vitamin d from the sun. (per previous post from Huck)
[http://aa.usno.navy.mil/data/docs/AltAz.php](http://aa.usno.navy.mil/data/docs/AltAz.php)

### Tanning — 2013-01-29T23:39:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-question.5621/post-73232>

The consensus among modern vitamin D researchers (as I just read in John Cannell's book about vitamin D for athletes) is that the original level of vitamin D for the human being is between 50-60 ng/ml independent of time of the year. Take influenza - why do think it comes only wintertime? Do the viruses have a calendar? No, it is because of vitamin D deficiency. If you want to know the best sources of vitamin D plus a everything else about healthy UV-exposure, here is a good place to start: [Natural sources of vitamin D](http://www.thetanningguru.com/sources-of-vitamin-d/)

# Thread: JUST GROUNDING,  Contrary opinions needed!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-grounding-contrary-opinions-needed.5627/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 96

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2013-01-26T17:51:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-grounding-contrary-opinions-needed.5627/post-72729>

My N-1 for "earthing" is going on about 9 months. However it's in conjunction with all the other epi-paleo stuff. I'm seasonally low carb epi paleo + raw dairy. My lab markers for inflammation are quite low and surprising considering I have excess fat (as of today about 190 lbs at 5'7).

I have not been using anything but nature. For the most part I spend a little time outside each day - with the multiple goals of grounding and bringing natural light cycles to scn, and bringing moonlight to scn.

The reason I've not done "more" on the grounding thing because my thoughts are as such:

1\. Although you can easily measure the ambient voltage changes with ground vs non grounding, I've seen nothing to indicate a zero has any different impact on one than say a 4.0.

My working theory is that this change/variety in voltage is something like excess food energy. In a healthy body that maintains homeostasis, it can easily account for the variances and adjust systems as needed. In some bodies that are dysregulated the negative effects are compounded.

Leading to. . .

2\. Is the excess voltage (shuman res aside for now) in itself harmful, or is it just a symptom of the J-factor (that's the original dysregulation inherent in your system). Will changing voltage do anything to correct for J-factor, or is it like a fat person restricting calories to reduce mass.

I am by no means well educated in the electrical matters - thus I'm looking for contrary opinions or people to point out where I'm wrong!!!

Your post intrigues me, because something still not adding up for how this all works. I'm not so sure I can measure voltage changes with height. I can place the probes of a voltmeter at 2 different vertical positions, and I really don't think there going to measure a potential difference. Honestly, if this were the case, we could stick a rod in the ground and produce a usable current. Talk about easy renewable energy.

What is a J-factor?

I get more confused the more I read about all this stuff. I'm truly trying to understand, but the more I try, the more skeptical I become.

J-Factor = made up term to stand in for the unknown original dysregulation of a system that causes lots of symptoms that are mistaken for "the problem" Example:

Obesity isn't a problem, over fat isn't a problem, there is an underlying problem (jfactor) causing accumulation of fat.

### diane — 2013-01-27T01:43:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-grounding-contrary-opinions-needed.5627/post-72787>

> [Anna K. said:](/goto/post?id=72779)
>
> As for some people's labs getting worse after starting new jobs in windowless buildings, is it possible that it's not the EMF but stress instead? I don't know about them, but most people get stressed while starting a new job and there is a plethora of evidence on how stress affects the hormones. And put anybody into windowless building even without EMFs, it will be stressful on its own. "

Anna - I was one of the people who said I had worse labs and gained weight after starting a new job. I didn't have more stress with the new job - I had less. I hated the job I was in and my job had changed significantly. I had a ND appointment a couple of months after I started, and asked her how I could have gained weight so fast. She didn't have an answer. Believe me, I have gone back and analyzed what it could be, and even before the EMF blog was written, I really felt that it was environmental. Whether it is EMF, lack of light - or a combination of a number of things I don't know, but I can only tell you my experience.

### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-27T03:03:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-grounding-contrary-opinions-needed.5627/post-72801>

I also have questions about the weight of importance of EMFs vs food intake. In my own experience I was sickest when I was most grounded, that is literally had my hands in the ground, whilst sitting on the ground, often being wet, always being damp. In addition I have issues with some of what I perceive is gaps in logic in Jack's argument.

I believe he is right from a clinical perspective in relation to the education he has given in relation to food and the different lab parameters - well certainly much, much better than the usual medical approaches - and I don't want to take away from that. Results trump theory in my book. It is the theoretical rationale that he believes underpins his clinical approach. I am not yet persuaded as he has leaps of faith in his argument, where I need a step by step logic. But as theory is secondary to results I'm not overly bothered emotionally. Intellectually I still question, if that makes sense.

### kathylu — 2013-01-27T04:00:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-grounding-contrary-opinions-needed.5627/post-72824>

I think the points everyone is making are valid. I haven't jumped into the fray because I know very little about this field. So I am taking Jack's ideas as a "jumping off" point and starting to do my own research. I do think excessive EMF's are dangerous. Some simple examples are colony collapse disorder in bees, now traced to man-made EMF's and the recent literature surfacing that cell phones are connected to significant increases in cancer rates, including brain and parotid tumors. Also, "cancer clusters" are appearing around cell and microwave towers.

Also, the way I tend to look at things is, if it helps, why not do it? How can you argue that it is not healthier to try to live with less technology, get better sleep, eat better food, meditate, exercise correctly, fix your hormones, attend to your circadian rhythms and live in tune with the seasons? Many things Jack has said have been spot on, so I'm willing to listen and ponder here, to find what is true.

### Lyndra — 2013-01-27T04:52:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-grounding-contrary-opinions-needed.5627/post-72836>

> [Anna K. said:](/goto/post?id=72826)

>
> Besides, EMF is everywhere, cell phones are too, just because you turn off your Wifi and cell phones doesn't mean you are not surrounded by it.

Since the strength of the field decreases in proportion to the square of the distance from the emitter, those fields you generate are more troublesome IMO than the antenna that is a mile away. So for me, getting rid of the cordless (DECT) phones, microwave, and limiting other sources (WIFI, cell phone powered on) can only be beneficial.

### Inger — 2013-01-27T11:07:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-grounding-contrary-opinions-needed.5627/post-72843>

> [Anna K. said:](/goto/post?id=72826)
>
> Because it could be more trouble then it's worth? I'm sure many people really don't understand how the grounding blankets work, or if they even work, yet buy them. I've yet to see any proof that they work.
>
> Besides, EMF is everywhere, cell phones are too, just because you turn off your Wifi and cell phones doesn't mean you are not surrounded by it.

Anna K, I am with you that we needs to be sceptical about stuff that are to buy out there.. like with anything one can make business of. Better maybe do/build stuff oneself, safe and tested.

About the EMF beeing everywhere. Sure. But the more we get the worse off we are.
It is getting more and more Anna. Stronger and stronger. THAT is the issue. And it helps huge to not have a cellphone close to your skin, wifi.. Iphones..etc. To make sure one is earhted. Every little thing helps. I have an old old Samsung cellphone wich I use very little. I always felt odd using cellphone so I tried to limit it long ago and I also never wanted a Iphone. I just felt it was off.
I am sure our body can handle a tiny bit EMF if we are healthy. But it is not a tiny bit anymore. The amount that surrounds us all the time + the amount we get close to our body through our wifi and stuff. It is way too much.

And then, again. Jack speaks to the broken. The ones that sees no way out. We are all different. If you feel great with what you do now, and want to continue, that is great. But Anna, many are not feeling great these days (even if they do exactly like you). For them it is a blessing to get to know what they can do to change it. We always need to think about the most broken, that is the way we have to look at it. Not from the angle of the healthy ones. Because they already are ok. They need no help. They are the ones that have to carry the broken ones. To help and encourage them to get well again. And then, Anna, the healthy ones might have to step down to the level of the sick to understand and see where they are coming from. Looks like not all are able to do this. That is okay too, but they should be careful not to critisize the ones that have this gift, because they do no favor in doing so but confuse further for the sick ones. In todays world it truly is not easy.. because of all the modern stuff we have created, destroying us.

It also is this thing about.. we might not even realize how it affects us. That is scary too. Like smoking tobacco. You get used to it and you do not realize at all that you get affected. Often we do not see ourselves. I speak from personal experience...
We might not even know what "real health" means. Because we are used to what we are, we have never experienced how it is when everything works like it is designed to do.. We might be able to gain much more, much more! Like stronger intuition, strengthening our 6th sense.. many things.

Jack has a special gift to make us aware. It truly is special. Of course he is human too, not a God that has everything 100% right. But so far I have gained only benefits from what he shines his light on. Absolutely.
This trip is not about ones ego. It is about finding solutions, getting better, gain understanding, having a community to support the not always so easy path. Like Jack says, the health can get destroyed very fast, but getting it back might be very slow.. and require drastic changes.

### Huck — 2013-01-27T14:07:41+0200

She didn't have an answer. Believe me, I have gone back and analyzed what it could be, and even before the EMF blog was written, I really felt that it was environmental. Whether it is EMF, lack of light - or a combination of a number of things I don't know, but I can only tell you my experience.

From September through December, I gained between 10 and 15 lbs and vitamin D, total testosterone, free testosterone, and dhea all dropped significantly. _No change in EMF/RF._

For those of us in northern hemisphere, there was a major drop off in sunlight during that time period. I did a test over the summer involving sunlight. I tried to get a minimum of 2 hours of sun every sunny day. Some days I got 4 or 5 hours. The increase in steroid hormones including vitamin d from Spring to the end of Summer was huge. My weight gain started right around the 1st of September.

Just pointing out that what you and Shijin experienced didn't control for sunlight. I'm not saying your results were definitely caused by a drop in sunlight, but that it is a possibility.

Just my feeling, I have nothing to back it up, but the EMF exposure in the office was one of the differences. Huck

In EMF2 Jack states the following " Cold is experienced in the winter. Food is also generally sparse in winter, because light levels are low and the growing season is shorter as a result. This implies in winter we would face diminished electron flow from foods because of scarcity. It was how form met function and not hormetic in the least bit. **Winter is supposed to cause a serious loss of electrons. Moreover, none of those electrons would be coming from foods that need light to grow. In the tropics, the dry seasons correlated with the cold at higher latitudes so this had the same net effect on what foods were available.**

**It seemed cold was how life preserved the fading charge in their mitochondria due to electron loss for any reason. Then it dawned on me again, we absolutely needed a constant flow of electrons to the mitochondria to maintain all life, healthy no matter what. So where were they?** Then I faced another paradox…….. we only eat 1 – 3 meals a day to get electrons? How did we do it?

WHAT THEY NEVER SAW, AND I REALIZED:

Using Einstein’ math, I thought how did we maintain electron flow 100% of the time to fulfill E = MC2? **I remembered the Schumann resonance and the Earth’s magnetic core. I mentioned this in the last blog. It has an endless supply of electrons come from Earth’s core…… That had to be the answer.** I realized that if we are disconnected from the earth for any reason that electron flow would end. I knew life had to have a plan for disconnection too, because we eat a few meals a day. I knew if the flow stopped time would stop because we would be dead I also realized right there, that if we increase electron flow we could make time stop and reverse too! This seemed counterintuitive at first, but that is what Einstein said would happen. This implied we would age less!"

Reading this - it tells me that the SCN doesn't pay attention to light, during cold - it pays attention to the magnetic resonance from the earth. So controlling for light wouldn't matter - b/c as the body gets cold - the SCN starts seeking the schumann resonance...

as for grounding... If I can get 20-30 mins/day I'm doing good... I've been shooting for more when I pick up Chaos and Havoc from day care... we've been playing in the front yard - rolling on the ground, and playing in the snow... w/in 3 days of doing this suddenly Chaos is going to sleep w/o issues, and waking up at the same time as Havoc - 5:30! Chaos never wakes up before we wake her up...

I will say from my own experience there are places I feel better when I'm connected to the ground... I can name them on one hand...

I think we have to explore all possibilities and question as well. There are also times when data's looking at you straight in the face and you might just have to go with crazy b/c its the only option you've got.

### RayB — 2013-01-27T23:54:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-grounding-contrary-opinions-needed.5627/post-72924>

FWIW, I am a licensed master electrician in my state and and avid electronics hobbyist. I also have been trying to make sense of all of this. This link shows some things: [http://www.groundology.com/videos?show=body-voltage#video](http://www.groundology.com/videos?show=body-voltage#video) If you look at that video it should be apparent that a circuit is completed. A persons EMF (induced AC voltage) will go down when they short themselves out to the ground. So, the circuit becomes complete. One cannot charge a battery or a capacitor with one lead no matter how many electrons are available at the other end of that lead. However, shorting oneself to ground will obviously cancel out induced EMF. Whether or not that is beneficial IMO is yet to be proven but I can see no harm in it. The EMF pollution is all AC (alternating current). The electron flow in our bodies is probably all DC(direct current). I suppose the AC could cause a certain amount of disruption of our circuitry. Here's some anecdotal info: My sister is very involved in ayurvedic medicine and her guru told her that when they do their diagnostic pulse reading on people living in developed areas there is an unexplainable static-like overlay on the pulse. This is something not found on patients living in the boonies. Regarding magnetism and the use of magnetic pads etc.: I still don't get it.
Quoting Aussie Nana "results trump theory". And because of Dr. Kruse's work, the quality of my life has improved tremendously and continues to improve.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2013-01-28T03:07:29+0200

> [shilohman said:](/goto/post?id=72910)

> Am I missing something in this thread? It appears that increased electron flow is being associated with weight loss. Maybe this is true if inflammation is low and our hormone levels are healthy, but increased electron flow by itself does not necessarily imply weight loss. If I am
> wrong let me know, because I must have really missed something.

If you look at the original query on this thread it's not about weight loss, but the relative effect of different levels of voltage.

Example. Ungrounded standing outside you could read 4.0, and when grounded Zero.

The query is for discussion on the difference to our systems from zero to 4.0.

In order to optimize, I would like to find the most efficient way to increase actual electron transfer from earth to human. The most interesting thing in that article to me was the reference to chinese medicine and the spots they say electrons enter the body. . .perhaps that's a clue.

### RayB — 2013-01-28T15:12:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-grounding-contrary-opinions-needed.5627/post-72986>

> [Anna K. said:](/goto/post?id=72967)
>
> RayB, how long does it take for shorting to cancel out induced EMF? wouldn't it be almost immediate?
>
> Also, what do you mean by "induced EMF" ? that me can store electrical charge from EMF?
>
> thanks.

The length of time for shorting to take place is going to be determined by resistance. The more "well grounded" you are the faster the equalization will take place. You can sort of think of induction kinda like hormonics. You know like when you strike one key on a piano other strings resonate with that note. Induction will typically only take place with AC power. Which would be sine wave in the case of your home's electrical circuitry or modulated waves from radio signals. EMF (ElectroMotive Force) is just a fancy word for voltage. Voltage has no power. It is just potential or pressure. It does not mean that there is a current flowing. That article that i cited above also states that it have never been measurably shown that any electrons actually flow when we are grounded. Which if true, would render my first statement incorrect 'cuz resistance is really only a factor when there is a flow of electrons. HA! that's a big GOTME! What we get from the surrounding electromagnetic radiation (EMR) couldn't be like static, 'cuz static is an accumulation of electrons which would/could leave us with an imbalance of electrons which might or might not be beneficial. I say that it can't be like static 'cuz induced EMF is from surrounding AC radiation and since AC is continually changing polarity at various rates and amplitude, it seems to me that any residual charge would be cancelled out immeadiately when the polarity of the radiation changed. Anyway, I gotta get to work. Thanks for the great question and helping me to think this thing through and share my confusion.

### RayB — 2013-01-28T22:13:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-grounding-contrary-opinions-needed.5627/post-73040>

> [Anna K. said:](/goto/post?id=73004)
>
> So if electrons were to flow, this would produce electrical current, which could be measured, but because nobody has been able to measure it, I'd say that they simply don't flow. How is it possible that we get some electrons from the earth if nobody can possibly measure it?

Yep, that would be electrical current. According to that article above nobody has been able to measure it. Is that a fact? IDK Is it possible to get electrons from the earth? I am sort thinking "no". Although, (firmly affix the tinfoil hat at this point...be sure it is grounded) there seems to be some research that would say "breatharians" exist and thrive. Supposedly, these people don't eat and claim to survive on prahna (cosmic energy). Me, I'd die without oysters. They may be mistaken...prahna could be electrons. You can take the hat off now.

The EMR that we experience is a God-awful mess of lots of different frequencies, intensities, proximities etc. If one could read that mess on an ocilloscope it wouldn't make any sense it would just be a bunch of seemingly random lines. If we consider how a microwave works: High frequency waves (read cell phone, portable phone etc.) interact with water molecules (read 90% of our bodies) and because of the dipole configuration of water molecules, with its north end and its south end, the h2o molecules spin and jiggle and get so excited that they get hot.
It seems to me that if we expose ourselves to this even on a minute scale but 24/7, it can't be good for us. I has to disrupt our natural flow of energy. So, if we can negate the potential
of this by grounding ourselves, we must if we are seeking optimal.

I do believe that grounding has it's merits for EMR avoidance. For collecting electrons...not so much.

Cavemam wrote "1. Although you can easily measure the ambient voltage changes with ground vs non grounding, I've seen nothing to indicate a zero has any different impact on one than say a 4.0."

I agree. If we are looking for definitive proof for the impact...I haven't been able to find it either. I can only say intuitively that it should have a positive impact on one's health and that no harm could come from it.

Grounding doesn't have to be elaborate or costly. Could be as simple as a metallic bracelet with a wire connected to the center screw of a grounded outlet's cover plate. I found a soft stainless steel "screen printing" screen that I put on top of my mattress and grounded. So far, my n=1 on this issue has sketchy results. Time will tell.

My cold tub is a mini ocean. Refrigerated, filtered, grounded, and UV sterilized, filled with 75 gallons of artificial sea water (reconstituted from epsom salt and magnesium chloride from the dead sea). I did the grounding and the salt addition at the same time. It seems to be a real gamechanger but I don't know which change should get the credit. My wife and I love it. BTW, haven't changed the water in over 3 months...don't need to.

### RayB — 2013-01-30T00:39:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-grounding-contrary-opinions-needed.5627/post-73243>

**Rethinking my Position**

In previous posts I wrote that I thought there was merit in grounding for EMR abatement but the idea that grounding could collect much needed electrons was a tad dubious for me to accept. All of that is beginning to change now because of an incident that happened earlier today.

As I was preparing to get in my cold tub, I put my hand in the water and I got a shock on the sensitive part of my hand where I was missing some epidermis from a burn. My initial reaction was WTF. I double checked the main cutoff switch and it was off. On the exterior of the tub is an air operated switch that turns off all circuits that the tub uses. For safeties sake, we always use it before entering the tub even though everything is safe and connected to a Ground Fault Circuit. You can never be too careful around water and electricity. Anyway, I pulled out the voltmeter to measure the potential (voltage) between the tub and the ground. I measured 213mv even though the tub was grounded. I knew that the current was being split because of the grounding so I ungrounded the tub and took another measurement. This time it was 470mv. That is nearly Â½ Volt DC. It seems like most everything this forum has been discussing for the past few days has to do with getting a never ending supply of electrons from the ground. To my surprise, the current (electron flow) in this case was flowing from the tub to ground. This whole current flow thing can be very confusing and a lot of that has to do with the nomenclature that was put into place when electricity was first discovered. Since the early days when “conventional flow” was adopted electron flow was always defined as moving from positive (considered and overabundance of electrons) to negative (a deficit of electrons). Conventional flow is still used today. But, in reality, electrons flow from – to +. Since the electrons were flowing away from the tub that means that the potential of the water in the tub is greater that the potential of the terra firma that I live on.

In another life I was an avid marine aquarist and I learned plenty about Redox potential and how it relates to the health of marine animals and invertebrates. I realized that all of the magnesium salts that I had put in my cold tub were very reductive and probably had a very negative potential. The voltmeter readings seemed to verify that.

I then spent several hours measuring everything. While the tub was ungrounded with the negative probe in the water and the positive in my hand +.085v with my rubber shoes on. Barefoot on wet concrete +.26v. With the tub grounded, .05 and .1 respectively. These measurements were repeated many times with the same results and two different voltmeters.

Then I started measuring uA (microamps). Which means electron flow, not just potential. I used a heavy aluminum plate and insulated it from the ground. I used my meter to verify that it was indeed insulated well and that there was no continuity from the plate to the ground. Then I grounded the plate so that I would have an electron path where I could take additional measurements. Again I put the black probe into the water and held the red probe while standing on the grounded aluminum plate with bare wet feet.
The reading of the current coming into my body was about 6 uA. Now I put another probe in the water and held it, removed the meter and reconnected it between the grounding plate and ground. As an electrician I know that all current flow needs to be accounted for. So I figured that current in (6uA) should equal current out. It didn’t! Not only was the tub giving up electrons to the ground but I was contributing about 2 uA. 6 in 8 out hmmm. This was totally unexpected. So I repeated many times, with similar results. I this time I had already spent 45 min. in the tub, had a BAB with shrimp, scallops and oyster. I have been for a while taking three kinds of Mag, Cq10, ALA, D-ribose etc. My wife, on the other hand, loves her some CT but other than that, she’s is not that dedicated. She hadn’t done CT yet today so I tested her. She was the opposite. She was getting 8uA worth of electrons from the tub and send only 6uA to the ground. Her body decided to keep some of the electrons passing through. I had her do the CT in the ungrounded tub with her feet out of the water. After she got comfy and I checked that her life insurance was up to date, I grounded her feet to see if we could charge up her batteries. After 20 min. she was out and we ran the test again. This time she was at equilibrium. About 5uA in and the same out.

When I started thinking of the possible implications of this, I was like DAMN!
Can we have an overabundance of electrons? Would that be good? If we do, would grounding do more harm than good? Would grounding be most beneficial to those that are deficient? Can this be developed further into a science that could actually quantify how needy a person is regarding electrons? By using a standard reference maybe a person’s bodily redox potential could be measured quickly and easily.

Very true, and it is very conductive. But current is going to take the path of least resistance and if you are swimming you ain't in that path. ### RayB — 2013-01-30T06:32:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-grounding-contrary-opinions-needed.5627/post-73307>

> [Anna K. said:](/goto/post?id=73294)
>
> Ray, when you are measuring potential (voltage), could it be that you are just measuring the surface potential of your body, or static charge, not the internal potential?
>
> Also,"The human body is an electrical conductor, full of salty fluids. Like any conductor, the human body has a capacitance, meaning that it stores electrical energy, with respect to its surroundings, such as the floor, the walls, or other people. Like a person's height or weight, a person's capacitance is one of his or her body's attributes, including it's posture, its relative position, and its proximity to other electrically conducting things."

I didn't take any voltage measurements of our bodies. The voltage measurements were of the tubs water in relation to ground. Also, voltage measurements were taken of the water to ground through various resistances of the body. ie. with shoes and without. The higher resistance provided by wearing shoes recorded a lower potential from tub to ground, as expected. The human body does have capacitance and it seems as though my potential was higher than the ground and my capacitor was dumping. My wifes capacitance was in need of charging. That article you posted said that the earths charge is surprisingly close to neutral. I have been under the impression that it is just a big capacitor bursting at the seams with excess electrons.

### TheKid — 2013-01-31T19:36:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-grounding-contrary-opinions-needed.5627/post-73483>

My own N=1 on grounding is that I saw what I believe to be an immediate change.

For a long time my sleep has been broken. I toss, turn, and have multiple micro-wakes (as measured by Zeo). Additionally, I would almost always awake to use the bathroom somewhere between 3:15 AM and 4:30 AM. That bathroom trip would be a zombie-like stumble. Upon returning to bed, I would toss and turn awfully, eventually fall back asleep and then awake around 8:00 AM.

The very first night I slept on my grounding sheet, I instead awoke at around 5:45 AM. I had to use the bathroom, but i was fully coherent in doing so. I also did not feel the need to go back to bed and was able to start my day. The next two days saw the same pattern hold, with an overall better feeling of rest upon waking.

As they say in the literature, the sicker you are, the more noticeable the results may be. And since I am quite ill, I am not surprised by this. I am hoping this continues.

It seems like it couldn't be, or you could just as well put aluminum foil under your regular cotton sheets and they wouldn't say not to put the pad under your current sheet. (Still, I would imagine that normal body condensation would allow conductivity through a cotton sheet.) I've been trying to imagine in my head what it looks like, but haven't yet succeeded.

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-31T20:33:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-grounding-contrary-opinions-needed.5627/post-73505>

OK so I used an earthing sheet on our bed for two nights...first night had a good sleep, my husband did not and the second night we both slept really badly. In my consult this am Dr. K said he does not advocate grounding sheets, mats etc. He is making a big distinction between earthing products and grounding naturally. Grounding through your house ground wires can mess you up. Our plan is to try the rod outside in the earth and see if there is any difference, but I'm very clear on my initial reaction to the mats....yuck. I'm going to try to do it all outside naturally or if we sleep well tonight, I will continue to use the rod and wire. The issue around grounding into your house system is exposure to dirty electricity, not the principal of being connected to the earth's field.

As for magnets...that's where we should be putting our money.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-01-31T22:30:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-grounding-contrary-opinions-needed.5627/post-73527>

Line conditioners are used on computer equipment. --> Automatic voltage regulation for protection against brownouts and overvoltages.

[http://www.apc.com/products/resource/include/techspec\_index.cfm?base\_sku=LE300&tab=documentation](http://www.apc.com/products/resource/include/techspec_index.cfm?base_sku=LE300&tab=documentation) (open PDF file on this webpage for product specs)

In theory, a line conditioner should filter out "dirty electricity". The question is how sensitive is the human body to minor variations in power? ... IMHO anybody using a grounding sheet or band that is using a power outlet should consider adding a line conditioner to the mix.

### freesia — 2013-01-31T22:33:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-grounding-contrary-opinions-needed.5627/post-73528>

I went bare footed at a campsite several days last week and plenty of my time was spent in a river or on dewy grass. We even left the screen in the end of the tent open so I could keep my bare feet on the damp earth when I was "inside". I've also waded in the ocean 3 days out of the past week.
...I've noticed no negative effects. I find it energising...but just subtly so.

Grounding in an icebath via the bath taps, which connect to earth via copper pipes seems stimulating, but I notice no obvious negative effects there either. (The water feels colder that's all....I guess this could be considered a negative effect? Three people in my family have reported that same feeling.)

However, grounding through the power outlets slams me! I tried all night the first night and I woke with swollen glands in my neck. I cut it back to an hour a day, but I have been trying to figure out what's bothering me about it vs "real" grounding. So, I think I'm understanding "dirty electricity" could be a player.
Next week's mission is to try an figure out how we measure it and what we do about it! Wonder if this is covered in the EMF Rx?

PS, Ray your bath sounds amazing!

### endless — 2013-01-31T22:55:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-grounding-contrary-opinions-needed.5627/post-73534>

I used my earthing stuff for the first time last night...I bought a ground rod and wire and a set of conductive straps to use instead of a mat or sheet. I have been suffering from acute neck and back pain for several weeks that has been resistant to all treatment (chiropractic, acupuncture, massage, ct, etc. etc...) and every night I wake frequently with sciatic pain, nerve pain in my arms and hands, neck pain and numb fingers. I put a wrist strap on, grounded with the grounding rod outside, and had the first night in WEEKS with no pain or numbness. I feel like I lost a lot of inflammation overnight. I'm convinced! I did decide to go with the ground rod instead of the plug-in stuff...just seems more like a direct connection.

Yes I've had them since I was 10. My dad has them as well, so not only do I have the messed up Pg:E2 ratio, I also have the epigenetic link as well.... They were getting better w/BHRT -but with the recent backward slide hormonally - I've taken a step backward. I'm trying to get Dzugan to increase my pg but he wants me to change from mg Threonate, orotate, and malate, to citrate and reduce my dose.... I'm not doing that but I've told him I would... this migraine is a bit different b/c its not so much painful but I've got the nausea, sensitivity to light/sound/smells and constant aura. I've had to teach myself how to function w/them b/c have 3-4/month at 2-3days/event - in the past I didn't have the sick leave or vacation to take off to deal w/them.

the mitochondrial Rx takes the pain away - just nothing else... DH lets me rest when I can, and I try to get to bed to get some sleep - my problem right now is that with my Pg being so low - I'm not sleeping at night and that just feeds the migraine ... whats interesting is the drugs that used to work no longer do.. its wild. so even If I saw a neurologist - I don't respond to the drugs - which is probably why Dzugan is having a hard time w/me b/c I'm not responding likes he thinks I should... I've told him time and again - I don't respond like normal ppl... and that I'm not the easy fix he thinks I am.... I'm updating him daily based upon how I'm feeling, which is what he asked me to do... I'm hoping to get some sleep tonight...

It all comes down to the fact I get things done is b/c I don't really have any other choices. Fortunately DH is a full partner in life, w/o him I'd be lost.

### nonchalant — 2013-02-19T17:38:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-grounding-contrary-opinions-needed.5627/post-76481>

I think we need an EMF/Grounding sub-forum. Yesterday I had the day off, but had few plans, and I canceled the ones I had. I just read, took in some sun, etc all day. It was nice, haven't done that in a while. For hours, while I read or was on the computer, I was in contact with my grounding mat, which is still plugged into the wall outlet. When I got some sun, either my feet were on the concrete, or I sat directly on the driveway. I touched some tall trees.

Last night, grounded as I have been for a couple weeks, I ... Slept Like A Log! I can't attribute this to a stress-less day, because my job, as I have recently been able to maneuver it, is usually low stress, and it's at home, no longer at a datacenter. All I can think of is the extra time grounding.

I want!

I'll have to be more conscientious about it. Buy another mat, I suppose, for the other room. I have another solution on order for the bed, which should be more comfortable and less awkward. I've been feeling the tingling in my feet lately, and it's not just while on the mat. I can be on the ground, and if it is wet, I feel it. This weekend I took a hand trowel out with me to dig a small hole for my feet to stand in, and that felt Soooo Nice! Cool, damp, and tingly!

Perhaps I'm being hasty, jumping to conclusions, but this grounding stuff seems to be making me happy.
Oh, and ... Oxytocin promotion can be ... extra nice while grounded!

> Last night, grounded as I have been for a couple weeks, I ... Slept Like A Log! I can't attribute this to a stress-less day, because my job, as I have recently been able to maneuver it, is usually low stress, and it's at home, no longer at a datacenter. All I can think of is the extra time grounding.
>
> I want!
>
> I'll have to be more conscientious about it. Buy another mat, I suppose, for the other room. I have another solution on order for the bed, which should be more comfortable and less awkward. I've been feeling the tingling in my feet lately, and it's not just while on the mat. I can be on the ground, and if it is wet, I feel it. This weekend I took a hand trowel out with me to dig a small hole for my feet to stand in, and that felt Soooo Nice! Cool, damp, and tingly!
>
> Perhaps I'm being hasty, jumping to conclusions, but this grounding stuff seems to be making me happy.
> Oh, and ... Oxytocin promotion can be ... extra nice while grounded!

I've been entertaining the idea of a grounding mat for work. I think I need this! I'm glad to hear you are having success.

### diane — 2013-02-19T20:42:22+0200

I slept with the band on, but think I was too conscious of rolling over and wrapping myself in the cord, so am going to do the mat in bed. Have the band on at work today to see if it does anything. I'm surrounded by fake light and computers all day so I am looking for at least a short term aid for now. ### Chrysanthemama — 2013-02-20T06:00:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-grounding-contrary-opinions-needed.5627/post-76625>

I've been doing my own experimenting with grounding. Beginning around Dec 18 I started grounding again, after a 2 month hiatus. (Because of the snow I had stopped being outside on the rocks regularly. Once I realized the errors of my ways, I hopped back onto the grounding boat!) I had been taking cold baths regularly, along with swimming, cold showers, and lots of cold-weather exposure. The baths were very cold (between 40 to 45 degrees F), but they never satisfied me. Then I tried snow CT and I was super excited. Because of the MovNat workshop, I knew I needed to up my CT, so I was outside, in the cold, in the snow, and doing some cold baths as well. Basically, I was just relishing as much cold as possible, whenever I could get it. During the MovNat workshop, all our CT was in natural environments. After the workshop, the first thing I did was find a location outside where I could continue my CT without too long of a drive or hike/run. (Most natural water is frozen right now, or farther away than I can drive easily for lunch.) Thankfully, I found my spot. So, now I've been grounding while doing CT, as most people doing CT outside in lakes, rivers, etc., are doing. Now, it is very hard for me to even consider CT in my bathtub. I'd rather hike or run the extra mileage to get to the natural water. I leave these CT sessions feeling powerful, alive, exhilarated, light (even with frozen feet), and fast. Those of you who are doing CT outside in the natural environment, grounding at the same time, how are you feeling? I think there is a very powerful connection with CT and grounding. I'm finding that this is affecting my intuition even more. Today, I planned a run into another prime natural CT location. I headed out, but the wind was blowing really hard. I started feeling really unsettled. I suddenly felt compelled to turn around, so I did. I ended up finding a really amazing rock to lie down on for a while. I took off my shoes, rested on the rock, and watched the pre-storm clouds rush by overhead. I was thankful I listened to my intuition, but I definitely pondered the fact that I think that CT AND grounding contributed to overwhelming feeling. Curious if others are having similar experiences.

### caroline — 2013-02-20T09:24:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-grounding-contrary-opinions-needed.5627/post-76636>

I completely agree - I can't be without going to the ocean everyday. It isn't cold enough but it will have to do for now. I feel such complete contentment. I stand on the rocks and I feel so alive and exhilarated.

Nice post, Cindy. I hope you are right. I've experienced some of this colander effect on my own allergies. After avoiding wheat and regular dairy, I find that I am still allergic to the same things (like tree pollen), but the reaction is really muted. Sniffles usually. No asthma, no sinus infections, ear infections, etc. Grounding should be one way of letting water out of the colander. **Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrons-health-emf1-2-already-discussed-in-book-titled-forbidden-healing.5634/post-72814>

.
The information on electrons and E=MC2 is identical to what T.C. Randall wrote about sometime ago in his book called "Forbidden Healing"
This information has been out there for sometime now. The medical community won't listen to it - there's no money it it.

See for yourself - here is a paragraph from the book:

"Life at its core is a phenomenon that requires energy in the form of electrons to operate and to keep its structures intact. Living cells are internal combustion engines that must have the right fuel and lots of oxygen to produce energy. Visualize lifeâ€™s molecules snatching/donating electrons, electrons powering cells, electrons traveling through nerves and across fascia. When electron energy is abundant, oxygen is abundant, immunity is strong and there is health, healing and celebration."

T.C. Randall goes into the same subject matter as EMF 1 & 2.... He's been researching this field for sometime now. Really amazing information on how we can heal ourselves. Interesting how EMF 1/2 and his book overlap with similar information. This isn't new information.

Here's another great paragraph from T.C. Randall's book:

Electrons and electro-chemistry are not quackery or spooky New-Age energy medicine (yet frequency of molecular vibration, biofields, biophotons and energy waves are involved on other levels). We are talking about the electron states of lifeâ€™s molecules themselves, + or â€“ , and the fact that like boys and girls, it is the gender and strength of charge that determines molecular relationships. Molecules with available electrons donate to or â€œmarryâ€ those with electron-hungry positive charges so energy in the form of electrons is constantly being transferred and exchanged; shared in bonds, taken by oxidants, given by antioxidantsâ€¦all to make your biochemistry work and cells function. The molecular world is locked in an endless game of â€œelectron grab-ass!â€

The charge of â€œsea-waterâ€ that lifeâ€™s molecules swim in constitutes the matrix of your cell-filled aquarium and by starting there the energy of life can be raised to maintain health or restore it. An aquarium could be acidic and exhibit weak properties of polarity and charge and gobble oxygenâ€¦ or be slightly alkaline and contain an oxygen-conducive electric potential (ORP, Oxidation Reduction Potential). Electric potential relies on optimum levels of electrolytes and pH buffers (such as bicarbonate, potassium, magnesium, sodium, chloride and calcium) that when in balance hold and enhance charge.

Charge and charge reversals activate or deactivate DNA function so various enzymes get transcripted or not. Even the building blocks of those enzymes assemble according to the charge terrain within the cell. Everything takes energy, electrons.

I've enjoyed the book as it's full of research based evidence into the inner-workings of humans.

### paleo-bunny — 2013-01-27T23:50:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrons-health-emf1-2-already-discussed-in-book-titled-forbidden-healing.5634/post-72921>

My personal experience is that EMFs increase release of histamine from my mast cells.

I have read that air-con in office environments that dehumidifies the air in combination with computing equipment, which releases positive ions, exacerbates electrosensitivity by helping keep common allergenic or irritant molecules airborne, as these are mostly negatively charged. This explains some of my symptoms.

I also have reason to believe that an extremely unnaturally altered electrical environment interferes with the central nervous system as well as the immune system. I have very thin skin and pick up electrical charge very easily. I blow lightbulbs and trip switches much more often than most people, and kill electrical watches on a regular basis.

I was extremely electrosensitive before I had my mercury amalgam fillings replaced. Just a few minutes on a USB soft-phone would set off chronic cattarh and lowering of blood pressure via a mercury/histamine release bomb.

These symptoms peak at around 3 pm when I am in an environment with lots of electrical equipment and/or air con (aeroplanes can be just as bad as offices).

Fortunately these symptoms are much reduced and rarely problematic, now that I eat a lowish histamine diet eating lots of quercetin and have radically reduced my exposure to mercury.

However, I am fascinated about EMF 1 and 2 - as am curious as to whether they might have a bearing on my experiences.

# Thread: Ultimate grounding experience
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ultimate-grounding-experience.5637/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ruby — 2013-01-27T05:39:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ultimate-grounding-experience.5637/post-72839>

"One group in Russia has come up with an unusual method to fight stress and beat depression by burying people alive â€“ only temporarily, though.
The organizers of this extreme treatment claim twenty minutes in a fresh grave might change your mind on many things in life.
When someone is buried alive, below the surface they are trapped with their greatest fears.
â€œOh fantastic! The sun feels much better than under the ground,â€ admits volunteer Pavel Gordeev once he has been dug out after 20 minutes of being buried alive: not a form of torture, but extreme therapy.
After his first time under the ground, Pavel's anxiety got the better of him. Now he says he can handle the pressure.
â€œThe first thing you experience is panic. Once your face is covered with earth, you start tasting it and thinking what the hell am I doing down here? But once you calm down there is simply no other place like this,â€ Gordeyev said.
The grave-digging therapists are modeling the burials on an ancient form of self-enlightenment practiced by shamans.
They wanted to make the rites more accessible, believing everyone can benefit.
â€œThis is the most effective and powerful method for overcoming internal problems,â€ claims Konstantin Mukhin, trainer at Enlightenment Territory. â€œA person can neither see nor hear anything, nor even move underground. They have no other option but to delve deep inside their minds.â€
One burial costs you around $160, and attracts both men and women from students to 50-something professionals, all aiming to bury their own worries.
A full day's psychological training claims to prepare them for the fears they will face.
â€œPanic conditions can be subconscious and uncontrollable. There are different ways to overcome them: working with mental images, rhythm, breathing,â€ explains the director of Enlightenment Territory Andrey Gorbov. â€œThere is an array of means for a person to subdue their fear. We teach them that.â€
The maximum burial is 40 minutes; beyond that the mind struggles to cope with the lack of physical function. The depth of burial is a mere 30 centimeters. Any deeper and the pressure of soil would be too great to stand.
Once under the dirt, volunteers breathe through a tube.
The organizer is a ten-year veteran of burials, and says this shouldn't be tried at home.
â€œWe have to be able to get the person out very fast and also contact them. Earth has to be distributed in a certain way across the body so that the key joints aren't pressurized. You can't practice burials without knowing about these things,â€ Andrey Gorbov warns.
Even if you have never suffered from claustrophobia, that could easily change if you are going to try this ritual out for yourself. You will be given a thick plastic tube to breathe through, with which they monitor you at all times. If you want to stop then all you have to do is make a noise.
You are unable to move your limbs because of the weight of the earth, and despite being securely enveloped in waterproof fabric, it feels very cold and only adds to the tension.
Such burials might seem an extreme pastime, but they are positively tame when compared to other death-defying stunts like bungee jumping or white water rafting."

....................................................

**Dangers Of Hair Dryers**

Leading scientists from both the USA and Japan had independently confirmed that both men and women can develop breast cancer when they are exposed to ELF MFs (extremely low frequency magnetic fields). Men were also able to develop testicular cancer resulting in Castration.

Leading Scientists from the USA discovered the linkage of children’s leukemia to regular hair dryers.

In 1990 the US EPA (Environmental Protection Agency) stunned the country by classifying the ELF MFs from a hair dryer as a human carcinogen. Its report, “An Evaluation of Potential Carcinogenicity of Electromagnetic Fields,” is identified as the cancer report. These findings of the health hazards from the ELF MFs by the EPA led the US Congress to initiate the EMF RAPID program at a cost of more than $60 million dollars. The purpose of this program was to assess the health hazards of ELF MFs to humans. Scientists from all over the world worked under this program and their assessments were documented in the US Government report, NIH98-3981 August 1998. This assessment included the health hazards from home appliances. One of the appliances identified in this document as a health hazard was the regular hair dryer. The linkage of leukemia to regular hair dryers was reported on page 180 of this report. It is stated on this page that the risks of leukemia from the regular hair dryers was discovered by Dr. Stephanie London, who studied 232 cases of children’s leukemia in Los Angeles County, CA.

The NCI (National Cancer Institute) also investigated the health hazards to children from hair dryers. They investigated 2530 cases of children’s leukemia within nine states (Ohio, Wisconsin, Minnesota, Illinois, Indiana, Iowa, Michigan, New Jersey and Pennsylvania). On the basis of this study, they concluded that children had an elevated risk of leukemia from regular hair dryers.

Based on the work of Dr. Henry Lai from the University of Washington at Seattle, Washington. He and the members of his team exposed rats to ELF MFs at 100mG (milligauss). The test showed that the brain cell DNA of rats can be damaged by exposing their brains to magnetic fields at 100mG. The test result on rats was extended to people, stating that human brain cell DNA damage will also occur when they are exposed to the ELF MFs at 100mG. The hair dryer was identified as a source of such high EMFs.

1.EPA staff classifies ELF MFs as “probable human carcinogens” (MWN, May/June, 1990).

### BJK77 — 2013-01-27T19:01:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/explain-to-me-how-emf-effects-glucose-metabolism.5642/post-72888>

I've read Shijin's and diane's posts on how they believe their indoor office environments are negatively impacting their labs and it really has me thinking. I have long thought that my work environment was bad for me and I had signs, but couldn't pinpoint exactly what was causing the problems. Thankfully I work from home 2 days a week, so I'm only in the office 3 days, but every single time I have an extended period of time away from the office I notice I feel so much better, have more energy, don't swell/gain weight, can eat anything (epi-paleo, of course) without repercussions.

I was off for 2 weeks around Christmas and could eat as many veggies as I wanted without obviously going totally overboard. As soon as I go back to work, I swear I gain weight just looking at a veggie. And things get progressively worse as the week goes on. By my third day in the office, my clothes are tight and I'm feeling awful. I'm always home Friday, Saturday and Sunday and by late Sunday I finally start feeling better . . . only to have the vicious cycle start all over again!

I thought there might be mold in the office, so I actually had it tested, but no dice! I'm leaning toward EMF being the problem. I've read anecdotes of how it inadvertently affects glucose metabolism, but I need to understand this in layman's terms . . . can anyone help?

On that note, I have histamine disregulation (high histamine symptoms, e.g. no detectable dental decay plus a sensitivity to mercury - I had 7 mercury fillings that I never needed which were put in by a corrupt NHS dentist when I was a child and my energy levels have gone stratospheric since I had them replaced a few years ago). Hi diane.

There is a wealth of information here.

[http://www.hypermobility.org/forum/index.php](http://www.hypermobility.org/forum/index.php)

Symptoms include an autonomic system that is slow to respond to changes in the environment, e.g. overheating and feeling the cold more than most. Also orthostatic hypotension. And mast cells releasing histamine very readily - very warm hands, and redness in the extremities (hands and ears), and attacks of low blood pressure. Also impaired proprioception especially in the hands (my hands, knees and ankles are the most adversely affected joints). These symptoms all relate to collagen disorders and other connective tissue disorders.

I've experienced increased discomfort and spraining in my fingers due to increased use of technology. Buttons exacerbate my symptoms (e.g. electric windows on car, chip and pin when I pay by debit card in a store).

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-30T01:35:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/explain-to-me-how-emf-effects-glucose-metabolism.5642/post-73258>

The CP system of ATP recycling is the fastest system humans have........we covered it in detail in the January webinar. This system is used only for very short durations of up to 3-10 seconds. The ATP-PC system neither uses oxygen nor produces lactic acid if oxygen is unavailable and is thus said to be alactic anaerobic. This is the primary system behind very short, powerful movements like a golf swing, a 100 m sprint or powerlifting. However, the usefulness of the ATP-PC system lies in the rapid availability of energy rather than quantity. Its rate limiting enzyme guanidinoacetate, requires methylation to work well. The methylation of guanidinoacetate to form creatine **consumes more methyl groups than all other methylation reactions combined in the human body**. The rate limiting step for the brain and muscle is the integrity of the methylation system and not creatine levels. This is where looking deeper matters more, because if you use this pathway to recycle ATP you deplete your methyl groups........this is where fibromyalgia patients and people with collagen vascular disease usually live. They never understand why they are poor methylators......you dont need the 677 or the 1298 to be a bad methylator. If you are energy inefficient you also become a poor methylator.

I have been taking 5 gm of d-ribose before walks and about 15 gm after a long walk or weightlifting. I wouldn't go crazy with it in huge quantities all at once. If you take more than you can metabolize you end up with a lot of ribose in your blood and I found research indicating it glycates proteins even worse than glucose does. But in the small quantities people are taking it I don't see much toxicity from what I can read.

No question the ribose is feeding something (maybe mitochondria?) that are not being fed well by glucose. It simply energizes me in a different way. It stabilized my weight loss. It seems to help me with exercise recovery greatly.

I hesitate to take large quantities of it without exercise simply because I'm insulin resistant, and I guess whatever is stopping glucose from metabolizing correctly could also - in theory - prevent ribose from metabolizing well. But there is no way to measure ribose levels in your blood, so I figure taking after exercise helps me to bypass insulin resistance and just feeds the ribose straight to hungry muscles and cells. So far I am pretty thrilled by it.

### persistence — 2014-03-19T21:13:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/explain-to-me-how-emf-effects-glucose-metabolism.5642/post-116489>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=116364)
>
> So glad the ribose is helping.. and that you have changed your habits to the better
>
> I think ribose lowers blood sugar.. so if you have high blood sugar isues it should be.. a good thing? I am no expert here but that is what I have read...

Inger, studies show that at around 10 grams of ribose, there is a brief hypoglycemic effect at between 45 and 60 minutes after ingestion. Five grams of ribose does not have much effect. Some studies claim this is explained by raised insulin levels alone. Other studies say that the insulin by itself cannot explain the effect and the action is unexplained. But this drop in sugar quickly resolves and you go back to glucose baseline. When I have high glucose in the morning, I have not seen ribose make much of a dent in that. But if I combine it with exercise I can get quite a big dip.

Think of ribose and glucose as two different sugars, and each of them can be measured in the blood. It would be possible for either of them to be high while the other is low. It would be possible for both of them to be high at the same time. So I imagine if you dumped 50 grams of ribose into an insulin resistant body, without any exercise, that you could end up with a quite-high level of ribose in your blood, not metabolizing quickly. Since ribose glycates protein even more rapidly than glucose does, that would be a very bad place to be.

So, I view ribose the way I view a lot of things: there is an amount that is incredibly beneficial. There is an amount that is toxic. You try to stay way clear of possible toxicity, and get enough to get the benefits. Since I don't have any device to measure levels of ribose in the blood, I tend to be cautious about it.

I'm really just at the start learning about ribose and its applications, but for me it is doing some spectacular things and this early success is driving a desire to learn as much about it as I can.

> The CP system of ATP recycling is the fastest system humans have........we covered it in detail in the January webinar. This system is used only for very short durations of up to 3-10 seconds. The ATP-PC system neither uses oxygen nor produces lactic acid if oxygen is unavailable and is thus said to be alactic anaerobic. This is the primary system behind very short, powerful movements like a golf swing, a 100 m sprint or powerlifting. However, the usefulness of the ATP-PC system lies in the rapid availability of energy rather than quantity. Its rate limiting enzyme guanidinoacetate, requires methylation to work well. The methylation of guanidinoacetate to form creatine **consumes more methyl groups than all other methylation reactions combined in the human body**. The rate limiting step for the brain and muscle is the integrity of the methylation system and not creatine levels. This is where looking deeper matters more, because if you use this pathway to recycle ATP you deplete your methyl groups........this is where fibromyalgia patients and people with collagen vascular disease usually live. They never understand why they are poor methylators......you dont need the 677 or the 1298 to be a bad methylator. If you are energy inefficient you also become a poor methylator.

I will check out that webinar.

The body has several ways to make ATP (the currency of energy in the body - broadly speaking). Most centralized doctors were taught this in school that ATP is the main energy currency of the body. What Jack has taught us is that the body stores energy in many other ways (gradients, energy flows, etc.).

The body has 3 main pathways for making ATP (biochemical pathways - level 1 of Jack's 7 energy levels... think biochemistry):
(1) CP (Creatine Phosphate) pathway: This is the fastest pathway. It doesn't require oxygen. The drawback is it uses **methyl groups**.
(2) Glycolysis: This is the second fastest pathway. It also doesn't require oxygen. **Most chronic diseases involve cells that primarily use this pathway** for making ATP instead of the TCA cycle (see below).
(3) TCA Cycle (also called oxidative phosphorylation) + ECT (electron chain transport): This is the slowest pathway BUT it generates the MOST ATP by producing metabolites that feed ETC to generate the most ATP. It also requires oxygen. **This is also why we breathe oxygen so we can use THIS pathway.** This pathway is usually broken because of a combination of too much nnEMF/blue light and not enough sunlight. **This is also the pathway where DDW is made.** Centralized doctors don't know this.

When we overuse the CP pathway, we use up our bank of methyl groups, so there isn't enough methyl groups for maintenance of muscle/brain tissue. Methyl groups are also important in normal function of turning on/off genes. Jack has discussed this extensively in the blogs.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-16T18:06:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/explain-to-me-how-emf-effects-glucose-metabolism.5642/post-356417>

Answer Woodstock: You are not stringing the lessons in the blogs together at all.........so you keep thinking the wrong way.

Did you know, these facts about figs might ruin your appetite:

Figs are technically not fruit– they're actually inverted flowers.

They require a specific kind of pollination that can only come from fig wasps, which are only 1.5mm in size.

Fig wasps have to DIE INSIDE the fruit in order for the fig to mature, since figs cannot be pollinated by wind or bees.

A pregnant fig wasp squeezes into a fig through a tiny hole at the bottom. She lays her eggs in the fig and then dies.

When her eggs hatch, the male wasps mate with the female wasps. The male wasps then dig an exit hole, leave the fig, and die.

The female wasps, already pregnant, leave the fig and find a new fig in which to lay their eggs and repeat the cycle.

Contrary to popular belief, ripe figs are not full of dead wasps and the "crunchy bits" in the fruit are only seeds.

The fig actually produces an enzyme called ficain (also known as ficin) which digests the dead wasp and the fig absorbs the nutrients to create the ripe fruits and seeds.

Why would nature do this?

Nature designed figs this way due to:

**GOE-Driven Redox Optimization:** The inverted structure protects internal flowers from oxidative stress, using the wasp’s death to recycle nutrients (via ficain) into a redox-rich environment, akin to peroxisomal ROS sinks maintaining mitochondrial health. This echoes the GOE’s demand for ROS management, repurposing death for life.

**Post-KT Specialization:** After the KT event, figs evolved a niche-specific strategy, relying on fig wasps (survivors of mass extinction) rather than generalist pollinators, ensuring reproductive isolation and resilience in a disrupted ecosystem.

**UPE and Light Signaling:** The enclosed fig amplifies UPEs from metabolic activity (e.g., wasp digestion), guiding wasps via quantum light cues, similar to how UV-A modulates retinal signaling. The backward design maximizes internal energy transfer, paralleling the retina’s photon capture efficiency despite its layout.

Like the retina, where photoreceptors face away from light but optimize oxygen and nutrient delivery, figs invert flowers to prioritize nutrient recycling and redox stability over external aesthetics. Both designs reflect nature’s willingness to embrace counterintuitive efficiency, driven by GOE and KT pressures.

WHY oxygen therapies are deadly dangerous, Sleep apnea is exploding and why oxygen is causing a holocaust of disease now?

The thesis shines here: breath holds and UV-A enhance this ancient adaptation, countering blue light and nnEMF assaults, teaching us that longevity lies in protecting nature’s quirky designs, much like the fig teaches us to see beyond the surface.

Time to upgrade your thinking...........

### digitald3 — 2025-07-17T17:02:05+0300

[@Woodstock](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/32840/) I have a good friend that also gets really frustrated trying to understand the science.. and I personally have along way to go... but this is how I see the Breath Holds and UVA light..

The wasp inside the flower is protected by the flower as a way to deal with the oxygen derived stress.. this is also called ROS or Reactive Oxygen Species build up.The flower gets rid of of extra oxygen so the the mitochondria can work as intended.. when there is too much oxygen build up they don't work well.. the breath holds allow the extra ROS buildup to clear.. I know it sounds counter intuitive but poorly running hypoxic mitochondria actually have too much oxygen built up around them in the form of Reactive Oxygen Species..

so the breath holds help clear the "oxidative stress(ROS)" or extra oxygen and since the ROS create their own quantum light signals, or Ultraweak Photon Emission and the mitochondria create their own UPE's too.. these light signals need to be aligned like a lock and key or else that creates problems.. so UVA light helps organize these light signals once the ROS is cleared from the breath holds.. to enhance energy transfer

Yes, they are... generally.

perplexity: "Mixed Mating: In some species, if more than one female (foundress) lays eggs in the same fig, offspring from different mothers may mate, introducing an element of outbreeding. However, sibmating (mating among siblings) is still very common, especially in figs with a single foundress."

There's nothing quite like insect sexuality.

Then again it makes you wonder also about bacterial and maybe even mitochondrial (?) reproduction.
For what I understand heteroplasmy is the result of changes to plasmids = DNA inside the mitochondria.
If mitochondira are foud travelling through the blood stream, could these be badasses sayin:

# Thread: Ashton Kutcher Was Hospitalized After Adopting "Fruitarian Diet" for Steve Jobs Movie
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ashton-kutcher-was-hospitalized-after-adopting-fruitarian-diet-for-steve-jobs-movie.5650/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ATL_Paleo — 2013-01-28T03:43:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ashton-kutcher-was-hospitalized-after-adopting-fruitarian-diet-for-steve-jobs-movie.5650/post-72954>

[http://movies.yahoo.com/news/ashton...rian-diet-steve-jobs-200000665-us-weekly.html](http://movies.yahoo.com/news/ashton-kutcher-hospitalized-adopting-fruitarian-diet-steve-jobs-200000665-us-weekly.html)

Ashton Kutcher definitely suffered for his art. To realistically channel the late Apple Inc. founder Steve Jobs in biopic jOBS, the actor, 34, went to extreme lengths: Watching "hundreds of hours" of footage, scouring the visionary's speeches, interviewing Jobs' friends . . . and even copying his "fruitarian diet," Kutcher told the Associated Press at the Sundance Film Festival premiere of the film in Park City, Utah on Friday Jan. 25.

The choice to go "fruitarian" -- consuming only fruits, nuts and seeds and no animal products, vegetables or grains -- "can lead to some serious problems," Kutcher revealed. Indeed, as a result, Kutcher "ended up in the hospital two days before we started shooting the movie," he said.

"I was like doubled over in pain, and my pancreas levels were completely out of whack, which was completely terrifying, considering everything," said the That 70s Show alum. (Jobs died in October 2011 at age 56 of pancreatic cancer.)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-28T16:32:40+0200

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-31T03:16:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ashton-kutcher-was-hospitalized-after-adopting-fruitarian-diet-for-steve-jobs-movie.5650/post-73411>

Well now Kutcher might always have a lingering pancreatic problem just from this one incident. Like he lit a match....good luck to him to not have bad consequences...unless he adopts epi-paleo or even regular paleo now and sticks with it for life. Hope he didn't do too much damage.

### ashryn — 2013-01-31T12:21:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ashton-kutcher-was-hospitalized-after-adopting-fruitarian-diet-for-steve-jobs-movie.5650/post-73444>

An interesting opportunity to start some conversations about the dangers of fructose and sugars in general though.. That was what got me started on this pathway, and if I hadn't already given up sugar, there's no way I could have gone cold turkey on the carbs.

# Thread: bitter melon - glucose and fat buster
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bitter-melon-glucose-and-fat-buster.5651/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ruby — 2013-01-28T04:30:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bitter-melon-glucose-and-fat-buster.5651/post-72960>

Supplementation with bitter melon (BM) slows weight gain and improves insulin sensitivity in rats fed an HF diet. The present results extend our previous study to indicate that the chronic consumption of
BM leads to a general decrease in the TG content of the major organs. Elevated plasma catecholamines and serum FFA suggest an enhanced sympathetic activity and lipolytic process. These
data are consistent with the view that bioactive ingredients in BM possess anti-obesity properties that might be beneﬁcial to body weight and glycaemic control.
http://web4.uwindsor.ca/units/biochem/web/biochemi.nsf/18e8732806421826852569830050331b/7a371e9af805f74e85256a4f00538021/$FILE/Karela6.pdf

# Thread: Week 1 is down - how's every one doing?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/week-1-is-down-hows-every-one-doing.5652/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Reset 2013
**Posts:** 65
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2013-01-28T15:12:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/week-1-is-down-hows-every-one-doing.5652/post-72985>

How's everyone doing on their 90 day optimal reset?

### kathylu — 2013-01-28T16:06:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/week-1-is-down-hows-every-one-doing.5652/post-72989>

Ok. Sleep is better, but not great. I've lost 5 lbs (which is not my focus at this point, by the way). I am hoping for great energy and to regain the ability to exercise, which I miss. The few times I have exercised vigorously recently, playing with my son, has resulted in hugely sore muscles the next day, which of course Jack says is due to mitochondrial inefficiency. I have seen so much improvement in my energy level from the taurine, acetyl L carnitine, d-ribose and ubiquinol that I am very excited to continue this path. My CT has been inconsistent, mostly face dunking the last week. Of course I tell myself that is because I have been working so much...but there is certainly an element of procrastination in there! The diet is easy...no cravings to speak of and I am barely hungry. Overall, a good first week!

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-28T23:45:37+0200
